gmw_00002096.name: 2MASX J08353333-0905302
gmw_00002096.overview: 2MASX J08353333-0905302 is an extended infrared galaxy [arXiv:2301.09999] that is identified as the counterpart to the hard X-ray source SWIFT J0835.5-0902 [arXiv:2301.09999]. The galaxy exhibits mid-infrared colors typical of an active galactic nucleus and is classified as a broad-line active galaxy [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J08353333-0905302 as the extended infrared AGN counterpart of SWIFT J0835.5-0902, with AGN-like WISE colors and broad-line X-ray properties.

quotes.quote: SWIFT J0835.5-0902 As evident in the XRT image shown in Fig. a2 (left panel), here too we find two hard X-ray emitters associated with a single INTEGRAL/BAT source, although a unique identification is provided in the 157-month Swift/BAT catalogue 2018ApJS..235....4O . The first object ( 1), the one reported in Table tab1 , is located at RA(J2000) = 08 35 33.34 and Dec(J2000) = 09 05 30.2 (3.5 arcsec X-ray positional uncertainty) and associated with the extended infrared object 2MASX J08353333-0905302. This galaxy is reported in Simbad with no redshift, while NED provides a photometric redshift of z=0.1124. The WISE colours, W1-W2 = 0.86 and W2-W3 = 2.87, are typical of AGN 2015ApJS..221...12S ; the source is listed in the UV-bright QSO survey of 2016AJ....152...25M and it is also identified with an active galaxy from multi-waveband analysis 2012ApJ...751...52E . The lack of intrinsic absorption in the X-ray spectrum indicates that this is most likely a broad line AGN.

gmw_00002097.name: LEDA 140000
gmw_00002097.overview: LEDA 140000 is an active galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.022 [arXiv:2301.09999]. It is classified as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy and is associated with the X-ray emitter IGR J12418+7805 [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper associates LEDA 140000 with IGR J12418+7805, classifying it as a z=0.022 Seyfert 1.9 X-ray emitter and estimating its 2–10 keV flux.

quotes.quote: IGR J12418+7805 Another interesting case is IGR J12418+7805, where possibly two objects contribute to the overall emission seen by INTEGRAL but, unfortunately, no Swift/XRT observation is available. We found one source lying within the IBIS error circle, which is clearly reported as an X-ray emitter both in the ROSAT Bright Source 1996IAUC.6420....2V and in the XMM Slew Survey 2008A&A...480..611S , and is therefore listed in Table tab1 . The source is associated with the galaxy LEDA 140000 at a redshift of z=0.022, which is classified as a Seyfert 1.9 in the 2010A&A...518A..10V catalogue and has a 0.2-12 keV flux of 2.1 10 ^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 sec ^ 1 . Assuming a typical value 1.8 for the power law photon index, we have extrapolated the 2-10 keV flux value reported in Table tab1 .

gmw_00002098.name: SWIFT J1937.5-4021
gmw_00002098.overview: SWIFT J1937.5-4021 is an active galaxy identified with LEDA 588288 [arXiv:2301.09999]. Optical spectroscopy classifies the object as a Seyfert 2 galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0193 [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SWIFT J1937.5-4021 with LEDA 588288, confirms it as a Seyfert 2 galaxy from X-shooter spectroscopy, and measures z=0.0193.

quotes.quote: SWIFT J1937.5-4021 SWIFT J1937.5-4021 is reported as an unidentified object in the 2022MNRAS.510.4796K list but we found that the most likely soft X-ray counterpart for both IBIS and BAT detection is the XRT source located at RA(J2000) = 19 37 13.47 and Dec(J2000) = -40 16 14.84 (3.6 arcsec error radius), detected at 27 in the 0.3-10 keV band (20 above 3 keV) and well inside the IBIS error circle (see source 1 in Fig. a2 , left panel). This X-ray source is also reported as an XMM Slew source with a 0.2-12 keV flux of 1.37 10 ^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and associated with the galaxy LEDA 588288 with z=0.075 in the Simbad archive but reported in NED with a photometric redshift of z=0.055776. Our analysis of the X-shooter optical spectrum of this source allowed us to confirm its nature, classifying it as Seyfert 2 galaxy, and measure its true redshift of z=0.0193 (see section 4.2).

gmw_00002099.name: UGC 12237
gmw_00002099.overview: UGC 12237 is a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2301.09999]. The host galaxy is viewed edge-on, with an axial ratio of 0.24 [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 12237 as one of two AGN within the AX J2254.3+1146 IBIS uncertainty region, classifying it as a Seyfert 2 in an edge-on host.

quotes.quote: AX J2254.3+1146 also SWIFT J2254.2+1147 A/B This is another case where two AGN fall within the IBIS positional uncertainty, although one is well inside the error circle while the other is just at its border but inside the 99 error circle (see Fig. a3 ). Both sources are well detected above 3 keV, they have similar redshifts but no interaction is evident between the two. The first object ( 1), UGC 12237, is a Seyfert 2 galaxy, while the other ( 2) is a Seyfert 1 (UGC 12243) according to 2010A&A...518A..10V ; both AGN are hosted in edge on galaxies of 0.24 and 0.34 axial ratio (major over minor axis), respectively. This is also an interesting set of sources to be observed with the NuSTAR observatory in order to study both AGN as already done for IGR J16058-7253 2021MNRAS.507.3423M .

gmw_00002100.name: UGC 12243
gmw_00002100.overview: UGC 12243 is an edge-on galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2301.09999]. While historically classified as a Seyfert 1, more recent analyses suggest it may be a type 2 active galactic nucleus and a potential Compton-thick source [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper flags UGC 12243 as an anomalous AGN with a low soft-to-hard X-ray ratio, possible type 2 classification, and potential Compton-thick nature.

quotes.quote: AX J2254.3+1146 also SWIFT J2254.2+1147 A/B This is another case where two AGN fall within the IBIS positional uncertainty, although one is well inside the error circle while the other is just at its border but inside the 99 error circle (see Fig. a3 ). Both sources are well detected above 3 keV, they have similar redshifts but no interaction is evident between the two. The first object ( 1), UGC 12237, is a Seyfert 2 galaxy, while the other ( 2) is a Seyfert 1 (UGC 12243) according to 2010A&A...518A..10V ; both AGN are hosted in edge on galaxies of 0.24 and 0.34 axial ratio (major over minor axis), respectively.
A further anomalous case is that of AX J2254.7+1146(2)/UGC 12243, which as already discussed, is a type 1 AGN with no absorption but with a very low soft to hard X-ray flux ratio.

For this reason, the source may have a highly absorbed soft X-ray spectrum which, if not properly corrected for the true column density value, provides again a wrong estimate of the flux ratio. In support of this last hypothesis we note that 2022ApJS..261....4O classify UGC 12243 as a type 2 AGN contrary to previous claim that it is a Seyfert 1 2010A&A...518A..10V . If this is true, then UGC 12243 could be another local Compton thick AGN, but in order to understand the true nature of this source, good quality X-ray observations are clearly needed.

gmw_00002101.name: IGR J19577+3339
gmw_00002101.overview: IGR J19577+3339 is an active galactic nucleus candidate and a strong radio emitter [arXiv:2301.09999]. The source is used as a flat-spectrum radio quasar calibrator and exhibits a bent jet on the parsec scale [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper says radio evidence establishes IGR J19577+3339 as an AGN candidate, noting strong radio emission, flat-spectrum quasar calibration use, and a parsec-scale bent jet.

quotes.quote: It is worth noting that radio data analysis performed in this work was crucial in unveiling the AGN nature of several objects as in the case of IGR J19577+3339. This source is the only one with just one WISE colour and no other diagnostic indicator of being extragalactic. IGR J19577+3339 is instead a strong radio emitter (see Fig. r1 , left panel in the Appendix) which is used in the literature as a flat-spectrum radio quasar calibrator 2020A&A...644A.159C ; a central bright component is visible in VLASS, with some additional emission at about 5 arcsec towards NE and SW, most probably not correlated with the source. Previous VLBI studies at 2.3 and 8.6 GHz 2012A&A...544A..34P showed a bent jet on the pc-scale.

gmw_00002102.name: IGR J16005-4645
gmw_00002102.overview: IGR J16005-4645 is a strong radio source that likely hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2301.09999]. It exhibits a core-jet structure with a steep radio spectrum, suggesting a possible radio galaxy morphology viewed at a large angle [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper describes IGR J16005-4645 as a strong, steep-spectrum radio source with core-jet structure, possible radio-galaxy morphology, and likely AGN X-ray counterpart.

quotes.quote: Finally, we note that two other objects, namely PKS 1413-36 and IGR J16005-4645, show strong radio fluxes above the 1 Jy level; the first is associated with a radio galaxy of large size (around 430 kpc, see Fig. r2 left panel), while the second one is so far still morphologically unclassified (although it is reported as extended in some surveys like ATCA, 2012MNRAS.422.1527M ) and poorly studied at radio frequencies. The RACS image of IGR J16005-4645 (see Fig. r2 , right panel) shows a core+jet like structure, but, combining data from various surveys, the radio spectrum is steep (-0.7/-1) over the entire 0.150-20 GHz range. This suggests a large viewing angle and a possible radio galaxy morphology with a lobe pointing towards us. We also note that the radio and X-ray positions are displaced by roughly 28 arcsec, this could be interpreted as a radio galaxy where the radio position refers to one of the two lobes while the X-ray position coincides with the source core.

In any case, the X-ray source, actually the secure counterpart of the INTEGRAL emitter, is likely an AGN not only on the basis of its WISE colours and possible radio emission, but also according to the SIX index developed by 2012ApJ...751...52E . This index was empirically constructed by combing infrared colours over the 2-10 micron waveband (to ensure an AGN like continuum) and the distance to the nearest X-ray source. A value below zero of the resulting SIX index (-0.258 in our case) indicates an object with a high likelihood (greater than 95 ) of being an AGN.

gmw_00002103.name: IGR J04085-6546
gmw_00002103.overview: IGR J04085-6546 is an active galactic nucleus candidate classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2301.09999]. Its optical spectrum displays broad Balmer emission lines and potentially a narrow [OIII] emission line at a redshift of z=0.116 [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IGR J04085-6546 as a Seyfert 1 AGN candidate despite transient contamination, citing broad Balmer emission and possible narrow [OIII].

quotes.quote: As we mentioned before, 3 objects deserve more in-depth discussion as their optical spectra are somehow different to that expected from an emission line AGN. The first source is IGR J04085-6546, associated with LEDA 310383: although the diagnostics all point to an AGN, this source has been associated with a transient event (OGLE 2013-SN-90) occurring on October 6, 2013 2013ATel.5488....1W , originating from a galaxy and lasting several months. The follow-up PESSTO observation confirmed the AGN class of the event 2013ATel.5537....1S despite the source photometric class pointed to a SN of type I 2014AcA....64..197W .

Our analysis of the PESSTO spectrum, shows the presence of broad H _ and H _ emissions and possibly a narrow [ OIII ] 5007 emission line, at a redshift z=0.116 0.001. However, since the spectrum was acquired about one month after the announcement of the OGLE transient, we cannot exclude a contribution from it, especially in the H _ region. Nevertheless, we suggest a Seyfert 1 AGN classification for this source.

gmw_00002104.name: IGR J08297-4250
gmw_00002104.overview: IGR J08297-4250 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1502.05054, arXiv:2301.09999] that may be a highly absorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1502.05054] or a BL Lac object [arXiv:2301.09999]. It is possibly hosted in a merging galaxy pair [arXiv:1502.05054] and exhibits a core-plus-jet radio morphology [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper argues IGR J08297--4250 is likely a highly absorbed AGN, possibly a Seyfert 2 hosted in a merging galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: Thus, we place IGR J04059+5416 in the class of Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN), and we suggest that IGR J08297--4250 is also an AGN. If this identification is correct, the near-IR images suggest that the host galaxy of IGR J08297--4250 may be merging with a smaller nearby galaxy. For IGR J04059+5416, the 0.3--86 keV spectrum from Chandra and INTEGRAL is consistent with an absorbed power-law with a column density of N_ H = (3.1^ +2.0 _ 1.5 ) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and a photon index of = 1.4 0.7 , and we suggest that it is a Seyfert galaxy. For IGR J08297--4250, the photon index is similar, = 1.5 0.8 , but the source is highly absorbed ( N_ H = (6.1^ +10.1 _ 4.3 ) 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ).

For IGR J08297--4250, the Chandra + INTEGRAL fits indicate a power-law with an index consistent with what would be expected for a Seyfert galaxy, and the high absorption may indicate a Seyfert 2 classification. Although the 6.0 keV emission line is only significant at the 2- level, if its rest-frame energy is 6.4 keV (neutral iron K ), then the redshift is z = 0.07 . For an unabsorbed 0.3--10 keV flux of 2 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , the corresponding luminosity would be 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , which is typical for hard X-ray selected Seyfert 2 AGN ajello12 . estimated that up to 25 of the AGN detected above 15 keV by Swift /BAT are found in hosts in an apparent state of merging with a nearby ( < 30 kpc) galaxy. This, together with the fact that AGN luminosity increases for decreasing separation of the galaxy pair koss2012 , highlights the role that merging has in triggering AGN activity. It is thus not unlikely to find an AGN hosted in a merging pair while following up on newly detected INTEGRAL sources. The majority of INTEGRAL Seyfert-like AGN are detected at z 0.1, implying that the separation between CXOU J082941.0--425158 and CXOU J082940.7--425143 (assuming they are a merging pair of galaxies) is < 30 kpc. found that systems with a close companion (e.g. < 30 kpc) often display AGN activity in both hosts, similarly to what we find here. Moreover, some of the hosts of close merging pairs present a disrupted morphology koss2012 , which might explain the offset of CXOU J082940.7--425143 from the brightest part of the extended near-IR emission. We thus believe that, on the basis of the evidence gathered so far, IGR J08297--4250 is an AGN that might possibly be hosted in a merging pair.

sources.summary: The paper suggests IGR J08297-4250 is a BL Lac: its spectrum lacks lines, radio morphology is core-plus-jet-like, and WISE colors place it in the BL Lac locus.

quotes.quote: Another interesting source is IGR J08297-4250. In this case the low S/N X-Shooter spectrum does not allow us to infer the nature of this source. However, the absence of emission and absorption lines, especially at z=0, suggests this source is not a Galactic objects. The fact that the radio morphology of the source is core plus jet like (see section 4.1 and Fig. r1 , right) seems to point to a BL Lac class; the absence of emission lines in the optical spectra further validates this hypothesis and we therefore suggest that IGR J08297-4250 is a BL Lac with unknown redshift.
In the case of IGR J08297-4250, W3-W4 is 2.56, which locates the source in the blazar region and well inside the BL Lac locus.

gmw_00002105.name: SWIFT J1839.1-5717
gmw_00002105.overview: SWIFT J1839.1-5717 is an active galactic nucleus located well below the Galactic plane [arXiv:2301.09999]. It is classified specifically as a BL Lacertae object based on its WISE colors and a smooth optical spectrum that lacks emission lines [arXiv:2301.09999].

sources.summary: The paper argues SWIFT J1839.1-5717 is not stellar but an AGN, specifically a BL Lac, based on WISE colors and a smooth featureless spectrum.

quotes.quote: A more controversial case is that of SWIFT J1839.1-5717: also in this source no emission lines are detected in the optical spectrum suggesting a similar classification as for IGR J08297-4250. However we notice that 2022ApJS..261....6K , using the same optical spectrum, classify this source as a galactic object, i.e. a star, given the detection of a CaII absorption line. The object is located well below the Galactic plane, has WISE colours typical of an AGN and not of a stellar object and its optical spectrum is characterised by a smooth continuum with no apparent absorption lines at redshift z=0. This gives us confidence to rule out the stellar nature for SWIFT J1839.1-5717 and given the above results, also in this case we suggest the source classification as an AGN, and more specifically as a BL Lac.

gmw_00002106.name: The host galaxy at z=2.1992
aliases_external: GRB 050922C host galaxy / GRB 050922C line of sight
gmw_00002106.overview: The host galaxy at z=2.1992 is a high-redshift system identified along a gamma-ray burst line of sight [arXiv:0811.0989]. Its complex interstellar medium contains at least seven components [arXiv:0811.0989], exhibiting neutral, low-ionization, high-ionization, and strong fine-structure absorption lines [arXiv:0811.0989].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB host at z=2.1992 as a high-redshift system with complex ISM absorption, many metal lines, fine-structure lines, and at least seven components.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy at z=2.1992 This is the system with the highest number of features in the UVES spectrum. Fig. ove shows the Ly- absorption at 3870 . The redshift inferred from this feature is confirmed by a high number of metal-line transitions, the narrowest of which allow a more precise determination: z=2.1992 0.0005 . This system exhibits a large number of absorption lines, from the neutral hydrogen Ly- and neutral metal-absorption lines (OI, NI and FeI) to low-ionization lines ( C II , Si II , Mg II , Al II , Fe II , Al III ) and high-ionization absorption features ( C IV , Si IV , N V , P V , O VI ). In addition, strong fine-structure lines ( C II* , Si II* , O I* , O II** , Fe II* , OI II** ) have been identified.

Five intervening systems between z = 2.077 and z = 1.5664 have been identified along the GRB line of sight, in addition to the main system at z=2.1992 , which we identify as the redshift of the GRB host galaxy. The spectrum shows that the ISM of this galaxy is complex, with at least seven components contributing to the main absorption system. These systems show both high- and low-ionization lines, fine-structure lines and neutral element absorption lines, thus suggesting wide ranges of distances to the GRB site and physical properties of the absorbing clouds.

gmw_00002107.name: I20210N
gmw_00002107.overview: I20210N is the northern member of the interacting galaxy pair IRAS 20210+1121 [arXiv:2107.12242]. Optical spectroscopy classifies the galaxy as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:2107.12242], which hosts a highly obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.12242].

sources.summary: I20210N is the northern member of the interacting IRAS 20210+1121 pair, with an obscured central engine and Seyfert 2 classification from optical spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: I20210N continuum and emission-line fitting We modeled the I20210N continuum under the assumption of a negligible AGN contribution to the continuum emission. This is justified by the fact that the I20210N central engine ( L_ X = 4.7 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ) is highly obscured by a column density N_ H 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 Pic10 and, therefore, no light from accretion activity is visible. We subtract the stellar continuum with absorption lines from the I20210N galaxy spectrum by using the penalized-pixel fitting public code pPXF Cap04,Cap12,Cap17 .

In this article, we present an optical spectroscopic analysis of the AGN pair hosted in the interacting system IRAS 20210+1121 I20210; P90; Hei95,Bur01,Dav02,Arr04,Pic10 at z = 0.056 . This study is based on spectroscopy taken through a slit aligned along the nuclei of the two interacting galaxies. The high-quality data taken at the Telescopio Nazionale Galileo allowed us to perform a detailed study of the light emission from both components and from their surrounding environment in the rest-frame wavelength range of 3500 -- 7300 , with the possibility of a comprehensive characterization of this interacting galaxy pair. Here we summarize our main findings: I20210N, the northern member of the I20210 system, can be definitively classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with an exceptional stellar velocity dispersion of _v^* 400 km s ^ 1 , hosting an AGN powered by a black hole with M_ BH 3 10^7 M _ that radiates at 1 of its Eddington limit.

gmw_00002108.name: I20210S
gmw_00002108.overview: I20210S is an active galactic nucleus-dominated, southern galaxy with quasar-like energetics [arXiv:2107.12242]. Its optical spectrum is heavily reddened by intrinsic dust, and it exhibits evidence of an ionized gas outflow [arXiv:2107.12242].

sources.summary: I20210S is modeled as an AGN-dominated, reddened southern member with quasar-like energetics, broad blueshifted emission, and evidence for an ionized gas outflow.

quotes.quote: We assume for I20210S an AGN-dominated object, that is, one exhibiting a power-law continuum reddened by foreground dust; such an assumption is well motivated by the quasar-like energetics of I20210S e.g., L_ bol 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 ; Pic10 . I20210S nuclear continuum and emission-line fitting The optical spectrum of I20210S is heavily reddened by intrinsic dust, which is particularly evident by the lack of a flux rise bluewards the \ emission.

Differently from I20210N, we used two components for each transition to account for the total emission profile in the spectrum, as was also done by Arr14 . The main narrow components obey the prescriptions presented in Sect. sec:fitprocn ; the additional emission, as already found by P90, consists in a broad line blueshifted by 400 km s ^ 1 with FWHM 2000 km s ^ 1 . The possibility that such features are an effect of the orientation of the I20210S narrow-line region (NLR) as described in Bio17 is ruled out. In fact, for emission lines associated with permitted transitions the asymmetries would be redshifted with respect to the line center (see e.g., their figure 3). Therefore, we can conclude that I20210S exhibits evidence of an ionized gas outflow. htbp .49 .49 To account for this additional emission, we include the broad components in the fit of the I20210S line profiles anchoring the blueshift and FWHM values of the transitions affected by severe blending -- namely, the \ 3726,3729 doublet, the H + \ 4363, the + 2 \ system and the 2 \ doublet -- to those of the \ 5007 (see Table tab:pars ).

gmw_00002109.name: S4 0954+65
gmw_00002109.overview: S4 0954+65 is a blazar active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of approximately z = 0.367 [arXiv:2311.01738]. It hosts a central black hole with an estimated mass of 3.3 billion solar masses and exhibits distinctive multi-waveband variability across optical, ultraviolet, X-ray, and gamma-ray bands [arXiv:2311.01738].

sources.summary: The paper studies S4 0954+65 as a blazar at z 0.367 with a 3.3e8 solar-mass black hole and distinctive optical/UV and gamma-ray variability.

quotes.quote: In this investigation, a sample of blazars (S4 0954+65, PKS 0903-57, and 4C +01.02) has been selected for a comprehensive study of multi-waveband variability within the time span of MJD 54683 - 59970. These sources are positioned at (RA: 149.69, Dec: 65.56) for S4 0954+65 with redshift, z 0.367 lawrence1986new,lawrence1996optical,stickel1993complete , (RA: 136.22, Dec: -57.58) for PKS 0903-57 with z 0.695 thompson1990spectroscopy , and (RA: 17.16, Dec: 1.58) for 4C +01.02 with z 2.099 hewett1995large . The BH mass of S4 0954+65 is 3.3 10^8 M_ , estimated from the width of the H _ line fan2004black .

In the case of S4 0954+65, the observed distinctive pattern in Fvar distribution suggests that optical/UV and ray emissions are associated with the synchrotron and inverse Compton (IC) processes involving high-energy electrons. On the other hand, the X-ray emission is linked to the IC process involving the lower-energy tail of the electron distribution. This observed variability can be attributed to relativistic electron cooling, wherein higher-energy electrons undergo more rapid cooling compared to their lower-energy counterparts. As a result, we observe higher variability in the emissions originating from high-energy electrons.

gmw_00002110.name: PKS 0903-57
gmw_00002110.overview: PKS 0903-57 is a blazar [arXiv:2311.01738] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2107.02702, arXiv:2310.10206] that features an extended radio jet pointing northwest [arXiv:2310.10206]. The galaxy is a bright gamma-ray source [arXiv:2103.13657] that exhibits gamma-ray flaring [arXiv:2107.02702, arXiv:2310.10206] and a fourfold increase in gamma-ray variability compared to the ultraviolet band [arXiv:2311.01738].

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0903-57 as a selected blazar at z 0.695, adopts a probable black-hole mass, and finds gamma-ray variability about four times the UV variability.

quotes.quote: In this investigation, a sample of blazars (S4 0954+65, PKS 0903-57, and 4C +01.02) has been selected for a comprehensive study of multi-waveband variability within the time span of MJD 54683 - 59970. These sources are positioned at (RA: 149.69, Dec: 65.56) for S4 0954+65 with redshift, z 0.367 lawrence1986new,lawrence1996optical,stickel1993complete , (RA: 136.22, Dec: -57.58) for PKS 0903-57 with z 0.695 thompson1990spectroscopy , and (RA: 17.16, Dec: 1.58) for 4C +01.02 with z 2.099 hewett1995large .

For PKS 0903-57, we did not find an estimated BH mass value. However, we have adopted the most probable BH mass value between 3-6 10^8 M_ from the distribution of BH mass of all radio-loud AGN from Ghisellini2015 .

In the cases of PKS 0903-57 and 4C +01.02, the ray emissions display a higher level of variability in comparison with the optical/UV and X-ray emissions. Specifically, for PKS 0903-57, an increment in ray variability is approximately fourfold when compared to the UV band (see middle panel of Figure fig:Fvar vs energy ), while for 4C +01.02, it increases by a factor of 2.5 (see right panel of Figure fig:Fvar vs energy ). A similar variability pattern has been reported in other blazars Abdo_2011, Rani2016, shah2021unveiling, malik2022multiwavelength, Prince23 as well.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0903-57 as a redshift-0.6956 blazar candidate and bright gamma-ray source, analyzing its 2020 flaring and arguing Compton dominance indicates an FSRQ/LBL-type blazar.

quotes.quote: PKS 0903-57 is classified as blazar candidate of uncertain type (BCU) in the fourth Fermi LAT catalog 4FGL; 2020ApJS..247...33A . Importantly, the presence of source in the first Fermi LAT catalog 1FGL; 2010ApJS..188..405A shows that PKS 0903-57 is bright ray source. The source is located at redshift of z 0.6956 1990PASP..102.1235T with coordinates R.A.: 136.221579 deg, Dec.: -57.58494 deg 2004AJ....128.2593F . PKS 0903-57 has been reported in active state by number of Astronomy telegrams, however, detailed study of the source has not been carried out so far.

The long-term flaring of PKS 0903-57 together with the availability of simultaneous observations from Fermi LAT and Swift XRT/UVOT has made possible for the first time to study the broadband temporal and spectral properties of PKS 0903-57 in detail.

Therefore in PKS 0903-57 , the observation of Compton dominance and the requirement of SSC and EC process to produce high energy emission indicates that PKS 0903-57 is more likely a FSRQ/LBL type blazar.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0903-57 as a BL Lac blazar, identifying 2018 and 2020 gamma-ray flares, analyzing substructures, and modeling multi-wavelength spectral energy distributions and jet properties.

quotes.quote: A detailed study of the BL Lacertae PKS 0903-57 has been done for the first time with 12 years of Fermi Large Area Telescope data. We have identified two bright gamma-ray flares in 2018 and 2020. Many sub-structures were observed during multiple time binning of these flares. We have performed detailed temporal and spectral study on all the sub-structures separately. A single-zone emission model is used for time-dependent leptonic modeling of the multi-wavelength spectral energy distributions.

PKS 0903-57 is a BL Lac refId0 type object also known as 3FGL J0904.8-5734 or 4FGL J0904.9-5734, located at redshift z= 0.695 Thompson_1990 with RA= 136.222 deg or 09h04m53.1790s DEC=-57.5849 deg or -57d35m05.783s Fey_2004 .

sources.summary: The paper presents preliminary VLT/FORS2 spectra of the blazar PKS 0903-57, notes its redshift remains uncertain, and says further observations are needed to determine the redshift.

quotes.quote: We present selected results from the two papers published by the task force and preliminary VLT/FORS2 spectra of the blazar PKS0903-57 with an uncertain redshift.

The right side of the figure shows three preliminary VLT/FORS2 spectra of the blazar PKS 0903-57. At the time of this proceedings, the analysis of the three spectra to determine the significance level of the detected emission feature -- marked with the vertical dashed line in the figure -- has not been finalized. Further observations are needed to determine the redshift of PKS 0903-57.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0903 573 as a Fermi-detected source now classified as a BL Lac, with gamma-ray flaring during the first S-band epoch and a northwest jet.

quotes.quote: 0903 573: 0903 573 was first detected in the GeV range by Fermi LAT in 2015 ojha15 . The source has been considered a blazar candidate of unknown type until recently, when it was classified as a BL Lac object by pita17 . 0903 573 exhibited a complex ray flaring activity during 2018 and 2020 mondal21 that coincides with our first S-band observation. Our image reveals a core and an extended jet in the northwest direction.

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative 2.8-day TESS quasi-periodic oscillation in ATPMN J090453.4 573503, significant in both GLSP and WWZ analyses.

quotes.quote: Fig. 1(a) shows the timing analysis results obtained for the 25-day sector 37 (cycle 3) observation of ATPMN J090453.4 573503. A strong signal at a period of around 2.8 days is at least 3 significant in the GLSP and WWZ results regardless of which of the red noise models is employed. The signal is found to be persistent throughout most of the observation as shown in the colour density (violet indicating the least power and red the most) plot of WWZ power (left plot of bottom panel) which also shows the presence of non-stationarity in the observation.

gmw_00002111.name: 4C +01.02
gmw_00002111.overview: 4C +01.02 is a blazar located at a redshift of approximately z = 2.099 [arXiv:2311.01738]. It hosts a central black hole with an estimated mass of 3 billion solar masses [arXiv:2311.01738].

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C +01.02 as a z 2.099 blazar with a 3e9 solar-mass black hole and compares its PSD and DRW break frequencies.

quotes.quote: In this investigation, a sample of blazars (S4 0954+65, PKS 0903-57, and 4C +01.02) has been selected for a comprehensive study of multi-waveband variability within the time span of MJD 54683 - 59970. These sources are positioned at (RA: 149.69, Dec: 65.56) for S4 0954+65 with redshift, z 0.367 lawrence1986new,lawrence1996optical,stickel1993complete , (RA: 136.22, Dec: -57.58) for PKS 0903-57 with z 0.695 thompson1990spectroscopy , and (RA: 17.16, Dec: 1.58) for 4C +01.02 with z 2.099 hewett1995large .
For 4C +01.02, a BH mass of 3 10^9 M_ was found through SED and spectropolarimetry modeling schutte2022modeling .

The break frequency is defined as the f _b = 1/(2 _ DRW ) and the _ DRW in all the cases is found as 0.0043, 0.0043, and 0.0007 day^ 1 for S4 0954+65, PKS 0903-57, and 4C +01.02, respectively. The break frequencies derived from PSD analysis for S4 0954+65 and PKS 0903-57 are consistent with results obtained from DRW modeling. However, in the case of 4C +01.02, the break frequency derived in both PSD analysis and DRW modeling is not quite consistent and it is because the light curve length is not sufficient to derive an actual PSD as can be seen in the PSD plot.

gmw_00002112.name: KKs 3
gmw_00002112.overview: KKs 3 is a highly isolated dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 2.12 megaparsecs away [arXiv:1411.1674, arXiv:1508.01289]. It is a gas-poor system composed primarily of old stars [arXiv:1508.01289, arXiv:1706.07344], featuring a nuclear globular cluster at its center [arXiv:1508.01289, arXiv:1706.07344].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/ACS observations of isolated dwarf spheroidal KKs 3, measuring a 2.12 Mpc distance, low luminosity, 2.3e7 solar stellar mass, and predominantly ancient star formation.

quotes.quote: Observations of the highly isolated dwarf spheroidal galaxy KKs 3 = [ KK2000 ] 03 with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) are presented. We measured the galaxy distance of 2.12 0.07 Mpc using the Tip of Red Giant Branch (TRGB) method. The total blue absolute magnitude of the galaxy is estimated as M_B = -10.8 mag.

We briefly discuss the star formation history of KKs 3 derived from its colour-magnitude diagram. According to our calculation, the total stellar mass of the galaxy is 2.3 10^7M_ , and most stars (74 ) were formed at an early epoch more than 12 Gyr ago. A full description of the properties of the colour-magnitude diagram requires some extension of star formation in metallicity and age.

Here we report the discovery in this volume of a dwarf spheroidal system KKs 3 ( [ KK2000 ] 03 = SGC 0224.3--7345 in the nomenclature of the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database) at a distance of D = 2.12 0.07 Mpc and well removed from any other known galaxy. The low surface brightness object KKs 3 with J2000 coordinates: R.A. \ = 02^h24^m44 4 , Dec. \ = 73^ 30 51 was detected in full sky surveys by kk2000 and whiting2002 as a potential dSph galaxy neighbouring the Local Group.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on KKs3, measuring a globular-cluster velocity, deriving surface photometry, placing the isolated dSph in the local Hubble flow, and noting old, gas-poor stellar content.

quotes.quote: We present the SALT spectroscopy of a globular cluster in the center of the nearby isolated dSph galaxy KKs3 situated at a distance of 2.12 Mpc. Its heliocentric radial velocity is 316 7 km s ^ 1 that corresponds to V_ LG = 112 km s ^ 1 in the Local Group (LG) reference frame. We use its distance and velocity along with the data on other 35 field galaxies in the proximity of the LG to trace the local Hubble flow.

The measured total magnitude and colour turned out V = 14.47 0.05 and (V - I) = 0.77 0.03 respectively. The estimated errors include the photometry and sky background uncertainties, as well as the transformation errors from instrumental ACS magnitudes to the standard V and I magnitudes (Sirianni et al. 2005). Azimuthally averaged surface brightness profiles for KKs3 were obtained by differentiating the galaxy growth curves with respect to semiaxes.

The measured radial velocity of the dSph galaxy KKs3 fixes its position by 45 km s ^ 1 lower than the regression line. The M31 galaxy exerts the greatest gravitational force on KKs3, although their mutual separation is too large for M31 to cause the high peculiar velocity of KKs3.

sources.summary: The paper describes KKs 3 as a highly isolated faint field dSph with old stellar populations, little gas or star formation, a nuclear cluster, and multiple past star-forming bursts.

quotes.quote: KKs 3 is a unique highly isolated dSph recently discovered by Karachentsev et al. (2015b). It is one of the faintest known field galaxies (Karachentsev et al. 2015a). The arguments are: the colour-magnitude diagram (CMD) of the object consisting only of old (age >1 Gyr) stars, the absolute magnitude M_V =-12.6 mag, effective surface brightness SB_ Ve = 24.9 mag/arcsec ^ 2 , neutral hydrogen mass-to-stellar mass ratio ( M_ HI /M^* = 10^ 2.34 ) estimated by the luminosity of the galaxy in the K-band and star formation rate per unit galactic luminosity (specific star formation rate) sSFR = 10^ 13.6 estimated from the ultraviolet flux (Karachentsev et al. 2015a).

There was no neutral hydrogen detected in KKs 3. The sSFR and M_ HI /M^* parameters presented by Karachentsev et al. 2015a indicate that the galaxy has transformed most of its gas into stars. Star formation histories of ESO 269-66 and KKs 3 look similar. Three star forming bursts likely occurred in KKs 3 12--14 , 4--6, and 0.8--2 Gyrs ago (Karachentsev et al. 2015b), where the oldest one was the most powerful with mean =-1.9 dex. Most stars (74 ) of the galaxy were formed during this period (Karachentsev et al. 2015b).

sources.summary: The paper compares KKs 3 as an extremely isolated dwarf spheroidal whose star-formation history, stellar density profiles, and nuclear cluster reveal age and metallicity gradients.

quotes.quote: We compare the properties of the stellar populations of the globular clusters and field stars in two dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs): ESO 269-66, a near neighbor of the giant S0 galaxy NGC 5128, and KKs 3, one of the few extremely isolated dSphs within 10 Mpc. The histories of star formation in these galaxies are known from previous work on deep stellar photometry using images from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). The age and metal content for the nuclear globular clusters in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66 are known from literature spectroscopic studies: T=12.6 billion years, [ Fe/H ] =-1.5 and -1.55 dex. We use the Sersic function to construct and analyze the profiles of the surface density of the stars with high and low metallicities (red and blue) in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66, and show that (1) the profiles of the density of red stars are steeper than those of blue stars, which is indicative of gradients of metallicity and age in the galaxies, and (2) the globular clusters in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66 contain roughly 4 and 40 , respectively, of all the old stars in the galaxies with metallicities [ Fe/H ] 1.5 1.6 dex and ages of 12-14 billion years.
It is probable that, because of its isolation, KKs 3 has lost a smaller fraction of old low-metallicity stars than ESO 269-66.

sources.summary: KKs3 is described as an isolated dwarf just outside the Local Group, with one massive globular cluster representing almost eight percent of its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: KKs3 (PGC 009140) This isolated dwarf has 1 GC ( M_V = -8.33 , M_ = 3.45 10^5 ) discovered in HST/ACS imaging by 2015AN....336..707K . The galaxy is located 2 Mpc just outside of the Local Group, with M_B = --10.8 ( M_ 4.5 10^6 ). Its sole GC represents almost 8 of the galaxy stellar mass. It has some HI gas but no HI rotation curve reported.

gmw_00002113.name: ESO 269-66
gmw_00002113.overview: ESO 269-66 is a nucleated dwarf spheroidal galaxy located near the giant elliptical galaxy Centaurus A [arXiv:1706.07344, arXiv:1002.0341]. The galaxy contains metal-poor stellar populations with a large metallicity dispersion [arXiv:1706.07344, arXiv:1002.0341], showing evidence of multiple past star-forming bursts [arXiv:1706.07344] but no ongoing star formation [arXiv:1002.0341].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 269-66 as a Centaurus A satellite dSph with multiple star-forming bursts, old metal-poor stars, large metallicity dispersion, and a studied nuclear globular cluster.

quotes.quote: ESO 269-66 is a dSph satellite of the peculiar giant elliptical Centaurus A = NGC 5128. The Cen A group with its numerous dwarf galaxies and tidal substructures is the only elliptical-dominated group within 10 Mpc (Karachentsev et al. 2007, Crnojevi et al. 2016). The projected separation between NGC 5128 and ESO 269-66 is 198 kpc, which is typical of dSphs (Karachentsev et al. 2005, 2013).

A considerable population of asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars as a signature of the 2-3 Gyrs old star formation was detected in ESO 269-66 (Crnojevi et al. 2011). Stellar photometry using Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical and infrared images indicates that at least three powerful star forming bursts happened in the galaxy 2 1.5, 3 1.5 (Crnojevi et al. 2011), and 12-14 Gyr ago (Makarova et al. 2007). The last of the mentioned events was the most powerful. The mean metallicity of the oldest red giant branch (RGB) stars in ESO 269-66 is 1.75 dex (Makarova et al. 2007). The stellar metallicity dispersion in ESO 269-66 is surprisingly large for a faint dSph (Karachentsev et al. 2007, Sharina et al. 2008).

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO269-66 as a nucleated dwarf elliptical in the Centaurus A subgroup, finding no recent star formation, little HI, metal-poor populations, and differing stellar-population properties among the target dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The six early--type dwarfs of our sample have a quite wide range of luminosities (see Table infogen ), and their morphological types vary from dwarf spheroidals (KKs57, KKs55, CenN and KK189), to dwarf ellipticals (KK197 and ESO269-66, the latter being a nucleated galaxy). What they have in common is the absence of any recent star formation (as traced by H , or young populations from CMD), nor is there any significant amount of HI detected that would allow for ongoing or future star formation. For example, bouchard07 investigated the gas content in three of our target dwarfs (KK189, KK197 and ESO269-66) and found only upper limits for their HI masses ( <1.6 , <2.9 and <1.0 10^ 5 M _ , respectively).

Just as the complex and different star formation histories seen in our neighbourghood, we see that the metallicity and stellar population properties in our target dwarfs differ from each other (e.g., median metallicity, metallicity spread, fraction of intermediate--age stars). However, it looks like in our sample there are no purely old dwarfs such as Ursa Minor or Draco in the Local Group. Coming to our results, all of the studied dwarfs are relatively metal--poor, as is expected for galaxies of such low luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper compares ESO 269-66 as a dwarf spheroidal near NGC 5128 with stellar population gradients, a massive old globular cluster, and greater stellar loss than KKs 3.

quotes.quote: We compare the properties of the stellar populations of the globular clusters and field stars in two dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs): ESO 269-66, a near neighbor of the giant S0 galaxy NGC 5128, and KKs 3, one of the few extremely isolated dSphs within 10 Mpc. The histories of star formation in these galaxies are known from previous work on deep stellar photometry using images from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). The age and metal content for the nuclear globular clusters in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66 are known from literature spectroscopic studies: T=12.6 billion years, [ Fe/H ] =-1.5 and -1.55 dex. We use the Sersic function to construct and analyze the profiles of the surface density of the stars with high and low metallicities (red and blue) in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66, and show that (1) the profiles of the density of red stars are steeper than those of blue stars, which is indicative of gradients of metallicity and age in the galaxies, and (2) the globular clusters in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66 contain roughly 4 and 40 , respectively, of all the old stars in the galaxies with metallicities [ Fe/H ] 1.5 1.6 dex and ages of 12-14 billion years.

ESO 269-66 has evidently lost a larger fraction of stars in the course of its evolution than has the isolated KKs 3. Based on the photometric data alone, is has not been possible to describe the star formation process in KKs 3 and ESO 269-66 in more detail and to discuss the reasons for the loss of stars.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ESO269-066 as an old-dominated Centaurus A dwarf elliptical with 2-5 Gyr intermediate-age populations that are more centrally concentrated than its old stars.

quotes.quote: We consider one dwarf spheroidal (CenA-dE1) and two dwarf elliptical (SGC1319.1-4216 and ESO269-066) galaxies that are dominated by an old population. The most recent periods of star formation are estimated to have taken place between 2 and 5 Gyr ago for SGC1319.1-4216 and ESO269-066, and approximately 9 Gyr ago for CenA-dE1. For ESO269-066, we find that the intermediate-age populations are significantly more centrally concentrated than the predominantly old underlying stars.
There are in total 13 AGB candidates for CenA-dE1, 41 for SGC1319.1-4216 and 176 for ESO269-066.

gmw_00002114.name: BRI 0952-0115
gmw_00002114.overview: BRI 0952-0115 is a doubly imaged, gravitationally lensed quasar at z = 4.4 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:1211.0695] that is powered by a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1211.0695]. The system exhibits both active galactic nucleus activity and extreme starbursts [arXiv:2312.10267], which are likely boosted by an ongoing merger with a nearby companion disk galaxy [arXiv:1205.4035].

sources.summary: The paper probes BRI 0952 0115 as a high-redshift quasar whose ISM shows extreme heating, likely star-forming emission, gas kinematics, and both AGN and starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Our aim is to better understand the processes governing the interstellar medium (ISM) of the quasar BRI 0952 0952 at z = 4.432 , specifically with regard to the individual heating processes at work and to place the quasar in an evolutionary context. We analyzed ALMA archival bands 3, 4, and 6 data and combined the results with high-resolution band-7 ALMA observations of the quasar. We detect (2--1), ( ^ 2 P_ 3/2 ^ 2 P_ 1/2 ), CO(5--4), CO(7--6), CO(12--11), OH ^ 2 _ 1/2 (3/2-1/2) , ( 2_ 11 2_ 02 ), and we report a tentative detection of OH ^+ .

Modeling from the CO SLED suggests that there are extreme heating mechanisms operating within the quasar in the form of star formation or AGN activity; however, with the current data it remains unclear which of the two is the preferred mechanism as both models reasonably reproduce the observed CO line fluxes. The updated lensing model suggests a velocity gradient across the line, suggestive of on-going kinematical processes within the quasar. We find that the emission in BRI 0952 is likely correlated with star-forming regions of the ISM. We use the molecular gas mass from to calculate a depletion time for the quasar. We conclude that BRI 0952 0952 is a quasar with a significant AGN contribution while also showing signs of extreme starburst activity, indicating that the quasar could be in a transitional phase between a starburst-dominated stage and an AGN-dominated stage.

sources.summary: The paper resolves [CII] emission in the lensed z=4.4 quasar host BRI 0952-0115, interpreting compact star formation and an extended companion as an interacting merger system.

quotes.quote: We use the new IRAM PdBI receivers at 350 GHz to map this line in BRI 0952-0115, the host galaxy of a lensed quasar at z=4.4 previously found to be very bright in [ CII ] emission. The [ CII ] emission is clearly resolved and our data allow us to resolve two [ CII ] lensed images associated with the optical quasar images. We find that the star formation, as traced by [ CII ] , is distributed over a region of about 1 kpc in size near the quasar nucleus, and we infer a star formation surface density 150 M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 , similar to that observed in local ULIRGs. We also reveal another [ CII ] component, extended over 12 kpc, and located at about 10 kpc from the quasar. We suggest that this component is a companion disk galaxy, in the process of merging with the quasar host, whose rotation field is distorted by the interaction with the quasar host, and where star formation, although intense, is more diffuse.

In the case of BRI 0952-0115 the galaxy interaction has both boosted compact star formation in the central region and black hole accretion in one of the two galaxies, while in the other galaxy the interaction has increased star formation all over the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: The paper presents BRI0952 0115 as the highest-redshift known lensed quasar, with a fundamental-plane lens redshift estimate and possible absorber contributions to lensing.

quotes.quote: BRI0952 0115 : A doubly imaged quasar, with 1 0 separation, discovered by McMahon Irwin (1992). At z=4.426 (the redshift measurement was refined by Storrie-Lombardi et al. 1996), this is the highest redshift lensed quasar known. The ``fundamental-plane redshift'' of the lensing galaxy is z=0.41 0.05 (Kochanek et al. 2000).

Storrie-Lombardi et al. (1996) identified several metal absorption systems, at redshifts of 1.993 , 3.294 , 3.475 , 3.719 , and 4.024 in the spectrum of the lensed quasar, one or more of which could contribute to the lensing. We note, however, that if these absorbers indeed contribute to the lensing then the fundamental-plane redshift may be biased toward lower redshifts. Even with over 3 hours of exposure time, the signal from the galaxy is too low to securely identify any features in the spectrum.

sources.summary: BRI 0952-0115 is very well deconvolved; its host emission appears in the background, the continuum is microlensed, and decomposition reveals microlensing of a significant line fraction.

quotes.quote: (g) BRI 0952-0115: Deconvolution: The deconvolution is very good. Spatially resolved narrow  is visible in the background image. This emission is not produced in the QSO but in the host galaxy of this remote quasar. Chromaticity: Because of the Lyman break, we do not estimate A in both sides of the emission but only in the continuum redward of . The ratio B/A seems however flat from the blue to the red with only an imprint of the broad emission lines. Therefore, the continuum emission is microlensed.

sources.summary: The paper describes BRI0952-0115 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: BRI0952-0115 (herein BRI0952) is a doubly imaged z_s=4.5 optical quasar discovered by irwin . The image separation is 0.9 . The quasar was also detected at millimeter wavelengths omont . keeton1998 find that the lens is a flattened early-type galaxy. my suggest that the lens galaxy may be associated with a z_ g =0.42 group of 5 galaxies with =170^ +150 _ 100 .

sources.summary: The paper describes BRI 0952-0115 as a z=4.4 lensed QSO with a massive black hole, nearby companion galaxy, low inferred stellar mass, and roughly solar gas metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI) observations of far infra-red emission lines in BRI 0952-0115 (B0952), a lensed QSO at z=4.4 powered by a super-massive black hole ( M_ BH =2 10^9 M_ ). In this source, the resolved map of the [ CII ] emission at 158 m allows us to reveal the presence of a companion galaxy, located at 10 kpc from the QSO, undetected in optical observations. From the CO(5-4) emission line properties we infer a stellar mass M_*<2.2 10^ 10 M_ , which is significantly smaller than the one found in local galaxies hosting black holes with similar masses ( M_* 10^ 12 M_ ). The detection of the [ NII ] emission at 205 m suggests that the metallicity in B0952 is consistent with solar, implying that the chemical evolution has progressed very rapidly in this system.

sources.summary: The paper discusses BRI 0952 0115 as a gravitational lens and measures a higher lensing-galaxy redshift, with the spectrum matching an elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: BRI 0952 0115 : This gravitational lens was discovered by McMahon et al. ( ) as a pair of z =4.50 quasars separated by 0.9 . Kochanek et al. ( ) found that the lens galaxy appears to be a typical early-type lens galaxy, with a fundamental plane redshift estimate of 0.41 . We measure a significantly higher value, namely =0.632 0.002 (Fig. BRI0952_lens ), and the spectrum of the lensing galaxy is well matched by that of an elliptical galaxy. With this new redshift, the lensing galaxy can no longer be considered as a member of the group of galaxies identified by Momcheva et al. ( ), but possibly of another small group in the field at z 0.64 .

gmw_00002115.name: NGC 4283
gmw_00002115.overview: NGC 4283 is located at a projected distance of 16 kpc from NGC 4278 [arXiv:1308.6585]. The galaxy has a systemic velocity of 1056 km/s and is estimated to host a total of 16 ± 12 globular clusters [arXiv:1308.6585].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates NGC 4283's globular cluster system near NGC 4278, finding consistent distance, distinct velocity, modest expected GC numbers, and an estimated total of 16 12 GCs.

quotes.quote: We also investigated the contribution of NGC 4283's GC system to NGC 4278's GC system. NGC 4283 is at a projected distance of 16 kpc in the sky and has a SBF distance consistent with NGC 4278's distance 2001ApJ...546..681T . The systemic velocities of the two galaxies (NGC 4278 v_ sys = 620 km s ^ 1 , NGC 4283 v_ sys = 1056 km s ^ 1 2011MNRAS.413..813C ) differ by 436 km s ^ 1 . Adopting an absolute magnitude of M_ V = -19.0 1991trcb.book.....D and a specific frequency of 1.3, typical of galaxies of this absolute magnitude (see table 3 of 2008ApJ...681..197P ), we would expect NGC 4283 to have only 26 GCs brighter than the turnover magnitude.

Within 1.5 kpc of NGC 4283 we would only expect 1.1 GCs, although we see 5 GCs candidates in the same area. In 100 000 realisations of the GC system we only saw this many GCs within 1.5 kpc of NGC 4283 in 578 realisations. We expect 11.3 GCs within 5 kpc; we see 15 GC candidates within 5 kpc. Making the rough assumption that the half number radius of NGC 4283's GC system is 5 kpc, we can estimate that NGC 4283 has a total of 16 12 GCs.

gmw_00002116.name: M1513-2524
gmw_00002116.overview: M1513-2524 is an optically faint, narrow-line active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 3.132 [arXiv:2101.01192, arXiv:2107.09705]. It is surrounded by a large, luminous nebula and features double-lobed radio emission spanning 184 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2101.01192, arXiv:2107.09705].

sources.summary: M1513-2524 is described as an optically faint z=3.132 radio galaxy with a large luminous nebula, 184 kpc radio lobes, weak core, distinct emission components, and blue-shifted associated absorption.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of a large ( 90 kpc) and luminous \ nebula [ \ = (6.80 0.08) 10^ 44 ] around an optically faint ( >23 mag) radio galaxy M1513-2524 at =3.132. The double-lobed radio emission has an extent of 184 kpc, but the radio core, i.e., emission associated with the active galactic nucleus (AGN) itself, is barely detected.

The optical spectrum has revealed , , \ and \ emission lines with a very weak continuum. Based on long-slit spectroscopy and narrow band imaging centered on the \ emission, we identify two spatial components: a ``compact component" with high velocity dispersion ( 1500 ) seen in all three lines, and an ``extended component", having low velocity dispersion (i.e., 700-1000 ). The emission line ratios are consistent with the compact component being in photoionization equilibrium with an AGN. We also detect spatially extended associated \ absorption, which is blue-shifted within 250-400 \ of the \ peak.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M1513 2524 as the sample's highest-redshift narrow-line AGN, optically faint, associated with a large luminous nebula and 184 kpc double-lobed radio emission.

quotes.quote: The highest redshift NLAGN (M1513 2524) is at z = 3.132 . The details of this optically faint ( >23 mag) AGN associated with a large ( 90 kpc) and luminous \ nebula are presented by Shukla2021 . The double-lobed radio emission associated with M1513 2524 has an extent of 184 kpc.

gmw_00002117.name: DES J003206.50 425325.22
gmw_00002117.overview: DES J003206.50 425325.22 is an active galaxy that exhibits a Mg II emission line [arXiv:2208.05491]. This emission line experienced broadening during two outlier epochs, which may be intrinsic to the line or caused by a drastic change in iron emission [arXiv:2208.05491].

sources.summary: The paper singles out DES J003206.50 425325.22 for two Mg II outlier epochs that broaden the line, distort JAVELIN lag fitting, and are excluded to obtain a cleaner lag distribution.

quotes.quote: A special feature of DES J003206.50 425325.22 (bottom panel of Figure fig:lag_examp ) is the two outliers at MJD 58073 and 58399. The excess flux is due to a significant broadening of the Mg II line. This can be intrinsic to the Mg II line or due to a drastic change in the iron emission that is not characterized by the model. The JAVELIN lag distribution would be dominated by a sharp aliasing peak at 540 days if we included these two epochs, which differs from the 470 day ICCF lag.

For lags of a few hundred days, we expect the signal to come from lightcurve features over several years rather than dramatic short time scale variability, so we exclude these two epochs for this particular source. Excluding these two epochs also gives a cleaner JAVELIN lag distribution compared to the Y5 results where we kept the MJD 58073 epoch, and the best-fit lag changes by 40 days relative to Y5.

gmw_00002118.name: Bo otes I
aliases_external: Boötes I dwarf spheroidal galaxy / Boo I
gmw_00002118.overview: Boötes I is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1401.1265] and a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:0705.4622, arXiv:astro-ph/0611693]. It is dominated by an ancient, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1310.0824, arXiv:1401.1265] and exhibits an elongated structure that suggests tidal disruption [arXiv:2308.09760, arXiv:2404.08054].

sources.summary: The paper describes Bo otes as a faint Milky Way dwarf satellite with metal-poor stars, a clear velocity peak, measured velocity dispersion, and high inferred mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: Bo otes was discovered in the SDSS as an overdensity of stars that are aligned in the colour-magnitude diagram and follow a well-defined red-giant and horizontal branch belokurov06a . Although very faint ( M_V=-5.8 0.5 , but this value may be underestimated, see munoz06 ), this new Milky Way satellite has characteristics typical of dwarf galaxies with a half-light radius of 227 13 belokurov06a , a heliocentric distance of 62 3 (determined from RR Lyrae stars; siegel06 ) and is dominated by a metal-poor population with estimates ranging from 2.5 munoz06,siegel06 to =-2.0 siegel06 . A first spectroscopic analysis of the dwarf galaxy has been performed by munoz06 and among their 58 targets, the 7 Boo members that are within the half-light radius yield a systemic velocity and velocity dispersion of v_ , Boo =95.6 3.4 and _ vr, Boo =6.6 2.3 respectively. As is often done for such studies, they use this velocity dispersion to derive a total mass of 1 10^7 for the system, implying a mass to light ratio that is higher than 100 in solar units.

Figure 4 summarizes the spectroscopic information of our Bo otes sample observed with DEIMOS and Table 1 lists the individual data on each observed star. In the central panel of the Figure, there is a clear separation between foreground dwarf stars with Na >0.8 (hollow circles) and stars that are aligned along the CMD features of the dwarf galaxy (filled circles) which are clustered around 100 as expected. However, three of the field stars fall within the Boo peak. Since they are also close to the RGB and HB of the dwarf galaxy and have Na <0.8 , we consider them as Boo members (the circled big dots of Figure 2a, 3a and 4).

sources.summary: The paper treats Boo I as an ultra-faint satellite dwarf with member-star abundance measurements, half-light-radius context, low enrichment, extended members, and internal metallicity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The `ultra-faint' satellite dwarf galaxies are clearly very interesting for study, with intriguing results even from very small samples. Our results for radial-velocity members of Bo o tes I (discovered by Belokurov et al. 2006; luminosity L _V 3 10^4 L _ , distance 60  kpc; Martin, de Jong Rix 2008) and of Segue 1 (discovered by Belokurov et al. 2007; L _V 335 L _ , distance 25  kpc; Martin et al. 2008) are shown in Fig. 2 (see also Norris et al. 2008; Norris et al. 2010b). Here I show derived iron and carbon abundances, based on intermediate-resolution spectra (from the 2-degree field of view, multi-object, fibre-fed spectrograph AAOmega on the Anglo-Australian Telescope) covering the blue Ca II K-line (from which the iron abundance is derived) and the CH G-band (synthesis of which is the basis for the carbon abundance). Thus the iron abundances are on the same scale as many of the surveys of metal-poor field halo stars (see Beers Christlieb 2005).

The left panel of Fig. 2 shows the iron abundances as a function of the projected radial distance of a given star from the centre of its parent galaxy, with the filled (blue) squares denoting stars that are radial-velocity members of Segue 1, and the open (red) star symbols denoting radial-velocity members of Boo I. The vertical arrows along the x axis mark the half-light radii of these two systems (`S' for Segue 1 and `B' for Boo I). The wide field-of-view of AAOmega allows efficient mapping of these very sparse, nearby systems (and is critical given the apparent contamination in these lines-of-sight by stars from the Sagittarius dSph; see Niederste-Ostholt et al. 2009). Three points are immediately obvious: the mean level of enrichment is low, with a fraction of `extremely metal-poor' stars (those with [ Fe/H ] < -3.0  dex; see Table 1 of Beers Christlieb 2005); there are members well beyond the nominal half-light radius; and there is a large internal metallicity dispersion in each system, suggestive of self-enrichment.

sources.summary: Bo otes 1 is described as a luminous nearby UFD with many spectroscopic members; the algorithm finds members to 9 half-light radii and leaves tides, accretion, or both as possible origins.

quotes.quote: Bo otes 1 Boo1 is one of the most luminous ( M_V 6.3 mag; L_V 2.9 10 ^ 4 L _ ; munoz2018 ) and relatively nearby (66 kpc; dall'ora2006 ) UFDs. With a physical half-light radius of 200 pc (10 , or 0.17 ^ ; longeard2022 ), it is comparable in r_ h to other MW classical systems. Boo1 has been the subject of many recent studies, resulting in a significant number of spectroscopic members ( > 70 stars; see compilation in waller2023 ). While its size and chemical trends [ /Fe ] plane suggest it is a more massive UFD, frebel2016 determined Boo1's abundances of neutron-capture elements are much lower than observed in MW halo stars, a trait unique to UFDs ( frebel2015 ).

For comparison to previous works, the most distant member identified via our algorithm is at an elliptical radius of 9 r_ h from the center of Boo1, and does show consistent PMs to other spectroscopically confirmed members of longeard2022 . As reported in Table tab:outer_profile_dwarfs , we find two possible solutions for the transition boundary in the elliptical ( r_ trans, ell ) and circular ( r_ trans, circ ) 2-component models. These values correspond to r_ trans, ell = 0.34 ^ ( 1.8 r_ h ) and r_ trans, circ = 0.57 ^ ( 3 r_ h ), respectively.
It is unclear at present if the outskirt members of Boo1 are due to the effect of tides, accreted members, or both.

sources.summary: The paper studies Bo o tes I as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal, using member-star spectroscopy to find large carbon and iron abundance spreads tied to early inhomogeneous enrichment.

quotes.quote: CHEMICAL ENRICHMENT IN THE FAINTEST GALAXIES: THE CARBON AND IRON ABUNDANCE SPREADS IN THE BO O TES I DWARF SPHEROIDAL GALAXY AND THE SEGUE 1 SYSTEM JOHN E.

We present an AAOmega spectroscopic study of red giant stars in , which is an dwarf galaxy, and , suggested to be either an extremely low-luminosity dwarf galaxy or a star cluster. Our focus is quantifying the mean abundance and abundance dispersion in iron and carbon, and searching for distant radial-velocity members, in these systems. The primary conclusion of our investigation is that the spread of carbon abundance in both and is large.

For , four of our 16 velocity members have [ C/H ] -3.1, while two have [ C/H ] -2.3, suggesting a range of [ C/H ] 0.8. For there exists a range [ C/H ] 1.0, including our discovery of a star with [ Fe/H ] = --3.5 and [ C/Fe ] = +2.3, which is a radial velocity member at a distance of 4 half-light radii from the system center. The accompanying ranges in iron abundance are [ Fe/H ] 1.6 for both and .

sources.summary: The paper treats Bootes 1 as a VERITAS dwarf-spheroidal dark-matter target, reporting astrophysical factors and non-detection limits on annihilation cross-section.

quotes.quote: Table Tab1 presents the current observations carried out by VERITAS on five well-studied and closeby dSphs, namely the Draco, Ursa Minor, Bootes 1, Willman 1 and Segue 1 galaxies. The so-called wobble pointing mode, where the pointing position is shifted by 0.5 deg from the target position, has been used during these observations.

The resulting astrophysical factors of Draco, Ursa Minor, Bootes 1, Willman 1 and Segue 1 are presented in Table Tab2 , along with their distance and profile modeling. Once the astrophysical factors have been estimated, the ULs in the number of ray candidates for each dSph can be translated into ULs on the total annihilation cross-section: v ^ 95 CL = 8 J ( ) N_ ^ 95 CL m_ DM ^ 2 T_ obs _0^ m_ DM A _ eff (E) dN_ dE dE , where T_ obs is the total observation time and A _ eff (E) is the effective area as a function of energy, zenith angle and the offset of the source from the pointing position. Figure Fig1 shows the VERITAS 95 CL ULs on the velocity-weitghted annihilation cross-section v as a function of the DM particle mass for each of the five observed dSph listed in Table Tab1 .

The Draco, Ursa Minor, Bootes 1 and Willman 1 limits have been obtained using a composite DM spectrum with a 90 b b branching ratio and a 10 ^ + ^ branching ratio . The Segue 1 limits have been obtained assuming a pure W^ + W^ DM annihilation spectrum, which is similar to the composite b b ^ + ^ spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper presents Bo o tes I as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal with old stars, low metallicity, dark matter domination, and chemical enrichment lasting until Type Ia supernovae contributed.

quotes.quote: Among them, the Bo o tes I dwarf spheroidal galaxy is of particular interest because of its exclusively old stellar population. Detailed chemical compositions of individual stars in this galaxy are a key to understanding formation and chemical evolution in the oldest galaxies in the Universe and their roles in building up the MW halo. Previous studies of the chemical abundances of Bo o tes I show discrepancies in elemental abundances between different authors, and thus a consistent picture of its chemical enrichment history has not yet been established.

Subsequent spectroscopy to measure line-of-sight velocities of candidate member stars suggests that Bo o tes I is a dark-matter-dominated system with a total mass on the order of 10^ 7 M_ munoz06,martin07 . koposov11 has developed a reduction technique to derive more accurate radial velocities, and they suggest that the velocity dispersion of this system is represented better by two components with 2.4 km s ^ 1 for a "cold" component and 9 km s ^ 1 for a "hot" one, which could be interpreted as the result of velocity anisotropy in this galaxy. Detailed analyses of a color-magnitude diagram (CMD) for Bo o tes I suggests that it consists of a very old stellar population with age >10 Gyr, which is similar to the most metal-poor globular clusters in the MW okamoto12 .

Instead, our results suggest that the star formation in Bo o tes I had lasted until Type Ia SNe started to contribute to the chemical enrichment, which has also been reported in other more luminous dSphs.

sources.summary: The paper models Bo otes I as an ultra-faint dwarf, using its distance, luminosity, half-light radius, abundance samples, dark halo, and short early star-formation history.

quotes.quote: Bo otes I UfD The Bo otes I UfD was discovered by belokurov2006 , from the analysis of SDSS data. siegel2006 estimated the distance of Bo otes I by using 15 RR Lyrae variable stars as standard candles; they found a distance: D=62 4 kpc, very similar to that of the outermost halo globular clusters in our Galaxy. Finally, belokurov2006 reported an absolute magnitude M_ V =-5.8 and an half-light radius r_ h 220 pc. The data samples of chemical abundances have been taken from the works of norris2010a and gilmore2013 (``NY'' analysis, from norris2010b ).

Chemical evolution of the Bo otes I UfD We assumed for the Bo otes I UfD a dark matter halo with mass M_ DM =0.30 10^ 7 M_ collins2013 . The ratio between the core radius of the DM halo and the effective radius of the galaxy baryonic component has been assumed to be S= r_ L r_ DM =0.2 , where the effective radius of the baryonic ( luminous ) component of the galaxy is r_ L =242 pc martin2008 . The SFH has been assumed to be made of only one episode lasting 4 Gyr, from 0 up to 4 Gyr dejong2008 .

sources.summary: The paper models Bo otes I as an ultra-faint dwarf and finds its best IGIMF stellar-mass model fails abundance ratios and MDF, while a Salpeter IMF better matches observations.

quotes.quote: We test the integrated galactic initial mass function (IGIMF) on the chemical evolution of 16 ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies discussing in detail the results obtained for three of them: Bo otes I, Bo otes II and Canes Venatici I, taken as prototypes of the smallest and the largest UFDs. These objects have very small stellar masses ( 10^3-10^4 M_ ) and quite low metallicities ( [ Fe/H ] <-1.0 dex). We consider four observational constraints: the present-day stellar mass, the [ /Fe ] vs. [ Fe/H ] relation, the stellar metallicity distribution function and the cumulative star formation history.

The model with the IGIMF which best reproduces the present-day stellar mass of Boo I is 1BooI, which is characterized by M_ infall = \ 1.0 10^7 \ M_ and =0.005 \ Gyr^ 1 . Nevertheless, this model is not able to match the abundance ratios for any of the chemical elements analysed here. In addition, it predicts too many metal-poor stars and a lack of stars at higher [ Fe/H ] , not fitting the observed MDF. On the contrary, the model 3BooI, with =0.1 \ Gyr^ 1 , is able to reproduce the abundance ratios and the MDF but it does not predict the correct present-day stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes Bo o tes I as a bright ultra-faint dwarf with many confirmed members, old populations, outer metal-poor carbon-normal stars, and possible halo material from tidal stripping or a minor merger.

quotes.quote: Bo o tes I Boo I is a UFD with total mass M 1.1 10^7 \ Munoz06 , half-light radius R_ h 217 pc, and is fairly close to the MW at a distance of D 66 kpc McConnachie2012 . Boo I is one of the brightest UFDs, with L 10^5L_ Munoz06 , with apparent and absolute magnitudes of m_V 12.8 and M_V 6.3 McConnachie2012 . Because of this, Boo I is one of the most observed UFDs with nearly 70 confirmed members, and high-resolution spectroscopic chemical abundances for 18 stars Feltzing09, Norris10a, Gilmore13, Ishigaki14, Frebel16 ; this can also be seen in Fig. fig:halflight . Photometric studies have found that Boo I has a narrow CMD at the MSTO, indicating an old single-age population 13.7 Gyr; Okamoto12 . However, HST photometry suggests there may be two ancient populations with a very small age spread Brown2014 .

In Bo otes I, the analysis of our star and two others with HRS at > 3R _ h show they are metal-poor and C-normal. Some inner stars may be slightly more metal-rich implying longer-lived star formation in the centre; alternatively, several newly discovered very metal-poor stars in the outerskirts suggests the halo remnant of a dwarf galaxy minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper constrains the low-mass IMF of Bo o tes I using HST imaging, Bayesian member selection, proper motions, and color-magnitude forward modeling, finding IMF parameters consistent with Milky Way stellar results.

quotes.quote: This paper presents improved constraints on the low-mass stellar initial mass function (IMF) of the Bo o tes I (Boo I) ultrafaint dwarf galaxy, based on our analysis of recent deep imaging from the Hubble Space Telescope. The identification of candidate stellar members of Boo I in the photometric catalog produced from these data was achieved using a Bayesian approach, informed by complementary archival imaging data for the Hubble Ultra Deep Field. Additionally, the existence of earlier-epoch data for the fields in Boo I allowed us to derive proper motions for a subset of the sources and thus identify and remove likely Milky Way stars. We were also able to determine the absolute proper motion of Boo I, and our result is in agreement with, but completely independent of, the measurement(s) by Gaia .

The best-fitting single power-law IMF slope is = -1.95_ 0.28 ^ +0.32 , while the best-fitting broken power-law slopes are _1 = -1.67_ 0.57 ^ +0.48 and _2 = -2.57_ 1.04 ^ +0.93 . The best-fitting lognormal characteristic mass and width parameters are M _ = 0.17_ 0.11 ^ +0.05 and =0.49_ 0.20 ^ +0.13 . These broken power-law and lognormal IMF parameters for Boo I are consistent with published results for the stars within the Milky Way and thus it is plausible that Bo o tes I and the Milky Way are populated by the same stellar IMF.

sources.summary: The paper uses high-resolution spectra of seven red giants in Bo otes I to confirm low mean metallicity, identify metallicity outliers, and find unusual Mg/Ca evidence for stochastic enrichment.

quotes.quote: We establish the mean metallicity from high-resolution spectroscopy for the recently found dwarf spheroidal galaxy Bo otes I and test whether it is a common feature for ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxies to show signs of inhomogeneous chemical evolution (e.g. as found in the Hercules dwarf spheroidal galaxy). We analyse high-resolution, moderate signal-to-noise spectra for seven red giant stars in the Bo otes I dSph galaxy using standard abundance analysis techniques. In particular, we assume local thermodynamic equilibrium and employ spherical model atmospheres and codes that take the sphericity of the star into account when calculating the elemental abundances.

We confirm previous determinations of the mean metallicity of the Bo otes I dwarf spheroidal galaxy to be --2.3 dex. Whilst five stars are clustered around this metallicity, one is significantly more metal-poor, at --2.9 dex, and one is more metal-rich at, --1.9 dex. Additionally, we find that one of the stars, Boo-127, shows an atypically high [ Mg/Ca ] ratio, indicative of stochastic enrichment processes within the dSph galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper includes Bootes I in a Fermi Pass 7 joint WIMP search, using J values from stellar velocity dispersions, a 0.5 degree ROI, empirical background PMFs, and dark-matter distribution uncertainties.

quotes.quote: We use Bootes I, Draco, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Ursa Minor, and Segue 1 because none are in a crowded field or near known ray sources. We utilize the updated values of J presented in 2011arXiv1108.3546T . The J values are derived based on modeling the velocity dispersion profiles of stars in each dwarf 2007PhRvD..75h3526S,2008ApJ...678..614S,2009JCAP...06..014M . We define a Region of Interest (ROI) to be a region of the sky with a radius of 0.5 containing all Pass 7 photons of evclass=2 available publicly on the Fermi Science Support Center (FSSC) , in the Mission Elapsed Time interval of [ 239557417-334619159 ] seconds (August 4, 2008 15:43:36 UTC to August 9, 2011 21:45:57 UTC), and with energies [ 1-100 ] GeV (at these energies, the point spread function (PSF) is always less than 1 ).

Here, the confidence belts are constructed without prior knowledge of the number of counts within the central ROI around each dwarf. Under the assumption that the empirically derived background PMFs, exposures, and J values are correct, the belts have the proper coverage.

sources.summary: The paper models Bo otes I as a well-observed ultra-faint dwarf whose chemical evolution is consistent with IGIMF theory, a mildly bottom-light and top-light gwIMF, and rapid early quenching.

quotes.quote: The chemical evolution of Bo otes I, one of the best observed UFD, is calculated. Our calculation suggests a mildly bottom-light and top-light gwIMF for Bo otes I, and that this UFD has the same gas-consumption timescale as other dwarfs but was quenched about 0.1 Gyr after formation, being consistent with independent estimations and similar to Dragonfly 44.

However, we consider only Bo otes I instead of all the other galaxies studied by 2019arXiv191108450L for the following reasons. (i) Unlike Bo otes I (see Section sec: Star formation history below), most galaxies have a complex gas flow and SFH. In 2019arXiv191108450L , there are in total 27 data points for the [ Si/Fe ] - [ Fe/H ] , [ Mg/Fe ] - [ Fe/H ] , and [ Ca/Fe ] relations for Bo otes I, while there are only 8 for Bo otes II, 4 for Canes Venatici I and less than 10 data points for each of the other UFDs.
The IGIMF theory thus leads to a remarkably simple and self-consistent understanding of the Bo otes I UFD galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents ultra-deep HST photometry of the Bo o tes I ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal, identifies likely members, and measures its lower-main-sequence luminosity function to 0.3 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of new extremely deep images of the resolved stellar population of the Bo o tes I ultra faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy. These new data were taken with the Hubble Space Telescope, using the Advanced Camera for Surveys (Wide Field Camera) and Wide Field Camera 3 (UVIS), with filters F606W and F814W (essentially V and I), as part of a program to derive the low-mass stellar initial mass function in this galaxy. We compare and contrast two approaches to obtaining the stellar photometry, namely ePSF and DAOPHOT. We identify likely members of Bo o tes I based on the location of each star on the color-magnitude diagram, obtained with the DAOPHOT photometry from the ACS/WFC data.

The probable members lie close to stellar isochrones that were chosen to encompass the known metallicity distribution derived from spectroscopic data of brighter radial-velocity member stars and are consistent with the main-sequence turnoff. The resulting luminosity function of the Bo o tes I galaxy has a 50 completeness limit of 27.4 in F814W and 28.2 in F606W (Vega magnitude system), which corresponds to a limiting stellar mass of 0.3 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper calibrates a chemical-evolution model to Boötes I and uses it to interpret the galaxy's carbon-enhanced metal-poor stellar populations and observed abundance trends.
