We show that two possible explanations could be invoked for this object: a barred spiral, with a pair of semi-circular arms forming the apparent ring, or a classical but rare PRG, as the immediate morphological evidence would argue.

The ring produces about the same amount of light as the central object in the i band, but is significantly brighter at bluer bands. The ring is also significantly bluer than the central object, with e.g., ( g- )=0.14 mag, vs. 0.87 mag for the central object. The enhanced brightness at R 9 arcsec, and the steady decrease in intensity further out, are even more accentuated in the u band and are almost invisible in z .

The option of a PRG has more + signs than the other option thus, on the balance of probabilities, we accept it over the alternative of a barred spiral with tight spiral arms and a strong disk warp. Therefore RG1 could be a rare case of a galaxy where the polar ring shows only a small inclination with respect to the equatorial plane of the CB.

sources.summary: The paper studies SPRC-7 as an inclined polar-ring galaxy, modeling its stellar and ionized-gas kinematics and favoring an oblate dark-matter halo potential.

quotes.quote: t Spectroscopic observations at the 6-m telescope of the SAO RAS were used to study two polar ring galaxies from the catalogue by moisav2011 : SPRC-7 and SPRC-260. SPRC-7 is an inclined system with the relative angle i = 70^ towards the central galaxy, meanwhile SPRC-260 is a classic polar case with i = 89^ . The observations were carried out with the multi-mode focal reducers SCORPIO AM05 and SCORPIO-2 AM12 in the scanning Fabry-Perot interferometer and long-slit spectroscopic modes. The results of observations are The line-of-sight velocity and velocity dispersion distributions of the stellar component along the central galaxy major axis; The ionized gas velocity fields for the polar component. The data for SPRC-7 were already presented in Brosch2010 .

For SPRC-7, multiple arms formed with a large pitch angle (fig. 4). The best fits (minima of ^2_ ring distributions) pointed out the oblate character of the DM halo potential distribution in the polar plane for both galaxies: s = 0.8 for SPRC-7 and s=0.95 for SPRC-260. For the halo density distribution q and s should be significantly smaller. We propose an oblate DM halo potential for SPRC-7 with the semiaxis relations: s=0.8 , q=1 and a triaxial halo for SPRC-260: s=0.95 , q=1.1 .

gmw_00002133.name: 3XMMJ091804.2+514113
gmw_00002133.overview: 3XMMJ091804.2+514113 is classified as a candidate Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1407.6944]. Its X-ray spectrum displays an extremely flat shape, indicating that the source is reflection-dominated with no sign of direct emission [arXiv:1407.6944].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 3XMMJ091804.2+514113 as a reflection-dominated Compton-thick AGN candidate, arguing its extremely flat X-ray spectrum is inconsistent with mild absorption.

quotes.quote: In four cases (3XMMJ091804.2+514113, 3XMMJ093952.7+355358, 3XMMJ140700.3+282714, and 3XMMJ215649.5-074531), we estimate that the column density is likely higher than 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 because the X-ray spectrum is reflection dominated, meaning that there is no sign of direct emission.

3XMMJ091804.2+514113: While we classified this source as a reflection-dominated CT candidate, an N _ H value of 6 10 ^ 22 is reported in georg06 (although consistent with being highly absorbed within errors). The same XMM-Newton observation was used in both analyses but because there are very few spectral counts, the differences in the spectral-fitting results might be due to the different spectral extraction. Nevertheless, our spectral data display an extremely flat shape that is not consistent with mild absorption.

gmw_00002134.name: SDSS J080359.20+234520.4
gmw_00002134.overview: SDSS J080359.20+234520.4 is classified in the literature as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1407.6944]. It is identified as a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus based on its X-ray luminosity to optical and mid-infrared luminosity ratios [arXiv:1407.6944].

sources.summary: The paper says SDSS J080359.20+234520.4 is a literature Compton-thick type 2 Seyfert, but its low-count hard-band fit prevented automated candidate selection.

quotes.quote: Of the sources in out parent sample that are classified as CT AGN within the sample of type 2 Seyfert galaxies extracted from the SDSS presented in lamassa09 , our classification agrees in all but two cases: SDSS J080359.20+234520.4, and SDSS J112301.31+470308.6. In both cases, the automated fits return very flat photon indices in the hard band, but because of the very low number of counts in that band, consistent with being larger than 1.4 at the 90 confidence level. As a result, these two sources are not flagged as candidates by our selection criteria. It is important to note that these two sources are classified as CT AGN in lamassa09 based on their X-ray luminosity to optical, and mid-IR luminosity ratios, and not from their X-ray spectral analysis.

gmw_00002135.name: SDSS J112301.31+470308.6
gmw_00002135.overview: SDSS J112301.31+470308.6 is classified in literature as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1407.6944]. It is identified as a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus based on its X-ray luminosity to optical and mid-infrared luminosity ratios [arXiv:1407.6944].

sources.summary: The paper says SDSS J112301.31+470308.6 is a literature Compton-thick type 2 Seyfert, but its low-count hard-band fit prevented automated candidate selection.

quotes.quote: Of the sources in out parent sample that are classified as CT AGN within the sample of type 2 Seyfert galaxies extracted from the SDSS presented in lamassa09 , our classification agrees in all but two cases: SDSS J080359.20+234520.4, and SDSS J112301.31+470308.6. In both cases, the automated fits return very flat photon indices in the hard band, but because of the very low number of counts in that band, consistent with being larger than 1.4 at the 90 confidence level. As a result, these two sources are not flagged as candidates by our selection criteria. It is important to note that these two sources are classified as CT AGN in lamassa09 based on their X-ray luminosity to optical, and mid-IR luminosity ratios, and not from their X-ray spectral analysis.

gmw_00002136.name: central brightest group galaxy (BGG)
aliases_external: central BGG of RXJ075243.6+455653 / RFC J0752+4556
gmw_00002136.overview: The central brightest group galaxy of RXJ075243.6+455653 [arXiv:1710.09229] is a local active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2110.13810] that exhibits changing-look Seyfert transitions [arXiv:2110.13810, arXiv:2205.13652]. Multi-epoch observations reveal that its broad emission lines and X-ray emission levels have faded over time [arXiv:2110.13810, arXiv:2205.13652].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B3 0749+460A as a local changing-look Seyfert AGN whose broad H emission faded while Swift/XRT showed declining X-ray emission and slope changes between optical states.

quotes.quote: Here we report an identification of B3 0749+460A as a new double-peaked local ``changing-look'' active galactic nucleus (CL-AGN) in terms of our multi-epoch spectroscopic analysis. By comparing our new spectra taken in 2021 with the ones taken by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey in 2004, BOSS in 2013, and MaNGA in 2016, we reveal type transitions of Seyfert (Sy) 1.9 Sy1.8 Sy1.9. In the transitions, the classical broad H emission fades away since 2013, and disappears in our 2021 spectrum, although the absence of broad H can be traced back to at least 2016. A follow-up observation in X-rays by the Swift /XRT reveals that (1) the X-ray emission level gradually decreases since 2005; and (2) the X-ray spectrum is soft in the optical ``turn-off'' state and hard in the `turn-on'' state. We argue that the disappearance of the classical broad H emission can be likely explained by the disk-wind broad-line-region model, in which the CL phenomenon is sensitive to luminosity in individual AGNs. galaxies: Seyfert --- galaxies: nuclei --- X-rays: galaxies --- quasars: emission lines --- quasars: individual (B3 0749+460A) Wang et al.

B3 0749+460A (=NPM1G+46.0092, = 07^h52^m44^s.2 , = +45 56 58 ; J2000) is a local AGN at a redshift of z=0.0518 . The object was classified as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy in the catalog of quasars and active nuclei (12th ed.; Veron-Cetty Veron 2006) and the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/. . It is part of a sample of CL-AGN candidates we selected by cross-matching the local partially obscured AGNs extracted from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) DR7 with the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE) catalog (Wright et al. 2010).

sources.summary: The paper describes RXJ075243.6+455653's central BGG as unusually faint for fossil systems but able to grow substantially by accreting nearby bright galaxies.

quotes.quote: RXJ075243.6+455653 has a central brightest group galaxy (BGG) with M_ = -22.67 , one of the faintest BGGs observed in fossil systems.

The BGG of RXJ075243.6+455653 has an absolute magnitude of M_ = -22.67 , and is one of the less luminous BGGs in the sample of FSs analyzed by . However, this galaxy can still grow by the accretion of the bright galaxies in the nearby environment due to dynamical friction. The galaxies 2 magnitudes fainter than the BGG, located in the galaxy groups, and with |V-V_ | < 3 _ , have a total luminosity of L_ = 1.05 10^ 12 \ h_ 70 ^ 2 \ L_ , . All these stars around RXJ075243.6+455653 could potentially produce a central BGG with M_ =-25.43 . This new galaxy would be comparable in luminosity to the brightest galaxies observed in other fossil clusters and will produce in the future a system with a large magnitude gap see zarattini2014 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 1-604860 as a MaNGA changing-look type-1 AGN candidate whose 2004-2019 spectra show changing narrow-line fluxes and varying broad-line components.

quotes.quote: An example is shown in Figure fig:8714 where we compare four different single-epoch spectra for the MaNGA galaxy 1-604860. The SDSS DR7 spectrum (black) was observed in 2004, the SDSS DR12 (purple) in 2013, the MaNGA spectrum (blue) in 2016, and an additional spectrum obtained at the Observatorio Astron omico Nacional San Pedro Mártir (yellow; OAN-SPM, México) in 2019. A direct inspection shows that the narrow lines fluxes change while the broad components show different level of prominence and shape along this time base. In the DR12 observation, the broad \ line shows two broad components, while in the DR15 MaNGA observations these components are barely present. In the OAN-SPM observation, the broad component located beetween 6600-6800 \ seems to be emerging again.

sources.summary: The paper reports a 150 mas radio-optical offset, a compact Chandra counterpart, broad H emission, and an OSSY type-1 AGN classification.

quotes.quote: SDSS J075244.19+455657.3 Morphology: E (NED) Brightest and nearest CLASS source 75.4 mJy (6C B074906.2+460422), offset by 150 mas from the SDSS position. Compact Chandra source detected at the same position. The spectrum has a broad component to H , and this source appears in the OSSY catalog of type 1 AGN.

gmw_00002137.name: C4
gmw_00002137.overview: C4 is an optically bright elliptical galaxy with a normal absorption-line spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0212036]. However, its flat-spectrum, core-halo radio morphology and steep X-ray power-law spectrum indicate that the galaxy is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0212036].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C4 as an optically bright absorption-line elliptical in the Chandra sample whose flat-spectrum, core-halo radio morphology and steep X-ray spectrum indicate a BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: Similarly inactive galaxies were found in our Chandra sample with C4 and C33, the two optically brightest absorption line galaxies, each having a magnitude of R 17.5 . Both are elliptical galaxies with normal optical spectra. However the radio data showed flat spectra and core-halo morphologies (Fig. optrad ), with very steep X-ray powerlaw spectra (Fig. xmmspec ), indicating that C4 and C33 are almost certainly BL Lac objects (M ^ Hardy et al., in preparation).

gmw_00002138.name: C33
gmw_00002138.overview: C33 is an optically bright elliptical galaxy with a normal absorption-line spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0212036]. However, its flat radio spectrum, core-halo morphology, and steep X-ray spectrum indicate that it is almost certainly a BL Lac active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0212036].

sources.summary: The paper describes C33 as an optically bright absorption-line elliptical and likely BL Lac object; its radio and X-ray properties include core-halo morphology, steep X-ray spectrum, and modest intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: Similarly inactive galaxies were found in our Chandra sample with C4 and C33, the two optically brightest absorption line galaxies, each having a magnitude of R 17.5 . Both are elliptical galaxies with normal optical spectra. However the radio data showed flat spectra and core-halo morphologies (Fig. optrad ), with very steep X-ray powerlaw spectra (Fig. xmmspec ), indicating that C4 and C33 are almost certainly BL Lac objects (M ^ Hardy et al., in preparation). Furthermore, the XMM spectrum of C33 shows a small amount of intrinsic absorption, N_H = 1.3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , which could mimic an intrinsic hard spectrum in a faint source for which only a Chandra two-band hardness ratio was available.

gmw_00002139.name: R117
gmw_00002139.overview: R117 is an optically bright emission-line galaxy with a low X-ray luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0212036]. Its steep-spectrum radio emission traces the optical extent of the galaxy, and its soft thermal X-ray spectrum is consistent with starburst activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0212036].

sources.summary: The paper identifies R117 as an optically bright, X-ray faint emission-line galaxy whose steep radio emission traces the galaxy, soft XMM spectrum indicates starburst activity, and Chandra non-detection suggests extended X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Fig. r32r117 shows 30'' Subaru R -band images of the ROSAT source R117 (left, Gunn et al. 2001) and the Chandra source C146 (right) with radio contours overlaid. For both galaxies, which are optically bright ( R 16 ), X-ray faint ( L_X 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 ), ie, low L_X/L_ opt , the radio emission has a steep spectrum ( 0.6 ) tracing the optical extent of the galaxy, and XMM shows a very soft thermal spectrum, consistent with a starburst origin. Furthermore, R117 is not detected by Chandra, suggesting that the X-ray emission may be extended. If R117 was a point source, it would have to be a factor of three fainter than observed with ROSAT to remain undetected by Chandra at this offaxis angle.

gmw_00002140.name: Source C
gmw_00002140.overview: Source C is a tailed radio galaxy associated with the galaxy 2MASX J17115542-2309423 [arXiv:1002.2332]. It features radio jets deflected by ram pressure, which are approximately 165 kiloparsecs long [arXiv:1002.2332].

sources.summary: Source C is treated as a typical tailed radio galaxy in Ophiuchus, associated with 2MASX J17115542-2309423; its velocity and radio morphology are interpreted as ram-pressure-bent jets from an infalling or merging galaxy.

quotes.quote: Instead, sources C and D are the typical cluster tailed radio galaxies. Source C is about 300 long (165 kpc) and is pointing north-east to south-west.

The apparent difference in radial velocity between sources C and D and the Ophiuchus cluster is 1000 km/s. We assume here a mean recession velocity of 9063 km/s for the central region of the Ophiuchus cluster (Wakamatsu et al. 2005), and that the two radio sources are associated to galaxies 2MASX J17115542-2309423 ( z = 8050 km/s) and 2MASX J17120908-2328263 ( z = 7469 km/s) (Hasegawa et al. 2000). These properties are further confirmed by the spectral aging analysis presented in Sect. aging and indicate that the ram-pressure model can easily explain the radio jet deflection. The two galaxies could have such a high velocity with respect to the ICM either because they are infalling individually towards the cluster centre or because they are part of merging sub-clusters.

gmw_00002141.name: Source D
gmw_00002141.overview: Source D is a faint tailed radio galaxy associated with 2MASX J17120908-2328263 in the Ophiuchus cluster [arXiv:1002.2332]. It features a radio tail approximately 127 kiloparsecs long, which is likely bent by ram pressure as the galaxy infalls or merges [arXiv:1002.2332].

sources.summary: Source D is described as a faint tailed radio galaxy in Ophiuchus, associated with 2MASX J17120908-2328263; its velocity and radio morphology support ram-pressure jet bending during infall or subcluster merging.

quotes.quote: Instead, sources C and D are the typical cluster tailed radio galaxies. Source C is about 300 long (165 kpc) and is pointing north-east to south-west. Source D is faint tail about 230 long (127 kpc) pointing from south to north.

The apparent difference in radial velocity between sources C and D and the Ophiuchus cluster is 1000 km/s. We assume here a mean recession velocity of 9063 km/s for the central region of the Ophiuchus cluster (Wakamatsu et al. 2005), and that the two radio sources are associated to galaxies 2MASX J17115542-2309423 ( z = 8050 km/s) and 2MASX J17120908-2328263 ( z = 7469 km/s) (Hasegawa et al. 2000). These properties are further confirmed by the spectral aging analysis presented in Sect. aging and indicate that the ram-pressure model can easily explain the radio jet deflection. The two galaxies could have such a high velocity with respect to the ICM either because they are infalling individually towards the cluster centre or because they are part of merging sub-clusters.

gmw_00002142.name: ULAS J1539+0557
gmw_00002142.overview: ULAS J1539+0557 is a heavily reddened, hyper-luminous quasar located at a redshift of z=2.658 [arXiv:1403.0853, arXiv:1910.00602]. The host galaxy contains a massive molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:1403.0853] and continues to actively form stars [arXiv:1403.0853].

sources.summary: The paper presents CO(3-2) observations of ULAS J1539+0557, finding a massive molecular gas reservoir, high gas fraction, active star formation, and an obscured hyper-luminous QSO host that remains cold-gas rich.

quotes.quote: We targeted CO(3-2) in ULAS J1539+0557, an hyper-luminos QSO ( L_ bol > 10^ 48 erg/s ) at z=2.658 , selected through its unusual red colors in the UKIDSS Large Area Survey (ULAS). We find a molecular gas mass of 4.1 0.8 10^ 10 M_ , and a gas fraction of 0.4-0.1, depending mostly on the assumed source inclination. We also find a robust lower limit to the star-formation rate (SFR = 250-1600 M _ /yr) and star-formation efficiency (SFE = 25-350 L _ /(K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 ) by comparing the observed optical-near-infrared spectral energy distribution with AGN and galaxy templates.

We detected CO(3-2) in the hyper-luminous QSO ULAS J1539+0557 at z=2.658 , a source selected by extremely red colors in the UKIDSS Large Area Survey (ULAS) and bright at 22 micron in the WISE survey. We find a molecular gas reservoir of 4.1 0.8 10^ 10 M_ . The dynamical mass is not well constrained but it could be 2-10 times higher, depending mostly on the gas disk size and inclination. In any case, the host galaxy does not appear deprived from cold gas, suggesting that it is still forming stars actively.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1539 as a previously targeted heavily reddened quasar modeled over 0.5-10 keV, with column densities consistent with earlier work.

quotes.quote: J0144 was covered as part of the Stripe 82 X-ray survey ( LaMassa16a ), J1216 was detected off-axis in an archival observation (PI Ponman), and J1539 was targetted (PI Zappacosta) and published in Martocchia17 .

For subsequent analyses of these sources, we limit to the energy ranges where there is significant signal above the background: 0.2 - 10 keV for J1234, J2200, and J2315; 0.2 - 4.5 keV for J2243; 0.5 - 10 keV for J1216 and J1539; and 0.5 - 4.5 keV for J2314.
For J1234, J1539, and J2315 the column densities measured are in agreement with previous works, which also used simple absorbed power law models, within the uncertainties ( Banerji14,Martocchia17 ).

gmw_00002143.name: GP0303-0759
gmw_00002143.overview: GP0303-0759 is a low-metallicity Green Pea galaxy [arXiv:2110.07741, arXiv:1506.02885] characterized by extreme nebular emission [arXiv:2110.07741] and a complex emission-line structure [arXiv:2309.09585]. It features a bi-conical outflow oriented parallel to the line of sight [arXiv:2011.02549] and is considered an active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1506.02885].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GP06 as the Green Pea with the most complex emission-line maps, extended high-excitation [O iii] gas, low-extinction outer regions, and possible ionizing-photon escape paths.

quotes.quote: As an example, the emission line maps of the galaxy GP06 are shown in Fig. GP06_maps . Among the galaxy sample, GP06 is the one that presents the most complex structure in these maps, where even in dim lines like [ S ii ] 6716 and [ S ii ] 6731 a small bump is seen in the south-western position with respect to the central burst. Furthermore, this galaxy presents the brightest nebular HeII4686 emission (also detected in GP20 and GP15) in our sample. This line indicates the presence of very high energy photons (E > 4 Ry) that strongly ionize the gas and raise the electron temperature e.g., kehrig2015extended .

In particular, GP06 presents two regions in the north east and north west (with respect to the H peak), where the [ O iii ] 5007 emission line extends much further (up to 10kpc) than the continuum (Figs. GP06_maps continuo_GP15 ). In these galactic regions, there is almost no extinction (H / H 2.86 ), and the gas has higher excitation ( ( [ O iii ] 5007/H ) 0.7 ) in comparison to the other outer regions where ( [ O iii ] 5007/H ) 0.45 (see Section seccion_resuelta , Figs. ExtinctionGP06 and combine ). Similar results were found for the galaxy SBS 0335–052E where [ O iii ] 5007 and H filaments with low H / H ratio were discovered 2023A&A...670A.121H . These observations point to the fact that these regions can be channels where LyC photons can escape.

sources.summary: The paper observes GP030321 as one of three Green Pea galaxies, noting its SDSS and SHOC identifiers, OSIRIS spectrum, extreme emission-line character, and low-metallicity analysis.

quotes.quote: The sample consists in three galaxies selected among the brightest GPs in the sample presented by . Their SDSS references are: http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?plate=458&mjd=51929&fiber=185 SDSSJ030321.41-075923.2 , http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?plate=1745&mjd=53061&fiber=463 SDSSJ101157.08+130822.0 and http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?plate=1766&mjd=53468&fiber=557 SDSSJ121903.98+152608.5 . The first GP can also be found in the SHOC catalogue (SDSS HII-galaxies with Oxygen abundances Catalog) by kniazev_strong_2004 as SHOC148. For simplicity's sake, however, the GPs will be referred within this manuscript as GP030321, GP101157 and GP121903.

In Fig. fig:sample_spectra , we present the OSIRIS spectra of the three observed galaxies in the rest frame. Both the spectra and the images illustrate the characteristic properties of GP galaxies, namely unresolved morphology in SDSS and green colour due to extreme nebular emission, in particular high EW( [ OIII ] 4959,5007 ) and emission lines falling in the '-band. In GP030321, a sky band has completely swallowed the [ NII ] 6548 emission.

sources.summary: The paper notes 0303-0759 has Milky Way contamination in one line, a parallel bi-conical outflow, and the lowest Ly escape in the Green Pea sample.

quotes.quote: Similarly, we fit the 1260.42 \ \ resonant transition in all galaxies except for 0303-0759 and 1424+4217 . Galaxy 0303-0759 's spectrum suffers contamination from Milky Way absorption and galaxy 1424+4217 does not have data at this wavelength range due to a failed observation.

Galaxies 0911+1831 , 1054+5238 , 1137+3524 , 1244+0216 , 1249+1234 , and 1424+4217 have spherical outflows in Si II, while galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 have perpendicular bi-conical outflows. Galaxy 0926+4427 is our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow observed edge-on. (Even though > in galaxy 0926+4427 we have chosen to include 0926+4427 in this group since within our uncertainties.) Lastly, galaxy 0303-0759 is our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow oriented parallel to the line of sight. Members of these groups are circled in blue, red, violet, and green, respectively, in Figures f8 and f9 . Because \ photons scatter multiple times in the \ medium the geometry of the gas plays a crucial role. Specifically, \ photons will escape toward directions with the minimum \ column density. In the case of bi-conical geometry, photons will encounter the lowest \ column density in the direction perpendicular to the outflow. The orientation of the outflow with respect to the line of sight, then, will determine the visibility of \ photons by an observer. This seems to be confirmed with the data: galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 , with outflows oriented perpendicular to the line of sight, have the first and third largest values of the escape fraction out of our 10 Green Pea galaxies, while galaxy 0303-0759 , our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow oriented along the line of sight, has the lowest \ with respect to all galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GP0303-0759 as an outlier with the lowest metallicity and reddening; excluding it and GP1249+1234 strengthens correlations between Lyman-alpha escape, dust reddening, and metallicity.

quotes.quote: One is GP0303-0759 (marked by a large circle) with the lowest metallicity and E(B-V). Its WISE three bands colors suggest it an AGN candidate (Malhotra et al. in preparation). The other one is GP1249+1234 (marked by a large square) which has single peaked line profile and large E(B-V).

After both GP0303-0759 and GP1249+1234 are excluded, the anti-correlation between \ and E(B-V) has Spearman coefficient =-0.62 and P=0.05 (note that =-0.35 and P=0.28 if only the AGN candidate GP0303-0759 is excluded). It suggests dust extinction is an important factor of \ escape even in this Green Peas sample covering a small E(B-V) range. Dust reddening is usually higher in galaxies with higher metallicity and stellar mass. So we expect \ may also anti-correlate with metallicity. Some previous studies have found that \ escape is easier in low metallicity galaxies, while others have found no dependence on metallicity. Cowie et al. (2011) showed that GALEX z 0.3 LAEs have lower metallicities than UV-selected galaxies with similar UV magnitudes but no \ emission. Finkelstein et al. (2011) also showed that GALEX z 0.3 LAEs have lower metallicities than similar-mass galaxies from the SDSS. In contrast, Atek et al. (2014) found that there is no correlation between \ and metallicity for a combined z=0 - 0.3 LAE sample. Hayes et al. (2014) showed that in the LARS sample of 14 LAEs, a few galaxies with high \ have on average lower metallicity than the other galaxies with low . Note that the metallicities in those studies are measured from the strong line indexes ( [ NII ] / , ( [ OII ] + [ OIII ] )/ , or ( [ OIII ] / )/( [ NII ] / ) and have large uncertainties. For the present Green Pea sample, we show the relation between \ and metallicity measured from the more accurate T_ e method (right panel of figure 6). If both outliers GP0303-0759 and GP1249+1234 are excluded, \ anti-correlates with metallicity with Spearman coefficient =-0.82 and P=0.004 (note that =-0.58 and P=0.06 if only the AGN candidate GP0303-0759 is excluded).

gmw_00002144.name: GP1249+1234
gmw_00002144.overview: GP1249+1234 is a starburst galaxy classified as a Lyman-alpha and emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1506.02885]. It exhibits a single-peaked Lyman-alpha profile, high dust extinction, and an unusually high shell expansion velocity of 354 km/s [arXiv:1506.02885].

sources.summary: The paper treats GP1249+1234 as an outlier with a single-peaked Lyman-alpha profile, large E(B-V), and unusually high fitted shell expansion velocity; excluding it strengthens escape-fraction correlations.

quotes.quote: The other one is GP1249+1234 (marked by a large square) which has single peaked line profile and large E(B-V). After both GP0303-0759 and GP1249+1234 are excluded, the anti-correlation between \ and E(B-V) has Spearman coefficient =-0.62 and P=0.05 (note that =-0.35 and P=0.28 if only the AGN candidate GP0303-0759 is excluded). It suggests dust extinction is an important factor of \ escape even in this Green Peas sample covering a small E(B-V) range. Dust reddening is usually higher in galaxies with higher metallicity and stellar mass. So we expect \ may also anti-correlate with metallicity. Some previous studies have found that \ escape is easier in low metallicity galaxies, while others have found no dependence on metallicity. Cowie et al. (2011) showed that GALEX z 0.3 LAEs have lower metallicities than UV-selected galaxies with similar UV magnitudes but no \ emission. Finkelstein et al. (2011) also showed that GALEX z 0.3 LAEs have lower metallicities than similar-mass galaxies from the SDSS. In contrast, Atek et al. (2014) found that there is no correlation between \ and metallicity for a combined z=0 - 0.3 LAE sample. Hayes et al. (2014) showed that in the LARS sample of 14 LAEs, a few galaxies with high \ have on average lower metallicity than the other galaxies with low . Note that the metallicities in those studies are measured from the strong line indexes ( [ NII ] / , ( [ OII ] + [ OIII ] )/ , or ( [ OIII ] / )/( [ NII ] / ) and have large uncertainties. For the present Green Pea sample, we show the relation between \ and metallicity measured from the more accurate T_ e method (right panel of figure 6). If both outliers GP0303-0759 and GP1249+1234 are excluded, \ anti-correlates with metallicity with Spearman coefficient =-0.82 and P=0.004 (note that =-0.58 and P=0.06 if only the AGN candidate GP0303-0759 is excluded).
The best-fit expanding velocities are between 27-127 \ for 8 Green Peas except GP1249+1234 which has V_ exp =354 \ and single peak \ profile.
Shell expansion velocity : The resulting shell expansion velocities are between 20-130 \ for 11/12 Green Peas (and larger for the single-peaked line in GP1249+1234).

gmw_00002145.name: HE 1136-2304
gmw_00002145.overview: HE 1136-2304 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus that transitioned from a Seyfert 1.95 or Seyfert 2 classification in the 1990s to a Seyfert 1.5 state by 2014 [arXiv:1606.04955, arXiv:2210.15376]. This transition was accompanied by a factor of 30 increase in X-ray flux [arXiv:1606.04955, arXiv:2210.15376] and strong continuum variability driven by accretion rate fluctuations [arXiv:1804.05911].

sources.summary: The paper reports HE 1136-2304 as a changing-look AGN whose X-ray flux rose sharply while broad Balmer lines strengthened, shifting it from Seyfert 1.95 to 1.5, likely through increased accretion.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of high-amplitude X-ray flaring of the AGN HE 1136-2304, which is accompanied by a strong increase in the flux of the broad Balmer lines, changing its Seyfert type from almost type 2 in 1993 down to 1.5 in 2014. HE 1136-2304 was detected by the \ slew survey at >10 times the flux it had in the \ all-sky survey, and confirmed with \ follow-up after increasing in X-ray flux by a factor of 30 . Optical spectroscopy with SALT shows that the AGN has changed from a Seyfert 1.95 to a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, with greatly increased broad line emission and an increase in blue continuum AGN flux by a factor of >4 . The X-ray spectra from \ and \ reveal moderate intrinsic absorption and a high energy cutoff at 100 keV.

We have presented X-ray and optical observations of the AGN HE 1136-2304 with , , , and SALT. The AGN was found to have increased in X-ray flux by a factor of 30 , coinciding with the appearance of broad lines in the optical spectrum (and hence a change in classification from Seyfert 1.95 to Seyfert 1.5). We find that the X-ray spectrum requires significant absorption in excess of the Galactic column, with a column density of 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . An increase in column density of approximately 1 order of magnitude (to 2--3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) would be enough to explain the lower flux observed in 1993. However, changes in the absorption cannot explain the increased flux seen by , meaning the majority of the short-term variability must be intrinsic to the source.

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength monitoring of the changing-look Seyfert AGN HE 1136-2304, finding strong continuum variability, an unchanged Seyfert 1.5 state, and variability likely tied to accretion-rate fluctuations.

quotes.quote: A strong outburst in the X-ray continuum and a change of its Seyfert spectral type was detected in HE 1136-2304 in 2014. The spectral type changed from nearly Seyfert 2 type (1.95) to Seyfert 1.5 type in comparison to previous observations taken ten to twenty years before.

HE 1136-2304 strongly varied on timescales of days to months from 2014 to 2017. No systematic trends were found in the variability behavior following the outburst in 2014. A general decrease in flux would have been expected for a tidal disruption event. This could not be confirmed. More likely the flux variations are connected to irregular fluctuations in the accretion rate.
The strongest variability amplitudes have been found in the X-ray regime: HE 1136-2304 varied by a factor of eight during 2015.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE 1136-2304 as an optical changing-look AGN and variable X-ray source whose simultaneous spectra are best represented by a Compton-thin torus model.

quotes.quote: HE 1136-2304 HE 1136-2304 changed its optical spectral classification from 1994 (Seyfert 2) to 2014 (Seyfert 1.5) and can be considered an optical changing look AGN 2018zetl . HE 1136-2304 has been detected as a variable X-ray source by the slew survey in 2014 2016parker . The 0.2–-2 keV flux increased by a factor of about 30 in comparison to the 1990 ROSAT All-Sky Survey flux (RASS; 2000voges ). However, no clear evidence of X-ray absorption variability has been seen. found an absorbing column density on the X-ray spectrum around 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 in addition to Galactic absorption. This spectrum shows a moderate soft excess and a narrow Fe line at 6.4 keV and a high energy cut-off at 100 keV.

Using PEXMON reflection model we found R _ f =3.99 _ 2.85 ^ 5.10 . We also fit the data using borus02 model instead of PEXMON , and we found that reflection features can be produced by a Compton thin torus with (N_ H,refl ) =23.52 _ 23.38 ^ 23.64 covering 59 of the sky.
The borus02 model is the best representation of the data.

gmw_00002146.name: Source 428
gmw_00002146.overview: Source 428 is a complex radio galaxy with multiple radio lobes [arXiv:1206.4571]. The most likely counterpart is a galaxy at a redshift of z=0.8260, which corresponds to a projected maximum lobe extent of 320 kpc [arXiv:1206.4571].

sources.summary: The paper treats Source 428 as a complex radio source with multiple lobes, identifies a likely z=0.826 galaxy counterpart, and estimates a 320 kpc lobe extent.

quotes.quote: Source 428 has multiple radio lobes, and the counterpart could be any of many sources visible in the IRAC image. We suggest the most likely counterpart is a z=0.8260 , R=22.7 galaxy bright at 8 \ and located near the inner edge of the northwest radio lobe. At the suggested redshift, the projected 42 \ maximum extent of the radio lobes corresponds to a length of 320 kpc.

gmw_00002147.name: HESS J1943+213
gmw_00002147.overview: HESS J1943+213 is classified as an extreme high-synchrotron-peak BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1502.03016, arXiv:1603.00877] hosted by a massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1504.03800, arXiv:1408.6976]. High-resolution radio observations reveal a compact core with a diffuse, asymmetric jet-like tail [arXiv:1603.00877] and core flux density variability [arXiv:1504.03800, arXiv:1610.05799].

sources.summary: VERITAS observations test whether HESS J1943+213 is an extreme BL Lac/blazar; its point-like high-energy behavior supports the scenario, though stable flux challenges it.

quotes.quote: In addition, the point-like appearance in the X-rays and the soft VHE spectrum with a power-law index of =3.1 0.3 (in contrast to the softest known PWN index of 2.7) motivated ref. to argue against the PWN scenario, leaving the blazar hypothesis. The authors found all observations to be consistent with the blazar scenario. The point-like nature in both X-rays and VHE, the soft VHE spectral index, and a preliminary IR spectrum showing lack of emission lines are expected for a blazar. Moreover, the hard X-rays observed with INTEGRAL IBIS and Swift BAT instruments show no evidence of a cutoff up to an energy of 195 keV. If the source is a blazar, it would be categorized as an extreme high-synchrotron-peak BL Lac object (extreme HBL), a class of blazars with the synchrotron peak located at energies 1 keV .

The agreement between fluxes measured approximately five years apart with VERITAS and H.E.S.S. and a lack of a variability detection in other energy bands is surprising if HESS J1943+213 is a blazar. Blazars are known to vary at all energies and at a wide range of timescales . Although the stable flux of the source observed to date does not rule out the blazar hypothesis, continued non-detections of variability are becoming a growing challenge for this scenario.

sources.summary: The paper discusses HESS J1943+213 as a TeV source whose counterparts and compact, flat-spectrum variable radio feature support a BL Lac interpretation, with near-infrared evidence for a massive elliptical host.

quotes.quote: HESS J1943+213 is a TeV source very close to the Galactic Plane discovered by the H.E.S.S. Collaboration . Counterparts of the source were identified in X-rays (CXOU J194356.2+211823), infrared (2MASS J19435624+2118233), and in radio (NVSS J194356+211826). In the discovery paper, three possible explanations were offered for the nature of the source: gamma-ray binary, pulsar wind nebula, or BL Lacertae object.

Recently, Peter et al. studied the near-infrared counterpart of the source in K band. The observations are consistent with the infrared source representing the host galaxy of HESS J1943+213, a typical massive elliptical galaxy.

The most recent papers by Tanaka et al. and Peter et al. suggest that the TeV source HESS J1943+213 is a BL Lac object. The source is point-like at the high resolution of EVN (10-mas scale) and also with the e-MERLIN (100-mas scale), according to our new L- and C-band data. The detected compact radio feature has a flat spectrum and in L-band showed flux density variability thus consistent with the proposed BL Lac nature of the source.

sources.summary: The paper classifies HESS J1943+213 as an extreme synchrotron BL Lac/blazar, reporting stable VHE emission plus VLBA core-jet structure, blazar-like spectral indices, and a potential near-infrared host galaxy.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The observations were targeted at the position reported from the EVN detection: = 19 ^ h 43 ' 56. '' 2372, Dec: 21 ^ 18 ' 23. '' 402.

quotes.quote: HESS J1943+213 is a very high energy (VHE; > 100 GeV) ray source in the direction of the Galactic plane. 38 hours of deep VERITAS observations taken over two seasons detect the source with 20 significance. Monitoring observations of HESS J1943+213 show a remarkably stable flux and spectrum in VHE rays. Studies exploring the classification of HESS J1943+213 are converging towards accepting the source as an extreme synchrotron BL Lac object. Specifically, overall SED characteristics of the source, the detection of a potential host galaxy in near-IR imaging, and VLBI observations of the HESS J1943+213 radio counterpart showing extended jet-like emission at milliarcsecond scale and core flux density variability establish the source as a blazar.

Recent Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations of the source (shown here for the first time) confirm the extended structure found in the 1.6 GHz band and detect the jet-like component in the 4.6 GHz and the 7.3 GHz bands. The spectral indices of the core and the jet-like components derived from the VLBA observations are in a range typical for blazars. HESS J1943+213 fits the extreme HBL description; however, with variability detected only at milliarcseconds scales in radio, it appears to be an abnormally stable VHE ray blazar.

sources.summary: The paper treats 2MASS J19435624+2118233 as the extended near-infrared counterpart and likely BL Lac host of HESS J1943+213, favoring an extragalactic elliptical blazar interpretation.

quotes.quote: We present K-band imaging from the 3.5 meter CAHA telescope of 2MASS J19435624+2118233. Furthermore, 5 years of Fermi LAT data were analyzed to search for a high-energy (HE, 100 MeV <E< 100 GeV) counterpart.

The CAHA observations revealed that the near-infrared counterpart is extended with an intrinsic half light radius of 2 \ - 2.5 \ . These observations also show a smooth, centrally concentrated light profile that is typical of a galaxy, and thus point toward an extragalactic scenario for the VHE gamma-ray source, assuming that the near-infrared source is the counterpart of HESS J1943+213. A high-S ersic index profile provides a better fit than an exponential profile, indicating that the surface brightness profile of \ follows that of a typical, massive elliptical galaxy more closely than that of a disk galaxy.

The infrared and HE data strongly favor an extragalactic origin of \ , where the infrared counterpart traces the host galaxy of an extreme blazar and where the rather sharp spectral break between the HE and VHE regime indicates attenuation on extragalactic background light. The source is most likely located at a redshift between 0.03 and 0.45 according to extension and EBL attenuation arguments.

sources.summary: The appendix describes HESS J1943+213 as a Galactic-plane VHE point source with hard X-rays, no significant VHE variability, an elliptical host-galaxy indication, VERITAS detection, and redshift constraints.

quotes.quote: The H.E.S.S. telescopes discovered VHE -ray emission from the point source HESS J1943+213 located in the Galactic plane and spatially coincident with the hard X-ray unidentified source IGR J19443+2117 during the Galactic plane survey between 2005 and 2008 . The time averaged differential VHE spectrum was described by a power law with a soft photon spectral index of 3.1 0.3 in the energy range 470 GeV--6 TeV. No significant variability was observed in the VHE ray emission of the source during this period. The X-ray spectrum of the source was observed to be hard with a spectral index 1.04 and without any cut-off up to 195 keV. The IR observations indicated an elliptical host galaxy with redshift z 0.14 .

The VERITAS observations of this source between May 2014 and November 2015 revealed a detection of VHE -rays described by a power law with a spectral index of 2.81 0.12 in the energy range of 180 GeV to 2 TeV . Near simultaneous observation with the Fermi LAT in the energy range 3-300 GeV was used to constrain its redshift z < 0.23 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses HESS J1943+213 as a point-like hard-spectrum source with possible blazar origins; VERITAS observed a strong, steady VHE excess.

quotes.quote: HESS J1943+213 is a point-like, > 500 GeV emitter found in the HESS Galactic Plane scan . It is a hard-spectrum Fermi LAT source and might be a blazar, but no flux variations have been observed, nor has any redshift been measured. To probe the object's possible blazar origins, VERITAS observed HESS J1943+213 for 22.5 h of good-quality live time in 2014. A strong excess (18 ; 3.7 h ^ 0.5 ) was observed, corresponding to a flux that is consistent with the HESS (2005-08) measurement. No flux or spectral variations are seen on daily or weekly time scales.

sources.summary: The paper presents EVN imaging of HESS J1943+213, finding a compact core plus diffuse jet-like tail and arguing its radio properties support an extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac classification.

quotes.quote: We report on the 1.6 GHz (18 cm) VLBI observations of the unresolved, steady TeV source HESS J1943+213 located in the Galactic plane, performed with the European VLBI Network (EVN) in 2014. Our new observations with a nearly full EVN array provide the deepest image of HESS J1943+213 at the highest resolution ever achieved, enabling us to resolve the long-standing issues of the source identification. The milliarcsecond-scale structure of HESS J1943+213 has a clear asymmetric morphology, consisting of a compact core and a diffuse jet-like tail.

The core component is characterized by the brightness temperature of 1.8 10^9 K, which is typical for low-luminosity blazars in general. Overall, radio properties of HESS J1943+213 are consistent with the source classification as an ``extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac object''.

sources.summary: The paper reports J1943+2118 was previously misassociated: earlier polarization came from a star, while the faint TeV AGN matches a nearby 2MASS source and cannot be measured in RoboPol images.

quotes.quote: J1943+2118: A few measurements for this TeV source have been presented in Hovatta2016 . However, reanalysing data for this paper, we discovered that the source was previously misassociated. The measured polarization corresponds to a 17 mag star, while the TeV AGN is associated with 2MASS source J1943562+2118233 Landi2009 that is located 5.3 arcsec North-West from this star. Since the AGN is very faint R 22.4 mag, it cannot be measured in our images. For this reason J1943+2118 is not present in the data table.

gmw_00002148.name: ALMA-only source
gmw_00002148.overview: The ALMA-only source, also known as COS--1, is a highly obscured, star-forming galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.5 to 5.857 [arXiv:1905.11996, arXiv:2102.07772]. It is undetected at optical and near-infrared wavelengths but possesses a substantial molecular gas mass [arXiv:1905.11996, arXiv:2102.07772].

sources.summary: The ALMA-only source is a real, optically undetected z 5.5 dusty star-forming galaxy with high stellar and gas mass, heavy obscuration, and potential importance for high-redshift mass density.

quotes.quote: The other source is missing from all previous optical/near-infrared/sub-mm/radio catalogs (``ALMA-only''), and remains undetected even in stacked ultradeep optical ( >29.6 AB) and near-infrared ( >27.9 AB) images. Using the ALMA position as a prior reveals faint SNR 3 measurements in stacked IRAC 3.6+4.5, ultradeep SCUBA2 850 m, and VLA 3GHz, indicating the source is real. The SED is robustly reproduced by a massive M^*=10^ 10.8 M _ and M_ gas =10^ 11 M _ , highly obscured A_V 4 , star forming SFR 300 M _ yr ^ 1 galaxy at redshift z=5.5 1.1.

Contribution to Stellar mass density The high stellar mass 10^ 10.8 M _ and large space density 3 10^ 5 Mpc ^ 3 of \ imply a considerable cosmic stellar mass density (CSMD) in similar objects at z 5 : ^*=1.9^ +4.4 _ 1.5 10^6 M _ Mpc ^ 3 , higher than reported for bright (S _ 850 > 4 mJy) sub-mm galaxies 0.5 10^6 M _ Mpc ^ 3 ; Michalowski2017 . Comparing to the estimate of the CSMD based on HST-selected galaxies ^*(z=5)=6.3 10^6 M _ Mpc ^ 3 Song2016 , suggests that they could contribute a significant fraction ( 22^ +25 _ 16 ) to the total and perhaps even dominate the high-mass end. The high-mass end of the galaxy stellar mass function at high redshift z>4 is still uncertain and susceptible to biases. Current estimates for the number density of 10^ 10.8 M _ galaxies at z 5 are 1-5 10^ 5 Mpc ^ 3 dex ^ 1 Duncan2014, Grazian2015, Song2016, Davidzon2017, Stefanon2017_900nm , comparable to the space density derived for our ALMA-only galaxy . Given that optical-IRAC dark galaxies are missing from these previous studies, it is therefore possible that about half the stellar mass density in high-mass galaxies at z 5 remains unaccounted for.

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative CO line for COS--1 at z=5.857, making it a possible record high-redshift 3 mm-selected dusty galaxy with substantial molecular gas.

quotes.quote: Zavala 1 The University of Texas at Austin, 2515 Speedway Blvd, Austin, TX 78712, USA I report a tentative ( 4 ) emission line at =100.84 GHz from ``COS--1'', a 3 mm-selected galaxy reported by Williams2019a that is undetected at optical and near infrared wavelengths. The line was found in the ALMA Science Archive after re-processing ALMA band 3 observations targeting a different source. Assuming the line corresponds to the CO(6 5) transition, this tentative detection implies a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.857 , in agreement with the galaxy's redshift constraints from multi-wavelength photometry. This would make this object the highest redshift 3 mm-selected galaxy and one of the highest redshift dusty star-forming galaxies known to-date.

The ^ 12 CO(6 5) line luminosity of L'_ CO(6 5) =(2.55 0.52) 10^ 9 K km s^ 1 pc^2 can be used to calculate the molecular gas mass of ``COS--1'' by, first, estimating the CO(1 0) line luminosity and, then, assuming a CO-to-H _2 conversion factor. I assume the line luminosity ratio reported by Bothwell2013a ( L'_ CO(6 5) /L'_ CO(1 0) =0.21 0.04 ) and a CO-to-H _2 conversion factor of _ CO =4.6 M_ K km s^ 1 pc^2 . Based on these assumptions, I infer a gas mass of M_ gas = (6 2) 10^ 10 M_ , which implies a depletion time scale ( t_ dep M_ gas / SFR ) of 200 Myr (adopting the star formation rate of 300 M_ yr^ 1 derived by Williams2019a ).

gmw_00002149.name: SDSS J123553.51+054723.5
gmw_00002149.overview: SDSS J123553.51+054723.5 is a barred S0 galaxy located in the green valley [arXiv:2005.07387]. It features an extended disturbed disk with a tail, which may indicate a past interaction with a nearby elliptical galaxy that triggered central star formation [arXiv:2005.07387].

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS J123553.51+054723.5 as the bluest-barred S0 in its sample, with disturbed disk, HI gas, possible neighbor interaction, and central star formation.

quotes.quote: The bluest barred S0 galaxy SDSS J123553.51+054723 (see Section sec:bluest for more details.) is in the green valley along with few more S0 galaxies with a blue bar.

In this study, we found that a galaxy SDSS J123553.51+054723.5 hosting one of the bluest bar in our S0 galaxy sample with bar (g-i) colour of 0.40, while bulge which is a classical bulge has (g-i) colour of 1.05 and disc (g-i) colour is 0.95. This galaxy is at redshift 0.042 and has an extended disturbed disc along with the tail (see Fig. fig:f7 ), a sign of past interaction, possibly with a nearby elliptical galaxy SDSS J123553.79+054539.8 at the same redshift. The HI gas was detected for SDSS J123553.51+054723.5 in the extended GALEX Arecibo SDSS Survey (xGASS) Catinella2010 which gives the integrated HI mass (M_ HI / M_ ) =8.86 . Considering the stellar mass of this galaxy, this implies M_ HI /M_ * 0.02 . Although compared to normal spiral galaxies the gas to stellar mass ratio is significantly lower, the galaxy is not literally devoid of gas as in many massive S0 galaxies.

Since the galaxy seems to be interacting with the neighbour or it could be even in flybys, it might be responsible in triggering star-formation in the central region of this S0 galaxy and might even have led to the formation of the bar Lokas2018 .

gmw_00002150.name: IC 664
gmw_00002150.overview: IC 664 is a massive elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.0338 [arXiv:2310.04629]. It serves as the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 1142, which has recently undergone a major merger that separated the galaxy from its orphan cool core by 80 kpc [arXiv:2310.04629].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 664 as Abell 1142's massive elliptical BCG, offset 80 kpc from the orphan cool core; its separation helps test BCG enrichment and feedback roles.

quotes.quote: Chandra Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer (ACIS)-S observation reveals that its X-ray peak is 80 kpc east of the BCG, IC 664, a massive elliptical galaxy at z=0.0338 Su2016 .

The BCG is at a redshift of 0.0338 , which is 800 km/s offset from the median redshift of the member galaxies at 0.0365 . We assume that the BCG travels at 800 km / s relative to the ICM of Abell 1142. Given that the BCG and the orphan cool core are offset by 80 kpc in projection, we infer that the orphan cool core may have been detached from the BCG for more than 100 Myr.

This result hints that the stellar mass loss of the BCG may have a limited contribution to the enrichment of cluster centers. However, the consistent abundance ratios within 1 error of the orphan cool core ( 0.95 0.28 Solar ratio) and the BCG ISM ( 0.65 0.28 Solar ratio) mean that the issue remains unresolved. Abell 1142 has clearly been through a recent major merger that has disassociated the cool core from the BCG and created a bimodal velocity distribution of the member galaxies.

gmw_00002151.overview: The object 0917+624 is a quasar located at a redshift of 1.453 [arXiv:1504.04141]. It historically exhibited strong intra-day variability in total and polarized flux density [arXiv:1504.04141, arXiv:astro-ph/9902328], though this strong variability largely ceased after the year 2000 [arXiv:1504.04141].

sources.summary: The paper monitors quasar S4 0917+624, finding its formerly strong intra-day variability largely ceased after 2000 while VLBI modeling measured core and inner-jet structure.

quotes.quote: In order to better understand the long-term behavior of IDV sources, we carried out an IDV monitoring program for a number of IDV sources, including quasar S4 0917+624 at the 6 cm band from August 2005 to January 2010 with the Urumqi 25 m radio telescope in Xinjiang Astronomical Observatory, China. The quasar S4 0917+624 (OK 630, z=1.453) was one of the first strong IDV sources found (Heeschen et al. 1987) with high variability amplitudes of 10-15 occurring on timescales of 1 day. Jauncey Macquart (2001) and Rickett et al. (2001) analyzed IDV data of S4 0917+624 and find a systematic variation of the variability timescale over the year.

The quasar S4 0917+624 exhibits only very weak or no IDV during our 4.5 year observing interval. Prior to the year 2000, the source S4 0917+624 was one of the most prominent IDV sources. Our new data indicate that the previous strong IDV has ceased. We analyzed the long-term VLBI structural variability using Gaussian model-fitting. From this we obtained the flux densities and the deconvolved sizes of core and inner-jet components of the source.

sources.summary: The paper presents 0917+624 as an IDV quasar with strong polarized variability, anti-correlated total and polarized flux, larger polarization-angle swings, and possible small-viewing-angle jet behavior.

quotes.quote: While total intensity variations range from a few percent up to 35 (e.g. \ in the case of the QSO 0804+499, Qui2 ), variability in the polarized intensity is usually larger and can reach a factor of two e.g. \ in the QSO 0917+624 Kraus1 . So far, we have found no significant correlation between the strength of the variations or the timescales with either the redshift of the source, the galactic latitude, or the spectral index. In Fig. \ fig1 , we show, as an example of the rapid variations, the variability observed in the quasar 0917+624 in total and polarized flux density and polarization angle (from top to bottom).

The anti-correlation between I and P is seen frequently in 0917+624, while other sources (e.g. \ the BL Lac object 0716+714) rather show a direct correlation between both values (e.g. \ Wagn1 , Kraus et al., in preparation). Similar polarization angle variability was also observed in other sources. In 0917+624 the angular variations usually are larger than shown in Fig. \ fig1 (e.g. \ Kraus1 ). Once, even a 180 swing was observed Qui3 .
In the case of a small viewing angle of the jet, even 180 swings (as observed in 0917+624 by Qui3 ) are possible.

gmw_00002152.overview: 11072 is a galaxy pair of comparable luminosity that has recently undergone its first pericenter passage [arXiv:1404.2281]. The system features a weak tidal bridge and a tail-like structure that lacks stellar clumps [arXiv:1404.2281].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 11072 as a comparable-luminosity galaxy pair just after first pericenter passage, with a weak tidal bridge and a tail-like structure lacking stellar clumps.

quotes.quote: Shown in Figure fig:fig2 , 11072 is a pair of galaxies of comparable z_ 850 band luminosity. The system has just undergone its first pericenter passage with a weak tidal bridge. A tail-like structure is seen with color V_ 606 z_ 850 =1.28 , similar to the color of the central components ( V_ 606 z_ 850 =1.33 ). No stellar clump is found on the tidal tail.

gmw_00002153.name: Galaxy 49368
gmw_00002153.overview: Galaxy 49368 is a disk galaxy that features bright stellar clumps of approximately 1 kpc in size located on one of its spiral arms [arXiv:1404.2281]. These structural features are likely formed by minor mergers or instabilities within a gas-rich, turbulent disk [arXiv:1404.2281].

sources.summary: The paper says Galaxy 49368 has 1 kpc bright stellar clumps on a spiral arm, biasing A_o and D_o and linking such clumps to minor mergers or disk instabilities.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 49368 : it has bright stellar clumps of 1 kpc size on one spiral arm, which seriously bias the estimation of A_ o and D_ o . Such features can be frequently seen in z>2 disk galaxies. Minor mergers and instabilities in gas rich, turbulent disks are likely responsible for the formation of such clumps 2008ApJ...687...59G,2009ApJ...694L.158B,2009ApJ...703..785D .

gmw_00002154.overview: The galaxy 1032 is characterized by a single, highly curved spiral arm containing star-forming clumps [arXiv:1404.2281]. Its morphology resembles a shrimp-type system, although it displays no apparent signatures of tidal disturbance [arXiv:1404.2281].

sources.summary: The paper groups 1032 with galaxies showing one prominent, highly curved spiral arm with star-forming clumps, resembling shrimp-type systems but lacking clear tidal disturbance.

quotes.quote: Galaxies 1032, 29488, 35796 and 61789 : they are characterized by one prominent spiral arm. Their morphologies are similar to the so-called shrimp-type galaxies defined by 2007ApJ...663..734E . The highly curved spiral arm together with star-forming clumps on it usually imply that they are gas-rich systems disturbed by minor mergers. However, no apparent signature is found for tidal disturbance in the four galaxies.

gmw_00002155.overview: The galaxy 29488 is characterized by a single, highly curved spiral arm [arXiv:1404.2281]. This arm contains star-forming clumps, which suggests the galaxy is a gas-rich system [arXiv:1404.2281].

gmw_00002158.overview: The object 20664 is an edge-on disk galaxy characterized by a clear dust lane feature [arXiv:1404.2281]. This dust lane divides the galaxy's light profile into two parts, causing the system to be treated as a double-core system where a thick dust lane can significantly shift the centroid from the geometric center [arXiv:1404.2281].

sources.summary: The paper groups 20664 with edge-on disk galaxies whose dust lanes split the light profile, causing double-core treatment and shifting the IHR centroid.

quotes.quote: Galaxies 20664, 30167, 30189 and 56918 : they are edge-on disk galaxies with a clear dust lane feature. The dust lane divides the light profile of the parent galaxy into two parts. In such cases, the system is treated as a double core system in our analysis. The centroid of the IHR would significantly deviate from the geometric center if the dust lane is thick and splits the galaxy into two non-equal parts.

gmw_00002159.overview: The object 30167 is classified as an edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:1404.2281]. It features a dust lane that divides its light profile into two parts, causing the system to be treated as a double core system [arXiv:1404.2281].

gmw_00002162.overview: The object 37335 is classified as an Sd late-type galaxy [arXiv:1404.2281]. It is characterized by a relatively low surface brightness and a clumpy light distribution [arXiv:1404.2281].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 37335 as an Sd galaxy with relatively low surface brightness, where clumpy light distribution and background noise strongly bias A_o and D_o measurements.

quotes.quote: Galaxies 37335 and 59517 : they are classified as Sd galaxies. Their surface brightness is relatively low. And their A_ o and D_ o are largely biased by clumpy light distribution and background noise.

gmw_00002164.name: Galaxy 51329
gmw_00002164.overview: Galaxy 51329 is an edge-on disk galaxy that exhibits an apparently warped disk [arXiv:1404.2281]. This warping produces a high asymmetry value for the galaxy and may be the result of minor mergers or interactions [arXiv:1404.2281].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy 51329 as an edge-on disk galaxy with an apparently warped disk; its warping gives a high asymmetry value and may reflect minor mergers or interactions.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 51329 : it is an edge-on disk galaxy. The disk is apparently warped. Minor mergers/interactions are often proposed as the cause of warped disks 1990MNRAS.246..458S,1991A&A...242..301F,1998A&A...337....9R,2006NewA...11..293A .
A high A is obtained for galaxy 51329 due to the warped disk as shown in Figure fig:fig4 .

gmw_00002165.name: NGC 5623
gmw_00002165.overview: NGC 5623 is an early-type galaxy classified as a merger remnant without a close companion [arXiv:1506.03819]. Its stellar velocity distribution reveals a kinematically decoupled core, which is oriented nearly perpendicular to its ionized gas field [arXiv:1506.03819].

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 5623 as an early-type merger remnant without a close companion, with a stellar kinematically decoupled core and nearly perpendicular ionized-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: Although the scope of this study is to present statistical results of the kinematic (mis)alignments of a merging sample in comparison to a isolated sample, for the sake of illustration we highlight one object with large morpho-kinematic misalignments, the early-type galaxy NGC 5623. This galaxy does not present a close companion, or any other readily observable signature of interaction from the images (we classify this object as a remnant); however, its stellar velocity distribution reveals a clear kinematically decoupled core (KDC). This KDC is aligned with the morphological PA of the galaxy. However, the ionized gas field (which extends 10 arcsec), has an orientation nearly perpendicular to the KDC orientation.

gmw_00002166.name: NGC 3303
gmw_00002166.overview: NGC 3303 is a merging galaxy system that exhibits a highly distorted morphology [arXiv:1506.03819]. The system features counter-rotating gas and stars, distorted ionized-gas kinematics, and two close nuclei embedded in a single disk-like structure with large tidal features [arXiv:1506.03819].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3303 as the sample's most distorted merging galaxy, with counter-rotating gas and stars, distorted ionized-gas kinematics, close nuclei, and large tidal features.

quotes.quote: The galaxy that shows the strongest distortion is the merging galaxy NGC 3303 (and its companion NGC 3303 NED01). It reaches the counter-rotating case ( _ gas-stars 180 ^ ), and as we already mention in Sect. sec:deltaK , this system presents a distorted ionized gas velocity field. Although this system is classified morphologically as a pair of early-type galaxies, the close projected separation between the nuclei ( 5 kpc) and the fact that they are embedded in a single disk-like structure with large tidal features suggest that this system is caught right before the two nuclei coalesced 2000MNRAS.318..124G, 2014ApJ...784...16M .

gmw_00002167.name: NGC 4772
gmw_00002167.overview: NGC 4772 is an early-type spiral galaxy located at the boundaries of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. It likely formed through a past minor merger [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243], which created dynamically decoupled central components [arXiv:0908.3564], concentric rings [arXiv:0908.3564], and a small, counter-rotating central dusty disc [arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: The paper probes dust extinction in NGC 4772 through SN 2012cu, finding a flat UV extinction law, weak or absent 2175 feature, and unusually large dust grains.

quotes.quote: Using photometric and spectroscopic data of Supernova (SN) 2012cu, a fairly reddened type Ia supernova, we derived its color excess curves and probed the dust extinction in its host galaxy, NGC 4772. In order to derive the extinction as a function of wavelength (i.e., A_ ), we model the color excess curves of SN 2012cu in terms of dust models consisting of silicate and carbonaceous (graphite or amorphous carbon) dust. The modeled extinction law towards SN 2012cu extends flatly to the far-ultraviolet (UV) bands, which is much flatter than those of the Milky Way and Magellanic Clouds, and the 2175 feature is very weak or absent. The flatness of the modeled extinction curve in the UV bands suggests a ``grey'' extinction law of the active galactic nucleus in the vicinity of the SN 2012cu-Earth line of sight. Our results indicate that the sizes of the dust in the ISM towards SN 2012cu in NGC 4772 are larger than those of the Milky Way and the Large Magellanic Cloud, and much larger than that of the Small Magellanic Cloud.

In addition, since the detailed abundance of NGC 4772 is not clear, we cannot simply eliminate the existence of carbonaceous dust in this host galaxy of SN 2012cu. In NGC 4772, Ciesla derived the mass fraction of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) 3.25 based on the dust model discussed in DL07 , even though there is no positive evidence for the presence of PAHs features.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 4772 as Core-halo, describing diffuse H-alpha around a nuclear source, dust-lane and H I gas, merger-spread gas, and possible off-plane ionized gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4772 (see Fig. Figure_AB8 ): The H emission of this galaxy is classified here as Core-halo . reported a centrally peaked emission surrounded by diffuse gas affected by the presence of a dust lane, coincident with HI gas. They suggest that this galaxy may have gone through a merger that spread out the gas through the disc. FalconBarroso2006 detected ionised gas with SAURON data that was oriented in the north-west and south-west direction, possibly coming from a ring out of the main galactic plane. Although there could be other interpretations as the main stellar and gas components of this galaxy are known to co-rotate.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4772 as a Virgo Sa likely produced by a minor merger, with decoupled central components, concentric H I rings, UV/H star formation, and a warped outer ring.

quotes.quote: NGC4772 is a Sa galaxy in the Virgo S cloud. Recently, carried out a detailed multiwavelength analysis of this object, showing that it is probably the result of a minor merger event. The main observational evidence supporting such scenario is: 1) the presence of dynamically decoupled central gas and stellar components, in particular in correspondence of the star forming ring visible in UV (Fig. FCtrans ) and H , 2) the unusual distribution, which is segregated in two concentric rings, the inner one where star formation is taking place and 3) the presence of a warp in the outer ring. The most likely scenario is that NGC4772 is at the end stage of a prograde merger in which the transfer of angular momentum leads to an outward spread of the disk quinn93,haynes00 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4772 as a bar-strength outlier whose SB0 morphology, large bulge, and weak disk make torque-based bar measurements small.

quotes.quote: As an example, the two systems with the largest H band F_ bar for their Q_g values (the points on the bottom right of Fig. 2b) are NGC 3166 and NGC 4772. Both these galaxies have H band morphologies of SB0 (Eskridge et al. 2002), with relatively large bulges and weak disks. The F_ bar statistic is very good at describing such bars, but Q_g is not. This is due to the relatively strong bulges causing the effective torque from the bars to be small.

sources.summary: NGC 4772 is described as a Virgo-boundary galaxy with an eastern dust lane, regular major-axis stellar rotation, and a central dusty disc aligned with the ionised gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4772 is a galaxy located at the boundaries of the Virgo cluster hjbbm00 . A strong dust lane is seen to the East. The stellar kinematics display regular rotation along the major axis of the galaxy. The unsharp-masked image reveals a small dusty disc in the centre of the galaxy, the orientation of which seems consistent with that of the ionised gas in the central 5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4772 as an intermediate-size galaxy with regular major-axis rotation, a small dusty central disc nearly counter-rotating relative to stars, and old SAURON-field populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4772 is a an intermediate size galaxy showing regular rotation along the major axis. The unsharp-masked image reveals a small dusty disc in the centre of the galaxy, the orientation of which seems consistent with that of the ionised gas in the central 5 , but rotating almost in the opposite sense of the stars, in agreement with Haynes et al. (2000). The absorption lines show that the stellar populations are predominantly old in the SAURON field.

sources.summary: NGC 4772 is classified as borderline truncated/anemic, with low normalized massive star formation rates across the disk and kinematic signatures indicating a past minor merger.

quotes.quote: Three additional galaxies, NGC 4579, NGC 4694 and NGC 4772, can be considered borderline truncated/anemic. All have NMSFRs which are low across the disk, but by slightly less than a factor of two, and so are classified as truncated/normal. The Southern Extension galaxy NGC 4772 shows kinematic signatures of a past minor merger (Haynes et al. 2000), and NGC 4694 exhibits peculiarities suggestive of a tidal interaction (Section trunnorm ).

gmw_00002168.name: NGC 3319
gmw_00002168.overview: NGC 3319 is a nearby, gas-rich barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1509.04881] characterized by a bulgeless structure [arXiv:1810.10283] and a grand-design spiral pattern [arXiv:astro-ph/0406494]. The galaxy's nucleus hosts an active intermediate-mass black hole candidate [arXiv:1810.10283] and exhibits a short central bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0406494].

sources.summary: The paper reports an active intermediate-mass black hole candidate in NGC 3319, a nearby barred bulgeless galaxy with nuclear X-ray, optical, and UV signatures and a prominent bar.

quotes.quote: Discovery of An Active Intermediate-Mass Black Hole Candidate in the Barred Bulgeless Galaxy NGC 3319 Ning Jiang 1,2 , Tinggui Wang 1,2 , Hongyan Zhou 1,3 , Xinwen Shu 4 , Chenwei Yang 3 , Liming Dou 5 , Luming Sun 1,2 , Xiaobo Dong 6 , Shaoshao Wang 4 , Huan Yang 7 1 Key laboratory for Research in Galaxies and Cosmology, Department of Astronomy, University of Science and Technology of China, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Hefei, Anhui 230026, China;  jnac@ustc.edu.cn 2 School of Astronomy and Space Sciences, University of Science and Technology of China, Hefei, Anhui 230026;  twang@ustc.edu.cn 3 Polar Research Institute of China, 451 Jinqiao Road, Shanghai, 200136, China;  zhouhongyan@pric.org.cn 4 Department of Physics, Anhui Normal University, Wuhu, Anhui, 241000, China 5 Center for Astrophysics, Guangzhou University, Guangzhou, 510006, China 6 Yunnan Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Kunming, Yunnan 650011, China; Key Laboratory for the Structure and Evolution of Celestial Objects, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Kunming, Yunnan 650011, China 7 Las Campanas Observatory, Carnegie Institution for Science, Casilla 601, La Serena, Chile We report the discovery of an active intermediate-mass black hole (IMBH) candidate in the center of nearby barred bulgeless galaxy NGC 3319. The point X-ray source revealed by archival \ and \ observations is spatially coincident with the optical and UV galactic nuclei from Hubble Space Telescope observations. The spectral energy distribution derived from the unresolved X-ray and UV-optical flux is comparable with active galactic nuclei (AGNs) rather than ultra-luminous X-ray sources, although its bolometric luminosity is only 3.6 10^ 40   . Assuming an Eddington ratio range between 0.001 and 1, the black hole mass ( ) will be located at 3 10^2-3 10^5   , placing it in the so-called IMBH regime and could be the one of the lowest reported so far.

Last but not least, the barred bulgeless host of NGC 3319 is also very distinguishing. The most analogous object before is NGC 4178, which also contains a NSC and is located at a similar distance, but with a \ two orders of magnitudes higher. The absence of a notable bulge also reduce the starlight contamination to recognize the signal from AGN. As a brief summary, the BH in NGC 3319 is a unique IMBH in terms of its close distance, low luminosity, high accretion rate, bulgeless host, and most interestingly the small central BH.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3319, a gas-rich barred spiral with a major-axis bar, to test rotation-curve corrections and show large effects on inferred dark-matter mass models.

quotes.quote: We illustrate this method using NGC 3319, which has its bar aligned with the major axis, as a test case. NGC 3319 is a gas-rich barred spiral galaxy classified as SBcd(rs) by at a distance of 14.3 Mpc 1999ApJ...523..540S . We compare the inferred mass profile for the uncorrected and corrected RCs and show that the conclusions about the dark halo structure vary greatly. While our method still needs refinement, this result clearly demonstrates the importance of taking into account non-circular motions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3319 as an outlier where short bars and grand-design spiral patterns produce low F_bar values but a large disk torque.

quotes.quote: In the same plot, the two galaxies with the largest Q_g for their H band F_ bar values are NGC 1042 and NGC 3319. Both these systems have grand design spiral patterns and short bars. The shortness of the bars appears to drive the low F_ bar values. But the combination of the bars and the strong spiral patterns results in a large disk torque.

gmw_00002169.name: Ton S180
gmw_00002169.overview: Ton S180 is a local, bare narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.06575, arXiv:2308.08528] characterized by a smooth soft X-ray excess [arXiv:1710.11166, arXiv:2007.06575] and a weak warm absorber [arXiv:0808.3904]. The galaxy exhibits X-ray flux variations [arXiv:astro-ph/0108096], with correlated soft and hard-band variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0402676] and evidence of a 2.08-day periodic signal [arXiv:astro-ph/0412636].

sources.summary: The paper describes Ton S180 as an NLS1 with broad Fe K emission and strong soft excess, fit well by high-density reflection with high spin and compact corona.

quotes.quote: Ton S180 Ton S180 is a NLS1 galaxy veron06 that shows both a broad Fe K emission line and a strong soft excess e.g. vaughan02, takahashi10, nardini12, parker18 . takahashi10 demonstrate that the soft excess emission shown in the \ spectrum of Ton S180 can be described as a disc blackbody-shaped model with a temperature of kT= 0.075 keV. nardini12 found that the broad band \ and \ spectra of Ton S180 can be modelled by a combination of two reflection components, one from the inner disc and one from a distant reflector. A more recent study by parker18 succssfully model the broad band \ spectrum of Ton S180 with a combination of a soft Comptonisation component, a hard Comptonisation component from the corona, and a relativistic disc reflection component. The soft Compotonisation component accounts for the soft excess.

Similar to previous analysis, a broad emission line in the iron band and a strong soft excess below 3 keV are found in the spectrum. Second, we model the broad band spectrum with MODEL2 . MODEL2 offers a very good fit of both the broad emission line feature and the soft excess emission with C-stat/ =301.71/179.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Ton S180's X-ray spectrum, finding a narrow iron line, smooth soft excess not explained by reflection, and broad-band Comptonization plus reflection models favoring low black-hole spin.

quotes.quote: We analyse a long archival \ observation of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy Ton S180, using the latest reflection models to explore the high quality X-ray spectrum. We find that the iron line is relatively narrow and sharp, and the soft excess is extremely smooth. We cannot find an acceptable reflection model that describes both components, and conclude that the soft excess cannot be produced by relativistic reflection. Fitting the 3--10 keV band with relativistic reflection to model the iron line strongly prefers low spin values ( <0.4 ), with the exact value depending on the model and not well constrained.

We then model the broad-band spectrum with a two-component Comptonization continuum plus relativistic reflection. This gives a much better fit than a pure reflection model, which again prefers a low spin value. The photon index of the reflection component is intermediate between the two Comptonization components, suggesting that both illuminate the disk a similar amount and therefore both contribute to the reflection.

sources.summary: The paper studies Tonantzintla (Ton) S180 as a luminous bare narrow-line Seyfert 1 whose broadband spectrum favors a warm-corona plus reflection model over pure relativistic reflection.

quotes.quote: Tonantzintla (Ton) S180 is a local z=0.06198 ; Wisotzki95 , luminous 5 10^ 45 ; Turner02 narrow line Seyfert 1 (NLSy1), which is considered one of the prototypical `bare' AGN with no trace of absorption and a featureless and prominent soft excess Turner01,Vaughan02b . The 2007 Suzaku observation (102 ks) suggested also an intriguing hard excess in the HXD/PIN energy range E 15 - 55 keV; Takahashi10 . However, since the HXD was a non-focusing detector Takahashi07 , proper background subtraction is very critical especially for faint sources at E>10 as .

The warm corona scenario In contrast, in case (ii), we find that the 0.4--30 keV band (and consistently down to the available optical/UV photometric points) can be explained by a combination of: (a) direct thermal emission from the accretion disc and/or (b) optical/UV seed disc-photons that are Compton up-scattered by a warm ( kT_ e 0.3 keV) and optically thick ( 10 ) plasma, dominating below 1 ; () a hot ( kT_ e >60 keV) and optically thin ( <0.5 ) Comptonizing corona responsible for the primary X-ray emission above 2 keV; and (d) a mildly relativistic reflection component responsible for the feature at 6.6 keV and the hard X-ray emission. This `hybrid' warm corona plus reflection model can simultaneously account for the soft and hard X-ray spectrum of .
