We note that all three hosts of repeating FRBs lie in the star-forming region and the host of repeating FRB20121102A remains an outlier as compared to the hosts of other FRBs Li+19 .
As a low metallicity dwarf galaxy, the host of FRB20121102A continues to be an outlier in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes the FRB 121102 host as a star-forming dwarf in the strongest star-formation, lowest-mass region, resembling LGRB and SLSN host environments.

quotes.quote: FRB 121102, the first repeater and the first FRB localized with an arcsecond precision, resides in the brightest region of a star forming dwarf galaxy tendulkar17, bassa17, chatterjee17,kokubo17 , whose properties are quite similar to the host galaxies of young stellar population transients, e.g., LGRBs and Superluminous Supernovae (SLSNe).
For FRBs, the FRB 121102 host is located in the region with the least stellar mass and strongest star formation.

The host of FRB 121102 has 90 probability to belong to the SLSN sample and 10 probability to belong to the LGRB sample, and its probabilities to belong to SN Ibc, SN II, SN Ia, and SGRB samples are significantly smaller.

gmw_00002188.name: Henize 2-10
gmw_00002188.overview: Henize 2-10 is a nearby blue compact dwarf starburst galaxy [arXiv:1705.09667, arXiv:2107.02695] that hosts a low-mass central black hole [arXiv:1705.09667] and numerous young super star clusters [arXiv:1501.04567, arXiv:2107.02695]. Its central region features a precessing bipolar outflow [arXiv:2201.08396] and turbulent giant molecular clouds [arXiv:1812.02180].

sources.summary: The paper uses IFU spectroscopy of He 2-10 to show dispersion-supported stellar motions, differing gas motions, and possible transformation from a starburst dwarf into a dwarf elliptical.

quotes.quote: Marquart Department of Astronomy and Space Physics, Box 515, SE-75120 Uppsala, Sweden thomas.marquart@astro.uu.se Instituto de Astrof sica de Canarias, C/ V a L a ctea s/n, 38200 La Laguna, Tenerife, Spain Stockholm Observatory, AlbaNova University Center, SE-106 91 Stockholm, Sweden Observatoire Astronomique Marseille-Provence, Laboratoire d'Astrophysique de Marseille UMR 6110, 2 place Le Verrier, F-13248 Marseille Cedex 4, France Received; June 22, 2007 We study the two-dimensional distribution and kinematics of the stellar and gaseous components in the centre of the prototype Wolf-Rayet blue compact dwarf galaxy, He 2-10. The aim is to compare the kinematics of gas and stars in order to determine whether they are consistent with one another, or if stars and gas can be decoupled due to gravitational perturbations and feedback from star formation. We have used the integral field unit ARGUS, part of FLAMES on the European Southern Observatory's Very Large Telescope, to target the \ 8498,8542,8662 \ triplet in the central 300 480 parsecs of He 2-10. The selected wavelength regime includes several prominent spectral features, including the Paschen series and the \ emission-line, which we have used to derive the kinematics of the ionised interstellar medium.

We find no systematic trend in the velocities of the stars over the observed field of view and conclude that the stellar kinematics is governed by random motions. This is in contrast to the motions the ionised interstellar medium, where we find spatial velocity variations up to 60 . Our gas velocity field is consistent with previous studies of both the molecular gas and the feedback-driven outflow in He 2-10. We interpret the kinematic decoupling between the stars and the gas as He 2-10 being in the process of transformation to a dwarf elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies He 2-10 as a nearby star-forming galaxy with four detected He II emitters whose differing reddening values suggest clusters at different evolutionary stages.

quotes.quote: For this study, we selected star-forming regions in eight nearby galaxies ( 0.0010 z 0.0095 , at distances of 3-37 Mpc) with existing He II 1640 HST-STIS UV observations from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). These UV spectra were previously obtained for He 2-10, NGC 3125, Mrk 33, NGC 4214, NGC 4670, and Tol 1924-416 as part of program 9036 (PI: C. Leitherer) taken in 2001–2003, and for NGC 3049 and Tol 89 as part of program 7513 (PI: C. Leitherer) observed in 1999-2000. Additional optical HST-STIS observations, providing He II 4686 line measurements were obtained from the HST program GO-15846 (PI: C. Leitherer), executed in 2020-21. These optical observations were carried out at the exact same location, same orientation, and with the same aperture sizes as the UV observations.

He 2-10  This is the only galaxy in our sample with multiple detected He II emitters aligned in the STIS slit. The individual E(B-V)_ He II values range from 0.10 mag (source A) to 0.34 mag (source D), suggesting that these 4 clusters are at different evolutionary stages. This is supported by the H emission and dust, seen in the HST images (see Figure fig:slit_alignment ). Source D with the highest E(B-V)_ He II value is visibly affected by diffuse strong H emission situated to its east.

sources.summary: The paper presents Henize 2-10 as a dwarf AGN host with two VLA-resolved water masers, linking the broad central maser to a BH outflow shocking molecular gas and positive feedback.

quotes.quote: Henize 2-10 is a dwarf galaxy experiencing positive black hole (BH) feedback from a radio-detected low-luminosity active galactic nucleus. Previous Green Bank Telescope (GBT) observations detected a \ ``kilomaser'' in Henize 2-10, but the low angular resolution ( 33 ) left the location and origin of the maser ambiguous. We present new Karl G. \ Jansky Very Large Array observations of the \ maser line at 22.23508 GHz in Henize 2-10 with 2 \ resolution. These observations reveal two maser sources distinct in position and velocity. The first maser source is spatially coincident with the known BH outflow and the region of triggered star formation 70 pc to the east.

Combined with the broad width of the maser ( W_ 50 66 ), this confirms our hypothesis that part of the maser detected with the GBT is produced by the impact of the BH outflow shocking the dense molecular gas along the flow and at the interface of the eastern star-forming region. The second maser source lies to the south-east far from the central BH and has a narrow width ( W_ 50 8 ), suggesting a star-formation-related origin. This work has revealed the nature of the \ kilomaser in Henize 2-10 and illustrates the first known connection between outflow-driven \ masers and positive BH feedback.

sources.summary: The paper detects diffuse X-ray emission in He 2-10, describes similarly sized X-ray and optical emission, and reports four ultraluminous X-ray sources plus a proposed merger scenario.

quotes.quote: Of the eight dwarf starburst galaxies we detect diffuse X-ray emission in all but two objects (detected in NGC 1569, NGC 3077, NGC 4214, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, and He 2--10) which can be attributed to hot, coronal gas stored in galactic winds (Paper I). The X-ray emission of NGC 1569 extends well beyond its optical and \ counterparts perpendicular to the disc. The situation is different for the other five galaxies: The X-ray, \ (see Fig. fig:profileboth ), and \ emission of He 2-10 are all fairly circularly shaped and have about the same size. Hot gas is almost entirely bound to the central region of NGC 4214 (note that the X-ray emission to the north has low significance and might be an artefact from the adaptive smoothing process). The optical and X-ray emission of NGC 3077 and NGC 4449 is surrounded by an extended \ envelope which can be ascribed to tidal debris due to a current interaction see, e.g., yun94,hun98 .

About ten X-ray point sources in our sample can be classified as ULXs. These sources -- intermediate-mass black holes or high-mass XRBs with accretion above the Eddington limit for a discussion of ULXs, see, e.g., kin04 - are only found in I Zw 18 (one source), NGC 4449 (five, including two which are best fitted by black body spectra), and He 2-10 (four). Indeed, I Zw 18 and NGC 4449 are clearly interacting galaxies vze98,hun98,the01 and for He 2-10 a merger scenario has been proposed in the literature kob95b,con94 .

sources.summary: The paper reports WC and WN stellar signatures in He 2-10, links its offset nebular structures to bubbles or loops, and identifies starburst regions with Wolf-Rayet emission.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of WC stars in five Wolf-Rayet (W-R) galaxies: He 2-10, NGC 3049, NGC 3125, NGC 5253 and Tol 89. The faint broad \ line requires sufficiently high S/N ( 40) to be detected explaining the non-detection of this WC feature in previous observations. From the measurement of W-R emission lines ( + , , and ), we conclude that all W-R regions contain a mixed population of WNL, and early WC stars.

Three starburst regions can be distinguished on the optical images of He 2-10 . Following the nomenclature of Corbin \ CORBINetal93 , the most prominent is region A at the center of the galaxy. The second starburst region B is located at 8.5 \ East of A . It is smaller and fainter in optical images and is seen at an even lower level in . The third region called C , located 2.5 \ West of region A , is not prominent in optical images but is more noticeable in \ and \ images .

sources.summary: The paper presents Henize 2-10 as a dwarf starburst whose super star clusters are embedded in rich molecular gas, with CO observations resolving massive clouds and central gas mass.

quotes.quote: In this paper we have presented new single dish and interferometric observations of the molecular gas and millimeter continuum of the starburst region in the dwarf galaxy Henize 2-10. Our observations confirm earlier detections of the ^ 13 CO and HCN(1-0) line emission. The relatively strong detections of high density molecular tracers associated with the young Super Star Clusters confirm that this galaxy is undergoing vigorous star formation and is an ideal laboratory to study extragalactic starbursts.

In this context, our CO(2-1) and continuum SMA observations, with a linear resolution of 60-80 pc, represent a first attempt to resolve the parent molecular clouds out of which SSCs may form. We reveal a rich population of molecular clouds with estimated masses and densities in the upper range of those measured in our Galaxy. The molecular gas accounts for approximately half of the total mass in the inner region of the galaxy, while the young stellar clusters account for the remaining mass. We find possible evidence that the super star clusters are associated with very massive and dense molecular cores, but our observations and tracers do not allow us to confirm unambiguously their presence.

sources.summary: The paper studies He 2-10 as a nearby dwarf starburst with a candidate accreting massive black hole, using LBA radio observations to isolate the nuclear source.

quotes.quote: A candidate accreting massive black hole (BH) with M_ BH 10^6 M_ has recently been identified at the center of the dwarf starburst galaxy He 2-10 Henize 2-10 (He 2-10). This discovery offers the first possibility of studying a growing BH in a nearby galaxy resembling those in the earlier universe, and opens up a new class of host galaxies to search for the smallest supermassive BHs. Here we present very long baseline interferometry observations of He 2-10 taken with the Long Baseline Array (LBA) at 1.4 GHz with an angular resolution of 0 1 0 03 .

A single compact radio source is detected at the precise location of the putative low-luminosity active galactic nucleus. The physical size of the nuclear radio emission is 3 pc 1 pc, an order of magnitude smaller than previous constraints from the Very Large Array (VLA), and the brightness temperature of T_ B > 3 10^5 K confirms a non-thermal origin. These LBA observations indicate that the nuclear radio emission originates from a single object and exclude the possibility of multiple supernova remnants as the origin of the nuclear radio emission previously detected with the VLA at lower resolution. A weaker, more extended, off-nuclear source is also detected with the LBA and a comparison with multi-wavelength ancillary data indicate that, unlike the nuclear source, the off-nuclear source is co-spatial with a super star cluster, lacks a detectable X-ray point-source counterpart, and is almost certainly due to a supernova remnant in the host star cluster.

sources.summary: The paper models Henize 2-10 as a dwarf starburst galaxy whose central massive black hole and young clusters can merge dynamically to form a nuclear star cluster.

quotes.quote: The central region of the galaxy Henize 2-10 hosts a black hole (BH) candidate with a mass Log M_ BH / M _ =6.3 1.1 . While this putative black hole does not appear to coincide with any central stellar overdensity, it is surrounded by 11 young massive clusters with masses above 10^5 M _ . The availability of high quality data on the structure of the galaxy and the age and mass of the clusters provides excellent initial conditions for studying the dynamical evolution of Henize 2-10's nucleus. Here we present a set of N body simulations in which we model the future evolution of the central clusters and the black hole to understand whether and how they will merge to form a nuclear star cluster.

Nuclear star clusters (NSCs) are present in a majority of galaxies with stellar mass similar to Henize 2-10. While the results depend on the choice of initial conditions, we find that a NSC with mass M_ NSC 4-6 10^6 M _ and effective radius r_ NSC 2.6-4.1 pc will form within 0.2 Gyr. This work is the first showing, in a realistic realization of the host galaxy and its star cluster system, that the formation of a bright nucleus is a process that can happen after the formation of a central massive BH leading to a composite NSC+BH central system.

sources.summary: The paper studies the star-forming blue dwarf Henize 2--10 with near-infrared integral-field data and argues that emission evidence supports an accreting massive black hole.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage Henize 2--10 is a blue dwarf galaxy with intense star formation and one the most intriguing question about it is whether or not it hosts an accretingmassive black hole. We use H and K-band integral field spectra of the inner 130 pc 130 pc of He 2--10 to investigate the emission and kinematics of the gas at unprecedented spatial resolution.

The most intriguing question on He 2--10 is whether or not it hosts an accreting massive black hole. reines11 report that it contains a compact radio source, co-spatial with a hard X-ray source and conclude that the X-ray and radio emission are consistent with an actively accreting black hole with a mass of 10^6 M _ . Follow up radio reines12 and X-ray reines16 observations provide further evidence of an accreting BH in He 2--10, radiating well below its Eddington limit. nguyen14 derive an upper limit of 10 ^7 M _ for the BH mass, based on the non-detection of increased stellar velocity dispersion at the location of the BH candidate using Gemini NIFS data. Recent ALMA observations reveal a velocity gradient consistent with solid-body rotation and implying in a combined mass of the star clusters and the BH candidate of 4 10^6 M _ imara19 .

sources.summary: The paper uses He 2-10 to illustrate its MUSE phase-space wind extraction; it has high ionized-gas dispersion, accretion-event signatures, and a small but extended spiral-like wind component.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:moment2 shows that the typical velocity dispersion for the ionised gas is in the range 40 - 60 (Fig. fig:moment2 ), with the exceptions of He 2-10 and Haro 11, both reaching values slightly above 100 . Thus the ionised gas in these systems is slightly more turbulent than that of typical star-forming galaxies where 30 10 e.g. Epinat+10,Green+14 , not surprising given their higher-than-average SFR e.g. Bacchini+20 . Clearly, gas turbulence can also be increased by recent mergers/interactions events, which may trigger the starburst episode in the first place. In fact, Haro 11 is thought to be a merger system Ostlin+15 , and He 2-10 shows signatures of a recent accretion event Vanzi+09 .

Fig. fig:phase_space_method illustrates the application of these two feedback scenarios to the \ data of He 2-10, showing the phase-space plots (panels d1 and d2) and the integrated \ intensity maps (panels e1 and e2) associated with the wind component alone. As expected, the two scenarios predict a different flux for the wind, equal to 2 of the total for the turbulence case and 8 of the total for the TES case. With respect to the turbulence case, the TES scenario outputs a more defined wind structure, which features a spiral-like wind morphology. Even though the \ flux of the wind component is small, its half-light radius is about twice that determined for the total \ distribution.

sources.summary: The paper treats Henize 2-10 as a WR galaxy at about 9 Mpc, with a UV-clustered central nucleus and compact radio sources in its central region.

quotes.quote: Henize 2-10 General description The radial velocity of \ ( 860-870 km s ^ 1 ) yields a distance anywhere from 6 to 14 Mpc depending on the chosen value of H_0 and assumptions on the Virgocentric flow. As done by other authors e.g. vacca92 , we adopt a reasonable compromise of 9 Mpc, which accounts for Galactic rotation but no Virgocentric flow, and assumes H_0=75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . At this distance, 1 \ corresponds to 43.6 pc. \ was the first emission-line galaxy to exhibit evidence for WR stars allen76 and it is considered the prototype for WR galaxies conti91 . High-resolution observations in the optical and near-IR see cabanac05 show a bright central nucleus, generally refered to as region A, resolved into several UV-bright super star clusters which lie in an arc of about 2 \ conti94 .

VLA radio continuum imaging kobulnicky99,johnson03 reveals 5 compact 1 mJy radio sources in the central region aligned in a east-west orientation. These radio sources are labeled 1 to 5 from west to east.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE to show He 2-10 has starburst-driven ionized outflows and metallicity variations, while finding no AGN ionization and favoring non-AGN X-ray sources.

quotes.quote: We study the physical and dynamical properties of the ionized gas in the prototypical HII galaxy Henize 2-10 using MUSE integral field spectroscopy. The large scale dynamics is dominated by extended outflowing bubbles, probably the results of massive gas ejection from the central star forming regions. We derive a mass outflow rate M_ out 0.30 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 , corresponding to mass loading factor 0.4 , in range with similar measurements in local LIRGs. Such a massive outflow has a total kinetic energy that is sustainable by the stellar winds and Supernova Remnants expected in the galaxy.

We find no sign of AGN ionization in the galaxy, despite the recent claim of the presence of a super massive active Black Hole in the core of He 2-10. We therefore reanalyze the X-ray data that were used to propose the presence of the AGN, but we conclude that the observed X-ray emission can be better explained with sources of a different nature, such as a Supernova Remnant.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA molecular-line observations of Henize 2-10, deriving gas mass, mapping molecular tracers, and linking line ratios to natal super star cluster evolution.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of the dwarf starburst galaxy He 2-10 in combination with previous SMA CO observations to probe the molecular environments of natal super star clusters. These observations include the HCO ^+ (1-0), HCN(1-0), HNC(1-0), and CCH(1-0) molecular lines, as well as 88 GHz continuum with a spatial resolution of 1''.7 1''.6 . After correcting for the contribution from free-free emission to the 88 GHz continuum flux density ( 60 of the 88 GHz emission), we derive a total gas mass for He 2-10 of M_ gas = 4-6 10^8  M _ , roughly 5-20 of the dynamical mass.

Henize 2-10 (He 2-10) is a remarkable blue compact dwarf galaxy that has attracted astronomers' interest for decades, largely due to its high star formation rate 0.7 M _  yr ^ 1 , lee09 and powerful outflows > 360  km s ^ 1 johnson00 . He 2-10 is the nearest ( D 9  Mpc) galaxy known to host multiple nascent super star clusters detected in radio and infrared observations see Figure  plot_radio_cont , kj99,jk03,vacca02, cabanac05 . More recently, a low-luminosity AGN was identified in this system, making He 2-10 the first known example of a starbursting dwarf hosting a massive black hole reines11, reines16 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Henize 2-10 is star-forming dominated, with strong central ionization and FUV radiation, a flat slightly subsolar metallicity profile, and dense central regions.

quotes.quote: Our main conclusions for the galaxy Henize 2-10 can be summarized as follows: It is a star-forming dominated galaxy with SFR 1.2 M _ yr ^ 1 , and a total gas mass of M_ gas 1.9 10^7 M _ . The ionization parameter in the central regions ( d 4 ) is U 10^ 3 , while at larger distances U 10^ 2 . The interstellar radiation field in the FUV band G is also strong, with a flat radially median profile and intensities around G/G_0 10 ^ 1.5 , while G reaches its peak in Regions A and B ( G/G_0 10^3 ).

The radially median profile for the metallicity is flat with slightly sub-solar values (i.e., log( Z/Z_ ) 0.2 ). However, the Z distribution inferred in this work covers a quite large range (i.e., -1.0 log( Z/Z_ ) 0.3) denoting the heterogeneous morphology of He 2-10 in terms of physical properties across its extension. The gas density in Regions A and B reaches values up to n 10^ 3.3 cm ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of Henize 2-10, measuring its molecular gas mass and resolved GMC population, with clouds resembling but more turbulent than Milky Way clouds.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the nominal center of the galaxy 08^ h 36^ m 15 13 , 26 24 33 77 ; Evans_2010

quotes.quote: We present new 12 (J=1-0) observations of Henize 2-10, a blue compact dwarf galaxy about 8.7 Mpc away, taken with the Atacama Large Millimeter Array. These are the highest spatial and spectral resolution observations, to date, of the molecular gas in this starburst galaxy. We measure a molecular mass of (1.2 0.4) 10^8 in Henize 2-10, and 75 of the molecular gas mass is contained within the northern region of the galaxy near the previously identified young super star clusters, which has a projected size of about 300 pc. We use the CPROPS algorithm to identify 119 resolved giant molecular clouds distributed throughout the galaxy, and the molecular gas contained within these clouds make up between 45 to 70 of the total molecular mass.

The molecular clouds in Henize 2-10 have similar median sizes ( 26 pc), luminous masses ( 4 10^5 ), and surface densities ( 180 ) to Milky Way clouds. However, Henize 2-10 clouds have velocity dispersions ( 3 ) about 50 higher than those in the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution multiwavelength observations of Henize 2-10, a nearby starbursting blue compact dwarf, resolving natal super star clusters and assessing their emission, extinction, masses, and survival prospects.

quotes.quote: Henize 2-10 (He 2-10) is a nearby (D = 9 Mpc) starbursting blue compact dwarf galaxy that boasts a high star formation rate and a low luminosity AGN Reines:2011, Reines:2016 . He 2-10 is also one of the first galaxies in which embedded superstar clusters (SSCs) were discovered. SSCs are massive, compact star clusters that will impact their host galaxies dramatically when their massive stars evolve. Here, we discuss radio, submillimeter, and infrared observations of He 2-10 from 1.87 m to 6 cm in high angular resolution ( 0.3 ), which allows us to disentangle individual clusters from aggregate complexes as identified at lower resolution.

We present observations of He 2-10 from 1.87 m to 6 cm to constrain the properties of 7 radio knots in the most complete multiwavelength survey on parsec scales to date of He 2-10. Our conclusions are as follows. We present maps at 5, 8, 15, 22, 33, 113, 250, and 340 GHz and 1.87 m of He 2-10. We discuss photometry results for the radio knots, which were initially identified in Kobulnicky:1999 and Johnson:2003 .

sources.summary: The paper presents HST optical observations of Henize 2-10, interpreting its ionized filament and sinusoidal gas kinematics as a black-hole outflow that triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: Henize 2-10 is a dwarf starburst galaxy previously reported to have a central massive black hole though that interpretation has been disputed since some aspects of the observational evidence are also consistent with a supernova remnant . At a distance of 9 Mpc, it presents an opportunity to resolve the central region and determine if there is evidence for a black hole outflow impacting star formation. Here we report optical observations of Henize 2-10 with a linear resolution of a few parsecs. We find a 150 pc long ionized filament connecting the region of the black hole with a site of recent star formation. Spectroscopy reveals a sinusoid-like position-velocity structure that is well described by a simple precessing bipolar outflow.
We conclude that this black hole outflow triggered the star formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses Henize 2-10 as a nearby starburst comparison, noting many SSCs, recomputed GMC properties similar to prior work, and physical properties most similar to the Antennae overlap.

quotes.quote: An important comparison galaxy black in the nearby universe is Henize 2-10, as another starburst galaxy that has had black numerous SSCs detected in both the optical and radio wavelengths (Johnson Conti 2000; Johnson Kobulnicky 2003). Given this similarity, we chose to re-define and compute cloud properties using identical methodology to the Antennae overlap. We were unable to reproduce values similar to those published in Imara Faesi (2019), and in particular the values in that paper have much smaller linewidths, masses, and effective radii, suggesting that they have decomposed into smaller sub-clouds than in our and other work (this is supported by their Figure 10 showing most of their clouds only subtending a small fraction of the velocity range along the line of sight). Re-identifying and calculating properties for Henize 2-10 GMCs in this manner black yields similar cloud properties to those found by 2018ApJ...867..165B using only the CPROPS algorithm on CO(3-2) observations of the same environment.
Some of the starbursts, like Henize 2-10, are very similar to the Antennae overlap, while others are much more similar to normal galaxies.
It is also important to note that Henize 2-10, which is the starburst with the most similar physical properties to the Antennae overlap of the galaxies in this analysis.

sources.summary: The paper presents Henize 2-10 as a blue compact galaxy whose central 300 pc hosts five optically obscured thermal radio sources, modeled as dense compact H II regions powered by hundreds of O stars.

quotes.quote: A Case Study of the Blue Compact Galaxy Henize 2-10 The blue compact galaxy Henize 2-10 contains five radio sources with no optical counterparts and constant flux density over 10 year time baselines (Kobulnicky Johnson 1999. Figure 1 (upper left) shows a Hubble Space Telescope F555W broadband image of the central 300 pc region along with a VLA 2 cm map (lower left) and an HST H image. The radio sources have no optical counterparts in the F555W image, but there is a good correlation between the radio map and the H morphologies, suggesting that the source of the radio emission is related to the ionized gas. The radio sources have inverted spectral indices between 2 cm and 6 cm ( S_ ^ +0.5 0.3 ), consistent with an optically-thick thermal bremsstrahlung origin (upper right).

We model the spectral shape and luminosity of the observed sources as spheres of uniform-density plasma with an electron temperature of 6000 K. H II regions with radii between 3 pc and 8 pc, densities of 5000 cm^ 3 , and 500-1000 ionizing O7V stars are most consistent with the data.

sources.summary: The paper presents He 2-10 as a blue compact dwarf merger with a tidal tail, resolved MIR subcomponents, dominant region A emission, and enough UV clusters to power infrared output.

quotes.quote: Both \ and CO maps show a tidal tail in the SE direction, suggesting that He 2-10 is the product of a merger between two dwarf galaxies. However, the smooth ^ 1/4 profile of the underlying low-surface-brightness extended component (CKV) implies that it is a moderately advanced merger as the underlying stellar component has had time to relax.

The galaxy is detected and resolved into sub-components in the N-band and 11.65 \ filters. The main component, region A, at the center of the galaxy, is elongated in the SE-NW direction and has the same position angle in both MIR images. The same shape and orientation are also seen in the optical. The arc of UV clusters seen by Conti Vacca (1994), and the \ emitting region detected by Sugai Taniguchi (1992) are contained entirely within the MIR emission of region A. The latter emits 2/3 of the MIR flux of the galaxy.

sources.summary: He2--10 is identified as the first Wolf-Rayet galaxy, too luminous to be a dwarf, with a regular extended envelope, central reddening, dust lanes, and radial gradients.

quotes.quote: HE2--10 (ESO495-G021) He2--10 is the first Wolf--Rayet galaxy ever discovered 1976MNRAS.177...91A . Contrary to popular belief this is not a dwarf as it is too luminous ( M_B=-19.1, M_K=-22.3 mag) and has an extended regular envelope spanning 13.8 kpc. The observed V-K color profile is centrally very red, reflecting both a higher Z , measured to be close to solar by 1999ApJ...514..544K , and a larger internal extinction. There is dust clearly visible in the RGB image (dark lanes in the North and South directions). The negative V-K gradient is thus due to a strong metallicity and extinction decrease with radius.

sources.summary: The paper cites Henize 2-10 as a dwarf starburst with no clear bulge but multi-wavelength evidence for an accreting massive black hole and a central ionized-gas structure.

quotes.quote: reinesetal2011 present multi-wavelength evidence for the first example of a massive BH in a dwarf starburst galaxy, Henize 2-10, which has an irregular central morphology and no discernible bulge. The evidence for an accreting massive BH includes Very Large Array radio and Chandra hard X-ray point sources at the center of the galaxy and clearly separated from the bright \ regions. Follow-up Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations reveal parsec-scale non-thermal radio continuum from the precise location of the putative active nucleus reinesdeller2012 and no star clusters are seen at this location in HST observations. The source lies at the center of a 250-pc-long ionized gas structure that is suggestive of bipolar flow and the position of the central source is consistent with the dynamical center of the galaxy. reinesetal2011 estimate a BH mass of log ( M_ BH / ) = 6.3 1.1 using the radio--X-ray-- M_ BH fundamental plane merlonietal2003 , and kormendyho2013 estimate that Henize 2-10 has a stellar mass of M_ 1.4 10^9   \ with an uncertainty of a factor of 3.

sources.summary: The paper discusses He 2-10 as a prototypical HII galaxy with loops, filaments, expanding bubbles, outflows, and many super star clusters.

quotes.quote: He 2-10 is one of the prototypical HII galaxies b:Allen_mnras76 . It is 9 Mpc away b:Vacca_apj92 and has dynamical mass 3 10^9 b:Kobulnicky_aj95 . In emission-line gas there are many notable loops and filaments (Figure f:img_he210 ) whose kinematics suggest expanding bubbles and possible outflows b:Mendez_aap99 . These bubbles are centered on sites of recent star formation. He 2-10 is the host of nearly 80 SSCs b:Johnson_aj00 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Henize 2-10 as a dwarf starburst with a central massive black hole and young super-star clusters, arguing that their infall can form a nuclear cluster.

quotes.quote: The starburst galaxy Henize 2-10 is a dwarf starburst galaxy located in the Pisces constellation. Its nucleus host a central MBH with mass 2.6 10^6 reines12 and 11 super-star clusters (SSCs) ngu14 , with ages below 10 Myr. The contemporary presence of SSCs and a MBH makes Henize 2-10 an ideal laboratory for testing the dry-merger scenario on a strong observational basis. Moreover, the similarity between the Henize 2-10 and the MW MBHs give us the opportunity to put further constraints on the formation of our own NC. Varying the SSCs orbital parameters, we have shown that their infall leads to the formation of a NC in any case ASCD15he .

sources.summary: The paper uses Henize 2-10 as a BCD test case, fitting B and R images with iFIT and finding stable Sersic parameters across filters and depth.

quotes.quote: As an illustrative example, we show in Fig. He210 the \ model obtained with \ for the BCD Henize 2-10 by fitting B and R images taken with the Danish 1.54m telescope at La Silla see for details PF98 . It can be seen that, \ yields for both filters similar S ersic model parameters, despite a difference by 4 mag in the adopted . The stability of the best-fitting \ solution with \ is to be contrasted with studies of BCDs with , which, as pointed out by Amorin07 , show a strong dependence on the surface brightness interval considered.

Furthermore, the capability of \ to robustly infer the best-fitting equivalent S ersic model for irregular galaxies that strongly deviate from the \ (as most high- z galaxies) is demonstrated on the analysis of the local BCDs He 2-10 and I Zw 18 .

sources.summary: Henize 2-10 is presented as a nearby starburst galaxy hosting multiple UD H2 regions and optically visible super star clusters, with luminous dust cocoons providing much of its mid-infrared flux.

quotes.quote: The starburst galaxy Henize 2-10 (He 2-10) is an excellent system in which to study the properties of ultra-young clusters in detail. At a distance of only 9 Mpc H_0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ; vacca92 , it is one of the nearest galaxies known to host multiple UD H2 s kj99 as well as a large number of SSCs that have already emerged into optical and ultraviolet light johnson00, conti94 .

The dust cocoons surrounding the UD H2 s in He 2-10 are so luminous that vacca02 find they are responsible for at least 60 of the entire mid-IR flux from this galaxy. This percentage of mid-IR flux due to clusters with ages 1 Myr old is especially remarkable considering that He 2-10 has been undergoing an intense starburst for several Myr, and hosts nearly 80 optically visible SSCs johnson00 .

sources.summary: The paper describes He 2-10's roughly circular total X-ray emission and five point sources, mostly in central and eastern star-forming complexes, with P1 the strongest source.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations The total X-ray emission of He 2-10 is approximately circularly shaped and has a diameter of 1 7 (4.4 kpc). Within the diffuse X-ray emission and the optical and \ counterpart we detect five point sources: three of them are at the same position as the central star forming complex (P1, P2, P3) and one is located close to the eastern complex (P4; see Fig fig:pointhe2-10 a ). The fifth source is placed further away to the east and is slightly shifted to the north (P5). P1 is the strongest of the sources, followed by P2 (see Table tab:pointhe2-10a for positions, count rates, and hardness ratios of the point source population).

sources.summary: The paper uses He 2--10 as a nearby Wolf-Rayet starburst comparison, finding HCO+ emission near the CO peak and a very low HCN/HCO+ ratio.

quotes.quote: To determine if such an extreme starburst shows a different HCN(1--0)/HCO ^ + (1--0) brightness-temperature ratio from a normal starburst, a nearby well studied Wolf-Rayet galaxy, He 2--10 (z = 0.003), dominated by massive, hot Wolf-Rayet stars all76 was also studied (Table 1).

The Wolf-Rayet galaxy He 2--10 shows a very low HCN(1--0)/HCO ^ + (1--0) brightness-temperature ratio. Some individual examples of the nearby Galactic high-mass-star-forming, HII-region cores show high HCN(1--0)/HCO ^ + (1--0) brightness-temperature ratios tur77,pir99 .

sources.summary: Henize 2-10 is presented as the first blue compact dwarf AGN discovery, a starburst galaxy whose X-ray and radio core reveal a low-mass central black hole.

quotes.quote: This yielded the discovery of the first AGN in a blue compact dwarf galaxy (Henize 2-10; 2011Natur.470...66R ). Although Henize 2-10 is a starburst galaxy whose optical emission is dominated by star formation, it hosts a low-mass BH ( M_ BH 10^ 6 M _ ) at its center as revealed by Chandra X-ray observations and VLA and VLBI radio observations ( 2011Natur.470...66R 2011, 2016ApJ...830L..35R ; 2012ApJ...750L..24R ). It was the discovery of Henize 2-10 that invigorated the quest for low-mass AGN in dwarf galaxies: found 136 optically selected dwarf galaxies at z < 0.055 that qualified as either AGN or composite objects in the narrow-line emission diagnostic diagram (see Fig. BPT ) and provided an AGN fraction (not corrected for incompleteness) of 0.5 .

gmw_00002189.name: RX J0947.0+4721
gmw_00002189.overview: RX J0947.0+4721 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 type object with a quasar-level luminosity at a redshift of z = 0.541 [arXiv:astro-ph/9712306]. Its optical spectrum exhibits strong Fe II emission, and the galaxy displays large X-ray count rate variations [arXiv:astro-ph/9712306].

sources.summary: The paper presents RX J0947.0+4721 as an extremely soft narrow-line Seyfert 1 QSO with quasar-level luminosity, strong FeII emission, ROSAT variability, and a z = 0.541 optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: Molthagen, kmolt@mpe.mpg.de Hamburger Sternwarte, Gojenbergsweg 112, D -- 21029 Hamburg, Germany Received ; accepted \ is a newly discovered example of a narrow--line Seyfert 1 type object with a quasar level luminosity ( M_B = 24.8 ). It was detected in the ROSAT All--Sky Survey (RASS) and later in 17 pointed observations of the ROSAT Position sensitive Proportional Counter (PSPC). The optical spectrum contains strong FeII emission.
Large count rate variations are visible with a maximum amplitude of a factor > 17.5 . The fastest change is a drop of a factor 2.7 in 25 hours. No spectral variations were observed.

The identification spectrum of \ was taken in February 1992 (Bade et al. ). It shows Mg II and narrow Balmer emission lines with a redshift of z = 0.541 and strong bumps due to the Fe II multiplets around 4570 and 5250 .

gmw_00002190.name: ESO 580-49
gmw_00002190.overview: ESO 580-49 is an edge-on spiral disk galaxy that hosted the gamma-ray burst GRB 111005A [arXiv:1610.06928, arXiv:1708.06270]. The galaxy features asymmetric ultraviolet emission and molecular gas distribution [arXiv:1610.06928, arXiv:2202.01188], though its atomic gas, radio continuum, and rotational patterns are generally smooth [arXiv:2202.01188].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 580-49 as the GRB 111005A host and describes it as an edge-on, star-forming disk galaxy with asymmetric UV emission and a symmetric near-IR disk.

quotes.quote: The question of whether is hosted by ESO 580-49, or is a background object is less definite, and must be addressed on statistical grounds. We estimated the chance coincidence of such a bright galaxy using the SDSS band counts their table 2 yasuda01 , showing 0.42 deg ^ 2 galaxies brighter than ESO 580-49 ( 14 mag). It is somewhat arbitrary to estimate the angular distance of to ESO 580-49, as it is located inside the galaxy disk. The sub-arcsec VLBA position is 1 from the galaxy center as seen on the 3.6 image (Fig. fig:im ), which gives a negligibly small probability of chance association of 10^ 7 , so that such a bright galaxy should not be found by chance even in a sample of 1000 Swift GRBs. This implies that ESO 580-49 is the host galaxy of .

Global properties of the host galaxy ESO 580-49, the host of , is clearly a star-forming disk galaxy viewed edge-on with multiple star-forming regions visible in the UV (Fig. fig:im ). The galaxy is asymmetric, with the northwest part being more star-forming (or less dust-obscured), as evidenced by more prominent UV emission. On the other hand, near-IR images (tracing the stellar mass distribution) are much more symmetric, and are showing a disk structure of the galaxy.

sources.summary: ESO 580-49 is presented as the edge-on spiral host of GRB 111005A, with the GRB near its center in a near-solar-metallicity, low-star-formation environment.

quotes.quote: The 90 confidence Swift /BAT error-circle of 2 1 radius 2011GCN..12415...1B contained ESO 580-49, a bright spiral galaxy seen nearly edge on, which if the host galaxy, would make GRB 111005A the second closest GRB detected with Swift , and the third closest GRB ever detected (after GRB 980425 and GRB 170817A). Early optical and near-infrared observations and Section sec:snlim 2016arXiv161006928M did not find a variable source in the BAT error-circle, but a relatively bright radio transient was identified a couple of days later, close to the centre of ESO 580-49 2016arXiv161006928M .

In this astrometric reference frame, the centre of the galaxy as defined by first order moments of the near-infrared light distribution (from the F160W WFC image) is located at coordinates RA(J2000) = 14:53:07.86 and Dec(J2000) = -19:44:13.1, where we estimate the uncertainty of the galaxy centroid to be approximately 0 15 in each coordinate. This position is 1 3 distant from the radio position of the GRB RA(J2000) = 14:53:07.8078, Dec(J2000) = -19:44:11.995 as given in 2016arXiv161006928M .

From these data we thus find that the GRB is not located within a region of ongoing star-formation, and its immediate environment has a near solar metallicity within the nearest region to the radio afterglow (Figs.  fig:ew and   fig:oh ). Although the absolute metallicity at the location of the GRB is somewhat uncertain due to the edge-on orientation of the galaxy, the lack of ongoing star formation is a robust result. The likelihood, that there is a region of star formation at the GRB position that is unseen due to dust extinction is small given the sensitivity of our data and the dust distribution that we measure (see Fig.  fig:av and section  sec:dustmap ).

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 580-49, the GRB 111005A host, finding smooth atomic gas and rotation but asymmetric molecular gas, subtle irregularities, and little concentration at the GRB site.

quotes.quote: obtained a detection of the CO(2-1) line in three pointings and noticed that ESO 580-49, the host galaxy of , is not symmetrically filled with molecular gas. The central and NW regions are molecule-rich for their star formation rates (SFR), but the SE region turned out to be molecule-deficient. The total molecular gas mass turned out to be similar as that of other galaxies with similar redshift, star formation rate (SFR), and stellar mass Michalowski2018co,Hatsukade2020 .

We found that the atomic gas, radio continuum, and rotational patterns are in general very smooth throughout the galaxy, which does not indicate a recent gas inflow or outflow. There is also no gas concentration around the GRB position. The ISM in this galaxy differs from that in hosts of other GRBs and SNe, which may suggest that the progenitor of was not an explosion of a very massive star (e.g. a compact object merger). However, there are subtle irregularities of the host (most at a 2 level) , which may point to a weak gas inflow or interaction.

gmw_00002191.name: DES J214425.25 405400.81
gmw_00002191.overview: DES J214425.25 405400.81 is a green-valley [arXiv:2211.02279] LINER-like galaxy [arXiv:2211.02279, arXiv:2005.13160] at a redshift of z=0.3214 [arXiv:2211.02279, arXiv:2005.13160] that hosts the fast radio burst FRB 20180924B [arXiv:2211.02279]. The galaxy exhibits a spiral structure [arXiv:2304.10377, arXiv:2312.01578] with ionized gas distributed throughout its disk [arXiv:2304.10377, arXiv:2005.13160] and unresolved CO molecular gas concentrated at its center [arXiv:2211.02279].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the FRB 20180924B host as DES J214425.25 405400.81 at z=0.3214, a green-valley LINER-like galaxy with measured stellar mass/SFR and detected unresolved CO(3-2) molecular gas.

quotes.quote: FRB 20180924B was detected on 2018 September 24 at 16:23:12.6265 UTC with the Australian Square Kilometre Array Pathfinder ASKAP; john08 and was localised to a host galaxy at z = 0.3214 , DES J214425.25 405400.81 bann19 . The host has the stellar mass of M_* = (13.2 5.1) 10^9 M_ , the SFR of 0.88 0.26 M_ yr ^ 1 , and a gas-phase metallicity of 12 + ( O/H) = 8.93^ +0.02 _ 0.02 hein20a . It is located between star-forming and quiescent galaxies (``green valley'') in a color-magnitude diagram and an SFR-- M_* plot hein20a . A Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich (BPT) diagram bald81 shows that the host has a characteristic of low-ionization narrow-emission line region (LINER) bann19 .

The CO(3--2) emission is significantly detected in the FRB 20180924B host with a peak S/N of 12 in the velocity-integrated intensity map. The emission is spatially unresolved with the synthesized beam. The peak position of the CO emission coincides with the host galaxy center.
The molecular gas mass of the FRB 20180924B host is estimated to be (2.4 0.2) 10^9 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper combines ALMA, VLT/MUSE, and HST data for HG20180924B, mapping gas kinematics and showing the FRB lies on a spiral arm without a disturbed H signature.

quotes.quote: For example, in the case of HG20180924B, we use optical integral field unit (IFU) observations from VLT/MUSE Bannister2019 and described in   , and infrared observations from HST Mannings2020 to pair with the ALMA observation and gain additional information about the galaxy, as shown in Figure  fig:20180924compare . The ALMA image (left panel) shows the moment-1 map of emission generated using spectral-cube with contours representing 3, 6, and 9 times the RMS of the velocity-integrated intensity. With high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) optical VLT/MUSE MUSE IFU observations we can map the emission flux and kinematics of the ionized gas in the galaxy. In the middle panel of Figure  fig:20180924compare we show the velocity map of H , the strongest line in the observation, which we calculated by fitting a Gaussian to the emission line in every spaxel and setting the zero velocity to be at the galaxy's redshift z=0.3212 .

From the HST image in Figure  fig:20180924compare (right panel), it is clear that the FRB localization falls on one of the spiral arms of the galaxy, as discussed previously in   . Generally, the velocity maps of the molecular and ionized gas traced by CO and H (left and middle panels of Figure  fig:20180924compare , respectively) appear to show similar kinematic structures. The velocity and velocity dispersion of H emission is consistent with disk rotation, and detailed inspection does not reveal any additional disturbed kinematic signature at the location of the FRB. Using a higher velocity resolution of 9 , report a positional offset between the two velocity components of CO emission and suggest this is indicative of a disturbed kinematic structure of molecular gas in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses DES J214425.25 405400.81 as the favored FRB 180924 host candidate, noting its close centroid offset and negligible chance-association probability compared with nearby fainter galaxies.

quotes.quote: Formalism Figure fig:chance shows a VLT/FORS2 g band cutout image ( 30'' 30'' ) of the field surrounding FRB 180924 with its localization marked by a red circle. As described by , this localization lies 1'' from the centroid of galaxy DES J214425.25 405400.81 and within 5'' of two additional galaxies. At their redshifts Bannister19 , the galaxies all have projected separations of less than 40 kpc, i.e. \ separations less than the estimated radii of their halos. Therefore, while one may be predisposed to assign FRB 180924 to the brighter and closer galaxy, one should also entertain the possibility that the FRB occurred in the stellar halo of one of the others.

Adopting these, we estimate = 0.01 for DES J214425.25 405400.81 and > 0.1 for the other sources owing to their fainter magnitudes and larger angular separations. From this perspective, the probability of a chance association is nearly negligible for DES J214425.25 405400.81 and sufficiently large for the other sources that one is inclined to favor the former.

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20180924B is modelled with disk and bulge Sérsic components, has the smallest effective radius in the sample, and shows hints of spiral structure recovered in AO imaging.

quotes.quote: The hosts of FRB 20180924B, FRB 20211127I, and FRB 20211212A were fit using two Sérsic components, equating to a disc and a bulge.
The physical effective radii range from 2.07 to 6.67 kpc, those values belonging to the host galaxies for FRB20180924B and FRB20191001A, respectively.

After removing the smooth light of the galaxies from the galight S e rsic models, the spiral structure is quite apparent for four out of five hosts, with hints of spiral structure for the host of FRB 20180924B. This host provides a test case for comparisons between space- and ground-based imaging. Though the HST image (and associated residual) presented in showed more extended, spiral structure, GeMS+GSAOI does somewhat recover the structure in this host as evidenced by Figure fig:Residual Image Gallery .

sources.summary: The paper describes the FRB 180924 host as a massive, off-center environment more consistent with older transient hosts than with LGRB or SLSN hosts.

quotes.quote: For example, FRB 180924, FRB 190523 and FRB 180916.J0158+65 are located in massive galaxies. Furthermore, FRB 180924 resides far away from the center of its host. These properties are similar to those of the host galaxies of old population transients, such as SGRBs bannister19,ravi19 .
FRB 180924 also has a very low probability to belong to the LGRB/SLSN sample but has a fairly high probability belonging to SN Ia or SGRB samples.

sources.summary: DES J214425.25 405400.81 is identified as FRB 180924’s luminous quiescent host at z=0.3214, with LINER-like emission and ionized gas but no enhanced star formation at the burst site.

quotes.quote: It was localized to a luminous, quiescent galaxy, DES J214425.25 405400.81 at a redshift of z=0.3214 based on well-detected , , H , H emission and stellar Ca H + K absorption spectral lines using the KCWI and Gemini-S/GMOS spectrograph. The \ and high \ /H flux ratio indicate low-ionization narrow emission-line region (LINER) emission yan12 . Analysis of the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) MUSE data from the VLT obtained on 2018 Nov 5 UT shows that the ionised gas is distributed throughout the host galaxy, including at the position of FRB 180924. The H emission-line flux at the burst position is similar to the average H emission at the same radius in the host, so the spectroscopic data show no evidence for any enhanced star formation at the position of the FRB although we are spatially limited by the seeing of the observations, with effective point spread function (PSF) of 0.8^ '' .

gmw_00002192.name: Leo I
gmw_00002192.overview: Leo I is a classical dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:2201.07304]. It is characterized by a lack of gas, an extended star formation history, and 14 candidate stellar streams that likely originated from disrupted star clusters [arXiv:2201.07304].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Leo I as a classical Milky Way dSph with extended recent star formation, combines 953 member-star measurements, and finds 14 candidate streams consistent with dissolving clusters.

quotes.quote: We made use of a new spectroscopic data set for the Milky Way dSph Leo I, combining 288 stars observed with Magellan/IMACS and existing Keck/DEIMOS data, to provide velocity and metallicity measurements for 953 Leo I member stars. We used a specially developed algorithm called Beacon to detect chemo-kinematical patterns in the observed and simulated data. After analysing the Leo I data, we report the detection of 14 candidate streams of stars that may have originated in disrupted star clusters. The angular momentum vectors of these streams are randomly oriented, consistent with the lack of rotation in Leo I. These results are consistent with the predictions of the dissolving cluster model.

It is one of the most distant MW satellites. At 257 13 kpc Sohn2013 and with a half-light radius of 244 2 pc McConnachie2012 , it is classified as a dSph galaxy McConnachie2012 , based on its current morphology and lack of gas Knapp1978,Grcevich2009 . Leo I displays a very extended star formation history Gallart1999a,Hernandez2000 , and is considered one of the youngest dSph galaxies in our Local Group in terms of average age Lee1993,Weisz2014,Weisz2014b .

gmw_00002193.name: CRLE
gmw_00002193.overview: CRLE is a dusty starburst galaxy at a redshift of z=5.667 [arXiv:1803.08048, arXiv:2112.11705]. It is an intermediate-stage merger with a large molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:1803.08048], though it is optically blocked from view by a foreground disk galaxy [arXiv:2112.13779].

sources.summary: CRLE is presented as a z=5.667 dusty starburst, the brightest and highest-redshift COSMOS DSFG, with line detections, large gas reservoir, short depletion time, and merger evidence.

quotes.quote: We have reported the serendipitous discovery of the bright, dusty, starbursting galaxy CRLE at z=5.667 in the first billion years of cosmic time. This galaxy represents the highest redshift and brightest DSFG in the COSMOS field known to date, providing a higher redshift and brighter analog to the z=5.3 massive starburst AzTEC-3. We report the detection of [ C ii ] , [ N ii ] , and CO(2--1) line emission, and we find properties that are common among the highest redshift DSFGs. CRLE displays a large molecular gas reservoir ( 7 10^ 10 M_ ), a short gas depletion time scale of order 50 Myr characterizing the intense starburst, and a high-intensity radiation field, as evidenced by a deep [ C ii ] deficit.

We find dynamical evidence and dust emission properties consistent with an intermediate-stage merger. The physical proximity of the previously known ``normal" Lyman Alpha Emitter (LAE) HZ10 to CRLE constitutes a high overdensity, and suggests that these two galaxies may coalesce in the future, forming a massive central cluster galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports NOEMA CO(5-4) and CO(6-5) detections of CRLE, finding high CO luminosities, z>5 DSFG-like excitation, and a large modeled molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: We report CO(5 4) and CO(6 5) line observations in the dusty starbursting galaxy CRLE ( z = 5.667 ) and the main-sequence (MS) galaxy HZ10 ( z = 5.654 ) with the Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA). CRLE is the most luminous z>5 starburst in the COSMOS field and HZ10 is the most gas-rich ``normal'' galaxy currently known at z>5 . We find line luminosities for CO(5 4) and CO(6 5) of (4.9 0.5) and (3.8 0.4) 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 for CRLE and upper limits of < 0.76 and < 0.60 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 for HZ10, respectively. The CO excitation of CRLE appears comparable to other z>5 dusty star-forming galaxies (DSFGs).

For CRLE we determine the most likely values for the H _2 density, kinetic temperature and dust temperature based on excitation modeling of the CO line ladder. We also derive a total gas mass of (7.1 1.3) 10^ 10 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 500R COSMOS T1 x26 as the z=5.667 galaxy CRLE, spectroscopically found in ALMA/VLA lines but optically blocked by a foreground disk galaxy.

quotes.quote: 500R COSMOS T1 x26 This object in fact is the z=5.667 galaxy reported by Pavesi2018 . Nicknamed ``CRLE'', it was serendipitously discovered during ALMA observations of a normal galaxy in its vicinity, and its redshift was determined through the identification of atomic fine-structure lines ( [ C 2 ] 158 m and [ N 2 ] 205 m) in the ALMA data as well as the CO(2-1) 1.301 mm transition line in the follow-up VLA observations. Had there not been such spectroscopic identifications, it would be impossible to reveal the nature of this source because it is completely blocked by a foreground, edge-on disk galaxy at z_ ph 0.35 from UV to mid-IR; its FIR-to-mm emissions, on the other hand, are transmissible through this foreground galaxy Pavesi2018 . Its redshift, however, is still below z=6 . We refer the reader to Pavesi2018 for the optical-to-NIR images around this position, and will not discuss this source further because the optical-to-NIR images would provide information only on the foreground object.

gmw_00002194.name: HZ10
gmw_00002194.overview: HZ10 is a main-sequence, star-forming Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.65 [arXiv:2005.07716, arXiv:1803.08048]. It is rich in molecular gas [arXiv:1812.00006] and exhibits a complex, multi-component structure that suggests an ongoing merger [arXiv:2405.12955, arXiv:2206.03503].

sources.summary: HZ10 is modeled as a z=5.6566 ALMA [C II] galaxy with solid-rotation-like behavior, an imperfect velocity-field fit, and possible merger interpretation.

quotes.quote: HZ9 ( z=5.5410 ) and HZ10 ( z=5.6566 ) were originally selected as LBGs in the COSMOS field leau07 , and were observed in [ C 2 ] and rest-frame-FIR emission in cycle 1 ALMA by capa15 . Both HZ9 and HZ10 were detected in rest-frame 150 m emission ( 0.52 0.04 mJy and 1.26 0.04 mJy) and [ C 2 ] ( 1.95 0.07 Jy km s ^ 1 and 4.5 0.3 Jy km s ^ 1 ). The star formation rate (SFR) of each source was estimated using SED fits to greybody models based on the 158 m continuum flux density and source intensities in COSMOS and Ultra-Vista images.

HZ10 Similarly to HZ9, the linear rotation curve suggests solid rotation. However, the gradient in the data is not fully reproduced by the model fit. In particular, the southwest region of the data shows a high-velocity area that is not aligned with the eastern velocity gradient. Observations of pave16 show that the centers of and [ N 2 ] emission are offset both spatially and in velocity. One possible interpretation of this is an active merger, which we lack the resolution to account for here.

sources.summary: The paper studies HZ10 as a z=5.657 main-sequence COSMOS galaxy observed in multiple ALMA programmes, including Band 8 data chosen to constrain its infrared SED.

quotes.quote: We focus on four main-sequence galaxies to which we refer to as HZ4 ( z=5.544 ), HZ6 ( z=5.293 ) This galaxy is also known as LBG-1 in RIECHERS14 . , HZ9 ( z=5.541 ), and HZ10 ( z=5.657 ) in the following. These galaxies have been previously discussed by RIECHERS14 and CAPAK15 and are initially spectroscopically selected via and UV absorption lines from a large spectroscopic campaign with Keck/DEIMOS HASINGER18 on the Cosmic Evolution Survey COSMOS, SCOVILLE07 field. All galaxies have been observed with different ALMA programmes covering their rest-frame wavelengths from 100 m to 200 m (see Table tab:observations ).

All four galaxies have been observed recently as part of the ALMA programme 2018.1.00348.S (PI: Faisst) at a frequency of 406.4 GHz (Band 8). This frequency was chosen to optimise the constraints on the infrared SED and to minimise the integration time to reach a S/N of 10 . At that frequency, the Band 8 atmospheric transmission is maximised and going to higher frequencies would increase the integration times significantly. On the other hand, Band 7 observes too low frequencies for robust constraints on the infrared SED together with the archival ALMA observations see Appendix in FAISST17b . In the rest-frame of HZ4 , HZ6 , HZ9 , and HZ10 , Band 8 corresponds to wavelengths of 112.8 m , 117.3 m , 112.9 m , and 111.0 m , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(2-1) and [N II] in HZ10, inferring a massive gas reservoir, long depletion time, higher metallicity, and main-sequence-like star-forming conditions.

quotes.quote: CO measurements We detect CO(2--1) line emission from HZ10 with a significance of 8 , and provide a constraining upper limit to the CO(2--1) emission toward LBG-1 (Figure fig:CO_spectra ). We extract an aperture spectrum for HZ10 We adopt elliptical apertures of sizes equal to the FWHM of the best fit 2D Gaussian to the integrated line emission. and a single pixel spectrum at the peak position of the [ C ii ] emission toward LBG-1, in order to measure or constrain the CO(2--1) line properties (Table table_lines ). The CO(2--1) emission toward HZ10 appears slightly resolved, although the coarse resolution of compact array configuration observations does not allow a precise size determination. We use casa uvmodelfit to fit a circular Gaussian model to the line visibilities in HZ10 and derive a deconvolved FWHM size of 1^ .2 0^ .4 for the CO(2--1) emission, corresponding to 7 2 kpc, which is compatible with the [ C ii ] and [ N ii ] size estimates.

Adopting our best estimate of the gas mass and the star formation rate in HZ10 (Table table_derived ) yields a gas depletion timescale (the inverse of the star formation efficiency) of 960^ +1200 _ 470 Myr, Uncertainties were propagated from both the star formation rate and the gas mass estimates. which is significantly longer than what is commonly measured in local and high redshift starburst galaxies ( 100 Myr; e.g., CarilliWalter ). HZ10 therefore appears to be very rich in molecular gas, and the efficiency of star formation appears compatible with what is commonly observed in lower redshift, disk-like, main-sequence galaxies ( 0.5-2 Gyr; e.g., Leroy13,Tacconi13,Tacconi18 ).

On the other hand, the lower line ratio observed in HZ10 suggests higher metallicity in this galaxy, confirming the inference based on high dust and CO emission, and suggesting a particularly ``mature, normal" galaxy at the same epoch (see also discussion by Faisst17 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST/NIRSpec IFU data to resolve HZ10 into distinct emission and continuum regions, measuring component conditions and evidence for an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: We present observations of the z 5.7 Lyman-break galaxy HZ10 with the JWST/NIRSpec IFU in high and low spectral resolution (G395H, R 2700 and PRISM, R 100 , respectively), as part of the GA-NIFS program. By spatially resolving the source, we find evidence for three spatially and spectrally distinct regions of line emission along with one region of strong continuum emission, all within a projected distance of <10 kpc.

Using the detected lines, we constrain the electron density _ 10 n_e / cm^ 3 2.5-3.3 and metallicity ( 0.5-0.7 solar) in each component. Spaxel-by-spaxel fits of each cube reveal a strong east-west velocity gradient and significant line asymmetries (indicating tidal features or outflows). The western component features a very red UV slope ( _ UV 1 ) and significant emission, suggesting an evolved population and active star formation. From a comparison to high resolution 158 m imaging obtained with the Atacama Large Millimetre/submillimetre Array (ALMA), we find that the continuum emitter is associated with a gas-poor stellar population. Altogether, these data suggest that HZ10 represents an ongoing merger, with a complex distribution of stars, gas, and dust <1 Gyr after the Big Bang.

sources.summary: HZ10 is a z=5.7 FIR-luminous LBG with extended [C ii] and [N ii] emission, spatial ionization variations, and an ISM interpreted as more mature than typical z=5-6 LBGs.

quotes.quote: The FIR-luminous LBG HZ10 We detect the [ N ii ] line in HZ10 with an integrated significance of 5.3 (peak signal-to-noise ratio of 3.2 per beam). The [ C ii ] emission is spatially resolved and shows a smooth velocity gradient suggestive of a disk, with deconvolved size of (0.80^ 0.07^ ) (0.42^ 0.06^ ) . The [ N ii ] emission also appears to be extended with a marginally larger size of (1.9^ 0.9^ ) (0.6^ 0.7^ ) , and it appears to be associated with only one part of the [ C ii ] emitting region (offset 0.8^ 0.3^ ).

The global [ C ii ] / [ N ii ] ratio of 20 measured for HZ10 is comparable to local galaxies with high SFRs Z16 , indicating a significant contribution of ionized gas to the [ C ii ] emission. In fact, under the same assumptions as for AzTEC-3, we find a fraction of [ C ii ] emission from ionized gas in HZ10 in the range 10--25 , which is similar to what is found in ``normal" local star-forming galaxies. This, together with our result for LBG-1, would support the interpretation that the ISM in HZ10 is more mature than in ``normal" LBGs at z=5 -6.

sources.summary: HZ10 is described as a z=5.654 normal, dusty LAE/LBG near CRLE; its close separation and catalog overdensity make it part of the likely protocluster environment.

quotes.quote: This new DSFG is located only 13 ^ ( 77 kpc) away from HZ10, which is an above average dusty but ``normal" (i.e., L^*_ UV ) galaxy at z=5.654 C15,Pavesi16 . HZ10 is a Lyman Alpha Emitter (LAE; Murayama07 ) and a Lyman Break Galaxy (LBG) which was selected for [ C ii ] 158 m and dust emission observations with ALMA based on its strong ultraviolet absorption features C15 .

Overdensity characterization htb

The close association in the sky and in redshift space to HZ10 ( z=5.654 , 13^ distance, corresponding to only 77 kpc, in projection and 580 km s ^ 1 of relative radial velocity) suggests that CRLE may be located in an overdense galaxy environment.

In the COSMOS2015 photometric redshift catalog, we find that HZ10, due to its very close separation, constitutes a significant overdensity of >50 , and that at a radius of 2 ^ , there is only a 1 probability of the observed overdensity (7 galaxies; corresponding to _g 2-3 ) to be produced by chance. The chance probability of at least one neighbor within 0.5 ^ is only 12 (the nearest, HZ10, is at 0.216 ^ ).

sources.summary: The paper reports NOEMA non-detections for HZ10, giving upper limits on CO line luminosities that constrain gas excitation in this z>5 main-sequence galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report CO(5 4) and CO(6 5) line observations in the dusty starbursting galaxy CRLE ( z = 5.667 ) and the main-sequence (MS) galaxy HZ10 ( z = 5.654 ) with the Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA). CRLE is the most luminous z>5 starburst in the COSMOS field and HZ10 is the most gas-rich ``normal'' galaxy currently known at z>5 . We find line luminosities for CO(5 4) and CO(6 5) of (4.9 0.5) and (3.8 0.4) 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 for CRLE and upper limits of < 0.76 and < 0.60 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 for HZ10, respectively.

For HZ10, these line luminosity limits provide the first significant constraints of this kind for a MS galaxy at z > 5 . We find the upper limit of L'_ 5 4 /L'_ 2 1 in HZ10 could be similar to the average value for MS galaxies around z 1.5 , suggesting that MS galaxies with comparable gas excitation may already have existed one billion years after the Big Bang.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HZ10 as a high-redshift star-forming galaxy with [C II] merger-like structure and a detected blended C III] doublet whose flux is measured from X-SHOOTER spectra.

quotes.quote: Specifically, for HZ10, the [ C II ] line reveals multiple components and the velocity gradient implies a merger scenario. measured [ C II ] luminosity of L_ [ C II ] = 2.5 0.5 10^8 L_ and L_ [ C II ] = 25.1 5.0 10^8 L_ for HZ1 and HZ10, respectively. found the total SFR of HZ1 and HZ10 of SFR =24_ 3 ^ +6 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 and SFR = 169_ 27 ^ +32 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 , respectively.

Next, in the NIR 2D spectrum and the extracted 1D spectra of the HZ10 galaxy, we identified the blended C III ] 1907, 1909 doublet line at the expected redshifted wavelength position of the C III ] line, at 1270 nm (Fig.

The X-SHOOTER spectrograph allows us to resolve the C III ] 1907, 1909 doublet. However, in order to detect the C III ] with a statistically significant S/N, we need to rebin the spectra via 15 channels ( 200 \ km \ s^ 1 ), which decreases the spectral resolution. Therefore, we did not ultimately detect the two lines separately, so we fit the C III ] doublet with a single Gaussian, from which we obtained a total integrated flux of 8.8 2.1 10^ 17 \ erg \ s^ 1 \ cm^ 2 with a S/N = 4.2 and a FWHM = 550.9 98.8 \ km \ s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper says CRISTAL-22 has a second dust-continuum component, high infrared surface density comparable to DSFGs and SMGs, and UV-size fitting limited to the masked main component.

quotes.quote: When we clearly find the existence of a second component in the residual map after subtracting the best-fit single component with S/N >10 (CRISTAL-22), we use the results of the two-component fitting.

Our representative population of galaxies at z 4--6 seems to undergo moderate star formation with a lower _ IR than those of DSFGs, except for some galaxies (CRISTAL-22 and 24) showing the high _ IR those are comparable with DSFGs and SMGs.
For CRISTAL-22, we only measure the size of the main component by masking the second component because the latter is too faint and the fitting does not converge (see Figure fig:thumbneils ).

gmw_00002195.name: SDSS J164348.22+410243.3
gmw_00002195.overview: SDSS J164348.22+410243.3 is a blue dwarf galaxy with a diameter of approximately 10 arcseconds [arXiv:2008.04321]. It has a low stellar mass of 7.8 * 10^7 solar masses and is the probable host galaxy of the broad-line Type Ic supernova SN 2018gep [arXiv:2008.04321].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J164348.22+410243.3 as the probable SN 2018gep host and characterizes it as a blue dwarf with low mass, measured metallicity, and SED-derived properties.

quotes.quote: We obtain IFU observations of the SN 2018gep host galaxy and derive a number of properties for it including M _ host = 7.8^ +2.4 _ 1.2 10^7 M _ and a metallicity of log(O/H) +12 = 8.31^ +0.07 _ 0.09 . We show that the derived host galaxy properties for both SN 2018gep and iPTF16asu are overall consistent with the SNe Ic-bl and GRB/SNe sample while being on the extreme edge of the observed Fast Evolving Transient sample.

Approximately ten days later on 19 September 2018, TNS2804 , as part of the Global Supernovae Project (GSP), obtained an optical spectrum (see Section sec:spec ) and classified the object as a broad-line Type Ic supernovae (Ic-bl) with an ejecta velocity of 24000 km/s and a redshift of 0.032 which is consistent with the probable host galaxy identified by TNS22559 , SDSS J164348.22+410243.3 with a z= 0.033 with a SN-host separation of 1.5 .

SED fit The host galaxy is a blue dwarf galaxy, with an observed SDSS mag of g^ =18.87 mag, and with a diameter of 10 . We used the Le Phare code to perform SED fitting of the host galaxy of SN 2018gep using broadband data from SDSS.

gmw_00002196.name: host of iPTF16asu
gmw_00002196.overview: The host galaxy of iPTF16asu is a low-mass dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1874 [arXiv:1706.05018]. It is characterized as a star-forming system with low metallicity [arXiv:1706.05018, arXiv:2008.04321].

sources.summary: The host galaxy of iPTF 16asu is described as a detected dwarf in PTF and SDSS data, with low metallicity and a starforming dwarf character.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of iPTF 16asu is detected both in the PTF templates and in SDSS images. The observed SDSS model magnitudes are u' = 22.90 0.37 mag, g' = 22.10 0.09 mag, ' = 21.82 0.11 mag, i' = 21.43 0.11 mag, and z' = 21.25 0.28 mag. At a redshift of z=0.1874 , this makes the host a dwarf galaxy, with an absolute magnitude M_g 17.5 mag .

While there is some scatter between the different strong-line metallicity estimators, they generally agree that the host galaxy of iPTF 16asu is low metallicity. For example, we find values of 12+ ( O/H ) to be 8.12_ 0.07 ^ +0.04 on the pp04 O3N2 scale, 8.22_ 0.07 ^ +0.18 on the mcg91 scale, and 8.39_ 0.05 ^ +0.11 on the kk04 R _ 23 scale, to name three commonly used indicators. Using a solar oxygen abundance of 12+ ( O/H ) = 8.69 0.05 ags+09 , this translates to a metallicity Z Z_ /3 . Taken together, the host galaxy of iPTF 16asu was a low-mass, low-metallicity, starforming dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The host of iPTF16asu is compared with the SN 2018gep host as a low-mass, low-metallicity dwarf whose mass and metallicity are consistent with Ic-bl and GRB-SN hosts.

quotes.quote: The hosts of SN 2018gep and iPTF16asu are low-mass low-metallicity dwarf galaxies that lie beneath the observed SDSS population and its standard deviation Kewley08 . The host galaxies of SN 2018gep and iPTF16asu have masses and metallicities that are broadly consistent with both the SN Ic-bl sample and the GRB-SN sample the hosts of which are also comparable to each other, Modjaz20 . The host of iPTF16asu has both a mass and metallicity close to the average of these two samples while the host of SN 2018gep is on the very low mass end while having a metallicity similar to the average. Comparing the hosts of SN 2018gep and iPTF16asu with those of the fast-transient hosts, we show that their host properties are on the extreme end of the observed distribution of fast-transient hosts. The host galaxies of SN 2018gep and iPTF16asu have metallicities comparable to that of the lowest measured host from the PS1 Fast Evolving Transient sample and with the SN 2018gep host galaxy having a mass similar to the least massive and most metal-poor hosts from the PS1 sample simultaneously.

gmw_00002197.name: UV-230929
gmw_00002197.overview: UV-230929 is a spectroscopically confirmed massive quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z~2 [arXiv:2310.09703]. It is characterized by an old stellar population with a high stellar mass and a low star formation rate limit [arXiv:2310.09703].

sources.summary: UV-230929 is selected by both color criteria as a spectroscopically confirmed massive quiescent z~2 galaxy, with high stellar mass, old age, and a low [O II] SFR limit.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopically confirmed galaxies One object from 2020ApJ...888....4S (UV-230929) and two objects from the 3D-HST ( 2012ApJS..200...13B, 2016ApJS..225...27M , included in the HSC-SSP PDR3 spectroscopic redshift catalog) are selected with both the JH and i [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color criteria. Their photometric properties from the COSMOS2020 catalog are presented in Figure fig:cosmos_sedparams and Table tab:specz . Our color criteria extract only one object from massive quiescent galaxies confirmed spectroscopically at z 2 in 2017ApJ...834...18B and 2020ApJ...888....4S because our color criteria are only sensitive to massive quiescent galaxies with specific quenching timescales and ages. Based on the spectroscopic analyses in 2020ApJ...888....4S , UV-230929 has a stellar mass of (M_ / M _ )=11.48^ +0.16 _ 0.16 , (Age/yr) = 9.10^ +0.28 _ 0.28 and the upper limit of SFR from [ O II ] <4 M _ yr^ 1 .

gmw_00002198.name: 3D-HST 11142
gmw_00002198.overview: 3D-HST 11142 is a massive, high-redshift galaxy at z=2.445 with a compact elliptical morphology [arXiv:2310.09703, arXiv:2308.05795]. It is in a post-starburst phase with low star formation, featuring strong neutral outflows likely driven by active galactic nucleus feedback [arXiv:2308.05795].

sources.summary: 3D-HST 11142 is one of two selected spectroscopic objects whose spectra show Dn(4000) consistent with an old burst, no significant lines, and compact elliptical morphology.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopically confirmed galaxies One object from 2020ApJ...888....4S (UV-230929) and two objects from the 3D-HST ( 2012ApJS..200...13B, 2016ApJS..225...27M , included in the HSC-SSP PDR3 spectroscopic redshift catalog) are selected with both the JH and i [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color criteria. Their photometric properties from the COSMOS2020 catalog are presented in Figure fig:cosmos_sedparams and Table tab:specz .

The spectra of 3D-HST 11142 and 16527 are presented in Figure fig:speczimage . They show Dn(4000) 1.4 , consistent with a single burst model with age 1 Gyr 1999ApJ...527...54B . There is no emission and absorption line above 2 significance.

Figure fig:speczimage shows the images of 3D-HST 11142 and 16527 from Cosmic Assembly Near-Infrared Deep Extragalactic Legacy Survey (CANDELS, 2011ApJS..197...35G,2011ApJS..197...36K ). They are fitted by compact ( r_e 1 kpc) and elliptical ( n>2.5 ) morphologies. Their sizes are similar to those of quiescent galaxies with similar stellar masses at z 2 2014ApJ...788...28V .

iJH vs. i [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color criterion The objects satisfying both color criteria are circled in Figure fig:cosmos_color_z2 .

sources.summary: The paper studies COSMOS-11142 as a massive post-starburst quenching galaxy at z=2.445, finding low star formation, strong neutral outflows, and AGN feedback as the likely driver.

quotes.quote: COSMOS-11142 is among the most massive targets, with / = 10.9 , and is one of the 17 objects that are classified as quiescent according to the rest-frame UVJ colors . This is confirmed by the JWST/NIRSpec spectrum, shown in Figure fig:spectrum , which covers the rest-frame range from 3000 \ to 1.4 \ and features several stellar absorption lines and relatively weak emission lines from ionized gas. The presence of deep Balmer absorption lines clearly indicates that the galaxy is in the ``post-starburst'' phase immediately following the rapid quenching of a star formation episode.

The star formation rate of COSMOS-11142 is a key physical quantity, since it is used both to confirm the quiescent nature of the system and as a comparison to the mass outflow rate. We thus conclude that the observed outflow likely plays a key role in the rapid quenching of COSMOS-11142; this conclusion is further strengthened when considering that the molecular phase (not probed by our observations) is expected to be equally effective as the neutral phase at ejecting gas . The only reasonable explanation is therefore that the observed outflow is driven by AGN feedback.

gmw_00002199.overview: 16527 is a spectroscopically confirmed galaxy that exhibits a compact, elliptical morphology [arXiv:2310.09703]. Its spectrum shows a break consistent with a single stellar burst model of approximately 1 Gyr in age, with no emission or absorption lines detected above a significance level of two [arXiv:2310.09703].

sources.summary: 16527 is treated as a 3D-HST spectroscopic object selected by the color criteria, with old-burst spectral break, no significant lines, and compact elliptical morphology.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopically confirmed galaxies One object from 2020ApJ...888....4S (UV-230929) and two objects from the 3D-HST ( 2012ApJS..200...13B, 2016ApJS..225...27M , included in the HSC-SSP PDR3 spectroscopic redshift catalog) are selected with both the JH and i [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color criteria. Their photometric properties from the COSMOS2020 catalog are presented in Figure fig:cosmos_sedparams and Table tab:specz .

The spectra of 3D-HST 11142 and 16527 are presented in Figure fig:speczimage . They show Dn(4000) 1.4 , consistent with a single burst model with age 1 Gyr 1999ApJ...527...54B . There is no emission and absorption line above 2 significance.
