Figure fig:speczimage shows the images of 3D-HST 11142 and 16527 from Cosmic Assembly Near-Infrared Deep Extragalactic Legacy Survey (CANDELS, 2011ApJS..197...35G,2011ApJS..197...36K ). They are fitted by compact ( r_e 1 kpc) and elliptical ( n>2.5 ) morphologies. Their sizes are similar to those of quiescent galaxies with similar stellar masses at z 2 2014ApJ...788...28V .

iJH vs. i [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color criterion The objects satisfying both color criteria are circled in Figure fig:cosmos_color_z2 .

gmw_00002200.name: NGC 3041
gmw_00002200.overview: NGC 3041 is a spiral galaxy that features an arc-like stellar stream located to its northeast [arXiv:1804.03330]. This stream extends approximately 4 kiloparsecs along its longest dimension and may represent the brightest portion of a larger great-circle structure [arXiv:1804.03330].

sources.summary: The paper reports a 4 kpc northeast arc-like stream around NGC 3041, visible in STSS and DECaLS data and interpreted as a possible great-circle stream.

quotes.quote: NGC 3041 shows an arc-like stream with an extent along its longest dimension of 4 \ kpc , northeast of the central galaxy. STSS and DECaLS data (Fig. fig:misk_vs_stss ) show this feature clearly, but they do not reveal any further detail. This may be the brighter part of a great circle structure similar to the Milky Way Sagittarius stream, but no surviving progenitor is apparent.

gmw_00002201.name: NGC 3049
gmw_00002201.overview: NGC 3049 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2301.11297] in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015] that features an inner ring [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015] and a thin bar [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015]. The galaxy contains star-forming regions [arXiv:2311.16950] hosting a high-metallicity Wolf-Rayet stellar population [arXiv:2301.11297, arXiv:astro-ph/9809015], alongside outer tidal-like substructures [arXiv:1804.03330].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3049 as a high-metallicity Wolf-Rayet barred spiral, estimating its WN and WC populations from blue and yellow emission bumps.

quotes.quote: NGC 3049 (Mrk 710) Our high metallicity target, NGC 3049 is a barred spiral at a distance of 19.3 Mpc 2013AJ....146...86T which was identified as a WR galaxy by 1986A&A...169...71K . WR populations of NGC 3049 have been studied using ESO 2.2m/EFOSC2 1999A&A...341..399S and HST/STIS 2002ApJ...580..824G , and exhibits broad emission at N iii 4634,41/C iii 4647,51, He ii 4686, C iii 5696 and C iv 5801,12. NGC 3049 is unusual in the 4634,41 feature being comparable in strength to He ii 4686 see 1999NewA....4..197S . Fig.

n3049-WR (left panels) presents de-reddened, velocity corrected WR bumps including gaussian fits to stellar and nebular emission lines. Table ngc3049 provides a summary of nebular and stellar line luminosities in NGC 3049, remeasured from EFOSC observations which provides a spectral coverage of 4370--6550 \ at a resolving power of R 750 1999A&A...341..399S . The spectroscopic L(H ) corresponds to an ionizing output of Q_ H = 1.6 10^ 52 ph s ^ 1 , comparable to 30 Doradus in the LMC, although the integrated output is likely to be considerably higher. Strong line calibrations indicate NGC 3049 is supersolar O/H+12 8.9, 2000ApJ...531..776G so we apply Galactic calibrations to estimate its WR populations.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3049 as a nearby star-forming galaxy where source A has consistent He II and UV reddening while source B may be dustier or older.

quotes.quote: For this study, we selected star-forming regions in eight nearby galaxies ( 0.0010 z 0.0095 , at distances of 3-37 Mpc) with existing He II 1640 HST-STIS UV observations from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). These UV spectra were previously obtained for He 2-10, NGC 3125, Mrk 33, NGC 4214, NGC 4670, and Tol 1924-416 as part of program 9036 (PI: C. Leitherer) taken in 2001–2003, and for NGC 3049 and Tol 89 as part of program 7513 (PI: C. Leitherer) observed in 1999-2000. Additional optical HST-STIS observations, providing He II 4686 line measurements were obtained from the HST program GO-15846 (PI: C. Leitherer), executed in 2020-21. These optical observations were carried out at the exact same location, same orientation, and with the same aperture sizes as the UV observations.

NGC 3049    We find good agreement between the He II and UV dust reddening estimates for Source A. Source B has no significant He II emission but we derived the E(B-V)_ UV value of 0.42 mag. This value is significantly higher then for Source A (0.13 mag) and might be the reason for the non-detection of the He II lines due to dust attenuation. However, we do not see any traces of nebular H emission around source B, which might be due to the fact that this source is already older than 5 Myr and the W-R star population has already disappeared.

sources.summary: The paper detects WC stars in NGC 3049, finds its Wolf-Rayet region aligned with continuum and nebular peaks, and notes its high-metallicity region is dominated by late WC stars.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of WC stars in five Wolf-Rayet (W-R) galaxies: He 2-10, NGC 3049, NGC 3125, NGC 5253 and Tol 89. The faint broad \ line requires sufficiently high S/N ( 40) to be detected explaining the non-detection of this WC feature in previous observations. From the measurement of W-R emission lines ( + , , and ), we conclude that all W-R regions contain a mixed population of WNL, and early WC stars. The exception is the high-metallicity region NGC 3049 where late WC stars prevail.

NGC 3049, NGC 3125 In NGC 3049 and NGC 3125, the W-R regions coincide with the peaks of the continuum emission and nebular lines distributions. NGC 3049 is a barred spiral galaxy belonging to the Virgo cluster. This low-mass galaxy has a small bulge and a thin bar of constant surface brightness surrounded by an inner ring .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3049 as an edge-on spiral where the cold dust appears closer to the center and outer submillimetre structure is only weakly indicated.

quotes.quote: Two of the sources (NGC5701 and NGC5669) were observed as part of the Herschel Reference Survey (Boselli et al. 2010), and a third (NGC3049) was observed by the KINGFISH programme (Kennicutt et al. 2010). The SPIRE images were obtained from the Herschel or HerMES data archives. For each of these objects we used an SDSS i band optical image to trace the optical surface brightness profile from stellar emission after smoothing by a gaussian matched to the size of the 250 m SPIRE beam, and then compared this to the 250 m surface brightness profile resulting from dust emission.

NGC3049 is an edge on spiral, which makes this analysis a little less clear, and we see only a slight hint of changes to the optical to submm surface brightness ratio in the outer parts of this object, or possibly weak submm emission beyond the optically detected emission.
NGC5701 shows a large ring of cold dust located outside most of the optical light distribution but in NGC3049 the cold dust appears to be located closer to the centre of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 3049 slightly ambiguous, fitting a region inside the bar radius, noting possible outer excess light, and updating bar measurements from Spitzer imaging.

quotes.quote: This profile is slightly ambiguous, since the break is fairly close to the end of the bar, and the profile between the end of the bar and the break suggests a steeper profile (with another break inside). This is the only profile for which we include a region inside the bar radius in the fit. There is a slight hint of excess light at > 90 , hence we include `` [ + III? ] '' in the label on the plot. The bar size measurements have been slightly updated from those published in erwin05 , using a Spitzer 3.5 m (IRAC1) image from the Spitzer Infrared Nearby Galaxy Survey kennicutt03 .

sources.summary: The survey finds an eastern 3 kpc arc-like feature and a western diffuse substructure around NGC 3049, suggestive of tidal disruption but needing deeper observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3049 shows an arc-like feature east of the central galaxy, with a size of 3 \ kpc . Another very diffuse substructure can be identified to the west, suggestive of a shell formed by tidal disruption. More definitive statements require deeper observations.

gmw_00002202.name: NGC 3611
gmw_00002202.overview: NGC 3611 is a peculiar galaxy characterized by a dusty, slightly asymmetric inner region and a bright, off-center ring-like structure [arXiv:1804.03330, arXiv:1108.3662]. The galaxy's outer regions feature an umbrella-like stream with bilateral shells, a blue arc, and a tidal tail that suggest an ongoing interaction [arXiv:1804.03330, arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: NGC 3611 is described as having a known off-center ring plus DECaLS evidence for an umbrella-like stream, bilateral shells, and a blue arc of uncertain common origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 3611 is well known for the peculiar 30 \ bright off-center ring-like structure previously noted by Schweizer1990 . These authors favored merging as the origin of this feature, excluding the possibility of a disturbed polar ring. DECaLS data (Fig. fig:decam_final ) show two distinct features: a clear umbrella-like stream with shells on both sides of the galaxy (the most prominent to the east), and an incomplete blue ring or arc encircling the disk. The colors of both structures are clearly different, and it is unclear whether they have a common origin (e.g., the tidal disruption of a Magellanic-type dwarf galaxy).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3604's outer profile as III-d(?) and describes dust features, antitruncation, interaction signatures shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3604 (III-d(?); SA(s)a pec) : The inner region of this galaxy is dusty and slightly asymmetric, while one or more arcs and a spectacular off-center tidal tail dominate the outer regions, indicating that this galaxy is in the midst of an interaction. The outer profile is clearly affected by the arcs and tidal arm; we tentatively classify the profile as III-d only because the outer light is so clearly not anything like a ``spheroid''; it is conceivable that the tidal structures could eventually evolve into something more spheroid-like. This antitruncation might possibly be caused by UGC 6306 at 3 arcminutes from NGC 3604, if the measured radial velocity difference (170 ) is due to orbital motion rather than the Hubble flow.

gmw_00002203.name: NGC 3631
gmw_00002203.overview: NGC 3631 is a spiral galaxy characterized by two spiral arms with differing pitch angles [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. Its arms exhibit detectable dust extinction [arXiv:astro-ph/9602098], and a giant cloud or stream-like structure is located in its outer disk [arXiv:1804.03330, arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3631 radial surface-brightness profiles and finds arm scale-lengths decline with wavelength, indicating detectable dust extinction in the arms but not interarm zones.

quotes.quote: NGC 3631 In Fig. 5 (a) we present the radial surface brightness profiles for this object in the B , V , R , and I bands, in Fig. 5 (b) we show the profiles for the arms only, and in Fig. 5 () for the interarm zones. The fitted values for the observed scale-lengths and the extrapolated on-axis surface brightnesses are given in Table 1. The scale-length behavior of NGC 3631 is qualitatively similar to that of M 51. In the arms there is a systematic decline of scale-length with increasing wavelength, which is barely evident in the interarm disk.

The scale-lengths in the arms are systematically longer than those in the interarm zones, but converge towards equality as the wavelength increases. We do not have K-band information here, but in this convergence, NGC 3631 shows similarity to M 51. Here, too, the most straightforward interpretation of the trends observed is that in the arms the optical effects of dust extinction are readily detectable, whereas in the inter-arm zones they are not.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a giant cloud 19 arcminutes from NGC 3631, compares it to the M83 stream, and notes STSS confirmation that it is not an SDSS artifact.

quotes.quote: NGC 3631 shows a giant cloud at a galactocentric distance of 19 \ . This is very similar to the M83 stream Malin97 . It is not clear whether the structure is part of a very faint outer disk or a tidal structure in the galactic halo. This overdensity has also been observed by the STSS (Martinez-Delgado et al., in preparation), indicating that it is not an artifact in the SDSS image.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3631 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3631 : J112102.4+531008 The galaxy forms a nearby group together with NGC 3178, NGC 3898, NGC 3953 and NGC 3992 (also part of our sample) in the Ursa Major cluster and shows plenty of substructure. It has two prominent spiral arms with different pitch angles, one of them turns nearly straight, classified by as a spiral galaxy with one heavy arm. In addition, it has a faint extended spiral arm (or maybe stream-like structure) in the far outer disk towards North, therefore the outer disk is slightly asymmetric and the mean ellipticity and PA are difficult to fix.

gmw_00002204.name: NGC 4643
gmw_00002204.overview: NGC 4643 is a massive, strongly barred early-type disk galaxy [arXiv:2101.05321] that hosts a 73 kpc stellar tidal stream [arXiv:1804.03330]. The galaxy lacks a nuclear ring [arXiv:2010.11815] and is dominated by an old stellar population with no clear sign of gas in its bar [arXiv:2003.08946, arXiv:2010.11815].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4643 as a similar barred early-type disk galaxy, but finds its center is dominated by a rapidly rotating nuclear disc rather than a classical bulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 4608 and NGC 4643 are both massive, strongly barred, early-type disc galaxies. The former is located in the Virgo Cluster at a distance of 17.3 0.8 Mpc surface-brightness-fluctuation distance; cantiello18 ; the latter is in the nearby field (approximately 2.4 Mpc away from NGC 4608), with a redshift-based distance estimate of 19.3 2.9 Mpc. Assuming H_ 0 = 72 kpc ^ 1 and a Virgocentric-infall-corrected redshift of 1392 from HyperLEDA, along with an assumed uncertainty of 15 . These distances give scales of 83.9 pc/ for NGC 4608 and 93.6 pc/ for NGC 4643. NGC 4608 is classified in rc3 as SB0 and NGC 4643 as SB0/a; very faint, wispy spiral arms account for the latter classification e.g., erwin-sparke03 .

Summary Our final model for NGC 4643 uses 's BrokenExponential component for the main disc; the new FlatBar component for the outer part of the bar and a mildly elliptical Sersic GenEllipse component for the inner, B/P part of the bar; another BrokenExponential component for the nuclear disc; and a central compact, nearly circular S ersic component. Table tab:n4643-decomp summarizes the best-fitting parameter values, with the absolute and fractional luminosities in Table tab:fluxes , and Figures fig:n4643-bestfit-irac1 and fig:n4643-bestfit-f160w compare this model with the IRAC1 and F160W data.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4643 lacks clear bar gas, has no determinable rotation sense from spiral arms, and shows old, metal-poor, Mg-enhanced bar-edge trends.

quotes.quote: Only NGC 4371 and NGC 4643 show no clear sign of gas in the bar. For two galaxies, NGC 4371 and NGC 4643, we were not able to determine the sense of rotation due to the lack of spiral arm features. An alternative way of visualising the data and results that we discussed in Sects. sect:maps and sect:4cuts is shown as an example for NGC 4643 in Fig. fig:ref_NGC4643 light-weighted and for the whole sample in Figs. fig:ref_age_z_lw and fig:ref_age_z_mw light-weighted and mass-weighted, respectively.

The left panel in Fig. fig:ref_NGC4643 shows some clear trends for NGC 4643 that agree with our previous analysis. As we move away from the bar major axis the stars become more [ Mg/Fe ] enhanced and more metal-poor. In the right panel we see that the stars in this particular bar are predominantly old, even in light-weighted mean ages that are biased towards younger populations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4643 as a quiescent control galaxy without a nuclear ring, dominated by old stellar populations and showing a homogeneous old age map.

quotes.quote: NGC 4643 was added to our sample as a control galaxy. It is an SB(rs)0/a galaxy without a nuclear ring, which hosts little gas and in which the old component of the stellar population dominates the disc 2019MNRAS.482..506G .
NGC 4643 does not, is dominated by old stellar populations, and is used here as a control galaxy.

As expected, NGC 4643 is very different from the other three galaxies in the sample. In this galaxy there is no ring structure in the age map, which appears rather homogeneous. In NGC 4643 we find a rather old stellar population with a mean age around 11 Gyr with a small standard deviation of 0.7 Gyr.

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 4643 as a possible barlens galaxy with a small embedded central component whose low Sersic index argues against a classical bulge.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 4643 (S0 ^+ ) might have also a small central bulge embedded in the barlens. Looking at the surface brightness profile alone we don't know for sure whether the central peak is really a distinct bulge component, or is it rather formed of the same material as the rest of the bar, at the epoch of bar formation. The S ersic index of the central component is n =0.7 indicating that it is not a classical bulge. One possibility is that it is a manifestation of an old pseudobulge formed at high redshift, composed of old stars.

sources.summary: NGC 4643 is described as hosting a clear 73 kpc stellar tidal stream, with DECaLS evidence for a northern-tail progenitor and a separate inner arc.

quotes.quote: NGC 4643 shows a clear stellar tidal stream apparently perpendicular to the plane of the galaxy. DECaLS data show evidence for a progenitor in the northern tail. Assuming that both structures apparent in the image are part of the same feature (for example, an arc viewed edge-on), this feature has an extent of 73 \ kpc . Whitmore90 reported an inner, edge-on arc structure in the main body of the galaxy that is also visible in our images, but not related to the giant tidal structure we report here.

gmw_00002205.name: NGC 5750
gmw_00002205.overview: NGC 5750 is a barred S0/a transition galaxy that features a ring-like structure [arXiv:1808.04491]. It exhibits irregular overdensities on both sides of its disk, including a distorted satellite-like feature to the west and a possible tidal stream to the east [arXiv:1804.03330].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5750 as a barred S0/a transition object with a ring-like structure, old near-solar stellar population, and atypical pseudobulge classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 5750 This is an interesting object which exhibits a ring like structure. It is classified as an S0/a galaxy which designates it as a transition class object, i.e. an object which might be speculated to be transforming between S0 and Sa morphology. Sandage, in The Carnegie Atlas of Galaxies speculates that the ring might actually be made of two very tightly bound spiral arms. The galaxy is barred and in terms of spectral properties, is similar to NGC 1533, comprising of an old population with near solar metallicity. It is also the second barred galaxy in our sample which has been classified as a pseudobulge based on photometric properties, but it shows characteristics that are atypical and not expected of pseudobulges.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 5750 as having irregular overdensities on both sides of the disk, including a west distorted-satellite-like feature and a possible eastern tidal stream.

quotes.quote: The second column presents NGC 5750, which shows a remarkable irregular substructure apparently emerging from its disk; deeper STSS observations reveal an additional overdensity on the other side of the galaxy, which is only barely visible at the detection limit of our SDSS images.

NGC 5750 Both images from our analysis and the STSS deeper images (see Fig. fig:misk_vs_stss ) show a truncated overdensity west of the central galaxy, which resembles a faint, distorted satellite galaxy. In addition, an elongated, irregular feature east of the disk (clearly visible in the STSS image) could be part of a tidal stream associated with that satellite.

gmw_00002206.name: R.A. (J2000) 13^h 11^m 27 62 Dec. (J2000) 01 21 35 62
gmw_00002206.overview: The galaxy R.A. (J2000) 13^h 11^m 27 62 Dec. (J2000) 01 21 35 62 is a high-redshift, star-forming dwarf Lyman-alpha emitter [arXiv:1906.11849]. It is gravitationally lensed with a magnification of 7.9 and exhibits a low stellar mass [arXiv:1906.11849].

sources.summary: The coordinate-identified lensed dwarf is a high-redshift star-forming Ly emitter with 7.9 magnification, [O III] 4363 detection, direct oxygen abundance, and nebular-condition measurements.

quotes.quote: We present Keck/MOSFIRE and Keck/LRIS spectroscopy of , a lensed (magnification 7.9 ), star-forming (SFR 16 M_ \ yr^ 1 ), dwarf (log( M_ /M_ ) = 8.07-8.59 ) Ly emitter ( EW_0 138 ) at z = . Dwarf galaxies similar to \ are common at high redshift and likely responsible for reionization, yet few have been studied with detailed spectroscopy. We report a 4.2 detection of the electron-temperature-sensitive [ O 3 ] 4363 emission line and use this line to directly measure an oxygen abundance of 12+ (O/H) = 8.06 0.12 ( 1/4 \ Z_ ). \ is the lowest mass galaxy at z> 2 with an [ O 3 ] 4363 detection. Using the rest-optical emission lines, we measure 's other nebular conditions including electron temperature ( T_e ( [ O 3 ] ) 14,000 K), electron density ( n_e 220 cm^ 3 ) and reddening ( E( ) 0.39).

sources.summary: The paper reports a newly identified low-surface-brightness object at these coordinates as a possible nucleated dwarf near M101, with m_r about 21.2 and potentially extremely low luminosity if at M101's distance.

quotes.quote: This low surface brightness object is a possible nucleated dwarf galaxy, at RA =14^ h 3^ m 27 38 , DEC =53 37 51 8 (J2000). It is not in the sample of faint low surface brightness objects of Carlsten et al. \ (2019), and given its proximity to M101 it is unlikely that it is a member of the background NGC 5485 group (see Merritt et al. \ 2016b, Karachentsev Makarova 2019). Its band magnitude in the Dragonfly data is m_r 21.2 . Note that the star that is visible in the CFHT image in the Northwest quadrant of the galaxy was subtracted in the MRF process. If it is at the distance of M101 its absolute magnitude is M_r 8.0 , which would place it among the lowest luminosity dwarf galaxies yet identified outside of the Local Group (see, e.g., Smercina et al. \ 2017, Mihos et al. \ 2018).

gmw_00002207.name: Antlia II
gmw_00002207.overview: Antlia II is a highly diffuse dwarf satellite galaxy of the Milky Way with an extremely low surface brightness [arXiv:1811.04082, arXiv:2106.08819]. Its unusually large spatial extent and low velocity dispersion may be the result of tidal stripping acting on a cored dark matter halo [arXiv:1811.04082, arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: The paper reports Antlia 2 as a newly discovered Milky Way dwarf satellite, unusually diffuse and low surface brightness, with spectroscopy indicating a low-density dark-matter halo shaped by cored profiles and tidal stripping.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We searched for DECam data in the g and bands in a 100 square degree region around (ra,dec)=144.1558,-37.07509 , and retrieved the instcal and weight frames provided by NOAO.

quotes.quote: The Antlia 2 dwarf galaxy is located behind the Galactic disc at a latitude of b 11^ and spans 1.26 degrees, which corresponds to 2.9 kpc at its distance of 130 kpc. While similar in spatial extent to the Large Magellanic Cloud, Antlia 2 is orders of magnitude fainter at M_V=-9 mag, making it by far the lowest surface brightness system known (at 31.9 mag/arcsec ^2 ), 100 times more diffuse than the so-called ultra diffuse galaxies. The satellite was identified using a combination of astrometry, photometry and variability data from Gaia Data Release 2, and its nature confirmed with deep archival DECam imaging, which revealed a conspicuous BHB signal. We have also obtained follow-up spectroscopy using AAOmega on the AAT, identifying 159 member stars, and we used them to measure the dwarf's systemic velocity, 290.9 0.5 km/s, its velocity dispersion, 5.7 1.1 km/s, and mean metallicity, [ Fe/H ] =-1.4 . From these properties we conclude that Antlia 2 inhabits one of the least dense Dark Matter (DM) halos probed to date.

Dynamical modelling and tidal-disruption simulations suggest that a combination of a cored DM profile and strong tidal stripping may explain the observed properties of this satellite. The origin of this core may be consistent with aggressive feedback, or may even require alternatives to cold dark matter (such as ultra-light bosons).

sources.summary: Antlia II is described as unusually diffuse and kinematically cold; the paper finds tidal sculpting more plausible in perturbed and heavy Milky Way potentials.

quotes.quote: The stellar component of both Antlia II and Crater II has peculiar properties, with an extremely low surface brightness, large half-light radius and low l.o.s. velocity dispersion when compared to other MW satellites of similar stellar mass Torrealba_16a, Caldwell_17, Torrealba_19 . It has been postulated that also these galaxies have been "sculpted" by tidal disturbances by the MW Fattahi_17,Sanders_18,Torrealba_19 . According to the orbital parameters derived in this work, this hypothesis appears fairly robust for Crater II, confirming the GDR2-based results, while for Antlia II it appears more sound when considering the "perturbed" and "heavy MW" potentials, than in the "light MW" one. The pericentric distances obtained for these two models are compatible with that explored by Torrealba_19 to study whether tidal effects onto a cored DM halo could explain the low surface brightness, large half-light radius and low l.o.s. velocity dispersion of Antlia II.

gmw_00002208.name: Crater II
gmw_00002208.overview: Crater II is a Milky Way satellite dwarf galaxy characterized by an extremely low surface brightness and a large half-light radius [arXiv:2106.08819]. It exhibits a low line-of-sight velocity dispersion and shows robust evidence of being tidally sculpted by the Milky Way [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: Crater II is described as an unusually low-surface-brightness, large, cold dwarf whose orbital parameters robustly support tidal sculpting by the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: The stellar component of both Antlia II and Crater II has peculiar properties, with an extremely low surface brightness, large half-light radius and low l.o.s. velocity dispersion when compared to other MW satellites of similar stellar mass Torrealba_16a, Caldwell_17, Torrealba_19 . It has been postulated that also these galaxies have been "sculpted" by tidal disturbances by the MW Fattahi_17,Sanders_18,Torrealba_19 . According to the orbital parameters derived in this work, this hypothesis appears fairly robust for Crater II, confirming the GDR2-based results, while for Antlia II it appears more sound when considering the "perturbed" and "heavy MW" potentials, than in the "light MW" one.

gmw_00002209.name: Aquarius II
gmw_00002209.overview: Aquarius II is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2302.03708]. It is highly dark-matter dominated, with a dynamical half-light mass of approximately 3.1 million solar masses and a high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:2302.03708].

sources.summary: The paper presents Magellan/IMACS spectroscopy for Aquarius II, expands RGB metallicity measurements, updates velocity and metallicity, and finds it highly dark-matter dominated.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Aqu II & June 18, 2021 & 59384.35 & 22:33:53.079 & -09:18:08.63 ... Property & Description & Aquarius II & Bo o tes II & Units _ 2000 & Right Ascension & 338.4813 0.005 a & 209.5141 0.005 b & deg _ 2000 & Declination & 9.3274 0.005 a & 12.8553 0.006 b & deg

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a chemical and kinematic analysis of two ultra-faint dwarf galaxies (UFDs), Aquarius II (Aqu II) and Bo o tes II (Boo II), using Magellan/IMACS spectroscopy. We present the largest sample of member stars for Boo II (12), and the largest sample of red-giant-branch members with metallicity measurements for Aqu II (8). In both UFDs, over 80 of targets selected based on Gaia proper motions turned out to be spectroscopic members. In order to maximize the accuracy of stellar kinematic measurements, we remove the identified binary stars and RR Lyrae variables. For Aqu II we measure a systemic velocity of 65.3 1.8 km s ^ 1 and a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = 2.57^ +0.17 _ 0.17 .

For Aqu II, we estimated a dynamical half-light mass of 3.1^ +3.0 _ 1.4 10^6 M_ , and a mass-to-light ratio of 1300^ +1300 _ 600 M_ /L_ . This large mass-to-light ratio distinctly indicates the dark matter dominated nature of Aqu II, as expected due to its identity as a UFD.

sources.summary: Aquarius II has scattered proper motions but reasonable color-magnitude and sky distributions; quality cuts likely explain the larger systemic proper-motion uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Aquarius II There is significant scatter in the PM plane for the P>0.5 stars; on the other hand, the distribution on the colour-magnitude plane and on the sky seems reasonable. We noticed that for this system the cut ipd gof harmonic amplitude <0.2 excludes several sources whose spatial, photometric and astrometric properties are perfectly compatible with those of the sources classified as probable members. This is probably the reason for the significantly larger uncertainties in the systemic PM in this work with respect to McConnachie_EDR3 .

gmw_00002210.name: Bootes III
gmw_00002210.overview: Bootes III is a diffuse, metal-poor ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0904.3068, arXiv:2308.07394] that is likely in a transitional state of tidal disruption [arXiv:0907.3738, arXiv:2106.08819]. This highly eccentric system is potentially the progenitor of the Styx stellar stream [arXiv:2001.01107, arXiv:2308.07394] and contains a population of RR Lyrae variable stars [arXiv:2001.01107, arXiv:2312.07279].

sources.summary: The paper reports red clump evidence for Bo otes III, linking the stellar population to an old metal-poor system with bimodal horizontal-branch morphology and supporting its interpretation as a dwarf-galaxy relic.

quotes.quote: We report on the detection of a population of Red Clump (RC) stars probably associated with the recently discovered stellar system Bo otes III. The RC is identified as a 3 peak in the Luminosity Function (LF) of colour-selected stars extracted from the SDSS database. The peak is consistently detected in the g,,i and z LFs at the expected luminosity of a typical RC at the distance of Bo otes III. Moreover the stars around the LF peak show a maximum of surface density nearly coincident with the reported center of the system.

The colour of the putative RC of Boo III ( g-=0.37 ) is much bluer than that of M71 ( g-=0.62 and [ Fe/H ] =-0.73 ), and even bluer than that of Pal 4 ( g-=0.48 and [ Fe/H ] =-1.48 ), suggesting that these stars belongs to a population more metal-poor than [ Fe/H ] 1.5 , in agreement with the independent results by G09. If Boo III is a new stellar system and the LF peak detected here is indeed its RC population, we are facing an old metal-poor system that displays a complex (bi-modal?) HB morphology, having both a BHB and a RC.

With these tools in hand we can attempt a simultaneous fit of the RC and BHB of Boo III. In case of success this will lend strong support to the association of the two features and, in turn, to the interpretation of Boo III as a dwarf galaxy (or relic of).

sources.summary: The paper presents Bo o tes III/BooIII as a dwarf-satellite stellar overdensity with measured member velocities and metallicities, arguing that its high dispersion, radial orbit, and morphology indicate a dark-matter-rich system dissolving into a tidal stream.

quotes.quote: We report the results of a spectroscopic study of the Bo o tes III (BooIII) stellar overdensity carried out with the Hectospec multifiber spectrograph on the MMT telescope. Radial velocities have been measured for 193 BooIII candidate stars selected to have magnitudes and colors consistent with its upper main sequence and lower red giant branch, as well as a number of horizontal branch candidates. From 20 identified candidate BooIII members, we measure a systemic velocity of V_ =197.5 3.8 km s ^ 1 and a velocity dispersion of _ o =14.0 3.2 km s ^ 1 . We use the somewhat large velocity dispersion and the implied highly radial orbit, along with morphological evidence from Grillmair2009 and stellar abundances, to argue that BooIII is likely the first known object observed in a transitional state between being a bound dwarf galaxy and a completely unbound tidal stream.

Despite the seemingly ``normal" mass derived for BooIII we argue that a number of pieces of evidence support the idea that BooIII is not in dynamical equilibrium and it is likely in a transitional state between a bound system and a completely unbound stellar stream -- similar to the objects described by Kroupa1997 but with a high dark matter content. As we have already suggested, the fact that BooIII has the highest measured line-of-sight velocity dispersion of any dSph is consistent with the observation (from, e.g., Munoz2008 ) that a satellite in the throes of complete tidal disruption can have its velocity dispersion inflated by more than a factor of 2. Tidal disruption seems quite plausible, since the large measured Galactocentric velocity for BooIII places it in a highly elongated, and thus potentially destructive, orbit. Morphological evidence further supports the suggestion that Bo o tes III is a dissolving dwarf galaxy in the final stages of tidal destruction.

sources.summary: Bo otes 3 is a diffuse, large UFD candidate associated with the Styx stream; its eccentric, small-pericenter orbit supports a disrupted-dwarf interpretation.

quotes.quote: Bo otes 3 Boo3 was first identified in SDSS by grillmair2009 ; yet since its discovery, it has remained relatively unstudied. Compared to other UFDs, it is very typical in absolute magnitude ( M_ V 5.75 mag; correnti2009 ) and luminosity ( L_ V 1.74 10 ^ 4 L _ ). However, it is a particularly diffuse and large ( r_ h = 33.03 = 0.56 ^ ; moskowitz2020 ) system. At a distance of 46 kpc ( carlin2018 ), its physical half-light radius ( 450 pc) is relatively large for a UFD. Boo3's faint luminosity and diffuse nature in part explains the lack of substantial literature on this dwarf.

This requires the assumption of either prograde or retrograde motion for the stream, but recent proper motion estimates of Boo3 in pace2022 and battaglia2022 would be consistent with Styx if the stream is in a retrograde orbit, providing further evidence that Boo3 may be the progenitor of Styx. The updated orbit of Boo3 provided by pace2022 and battaglia2022 also strongly supports a disrupted dwarf. Both studies find Boo3's orbit to be highly eccentric ( 0.85 and 0.95, respectively) and to have a small pericenter distance (7 9 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper describes Bootes III as a likely disrupting large UFD, finds 32 candidate RRL around it, and notes possible Sgr stream and halo contamination.

quotes.quote: Bootes III is a large UFD, which is very likely in the process of being disrupted carlin09,carlin18 . It was discovered by grillmair09 as a strong overdensity in SDSS data. The same paper reports a stellar stream, Styx, which passes through Bootes III and has a similar heliocentric distance (Fig. fig:styx ), making a relationship between both structures very likely. Indications that the galaxy is being disrupted are its large size R_h = 33 03 , moskowitz20 , large velocity dispersion, and an orbit that brings Bootes III as close as 7.8 - 12 kpc from the Galactic center at pericenter carlin18,pace22 . vivas20 identified seven RRL in Bootes III based on Gaia DR2 in an area of 2 around the galaxy, or about 4 R_h .

Here we explored a much larger area around the galaxy, finding 32 RRL likely associated with Bootes III since they share the same magnitude (as can be seen in the upper right panel of Fig. fig:GvsBPRP ) and proper motions as the galaxy. They spread however in an area as large as the explored region, with quite a uniform distribution (as shown in the upper right panel of Fig. fig:SpatialProjection ). In particular, the selected RRL stars do not seem to trace the Styx stream, suggesting Styx is not responsible for many of these RRL.

sources.summary: The paper estimates Bo o tes III proper motion from Gaia DR2, separates it kinematically from nearby NGC 5466, and finds agreement with a proprietary radial-velocity membership study.

quotes.quote: We present mean absolute proper motion measurements for seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies orbiting the Milky Way, namely Bo o tes III, Carina II, Grus II, Reticulum II, Sagittarius II, Segue 2 and Tucana IV. For four of these dwarfs our proper motion estimate is the first ever provided. The adopted astrometric data come from the second data release of the Gaia mission. We determine the mean proper motion for each galaxy starting from an initial guess of likely members, based either on radial velocity measurements or using stars on the Horizontal Branch identified in the Gaia ( G_ BP G_ RP , G) colour-magnitude diagram in the field of view towards the UFD.

It is interesting to note that in the case of Bo o tes III, a third concentration of stars clearly stands out at ( _ , _ ) (-5, -1) : it is made up of stars belonging to the Galactic globular cluster NGC 5466, located very close to the galaxy also on the sky. Their quite different distances from the Sun (with the UFD 30 kpc farther away, ferraro99 ) and kinematics, with Bo o tes III having ( _ , _ ) = ( 1.21 0.13 , 0.92 0.17 ) , corresponding to a difference in velocity of about 100 km s ^ 1 , implies that the two systems are not associated to each other.

sources.summary: The paper treats Boo III as a disrupting UFD, recognizes seven coherent Gaia RR Lyrae, and notes two new variables beyond its tidal radius.

quotes.quote: Boo III is a UFD ( M_V = -5.8 mag) discovered in SDSS by grillmair09 . Boo III is a disrupted dwarf galaxy and could be the progenitor of the Styx stream grillmair09 . Its large velocity dispersion together with its morphological parameters and orbit suggest that Boo III is a UFD nearing complete destruction carlin09 . Its half-light radius is large, 1 . The search in this galaxy was done within a 2 radius from the center of Boo III. sesar14 detected one RRL belonging to Boo III. With Gaia DR2, we recognized seven RRLs (Figure fig:BooIII ), including the one previously known, within our search area around Boo III, with coherent proper motions and similar magnitudes. Boo III has then four , and three . The mean periods of the is 0.65 d, which agrees with being a Oo II system. In Table tab:RRL we renamed the star discovered by sesar14 as V1, and assigned V2 to V7 to the new detections. Two of the new RRLs are located beyond the tidal radius of Boo III, which is not surprising since it is known this galaxy is disrupting.

sources.summary: Bootes III is clearly detected in astrometric, photometric, and spatial observables; its lopsided distribution, tiny pericenter, and PM agree with tidal disruption expectations.

quotes.quote: Bootes III The system is clearly detected in the distribution of P>0.5 stars in the three observables (PMs, CMD< location on the sky). This strongly argues in favour of the actual existence of the system. The resulting spatial distribution is clearly lopsided with respect to the East-West axis, probably a result of tidal disruption, given the very small pericentric distance (7-9 kpc) found in both the potentials explored in this work (see Tab. tab:table_param ) and the work by Carlin_18 based on Gaia DR2. Our PM determination is in excellent agreement with that by those authors and with the predictions for the retrograde orbit of the Styx stream.

sources.summary: The paper notes BoöIII has signs of tidal disruption and relatively low average density, helping explain its lower spectroscopic-member recovery rate.

quotes.quote: The number of spectroscopic members retrieved by TucIII, BoöIII, TucIV and BoöI are relatively lesser (less than 85 ) compared to other satellite galaxies. In the case of TucIII, the core and tidal tails of TucIII contain a total of 48 spectroscopic members (36 present in Gaia - DECam catalogs). However, the implementation of DBSCAN (HDBSCAN) has successfully identified only 18 (26) of these members. TucIII has extended tidal tails, and we find members extending till the edge of the field that is considered using HDBSCAN . A larger area may yield better results through better characterization of low-density spatial frequencies. However, when utilizing DBSCAN, it is evident that the algorithm still needs to capture stars within the tidal tails. Additionally, our experiments with varying hyperparameters in DBSCAN ( lmtt eps and lmtt min samples ) resulted in a significant number of non-members, likely due to the density of TucIII being lower than the average density of the MW Pace_2022 . The elevated velocity dispersion, small pericenter, and the potential association with the Styx stream (agreement with the predicted retrograde motion of the coincident Styx stellar stream; ), BoöIII has been suggested to be experiencing tidal disruption Carlin_2009 . The lower member recovery rates in BoöIII could be attributed to its relatively low average density Carlin_2018 , pointing to its status as a potential tidally disrupting galaxy candidate.

gmw_00002211.name: Bootes IV
gmw_00002211.overview: Bootes IV is an ultra-faint dwarf satellite galaxy located at a distance of approximately 209 kpc [arXiv:1906.07332]. It features a highly elongated stellar structure with an ellipticity of about 0.6 [arXiv:2106.08819] and contains an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1906.07332].

sources.summary: The paper reports Bo otes IV as a new HSC-discovered ultra-faint dwarf satellite, measuring its distance, luminosity, half-light radius, ellipticity, and consistency with dwarf-galaxy size-brightness relations.

quotes.quote: This satellite, named Bo otes IV, is the third ultra-faint dwarf that we have discovered in the HSC-SSP. We have identified a statistically significant (32.3 ) overdensity of stars having characteristics of a metal-poor, old stellar population. The distance to this stellar system is D_ =209^ +20 _ 18 kpc with a V band absolute magnitude of M_V=-4.53^ +0.23 _ 0.21 mag. Bo otes IV has a half-light radius of r_h=462^ +98 _ 84 pc and an ellipticity of 0.64^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , which clearly suggests that this is a dwarf satellite galaxy.

The first high signal is found at ( _ 2000 , _ 2000 ) = (15^ h 34^ m 45^ s .36, +43^ 43'33''.6) in the direction of Bo otes ( S=32.3 with n =1.48 , S/S_ th =4.09 ) and the second one is found at ( _ 2000 , _ 2000 ) = (22^ h 17^ m 14^ s .16, +03^ 28'48''.0) ( S=31.8 with n =1.60 , S/S_ th =4.20 ). As explained below, the former is a dwarf galaxy candidate, hereafter called Bo otes IV, and the latter appears to be a compact globular cluster, hereafter called HSC 1.

Based on a maximum likelihood analysis, the half-light radius of Bo otes IV is obtained as r_h = 462^ +98 _ 84 pc and its V-band absolute magnitude is M_V = -4.53^ +0.23 _ 0.21 mag. This follows the size-brightness relation for other MW dwarf satellites, suggesting that it is a dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: Bootes IV has only five faint probable members; its proper-motion clump is uncertain, and the paper notes its highly elongated stellar structure.

quotes.quote: Bootes IV Our routine returns only 5 members with P >0.5 and all very faint. The distribution in PM has 3 stars clumping at _ around 2mas yr ^ 1 and the other 2 stars are found at at 0 mas yr ^ 1 and -2 mas yr ^ 1 . The error-bars in this component of the systemic PM are such that within 2 the clump at 2 mas yr ^ 1 would be included. It is one of the objects with the most elongated stellar structure, with an ellipticity 0.6.

gmw_00002212.name: Canes Venatici I
gmw_00002212.overview: Canes Venatici I is a dwarf galaxy that may have passed deep into the Milky Way halo [arXiv:2106.08819]. The spatial distribution of its probable members shows a possible elongation on the northwest side [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: Canes Venatici I shows a possible northwest elongation, may have passed deep into the Milky Way halo, and has orbital parameters matching dark-matter-free models.

quotes.quote: Canes Venatici I There seems to be a possible elongation on the N-W side in the spatial distribution of probable members. The pericentric distance could reach 20-30 kpc within the 1- uncertainties in both isolated potentials explored; while the results from the perturbed potential tend to suggest larger pericentric distances within the 1- confidence interval, we find that 5 out of the 100 realizations have pericenters below 20kpc, hence the results from the perturbed potential do not necessarily go against the possibility of Canes Venatici I having reached quite inward into the MW halo. Matus-Carillo_20 model Canes Venatici I as a DM free object and look for orbits that match several of its photometric and kinematic properties, including its ellipticity, half-light radius, position angle, velocity dispersion. Both their PM predictions and the orbital parameters are in line with our values.

gmw_00002213.name: Carina
gmw_00002213.overview: The Carina dwarf galaxy features an outer elongation in the spatial distribution of its member stars [arXiv:2106.08819]. Although it is unlikely to have experienced a close interaction with the Milky Way, it is possible that Carina was historically linked to the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: Carina member stars show an outer elongation consistent with earlier work; contamination is unlikely, and the galaxy may have been linked to the LMC.

quotes.quote: Carina The spatial distribution of the high probability member stars returned by our routine shows an elongation in the outer parts (Fig. fig:out_1 ), compatible with what seen in previous studies of the spatial distribution of Carina stars, based on red giant branch stars observed spectroscopically Munoz_06 and deep wide-area photometry Battaglia_12, McMonigal_14 . Even if there are some intervening LMC stars in the Carina's line-of-sight, it is unlikely the feature is due to that, given that these would be included in our contamination model. Given the orbital parameters that we obtain, it since very unlikely this might be the result of a close interaction with the MW, nor with the LMC (see e.g. Fig. fig:orbits_lmc_1 ) as suggested by Fritz_18 ). It is possible that this galaxy was linked to the LMC.

gmw_00002214.name: Draco II
gmw_00002214.overview: Draco II is a dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 24 kiloparsecs from the Milky Way [arXiv:2106.08819]. Its probable member stars are well clumped, though the galaxy exhibits an asymmetric spatial distribution and hints of tidal extension [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: Draco II has well-clumped probable members but an asymmetric spatial distribution and reported tidal extension; its present 24 kpc distance implies strong tidal forces.

quotes.quote: Draco II The distribution of probable member stars is reasonably well clumped in all properties. The spatial distribution seems asymmetric, although we have not verified whether this is statistically significant. Longeard_18a find hints of tidal extension along the major axis. Since this system galaxy is currently at 24 kpc from the MW, it can be presently experiencing significant tidal forces, independent of its past orbit.

gmw_00002215.name: Fornax
gmw_00002215.overview: Fornax is a nearby Local Group dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:0805.1054, arXiv:2106.08819]. Dynamical modeling of its stellar component and globular cluster system suggests that its dark matter halo contains a density core [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Fornax as an XMM-Newton-observed local dwarf spheroidal dark-matter target, finding no significant diffuse X-ray excess after matched blank-field background processing.

quotes.quote: Of the nearby Local Group dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies, three have observations in the XMM-Newton archive: Ursa Minor observed for 52 ks between August and October 2005 (ObsIDs 0301690201, 0301690301, 0301690401, 0301690501), Fornax observed for 104 ks in August 2005 (ObsID 0302500101), and Carina observed for 42 ks in May 2004 (ObsID 0200500201).
All of the XMM observations were taken in Full Frame mode; for Ursa Minor and Carina the thin optical blocking filter was used, while for Fornax the medium filter was used.

Finally, we re-project the appropriate blank-field event files (medium filter for Fornax and thin filter for Ursa Minor and Carina) to match the sky position of the dwarf galaxy observations using the routine skycast. Renormalizing the blank-field count rate in the source region (i.e. within =6^ excluding point source regions) using the ratio of the count rate in the blank-fields to that in the dwarf galaxy observations in an outer region of the detector (where the source flux is expected to be much lower), we find that none of the three dwarfs show significant diffuse X-ray emission above what is expected from the X-ray background.

sources.summary: Fornax is discussed as a cored-halo dwarf whose derived pericenters allow tidal mass loss to reduce peak circular velocity and match half-light-radius kinematics.

quotes.quote: Fornax is another interesting object, as dynamical modelling of the kinematic properties of its stellar component WP_11, Amorisco_13, Pascale_18 as well as considerations and modelling of its system of globular clusters e.g. Leung_20 suggest its DM halo to have a density core; this has sometimes been attributed to DM being heated up by stellar feedback e.g. Read_19 and more recently to the possibility of significant mass loss due to tides Genina_20 . The range of pericentric distances determined here (see Tab. tab:table_param ) is rather similar to that of the GDR2 determinations in F18 for the isolated potentials, probably because in the case of Fornax the uncertainty in the distance measurement plays also a role Borukhovetskaya_21 ; also the values themselves are very similar. The 1- range for pericenter in the perturbed potential (66-124kpc) is in agreement with those given by the isolated potentials. These include orbital trajectories that can significantly reduce the peak circular velocity of the DM halo due to tidal mass loss and reconcile it with the kinematic properties measured at the half-light radius Genina_20, Borukhovetskaya_21 .

gmw_00002216.name: Horologium II
gmw_00002216.overview: Horologium II is a dwarf galaxy characterized by a highly elongated stellar structure [arXiv:2106.08819]. It exhibits an ellipticity of 0.7 and features a sparse distribution of member stars [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: Horologium II is treated as a tricky, highly elongated system with sparse member distribution and only slightly improved systemic proper-motion uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Horologium II It is one of the objects with the most elongated stellar structure, with an ellipticity 0.7. Sparse distribution of members on the PM plane. All of them are outside the 1 rh ellipse. Compared to Fritz_19 , the uncertainties are only slightly reduced and the value of the new systemic PM is between the two GDR2-based options. This system remains still tricky.

gmw_00002217.name: Leo T
gmw_00002217.overview: Leo T is a faint dwarf galaxy located within the Local Group [arXiv:2106.08819]. It contains eight probable stellar members, though its large transverse-velocity error makes current orbital parameter determinations unreliable [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: Leo T has eight faint probable members; improved proper-motion errors still cannot rule out backsplash origin, and its large transverse-velocity error makes orbits unreliable.

quotes.quote: Leo T The routine returns 8 members with P >0.5 and all very faint. Our statistical PM errors improve compared to McConnachie_SOLO , but they are still too large to exclude a backsplash origin for Leo T (see their Fig. 4). The error in the transverse velocity is extremely large, 660 km s ^ 1 , hence the current determination of orbital parameters cannot be considered robust. Nonetheless, it is unlikely that such a faint galaxy might have hold on to its gas if entering the MW halo.

gmw_00002218.name: Reticulum II
gmw_00002218.overview: Reticulum II is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy with a highly elongated stellar structure [arXiv:2106.08819, arXiv:2207.03499] and was likely a satellite of the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2106.08819]. Its stellar population shows homogeneous metal mixing, providing evidence of bursty early star formation [arXiv:2207.03499].

sources.summary: The paper studies Reticulum II as an r-process-enhanced ultra-faint dwarf, using member-star abundances to infer homogeneous metal mixing and bursty early star formation.

quotes.quote: The ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum II was enriched by a single rare and prolific process event. The process content of Reticulum II thus provides a unique opportunity to study metal mixing in a relic first galaxy. Using multi-object high-resolution spectroscopy with VLT/GIRAFFE and Magellan/M2FS, we identify 32 clear spectroscopic member stars and measure abundances of Mg, Ca, Fe, and Ba where possible.

We find 72^ +10 _ 12 of the stars are process-enhanced, with a mean [ Ba/H ] =-1.68   0.07 and unresolved intrinsic dispersion _ Ba/H < 0.20 . The homogeneous process abundances imply that Ret II's metals are well-mixed by the time the enhanced stars form, which simulations have shown requires at least 100 Myr of metal mixing in between bursts of star formation to homogenize. This is the first direct evidence of bursty star formation in an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: Reticulum II is described as elongated, possibly lopsided in its member-star distribution, and highly likely to have belonged to the LMC satellite cohort.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II It is one of the objects with the most elongated stellar structure, with an ellipticity 0.6. Possibly lopsided spatial distribution of member stars. Highly likely to have been part of the cohort of LMC satellites.

gmw_00002219.name: Reticulum III
gmw_00002219.overview: Reticulum III is a distant dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:1805.07350, arXiv:2001.01107] whose large size and lack of mass segregation disfavor classification as a star cluster [arXiv:1806.02345, arXiv:2112.04689]. The system is very metal-poor [arXiv:1805.07350] and is generally considered to be gravitationally bound to the Milky Way [arXiv:2106.08819].

sources.summary: The paper finds clumped Reticulum III proper-motion members, infers a possibly more metal-rich population, and argues its size/luminosity disfavors a star cluster.

quotes.quote: Reticulum III has 5 members with high probability and 1 member with medium probability (which is also a BHB star). Interestingly, all 5 high probability members lie below the stellar locus. As these 5 stars are clumped in proper motion space, it is unambiguous that these stars are Reticulum III members. The location in color-color space indicates that it might have a relatively more more-metal rich population. Based on its size r_ 1/2 60 pc and luminosity it is not expected to be a star cluster Drlica-Wagner2015ApJ...809L...4D .

sources.summary: Reticulum III is treated as an ultra-faint candidate with a formal metallicity dispersion, uncertain galaxy status, and orbital constraints that do not firmly exclude Magellanic association.

quotes.quote: Here we aim at deriving the bulk motions and estimates of the internal velocity dispersion and metallicity properties in four recently discovered distant southern dwarf galaxy candidates, Columba I, Reticulum III, Phoenix II and Horologium II. We combine Gaia DR2 astrometric measurements, photometry and new FLAMES/GIRAFFE intermediate resolution spectroscopic data in the region of the near-IR Ca II triplet lines; such combination is essential for finding potential member stars in these low luminosity systems. We find very likely member stars in all four satellites and are able to determine (or place limits on) the systems bulk motions and average internal properties. The systems are found to be very metal-poor, in agreement with dwarf galaxies and dwarf galaxy candidates of similar luminosity.
For Ret III we measure a formally robust metallicity dispersion of 0.35 dex, but since the stars are rather faint, we do not consider it as conclusive to classify the system as a galaxy.

Ret III matches the predictions by Jethwa_16 in all quantities within 1-2 both for an association to the LMC or to the SMC. It also agrees well with the model in K18 in _ and v_ helio , but not in _ * .

sources.summary: Reticulum III has unconvincing member distributions and large PM uncertainty, but the authors find their measurement and most literature values imply it remains bound.

quotes.quote: Reticulum III The distribution of P>0.5 stars on the sky, PM and CM-plane does not appear overly convincing. The different EDR3 estimates error bars overlap only partly, overall the error is still large in km s ^ 1 . The determination by Fritz_19 is just compatible with ours, given the large error-bars in both cases. While with Pace_19 systemic PM, Reticulum III would be unbound to the MW even for a massive (1.6 10 ^ 12 M _ ) MW DM halo, the other measurements in the literature, and ours, suggest it is bound (see Fig. fig:pms_lit4 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes Ret III as a distant DES galaxy with one faint RR Lyrae candidate far outside its tidal radius, requiring further confirmation.

quotes.quote: Ret III is another distant DES galaxy drlica15 . Similar to Eri III, we found also one RRLs, a type ab in this case, at 50 from the center of the galaxy. This star is also located well outside the tidal radius of Ret III, but being also faint, G=20.1 , it would also be a rare chance if it is a field Halo star. The lightcurve is also noisy since this star is in the faint end for Gaia, requiring further confirmation.

sources.summary: The paper classifies Reticulum III as a dwarf galaxy because it lacks velocity or metallicity classification data but has no mass segregation and a large size.

quotes.quote: No velocity or metallicity data is available for Reticulum III which would allow a classification of the system. We obtain a mass segregation ratio of R_ H,Bright /R_ H,Faint = 1.20 0.10 , which is compatible with no mass segregation. Given the lar lack of mass segregation and the large size of the system, we conclude that Reticulum III is a dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00002220.name: Tucana V
gmw_00002220.overview: Tucana V is an ultra-faint Milky Way satellite with an uncertain classification as a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1911.08493]. It has a systemic velocity of -36.2 km/s and is likely approaching the Milky Way for the first time [arXiv:1911.08493].

sources.summary: The paper measures Tucana V spectroscopy and orbit, finding low metallicity, no useful dispersion limit, likely first infall, and an uncertain dwarf-galaxy nature.

quotes.quote: We present Magellan/IMACS spectroscopy of three recently discovered ultra-faint Milky Way satellites, Grus II, Tucana IV, and Tucana V. We measure systemic velocities of v_ hel = -110.0 0.5 , v_ hel = 15.9^ +1.8 _ 1.7 , and v_ hel = -36.2^ +2.5 _ 2.2 for the three objects, respectively. Their large relative velocities demonstrate that the satellites are unrelated despite their close physical proximity. We determine a velocity dispersion for Tuc IV of = 4.3^ +1.7 _ 1.0 , but we cannot resolve the velocity dispersions of the other two systems. For Gru II we place an upper limit (90 \ confidence) on the dispersion of < 1.9 , and for Tuc V we do not obtain any useful limits.
Tuc V likely has an apocenter beyond 100 kpc, and could be approaching the Milky Way for the first time.
Based on their sizes, masses, and metallicities, we classify Gru II and Tuc IV as likely dwarf galaxies, but the nature of Tuc V remains uncertain.

sources.summary: Tucana V has three probable members with offset bright stars; its baseline PM PDF is problematic but improves strongly when spectroscopic information is included.

quotes.quote: Tucana V Our routine returns three members with P >0.5 , of various magnitudes, but the brightest ones are quite offset from the centre. Problematic PDF, with strong lopsideness and/or very extended wings of high amplitude when not including the spectroscopic information. The situation improves very significantly when including the spectroscopic information.

gmw_00002221.name: DLA galaxy
gmw_00002221.overview: The DLA galaxy is a compact, gas-rich starburst galaxy at a redshift of 2.207 [arXiv:1202.0280, arXiv:1401.5073]. It exhibits a high star formation rate that powers starburst-driven outflows [arXiv:1202.0280], and its gravitational lensing effect magnifies a background quasar [arXiv:1401.5073].

sources.summary: The paper presents a compact gas-rich z=2.207 DLA galaxy with small impact parameter, high star formation rate, extended double-peaked emission, low mass, and evidence for starburst-driven outflows.

quotes.quote: The [ ] H emitting region has a very small impact parameter with respect to the QSO line of sight, b 0.1 \ (0.9 kpc proper distance), and is unresolved. From the \ line, we measure a significant star formation rate SFR 25 M _ yr ^ 1 (uncorrected for dust). The shape of the \ line is double-peaked, which is the signature of resonant scattering of \ photons, and the \ emission is spatially extended. More strikingly, the blue and red \ peaks arise from distinct regions extended over a few kpc on either side of the star-forming region.

The presence of starburst-driven outflows is also in agreement with the large SFR together with a small size and low mass of the galaxy ( M_ vir 10^ 10 M _ ). From putting constraints on the stellar UV continuum luminosity of the galaxy, we estimate an age of at most a few 10^7 yr, again consistent with a recent starburst scenario. We interpret the data as the observation of a young, gas rich, compact starburst galaxy, from which material is expelled through collimated winds powered by the vigorous star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J113520.39 001053.5 as a z=2.888 QSO magnified by a foreground DLA, with red-band photometry about 2.2 magnitudes brighter than the median comparable QSO.

quotes.quote: We present here the extraordinary case of the QSO SDSS J113520.39 001053.5 (hereafter J1135 0010; see Fig. fig1 and Table tab1 ), at redshift 2.888, that is magnified by the gravitational lensing effect produced by a foreground DLA, at redshift 2.207 ( not12 ; kul12 ; see also the clear absorption feature at 3900 in Fig. fig1 ).

We find that approximately 19 and 5 of all QSOs are brighter than J1135 0010 in the u and g bands, respectively, but only less than 2 at longer wavelengths, i.e., in the , i , and z bands. This matches very well with the picture that J1135 0010 is substantially magnified by the DLA galaxy at redshift 2.207. In fact, although J1135 0010 is relatively brighter than most QSOs in the same redshift range in the u and g bands, here the absorption of the DLA around 3900 , visible in Fig. fig1 and affecting the magnitudes measured in these two filters, makes the lensing magnification effect less evident. In the , i , and z bands instead, the unabsorbed continuum of J1135 0010 is measured more accurately and its intensity appears significantly higher than that of the average QSO. More quantitatively, from Table tab2 (third row) we see that in the , i , and z filters J1135 0010 is approximately 2.2 mag brighter (i.e., 8 times more luminous) than the median QSO in the sample.

gmw_00002222.name: G0248+430
gmw_00002222.overview: G0248+430 is a highly star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0519 [arXiv:1806.00172]. A tidal tail emanating from the galaxy produces OH absorption toward the background quasar Q0248+430 [arXiv:1806.00172].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G0248+430 as a highly star-forming z=0.0519 galaxy whose tidal tail produces OH absorption toward Q0248+430, yielding diffuse-gas column density and fundamental-constant constraints.

quotes.quote: The absorbing gas detected towards Q0248+430 ( z_q =1.313) originates from a tidal tail emanating from a highly star forming galaxy G0248+430 ( z_g =0.0519) at an impact parameter of 15 kpc. The measured column density is N (OH) = (6.3 0.8) 10 ^ 13 ( T_ ex 3.5 )( 1.0 f_c^ OH ) , where f_c^ OH and T_ ex are the covering factor and the excitation temperature of the absorbing gas, respectively.

Using \ 21-cm and OH 1667 MHz absorption lines detected towards Q0248+430, we estimate ( F/F ) = (5.2 4.5) 10^ 6 , where F g_p ( ^2/ )^ 1.57 , the fine structure constant, the electron-proton mass ratio and g_p the proton gyromagnetic ratio.

gmw_00002223.name: JO85
gmw_00002223.overview: JO85 is a galaxy hosting a LINER-like nucleus [arXiv:2208.11955] that is experiencing a mostly face-on ram-pressure stripping interaction [arXiv:2010.09733]. This interaction has produced a long tail of stripped gas [arXiv:2006.16032] and unwound spiral arms with increased pitch angles [arXiv:2010.09733].

sources.summary: JO85 is used as an archetypal face-on stripped galaxy, with gas kinematics bent at the disk edges and outer tails with increased pitch angles.

quotes.quote: In the ionised gas of JO85, the kinematics are similar to the central region of the stellar disc, and significantly different around the edges. Whilst the stellar disc velocity contours are smoother and straighter at the northern and southern edges of the disc, the gas kinematics contours bend up to the right at the northern edge of the disc. This is likely to result from JO85's mostly face-on interaction with the ICM (see section sec:orbits ), pushing the gas around the edges of the disc to higher line-of-sight velocities.
In the case of JO85, the spiral arms have a mean pitch angle of 17.3^ in the disc, which increases to around 23.0^ in the unwound tails.

The velocity pattern in JO85 shows similarities to the face-on stripping simulated galaxy, particularly in the curvature of the zero contour where the top end bends to the right as the stripping begins to skew the velocities at the edge of the disc. The similarities suggest that the JO85 is experiencing close to face-on motion, but differences and asymmetries suggest that some component of edge-on motion is also present.

sources.summary: JO85 is hosted by one of the most massive clusters, belongs to seven massive systems in exceptional ram-pressure conditions, and has a long stripped-gas tail whose projected velocity dominates.

quotes.quote: Let us consider the 7 massive galaxies with very high values. JW100 is the most massive galaxy in the GASP sample and it is hosted in a relatively low-mass cluster, but it is moving at very high speed and it is very close to the cluster centre, where the ICM is denser. JO85 and JO171 are hosted in two of the most massive clusters ( >1000 ), while JO201 is moving at extremely high speed in the ICM of a relatively massive cluster. Finally, JO204, JO206 and JW39 are very close to the centre of their host cluster centre and moving at quite high speed.

All these seven galaxies are in exceptional conditions regarding at least one the main parameters regulating the ram-pressure intensity. We note that, besides JO201, all other six galaxies have a tail of stripped gas with a long extension on the plane of the sky, indicating that the component on the plane of the sky of the velocity of the galaxies in the ICM is dominant with respect to the one along the line of sight; the measured radial velocity is therefore a lower limit of their actual speed. Five of these galaxies are studied in detail in dedicated papers ( JW100, pogg+jw100 ; JO171, gaspV ; JO201, gaspII,gaspXV ; JO204, gaspIV ; JO206, gaspI ).

sources.summary: The paper describes JO85 as a LINER-like GASP galaxy that is the exception to disk radio truncation in the sample and lacks radio-devoid regions inside its stellar disk.

quotes.quote: Finally, the sample also presents a high incidence of AGN with 5 out of 6 galaxies showing nuclear activity according to the Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich diagnostic diagrams BPT, Baldwin1981 : JW39, JO85, JO49 Peluso_2021 host LINER-like nuclei, while JW100 and JO206 have a Seyfert nucleus Poggianti2017b, Radovich2019 .

The new LOFAR images reveal that in the stellar disk of these galaxies, with the only exception of JO85, the radio emission is truncated with respect to the stellar emission (i.e., the radio emission is less extended than the stellar one, leaving radio-devoid regions at the outer edges of the stellar disk).
At odds with the other galaxies, JO85 shows no radio-devoid regions within its stellar disk, although they are present on its eastern edge.

gmw_00002224.name: JO171
gmw_00002224.overview: JO171 is a massive galaxy located within a highly massive galaxy cluster [arXiv:2006.16032]. The galaxy features a long, ram-pressure stripped tail of gas where star formation is the predominant ionization mechanism [arXiv:2006.16032, arXiv:1811.00823], accounting for 10 to 20 percent of the system's total star formation rate [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: JO171 is hosted by one of the most massive clusters, belongs to the seven exceptional ram-pressure systems, has a long stripped-gas tail, and is cited as studied in a dedicated GASP paper.

quotes.quote: Let us consider the 7 massive galaxies with very high values. JW100 is the most massive galaxy in the GASP sample and it is hosted in a relatively low-mass cluster, but it is moving at very high speed and it is very close to the cluster centre, where the ICM is denser. JO85 and JO171 are hosted in two of the most massive clusters ( >1000 ), while JO201 is moving at extremely high speed in the ICM of a relatively massive cluster. Finally, JO204, JO206 and JW39 are very close to the centre of their host cluster centre and moving at quite high speed.

All these seven galaxies are in exceptional conditions regarding at least one the main parameters regulating the ram-pressure intensity. We note that, besides JO201, all other six galaxies have a tail of stripped gas with a long extension on the plane of the sky, indicating that the component on the plane of the sky of the velocity of the galaxies in the ICM is dominant with respect to the one along the line of sight; the measured radial velocity is therefore a lower limit of their actual speed. Five of these galaxies are studied in detail in dedicated papers ( JW100, pogg+jw100 ; JO171, gaspV ; JO201, gaspII,gaspXV ; JO204, gaspIV ; JO206, gaspI ).

sources.summary: JO171's tail is classified as star-formation dominated in the main diagnostics, with the pure-SF component dominating the tail and 10-20% of total SFR occurring in the long tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). The SFR occurring in the tail represents 10 to 20 of the total in 6 of our galaxies (JO206, JW39, JO204, JO201, JW100 and JO171), which are those with the longest tails, as it is reasonable to expect.

gmw_00002225.name: JW39
gmw_00002225.overview: JW39 is a jellyfish galaxy with a LINER-like nucleus [arXiv:2208.11955] that is undergoing advanced ram pressure stripping near its cluster center [arXiv:2006.16032, arXiv:2208.11955]. It features a long, star-formation-dominated tail [arXiv:1811.00823, arXiv:2006.16032] with systematically low gas metallicities that decrease along the stripping direction [arXiv:2111.04755].

sources.summary: The paper maps JW39 as a jellyfish galaxy with systematically low tail metallicities; metallicity declines outward along sub-tails, and 30-40% of the most metal-poor clump gas may come from the ICM.

quotes.quote: The panels (a) of Figs. fig:JW100 , fig:JO206 , and fig:JW39 clearly show that the gas metallicities in the tails of all galaxies are systematically lower than those in the galaxy disks. These trends are better identified looking at the panels ( _ i ), where we present the distribution of the metallicities of the clumps in each selected sub-tail against their position along the tail. As a reference of the metallicity in the disk, for each galaxy we indicate (with a horizontal solid line) the value at the effective radius taken from franchetto2020 . In most cases, metallicities in the tails are much lower than this value. Furthermore, in all sub-tails we detect a decrease of the gas metallicity moving along the stripping direction; the decrease varies between 0.1 and 0.4 dex, with the lowest metallicities mainly located in the furthest regions.

For JW100 and JW39 we derive that 30 - 40 of the most metal-poor clumps could be constituted by gas from the ICM; for JO206 the mixing can reach values as high as 60 ; while, for JO201 the effect is evaluated up to the 80 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies JW39 as an advanced-stripping jellyfish galaxy with a LINER-like nucleus, truncated radio and H emission, radio-devoid edges, and extraplanar radio tails.

quotes.quote: According to the SFR-stellar mass relation Vulcani_2018c , half of the sample (JW100, JW39 and JO49) shows a deficiency in SFR that indicates an advanced stage of stripping. Finally, the sample also presents a high incidence of AGN with 5 out of 6 galaxies showing nuclear activity according to the Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich diagnostic diagrams BPT, Baldwin1981 : JW39, JO85, JO49 Peluso_2021 host LINER-like nuclei, while JW100 and JO206 have a Seyfert nucleus Poggianti2017b, Radovich2019 .

Figure radio-halpha shows galaxies in which radio and H emission are similarly truncated with respect to the stellar disk (JW39 and JO49), or where radio emission appears to be more truncated than H (JW100 and JO206), or where only radio is truncated whilst H closely follows the stellar disk (JO60). JW39, JO49 and JO206 show radio-devoid regions located on their leading edge, that become more evident in the smoothed images (Figure smooth ).
JW39, JO60 and JW100 show unilateral, extraplanar radio emission extending for up to 40 arcsec in the direction of the stripped H tail. JW39 shows also radio-devoid edges in the opposite direction.

sources.summary: JW39's tail is star-formation dominated in the main diagnostics, with pure-SF emission dominating, a strong OI-diagram SF component, and 10-20% of total SFR occurring in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). Four of our galaxies have a particularly strong SF component according to the OI DD ( 40-60 in JO206, JO49, JW39 and JO194). The SFR occurring in the tail represents 10 to 20 of the total in 6 of our galaxies (JO206, JW39, JO204, JO201, JW100 and JO171), which are those with the longest tails, as it is reasonable to expect.

sources.summary: JW39 lies close to the center of its host cluster and moves quickly; the paper includes it among seven exceptional ram-pressure systems with long stripped-gas tails.

quotes.quote: Let us consider the 7 massive galaxies with very high values. JW100 is the most massive galaxy in the GASP sample and it is hosted in a relatively low-mass cluster, but it is moving at very high speed and it is very close to the cluster centre, where the ICM is denser. JO85 and JO171 are hosted in two of the most massive clusters ( >1000 ), while JO201 is moving at extremely high speed in the ICM of a relatively massive cluster. Finally, JO204, JO206 and JW39 are very close to the centre of their host cluster centre and moving at quite high speed.

All these seven galaxies are in exceptional conditions regarding at least one the main parameters regulating the ram-pressure intensity. We note that, besides JO201, all other six galaxies have a tail of stripped gas with a long extension on the plane of the sky, indicating that the component on the plane of the sky of the velocity of the galaxies in the ICM is dominant with respect to the one along the line of sight; the measured radial velocity is therefore a lower limit of their actual speed. Five of these galaxies are studied in detail in dedicated papers ( JW100, pogg+jw100 ; JO171, gaspV ; JO201, gaspII,gaspXV ; JO204, gaspIV ; JO206, gaspI ).

gmw_00002226.name: JO36
gmw_00002226.overview: JO36 is a ram-pressure-stripped spiral galaxy with a truncated H-alpha disc [arXiv:2006.16032, arXiv:2309.02002]. It is embedded in the northern radio plume of the giant radio galaxy GIN 049 [arXiv:2309.02002], featuring a giant 150 kpc radio tail with a very low star formation rate [arXiv:2006.16032, arXiv:2309.02002].

sources.summary: The paper presents JO36 as a ram-pressure-stripped spiral embedded in GIN 049's northern plume, with an unusually luminous 150 kpc radio tail and past mild star-formation enhancement attributed to plume interaction.

quotes.quote: We presented brand new LOFAR and uGMRT images of a peculiar interaction between two galaxies, namely JO36, a ram-pressure stripped spiral, and GIN 049, the FRI BCG of Abell 160. The new LOFAR data revealed that GIN 049 is a giant radio galaxy, with a projected physical size of, at least, 744 kpc, and that JO36 is embedded in the northern radio plume. The new radio images at 144 and 675 MHz allowed us to produce new high-resolution spectral index maps. We investigated the nature of the JO36-GIN 049 interaction and its implications by studying the properties of the galaxy and the radio plume.

JO36 has an integrated radio luminosity of 5.4 10^ 23 W Hz ^ 1 and a giant radio tail with a projected physical size of 150 kpc.
