Optical images hint at a morphology of possibly S0 or E7, with a bright nucleus. This offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 4-sigma confidence.

gmw_00002277.name: SDSS J115000.07+552821.3
gmw_00002277.overview: SDSS J115000.07+552821.3 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1801.03456]. Its host galaxy exhibits a disturbed morphology and is considered a strong candidate for hosting an offset active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper presents this Seyfert as a strong offset-AGN candidate with a 1.13 arcsec radio offset, disturbed host morphology, prior offset report, and 4-sigma deviation.

quotes.quote: SDSS J115000.07+552821.3 AGN class: Seyfert Single CLASS detection of 70.0 mJy (NVSS J115000+552821), offset by 1.13 arcsec from the SDSS position. Host galaxy shows signs of being disturbed, and appears a good candidate for an offset-AGN host. The offset of the radio source in SDSS J115000.07+552821.3 has also been noted by . This offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 4-sigma confidence.

gmw_00002278.name: SDSS J115133.06+050605.2
gmw_00002278.overview: SDSS J115133.06+050605.2 is a compact elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1801.03456] that displays a passive optical spectrum [arXiv:1801.03456]. However, its emission line strengths suggest the galaxy hosts a LINER [arXiv:1801.03456], and optical imaging reveals a neighboring object located approximately 4 arcseconds to the northeast [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper describes this compact elliptical candidate as having a 157 mas radio offset, a nearby optical object, passive SDSS spectrum, and LINER-like OSSY strengths.

quotes.quote: SDSS J115133.06+050605.2 Morphology: E, compact (NED) Single CLASS detection of 27.6 mJy (NVSS J115133+050608), offset by 157 mas from the SDSS position. Optical images show an object about 4 arcsec to the north east. SDSS Spectrum of SDSS J115133.06+050605.2 indicates a passive elliptical galaxy, although the OSSY line strengths suggest that this galaxy hosts a LINER.

gmw_00002279.name: SDSS J115410.41+122509.7
gmw_00002279.overview: SDSS J115410.41+122509.7 is a galaxy that represents a promising candidate for hosting an offset active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1801.03456]. Its optical spectrum displays strong narrow emission lines, particularly of ionized oxygen [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper marks this galaxy as a very promising offset-AGN host, based on a 221 mas radio offset and strong narrow optical lines.

quotes.quote: SDSS J115410.41+122509.7 Single CLASS detection of 187.5 mJy (NVSS J115410+122509), offset by 221 mas from the SDSS position. The spectrum shows some strong narrow lines, especially [ O ii ] . This galaxy represents a very promising candidate for an offset-AGN host.

gmw_00002280.name: SDSS J115905.67+582035.5
gmw_00002280.overview: SDSS J115905.67+582035.5 is a LINER-type radio galaxy [arXiv:2006.03717, arXiv:2305.10576] that exhibits a complex, X-shaped radio morphology with double-double lobes indicating restarted jet activity [arXiv:2305.10576, arXiv:1203.0538]. Its host galaxy displays a disturbed, post-merger structure with extended tidal tails [arXiv:2006.03717, arXiv:2305.10576].

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of CGCG 292 057, describing a LINER-type radio galaxy with merger signatures, restarted radio lobes, hot bulge gas, and an ionized iron nuclear line.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The source (J2000 position, RA = 11h59m5.7s, Decl. = +58d20 ^ 36 ^ ) was placed at the aim-point of the back-illuminated ACIS-S3 CCD with the data collected in VFAINT mode.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the new, deep (94 ksec) Chandra ACIS-S observation of radio-loud active galaxy CGCG 292 057, characterized by a LINER-type nucleus and a complex radio structure that indicates intermittent jet activity. On the scale of the host galaxy bulge, we detected excess X-ray emission with a spectrum best fit by a thermal plasma model with a temperature of 0.8 keV. We argue that this excess emission results from compression and heating of the hot diffuse fraction of the interstellar medium displaced by the expanding inner, 20 kpc-scale lobes observed in this restarted radio galaxy. The nuclear X-ray spectrum of the target clearly displays an ionized iron line at 6.7 keV, and is best fitted with a phenomenological model consisting of a power-law (photon index 1.8 ) continuum absorbed by a relatively large amount of cold matter (hydrogen column density 0.7 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ), and partly scattered (fraction 3 ) by ionized gas, giving rise to a soft excess component and K line from iron ions.

The nearby galaxy CGCG 292 057 redshift, z = 0.054 ; Stoughton02 has some unusual properties revealed in studies at radio, infrared, optical, and UV wavelengths by Koziel12 and Singh15 . It is a complex, bulge-dominated system viewed nearly face-on, for which the spiral arm tails extend up to 80 kpc, and has a bulge radius of 30 kpc. The concentration index C r_ 90 /r_ 50 =2.81 0.03 , which is the ratio of radii containing 90 and 50 of the Petrosian light, places the galaxy at the border between early-type and late-type galaxies see Strateva01,Shimasaku01 . Its disturbed optical morphology indicates that it is a relatively recent merger, and the lack of an over-density at the position of the source, as observed in the Tenth Data Release of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; Ahn14 , suggests that it is not associated with any group or cluster of galaxies Singh15 .

sources.summary: The paper studies J1159+5820 as an X-shaped, restarting radio galaxy whose CGCG 292-057 host shows post-merger tidal structure; radio spectra favor fast jet realignment over a few Myr.

quotes.quote: Here we investigate a unique radio galaxy J1159+5820, whose host CGCG 292-057 displays the optical signature of a post-merger system with a distinct tidal tail feature, and an X-shaped radio morphology accompanied by an additional pair of inner lobes. We observed the target on a wide range of radio frequencies ranging from 147 MHz to 4959 MHz, using dedicated GMRT and VLA observations, and supplemented it with publicly available survey data for broadband radio analysis. Particle injection models were fitted to radio spectra of lobes and different parts of the wings. Spectral ageing analysis performed on the lobes and the wings favors a fast jet realignment model with a reorientation timescale of a few million years.

In this paper, we examine radio data of CGCG 292-057, host of J1159+5820, which is a unique RG that presents a non-standard X-shaped radio morphology and also shows a restarting pair of radio lobes as a DDRG. The target source displays low-surface-brightness radio wings that span 7 ' and high-surface-brightness lobes that span to 5 ' . First described in the paper of , this object at RA = 11 59 05 7 , Dec = + 58 20 36 (J2000.0) with z = 0.0537 ( 2008ApJS..175..297A ) is classified as a low ionization nuclear emission line region (LINER) galaxy, based on its emission line fluxes. The host galaxy morphology was determined by the concentration index of about 2.81±0.03, which places it almost exactly at the separation line between elliptical and spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes this disturbed possible spiral as hosting a compact Chandra source and an extended radio-galaxy core with a 291 mas CLASS offset.

quotes.quote: SDSS J115905.67+582035.5 (MCG +10-17-121) Single CLASS detection of 9.5 mJy (CRATES J1159+5820), offset by 291 mas from the SDSS position. Compact Chandra source detected at the same position. Galaxy shows some evidence of being disturbed. Radio imaging of this galaxy shows that the CLASS-detected source is the core of an extended radio galaxy KozielWierzbowska2012 . SDSS image shows a disturbed, possibly spiral, structure, which is unusual for the host of a radio galaxy.
This offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 4-sigma confidence.

sources.summary: CGCG 292 057 is presented as a merger radio galaxy with X-like and double-double morphology, low-power polarized outer lobes, a LINER-type host, and double-peaked narrow emission lines.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: This galaxy is located on the sky at RA = 11 ^ h 59 ^ m 05 7, Dec = +58 20 ^ 36 (J2000.0) and lies at a redshift of z=0.054 (Stoughton et al. 2002).

quotes.quote: We announce the discovery of a unique combination of features in a radio source identified with the merger galaxy CGCG 292 057. The radio galaxy both exhibits a highly complex, X-like structure and shows signs of recurrent activity in the form of double-double morphology. The outer lobes of CGCG 292 057 are characterized by low radio power, P_ 1400MHz 2 10^ 24 W Hz^ 1 , placing this source below the FRII/FRI luminosity threshold, and are highly polarized (almost 20 per cent at 1400 MHz) as is typical of X-shaped radio sources. The host is a LINER-type galaxy with a relatively low black hole mass and double-peaked narrow emission lines. These features make this galaxy a primary target for studies of merger-triggered radio activity.

gmw_00002281.name: SDSS J122513.09+321401.5
gmw_00002281.overview: SDSS J122513.09+321401.5 is a large early-type galaxy situated at the center of a cluster [arXiv:1801.03456]. It is a candidate host for an offset active galactic nucleus and exhibits LINER-like emission line strengths [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper calls this cluster-central early-type galaxy a good offset-AGN candidate, with an 80.5 mJy CLASS source offset by 408 mas and LINER-like line strengths.

quotes.quote: SDSS J122513.09+321401.5 Single CLASS detection of 80.5 mJy (NVSS J122513+321401), offset by 408 mas from the SDSS position. Host galaxy is a large early-type galaxy at the centre of a cluster. The AGN is classified as a Seyfert 2 by , but the OSSY line strengths suggest that this galaxy may be a LINER. Appears a good candidate for an offset-AGN host, with an offset that deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 5-sigma confidence.

gmw_00002282.name: SDSS J124135.08+285036.5
gmw_00002282.overview: SDSS J124135.08+285036.5 is an elliptical LINER galaxy that appears to be in a late-stage merger [arXiv:2001.02837, arXiv:2106.09304]. The host galaxy contains two optical nuclei [arXiv:2001.02837] and multiple parsec-scale radio cores, which may indicate a binary active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2001.02837, arXiv:2106.09304].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this galaxy as a high-priority offset-AGN candidate with a 467 mas radio offset, elongated nucleus, strong [O II], and prior spectral-line offset selection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J124135.08+285036.5 Single CLASS detection of 31.4 mJy (NVSS J124135+285034), offset at 467 mas from the SDSS position. Galaxy is extended along a north-west to south-east axis, and the offset of the radio source is towards the south east. The nucleus appears elongated, and we can't exclude the possibility of a double nucleus. The spectrum shows extremely strong [ O ii ] emission. This object was selected by as an offset-AGN candidate on the basis of a spectral-line offset.
This offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 5-sigma confidence, and the galaxy appears a good candidate for an offset-AGN host.

sources.summary: The paper reports KISSR 102 has an elliptical double-nucleus host and parsec-scale radio components, interpreted as jet-medium interaction or a possible binary radio AGN.

quotes.quote: The elliptical host galaxy includes two optical nuclei at a projected separation of 1.54 kpc, N1 and N2, to the south-east and north-west, respectively. Phase-referenced VLBA observations at 1.5 and 4.9 GHz of this LINER galaxy, have detected double radio components (A and B) at a projected separation of 4.8 parsec at 1.5 GHz, and another partially-resolved double radio structure at 4.9 GHz coincident with the brighter radio component A. These radio detections are confined to the optical nucleus N1. The brightness temperatures of all the detected radio components are high, 10^8 K, consistent with them being components of a radio AGN.

To be consistent with a ``core-jet'' structure, the jet in KISSR 102 would need to be undergoing strong jet-medium interaction with dense surrounding media resulting in a drastic spectral steepening of the jet. Alternatively, the results could be consistent with the presence of a parsec-scale binary radio AGN, which is the end result of a three-body interaction involving three supermassive black holes in the center of KISSR 102.

sources.summary: The paper highlights KISSR 102 as the only KISSR pilot source with dual compact cores, a late-stage-merger host, and shock-only line modeling favored over AGN photoionization.

quotes.quote: In the nine sources, dual compact cores are only detected in the ``offset AGN'', KISSR 102.

For the LINER and dual-optical-nuclei galaxy, KISSR 102, the shock-only model works best, while the AGN photoionization model underpredicts the observed line ratios Kharb20 . Its VLBA images reveal three compact radio cores, while its host galaxy appears to be in late-stage merger, consistent with a turbulent galactic environment.
For the nine sources, we detect dual parsec-scale cores which could be the signature of binary supermassive black holes, only in the case of KISSR 102 Kharb20 .

gmw_00002283.name: SDSS J125433.25+185602.1
gmw_00002283.overview: SDSS J125433.25+185602.1 is a low-luminosity compact symmetric object located within the Abell 1638 cluster [arXiv:1801.03456, arXiv:2303.11361]. The galaxy features a dust lane [arXiv:1801.03456], a flat-spectrum core with two fading jets [arXiv:2303.11361], and narrow, asymmetric neutral hydrogen with non-rotational motions [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J125433+185602 as a flat-spectrum CRATES source and VISP CSO with 15 pc lobe separation, redshifted narrow asymmetric H I, and non-rotational gas motions.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J125433+185602 The source belongs to the CRATES sample Healey2007 . It has been classified as a point source with flat spectrum (spectral index =0.282 ). Observations at 5 GHz, as part of the VISP survey (  Helmboldt2007 ), identify this source as a CSO. Its lobes are separated by 7.3 mas ( 15 pc at z 0.0115 ). We detect \ at redshifted velocities compared to the systemic.

The line is narrow and asymmetric, with a broader wing towards lower velocities. The redshift of the line, along with the compactness of the source, suggests that the neutral hydrogen may have motions different from simple rotation in a disk.

sources.summary: The paper reports this Abell 1638 source has a 273 mas radio offset, extended 20 cm structure, a dust lane, and a 4-sigma Rayleigh deviation.

quotes.quote: SDSS J125433.25+185602.1 Single CLASS detection of 128.2 mJy (NVSS J125433+185602), offset by 273 mas from the SDSS position. This source lies within Abell 1638, and show a 20 cm VLA map with extended structure. This source has a dust lane, which would make the optical position less reliable, but the offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 4-sigma confidence.

sources.summary: J1254+1856 is a CSO 1 with a flat-spectrum core, two fading edge-dimmed steep-spectrum jets, low luminosity, and matching MOJAVE morphology.

quotes.quote: J1254+1856, CSO 1 [ 48 ] : The 5 GHz and 8 GHz maps of 2016MNRAS.459..820T show a flat spectrum core with two fading, edge-dimmed steep spectrum jets. The morphology and low luminosity clearly indicate a CSO 1. The MOJAVE ^ image shows the same features.

gmw_00002284.name: SDSS J133435.06+344639.9
gmw_00002284.overview: SDSS J133435.06+344639.9 is an S0/E lenticular or elliptical galaxy classified as a Seyfert [arXiv:1801.03456]. Although its optical spectrum appears passive, the galaxy exhibits possible dust obscuration to its west and north, alongside a 176 mas radio offset [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper describes this possible Seyfert as an S0/E galaxy with a 176 mas radio offset, possible dust obscuration, and passive-looking SDSS spectrum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J133435.06+344639.9 (NGC 5228; UGC 8556; MCG +06-30-043) AGN class: Seyfert, Morphology: S0?, S0/E (NED) Single CLASS detection of 20.9 mJy (NVSS J133435+344639), offset by 176 mas from the SDSS position. Possible dust cloud, dust lane, or other form of obscuration, to the west and north. The SDSS spectrum appears to be that of a passive elliptical galaxy, but the OSSY line strengths indicate that this galaxy is a Seyfert.

gmw_00002285.name: SDSS J133621.18+031951.0
gmw_00002285.overview: SDSS J133621.18+031951.0 is an elliptical galaxy candidate [arXiv:1801.03456]. While its optical spectrum appears passive, emission line strengths suggest it may host a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper describes this elliptical candidate as having a 162 mas CLASS offset, a nearby faint source, passive-looking SDSS spectrum, and possible LINER line strengths.

quotes.quote: SDSS J133621.18+031951.0 (MCG +01-35-014) Morphology: E (NED) Brightest CLASS detection has a flux density of 21.5 mJy (NVSS J133621+031952; offset by 162 mas from the SDSS position). An extremely faint CLASS source is found nearby. The SDSS spectrum appears to be that of a passive elliptical galaxy, but the OSSY line strengths indicate that this galaxy may be a LINER.

gmw_00002286.name: SDSS J134243.62+050432.1
gmw_00002286.overview: SDSS J134243.62+050432.1 is a narrow-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1801.03456]. While classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy, its line strengths suggest it may instead be a LINER [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper describes this narrow-line AGN as having multiple radio offsets, Seyfert-1 and LINER classifications, and good offset-AGN candidacy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J134243.62+050432.1 Bright CLASS detection of 168.7 mJy (NVSS J134243+050431, 4C +05.57), offset by 163 mas from the SDSS position. A second bright source (51.2 mJy) is offset by 374 mas, and three fainter sources of up to 13.7 mJy in flux density are offset by at least 333 mas. SDSS spectrum appears to be a typical narrow-line AGN. The AGN is classified as a Seyfert 1 by , but the OSSY line strengths indicate that this galaxy may be a LINER. This galaxy appears a good candidate for an offset-AGN host.

gmw_00002287.name: SDSS J135022.12+094010.7
gmw_00002287.overview: SDSS J135022.12+094010.7 is a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2210.14922] hosting an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert 1.9 or LINER [arXiv:1801.03456]. The galaxy features optical substructure [arXiv:1801.03456], a massive inclined molecular gas disc [arXiv:2210.14922], and a compact parsec-scale radio source [arXiv:2303.04821].

sources.summary: The paper reports this candidate has CLASS, Chandra, and VLBA-aligned offsets, Seyfert/LINER classifications, optical substructure, and a narrow-line AGN-like spectrum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135022.12+094010.7 Single CLASS detection of 250.4 mJy (NVSS J135022+094010), offset by 154 mas from the SDSS position. A compact Chandra source is also detected at the same position. This source is found within the VLBA calibrator list, with milliarcsec-precision position. The VLBA/SDSS offset is a slightly greater 166 mas. The AGN is classified as a Seyfert 1.9 by , but the OSSY line strengths indicate that this galaxy may be a LINER.
The SDSS images suggest a lot of optical sub-structure, and the spectrum looks like a typical narrow-line AGN.

sources.summary: The paper treats the radio galaxy in RX J1350+0940 as a young GPS source with 90 pc parsec-scale size; its cluster shows asymmetric cool ICM, H-alpha spiral gas, and pre-feedback cooling.

quotes.quote: Both radio galaxies in RX J1350+0940 and ClG J0242-2132 are unresolved on arcsecond (kiloparsec) scales ( 2020ApJS..247...53K and 2015MNRAS.453.1223H , respectively), and reveal small extension only on milliarcsecond (parsec) scales 2015MNRAS.453.1223H . The nondetection of extended emission at MHz frequencies implies -- assuming a typical magnetic field of a few G (e.g., 2004IJMPD..13.1549G ) -- a timescale of at least 10^ 8 yr since the last radio activity (e.g., 2017A&A...600A..65S ). Information on the parsec-scale properties of both sources are available from the VLBA Calibrator List Tool https://obs.vlba.nrao.edu/cst/ ; 2020A&A...644A.159C . : at 2.3 GHz, the radio galaxy in RX J1350+0940 has a largest linear size (LLS) of 90 pc, while that in ClG J0242-2132 has a LLS of 200 pc.

In RX J1350+0940, the Chandra image shows an asymmetric ICM distribution, with a surface brightness edge west of the center. The RGB image in the middle panel confirms this asymmetry and reveals that the cool ICM is primarily located west of the central AGN. The MUSE H contours (total luminosity L _ H = 9.6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 ) show an extended spiral morphology (with LLS 20 kpc), cospatial with the X-ray brightest region of the ICM.

sources.summary: RXC J1350.3+0940 is one of the emission-only BCGs, with a massive inclined molecular disc and no detected absorption in the paper.

quotes.quote: RXC J1350.3+0940 has 2.5 0.3 10^ 10 M _ of molecular gas in a slightly inclined edge-on disc with a smooth velocity gradient, a velocity range of approximately 300 km/s and diameter of 10 kpc.

In the three galaxies in our sample which contain emission but no absorption (Fig. fig:maps_of_emitters ), one has a smooth velocity structure (RXC J1350.3+0940), while two are irregular (MACS 1931.8-2634 and RXC J1603.6+1553). Although there is no detectable absorption in these systems, we have provided upper limits in Table tab:gas_densities . By comparing these upper limits with the absorption column densities in the other galaxies, we can judge whether or not their lack of absorption is due to the sensitivity of the observations.

gmw_00002288.name: SDSS J135036.01+334217.3
gmw_00002288.overview: SDSS J135036.01+334217.3 is a spiral galaxy and the brightest member of its galaxy group [arXiv:1801.03456]. It hosts a bright nuclear radio source and is classified as a possible Seyfert or LINER galaxy [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5318 NED01 as a group-brightest spiral with a bright nuclear radio source, possible Seyfert/LINER activity, and a 208 mas offset.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135036.01+334217.3 (NGC 5318 NED01; UGC 8751) Morphology: SO? (NED) Single CLASS detection of 95.8 mJy (NVSS J135036+334218), offset by 208 mas from the SDSS position. NGC 5318 is the brightest member in a group of galaxies, and has been studied as an example of a spiral galaxy with a bright nuclear radio source see and references therein Mirabel1983 . The AGN is classified as a possible Seyfert by , but the OSSY line strengths indicate that this galaxy may be a LINER.

gmw_00002289.name: SDSS J135927.60+465045.9
gmw_00002289.overview: SDSS J135927.60+465045.9 is a passive elliptical galaxy that may contain a faint disk [arXiv:1801.03456]. Optical line strengths indicate the galaxy is a LINER, and it is considered a candidate for hosting an offset active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper marks this passive-elliptical-looking LINER with a possible disk as a good offset-AGN candidate despite its comparatively weak radio source.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135927.60+465045.9 Single CLASS detection of 10.2 mJy, offset by 249 mas from the SDSS position. SDSS spectrum appears to be that of a passive elliptical, but optical images hint that a faint disk may be present, and the OSSY line strengths indicate that this galaxy is a LINER. This source, albeit a little weak, appears to be a good candidate for an offset AGN.

gmw_00002290.name: SDSS J142730.26+540923.7
gmw_00002290.overview: SDSS J142730.26+540923.7 is an active galactic nucleus host classified as a possible BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1801.03456]. The galaxy exhibits a possible face-on disk with faint spiral structure [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper describes this possible BL Lac host as having duplicate CLASS detections, a spectrum inconsistent with a classic BL Lac, possible spiral structure, and a 4-sigma offset.

quotes.quote: SDSS J142730.26+540923.7 Two CLASS sources detected in almost the same position (NVSS J142730+540923), one of 19.5 mJy (offset by 173 mas) and another of 18.9 mJy (offset by 273 mas). These detections are likely to be from a single source, and have somehow become duplicated in the CLASS database. The AGN is classified as a possible BL Lacertae object by , and include this galaxy in their study of BL Lac hosts. However, the spectrum does not show an obviously depressed 4000 break as one would expect for a BL Lac. There is a possible face-on disk with faint spiral structure.
The larger offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 4-sigma confidence.

gmw_00002291.name: SDSS J154912.33+304716.4
gmw_00002291.overview: SDSS J154912.33+304716.4 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1801.03456] that functions as a gravitational lens system [arXiv:1801.03456]. The system images a lobe of a background source into an Einstein ring, and the galaxy itself lacks a nuclear X-ray source [arXiv:1801.03456].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this Seyfert-classified object as a gravitational lens system rather than an offset-AGN host, with a likely background quasar and no nuclear X-ray source.

quotes.quote: SDSS J154912.33+304716.4 AGN class: Seyfert Nearest CLASS detection is 37.3 mJy (FIRST J154912.3+304716), offset by 370 mas from the SDSS position. Two more bright sources, 38.0 mJy and 17.7 mJy, are offset by just over two arcsec. The 16.2 mJy source to the south-east (4C +30.29; NVSS J154912+304715) is coincident with a compact Chandra source, and with a redshift of 1.17 (NED) is probably a background quasar. This galaxy is a well-known gravitational lens system Lehar1993 , where a lobe of a background source is imaged into an Einstein ring. No X-ray sources are found in the nucleus of SDSS J154912.33+304716.4, and this galaxy is not an offset-AGN host.

gmw_00002292.name: IRAS 23060+0505
gmw_00002292.overview: IRAS 23060+0505 is optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:0709.1344] that exhibits starburst activity [arXiv:0709.1344]. Optical imaging reveals a complex morphology with tidal tails [arXiv:0709.1344], while its infrared spectral energy distribution and luminosity are typical of quasars [arXiv:0709.1344].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 23060+0505 as a luminous Seyfert 2 with quasar-like infrared output, prominent tidal tails, and an unobscured AGN-dominated 3-5 micron spectrum.

quotes.quote: IRAS 23060+0505 This galaxy has been optically classified as a Seyfert 2. In the infrared its spectral energy distribution and the luminosity (L _ IR 1.3 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ) are typical of quasars (Hill et al. 1987). Optical images show a complex morphology with prominent tidal tails (Hutchings Neff 1988). Optical and near-infrared spectropolarimetric studies reveal a reflection continuum with an extinction A _V 2.9 mag, combined with unpolarized emission extincted by A _V 20 mag (Young, Hough Axon 1996). The X-ray data confirm the presence of a moderately obscured (N _H 8 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) AGN with L _ 2-10 keV 1.5 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 (Brandt et al. 1997).

In our sample we can distinguish nuclei with an unobscured AGN (low EW_ 3.3 , flat continuum and absence of strong absorption) in IRAS 05189-2524, IRAS 23060+0505 and the brighter nucleus of NGC 6240, and sources with a heavily absorbed AGN (significant EW_ 3.3 , steep inverted continuum with strong dust and gas absorptions) in IRAS 19254-7245, IRAS 20551-4250 and the fainter nucleus of NGC 6240. The detection of PAH emission line points to the presence of starburst activity in all sources.

gmw_00002293.name: PSO J167.6415--13.4960
gmw_00002293.overview: PSO J167.6415--13.4960 is a type-1 quasar at a redshift of z = 6.515 [arXiv:1906.04241] that is powered by a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1502.01927]. The host galaxy is interacting with a close, star-forming companion galaxy located at a projected distance of only 5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1906.04241, arXiv:1907.02607].

sources.summary: PSO J167 13 is a z>6.5 quasar whose ALMA data show luminous, extended continuum and line emission plus a distinct companion galaxy about 5 kpc away.

quotes.quote: PSO J167 13 This is one of three z>6.5 quasars discovered in the Pan-STARRS1 survey by . The optical--near-IR spectrum shows broad \ and \ giving a redshift of 6.508 and black hole mass of 4.9 10^ 8 M_ , the only one in their paper with M_ BH < 10^9 M_ , which is why we targeted it with ALMA. The ALMA data reveal PSO J167 13 as a very luminous and extended source in both continuum and \ line emission. A Gaussian fit to the continuum yields a deconvolved size of 0.98 0.06 0.43 0.05 arcsec at position angle of 120 ^ , equivalent to 5.5 2.4 kpc. The continuum emission is asymmetric being more extended to the south-east than north-west of the peak (Figure fig:contlinemaps ).

As one moves to negative velocities a similar effect of shifting the centroid to the south-east is seen, but in addition there is a distinct source located 0.9 arcsec from the quasar centre at 11:10:34.030 13:29:46.301. This source is visible at a wide range of velocities from 450 to 0 km s ^ 1 with a peak at 270 km s ^ 1 or redshift z=6.5090 . Because this source is spatially and spectrally distinct from the main component we interpret this as a companion galaxy lying at a projected distance of only 5.0 kpc from the quasar.

sources.summary: The paper describes PSO167--13 as a z=6.515 type-1 QSO in a close galaxy pair and considers whether its hard X-ray emission indicates heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: One of the two galaxies hosts an optically classified type-1 QSO, PSO J167.6415--13.4960 (hereafter PSO167--13). Evidence for interaction between the two galaxies is reported in . Errors and limits are reported at the 68 confidence level, unless otherwise noted. We adopt a flat cosmology with H_0=67.7 km s^ 1 and _m=0.307 Planck16 . PSO167-13 was first selected as a high-redshift QSO candidate on the basis of its colors in the PanSTARRS-1 survey ( Venemans15a , see Fig. J1110 , left panel), and was then confirmed spectroscopically to lie at z=6.515 both in the rest-frame UV Venemans15a and sub-millimeter with Atacama large millimeter array (ALMA), via detection of the [ C II ] ( 158 m ) emission line Decarli18 .

To summarize, if PSO167--13, optically classified as a type-1 QSO, were found to be responsible for the high-energy emission, it would be an intrinsically X-ray normal but heavily obscured QSO, and the causes of the UV/X-ray misclassification would need to be investigated. Alternatively, if the companion galaxy were found to be the X-ray source, it would be a heavily obscured QSO in an interacting pair with PSO167--13. In this case, PSO167--13 would be intrinsically X-ray weak by a factor of 4 .

sources.summary: The paper follows up the z=6.515 radio-quiet QSO PSO167--13, finding no significant X-ray emission, extreme X-ray weakness relative to UV luminosity, and possible nuclear-wind signatures.

quotes.quote: We present the results of follow-up observations of the z=6.515 radio-quiet QSO PSO167--13, which is interacting with a close companion galaxy. The PSO167--13 system has been recently proposed to host the first heavily obscured X-ray source at high redshift. The goals of these new observations are to confirm the existence of the X-ray source and to investigate the rest-frame UV properties of the QSO. We observed the PSO167--13 system with Chandra /ACIS-S (177 ks), and obtained new spectroscopic observations (7.2 h) with . No significant X-ray emission is detected from the PSO167--13 system, suggesting that the obscured X-ray source previously tentatively detected was either due to a strong background fluctuation or is highly variable.

The upper limit ( 90 confidence level) on the X-ray emission of PSO167--13 ( L_ 2-10 keV <8.3 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 ) is the lowest available for a z>6 QSO. The ratio between the X-ray and UV luminosity of _ ox <-1.95 makes PSO167--13 a strong outlier from the _ ox L_ UV and L_X-L_ bol relations. In particular, its X-ray emission is >6 times weaker than the expectation based on its UV luminosity. The new spectrum of PSO167--13 is strongly affected by the unfavorable sky conditions, but the tentatively detected C IV and Mg II emission lines appear strongly blueshifted. The most plausible explanations for the X-ray weakness of PSO167--13 are intrinsic weakness or small-scale absorption by Compton-thick material.

sources.summary: The paper describes the companion galaxy as a [C II]-emitting member of a physical pair with PSO167--13 and a possible host of the heavily obscured X-ray QSO.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy was first detected as a [ C II ] emitter by Atacama large millimeter array (ALMA). An X-ray source is significantly ( P=0.9996 ) detected by Chandra in the 2--5 keV band, with <1.14 net counts in the 0.5--2 keV band, although the current positional uncertainty does not allow a conclusive association with either PSO167--13 or its companion galaxy. From X-ray photometry and hardness-ratio arguments, we estimated an obscuring column density of N_H>2 10^ 24 cm^ 2 and N_H>6 10^ 23 cm^ 2 at 68 and 90 confidence levels, respectively. Thus, regardless of which of the two galaxies is associated with the X-ray emission, this source is the first heavily obscured QSO candidate at z>6 .

The companion galaxy thus forms a physical pair with PSO167--13. Its existence was recently confirmed by a deep HST/WFC3 observation in the F140W ( 1.4 m ) band (with AB magnitude F140W =25.5 ) and new high-resolution ( 0.25" ) ALMA imaging (Fig. J1110 , center and right panels; Mazzucchelli19, Neeleman19 ). No rest-frame UV spectrum is currently available for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports PSO J167.6415--13.4960 as a newly discovered z about 6.5 quasar, deriving its Mg II redshift, faint UV luminosity, red continuum, black hole mass, and near-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: PSO J167.6415--13.4960 The quasar PSO J167.6415--13.4960 (hereafter P167--13) was discovered based on forced photometry on VHS images at the positions of PS1 candidates. Our FORS2 discovery spectrum revealed a source with a strong continuum decrement around 9100 , and we identified the object as a quasar with a redshift of z 6.52 . From the near-infrared spectrum we derive a redshift z_ MgII =6.508 0.001 .

This quasar is the faintest of our new discoveries with M_ 1450 =-25.58 0.13 . The power-law slope of =-1.0 0.1 is red compared to the slope of =-1.3 of the SDSS quasar composite spectrum of van01 . The estimated black hole mass is _ ,MgII (4.9 2.0) 10^8 , and the Eddington ratio is consistent with maximal accretion ( L_ Bol /L_ Edd =1.2 0.5 ).

sources.summary: PJ167c is a z~6 quasar companion detected in rest-frame UV; SED fitting suggests a star-forming galaxy with modest dust, and nearby gas may indicate advanced merging.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the rest-frame UV emission of PJ167c is detected in our HST /WFC3 observations (see Section secObs ). Its UV-to-submm ratio is comparable to that of the star--forming galaxy NGC6946, while the limits from our Spitzer /IRAC data suggest that it has a lower stellar content.

The SED of PJ167c is consistent with that of a star forming galaxy, SFR = 53 ^ +27 _ 19 M _ yr ^ 1 , with a stellar mass of M_ * = 0.84^ +0.64 _ 0.40 10^ 10 M _ , a moderate dust extinction ( A_ V =0.66^ +0.35 _ 0.25 mag) and a dust content of M_ d = 4.7^ +3.7 _ 1.7 10^ 7 M _ . Finally, we note that, given the close spatial/velocity separation of the companions and the quasar hosts, they are very likely found in physical connection. In particular, PJ167c is located at only 5 pkpc/140 km s ^ 1 away from PJ167, and emission linking these systems is observed both in the dust continuum and the [ ] line (with a smooth velocity gradient; Decarli17 , Neeleman et al. 2019) and, tentatively, in the rest--frame UV (see Fig. figPSFPJ167 ). This evidence, together with a measured high velocity dispersion of the cool gas ( 150 km s ^ 1 ; Neeleman at al. 2019) may suggest that these galaxies have already entered an advanced merging stage.

sources.summary: The paper treats the PSO167--13 companion galaxy as an ALMA/HST-detected interacting companion, computes X-ray photometry at its position, and does not confirm significant X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array ( ) imaging revealed that PSO167--13 is interacting with a companion galaxy at a projected distance of 0.9^ (i.e., 5 physical kpc at z=6.515 ) and v 140 km s^ 1 (i.e., z 0.004 ) in velocity space Willott17,Neeleman19 . Companion galaxies detected with have been found for a significant fraction of high-redshift QSOs Decarli18, Venemans20 . The PSO167--13 system is one of only two cases in which the companion galaxy has been detected in the rest-frame UV via deep Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaging Decarli19,Mazzucchelli19 .

Fig_Xray_images ; corresponding to 90 encircled energy fraction at 1.5 keV), centered on the ALMA position of the companion galaxy (RA=11:10:34.033, DEC= 13:29:46.29; Neeleman19 ) to compute its photometry. We computed the detection significance in each energy band using the binomial no-source probability P_ B presented by and , and set a significance threshold (1-P_B)=0.99 for source detection. We measured the X-ray background in an annular region with R_ in =4^ and R_ out =24^ , where no bright X-ray sources are found. We detect 1, 3, and 4 counts in the soft, hard, and full bands. Considering the expected background of 0.35/0.59/0.93 counts, respectively, we obtained a detection significance (1-P_B)<0.99 (i.e., the galaxy is not detected) in all of the bands.

X-ray emission from the companion galaxy In , we discussed the detection of a faint and hard X-ray source in the PSO167--13 system, consistent with the position of the companion galaxy, although the relatively large positional uncertainty prevented us from discarding an association with the optical QSO.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PJ167--13 as a resolved quasar host with a northwest-southeast velocity gradient exceeding 400 km/s, while noting unresolved ambiguity about rotation, complex kinematics, or a satellite.

quotes.quote: Velocity maps and dynamical masses Four sources in our sample are well--resolved in (i.e., the observed size of the continuum--subtracted emission from the 2D gaussian modeling is >1.4 the beam): PJ009--10, J0142--3327, PJ167--13, and J1306+0356 (see Figure fig_beam_ima_spc ).
On the other hand, PJ167--13 shows a clearly resolved velocity gradient from North-West towards South-East, with a peak--to--peak velocity difference exceeding 400 along the line of sight.

The resolution of the available data is insufficient to assess whether the kinematics of the -emitting gas in the host galaxies of these two quasars are dominated by ordered rotation or if the underlying velocity structure is more complex (see, for instance, the high-resolution studies of z>6 quasars presented in venemans17a and shao17 ). In particular, given the present data quality it is impossible to rule out whether part of the spatially--resolved emission is associated with a close satellite galaxy of the quasar host galaxy, similar to the cases discussed in decarli17 .

gmw_00002294.name: PSO J036.5078+03.0498
gmw_00002294.overview: PSO J036.5078+03.0498 is a highly luminous active quasar at a redshift of approximately 6.5 [arXiv:1502.01927, arXiv:2311.00771]. Its host galaxy features a thin, warped rotating disk [arXiv:2311.00771] with a large molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2401.04211] and an unstable, star-forming dust-continuum region [arXiv:2311.00771].

sources.summary: The paper presents PSO J036.5078+03.0498 as a bright z>6 quasar with blue continuum, extreme UV luminosity, high bolometric luminosity, a massive black hole, and near-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: PSO J036.5078+03.0498 PSO J036.5078+03.0498 (hereafter P036+03) was selected as part of our extended, bright z dropout search and was matched to a source in the UKIDSS and WISE catalogs. The high S/N FIRE spectrum revealed blue quasar continuum emission ( =-1.70 0.05 ) at a redshift z_ MgII =6.527 0.002 . The absolute magnitude of M_ 1450 =-27.36 0.03 makes this quasar one of the most luminous objects known at z>6 . The bolometric luminosity is estimated to be L_ Bol,3000 A =(2.38 0.09) 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 .

The central black hole has an estimated mass of _ ,MgII = (1.9^ +1.1 _ 0.8 ) 10^9 . The accretion rate is close to Eddington with L_ Bol /L_ Edd = 0.96 0.55 . The quality of the infrared spectrum is not sufficient to constrain the Fe 2 emission to better than 2 . We measure Fe 2 / Mg 2 =3.4 1.7 , fully consistent with previously discovered quasars at similar redshifts e.g., der14 .

sources.summary: The paper presents P036 + 03 as a z=6.5406 quasar host with a thin warped rotating disk, low dynamical mass, and an unstable dust-continuum region likely tied to the warp.

quotes.quote: Venemans Leiden Observatory, Leiden University, PO Box 9513, 2300 RA Leiden, The Netherlands We present 0 075 ( 400 pc) resolution ALMA observations of the \ and dust continuum emission from the host galaxy of the z = 6.5406 quasar, P036 + 03. We find that the emission arises from a thin, rotating disk with an effective radius of 0 21 (1.1 kpc). The velocity dispersion of the disk is consistent with a constant value of 66.4 1.0 , yielding a scale height of 80 30 pc. The \ velocity field reveals a distortion that we attribute to a warp in the disk. Modeling this warped disk yields an inclination estimate of 40.4 1.3 and a rotational velocity of 116 3 .

The resulting dynamical mass estimate of (1.96 0.10) 10^ 10 M_ is lower than previous estimates, which strengthens the conclusion that the host galaxy is less massive than expected based on local scaling relations between the black hole mass and the host galaxy mass.

We further detect a region with excess (15- ) dust continuum emission, which is located 1.3 kpc northwest of the galaxy's center and is gravitationally unstable (Toomre-Q < 0.04). We posit this is a star-forming region whose formation was triggered by the warp, because the region is located within a part of the warped disk where gas can efficiently lose angular momentum.

sources.summary: The paper detects bright line and far-infrared emission from the P036+03 quasar host at z>6.5, reporting extreme luminosities, possible water emission, and strong promise for early-ISM studies.

quotes.quote: In this Letter , we report a strong detection of the \ line in the host galaxy of the brightest quasar known at z>6.5 , the Pan-STARRS1 selected quasar PSO J036.5078+03.0498 (hereafter P036+03), using the IRAM NOEMA millimeter interferometer. Its \ and total far-infrared luminosities are (5.8 0.7) 10^9 \ and (7.6 1.5) 10^ 12 , respectively. This results in a / ratio of 0.8 10^ 3 , which is at the high end for those found for active galaxies, though it is lower than the average found in typical main sequence galaxies at z 0 . We also report a tentative additional line which we identify as a blended emission from the 3_ 22 3_ 13 and 5_ 23 4_ 32 H _2 O transitions. If confirmed, this would be the most distant detection of water emission to date.

P036+03 rivals the current prototypical luminous J1148+5251 quasar at z=6.42 , in both rest-frame UV and \ luminosities. Given its brightness and because it is visible from both hemispheres (unlike J1148+5251), P036+03 has the potential of becoming an important laboratory for the study of star formation and of the interstellar medium only 800 Myr after the Big Bang.

sources.summary: The paper observes PJ036+03 with multiple compact-array tracks, targeting CO(6--5) and CO(7--6), and later compares its high-resolution star-formation-density constraints with gas-mass estimates.

quotes.quote: PJ036+03 was observed in multiple short tracks in June--July 2015 (program ID: S15DA) and June--July 2017 (program ID: S17CD), in compact array configuration (5D / 6D in 2015; 7D / 8D in 2017) using the WideX correlator. The receiver was tuned to encompass the two CO(6--5) and CO(7--6) lines in two independent frequency settings. The calibration sources include the blazar 3C454.3 for bandpass calibration, the source MWC349 for absolute flux calibration, and the quasar 0215+015 for amplitude and phase calibration. The final cubes consist of a total of 22440 visibilities (13.33 h on source, assuming 7 antennas) on the CO(7--6) line, and 22317 visibilities (13.28 h on source, assuming 7 antennas) on CO(6--5).

Along the same lines, one could adopt the method proposed by sommovigo21 to infer _ CII and T_ d based on the surface brightness of for those quasars in our sample that have already been observed at high angular resolution in PJ036+03, J1048--0109, J1319+0950, J2054--0005, and PJ359--06; see venemans20 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J036+03 as a HYPERION QSO with measured CO, [CI], and [CII] properties, ordered gas motion, and large molecular gas reservoirs.

quotes.quote: PSO J036.5078+03.0498 hereafter J036+03, venemans2015 at z=6.5405 was observed for the first time in the Pan-STARRS1 survey venemans2015 , and it has a BH mass of M_ BH, MgII =(2.69-3.09) 10^ 9 \ M_ from the analysis of MgII emission line and a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol =(2.13-3.16) 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 zappacosta2023,mazzucchelli2023 . decarli2022 studied the CO(6-5), CO(7-6) and [ CI ] _ 2-1 emission lines of this QSO's host with NOEMA observations, and they found L_ CO(6-5) =12.7 10^9 \ K km s^ 1 pc^2 , L_ CO(7-6) =10.7 10^9 \ K km s^ 1 pc^2 and L_ CI =5.7 10^9 \ K km s^ 1 pc^2 , implying a molecular gas mass of M_ H2, CO =5.0^ +0.5 _ 0.6 10^ 10 \ M_ and M_ H2, [ CI ] =7.1^ +1.6 _ 1.4 10^ 10 \ M_ . The [ CII ] emission shows ordered motion, with a clear and regular velocity gradient in the moment-1 map and v_ rot / >3 , and it has a L_ CII =3.38 10^9 \ L_ and a size of 2.4 1.6 kpc ^2 venemans2020, neeleman2021 . From the modelling of the velocity rotation they estimated a dynamical mass of M_ dyn =2.9^ +1.1 _ 0.7 10^ 10 \ M_ , and from the [ CII ] underlying continuum emission they derived a gas mass of M_ H2, cont =2.8^ +15 _ 1.1 10^ 10 \ M_ , assuming a gas-to-dust ratio of 100 and a molecular-to-total gas mass fraction of 0.75.

gmw_00002295.name: PSO J338.2298+29.5089
gmw_00002295.overview: PSO J338.2298+29.5089 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.66 [arXiv:1502.01927]. The active galactic nucleus is powered by a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1502.01927], and its host galaxy has been observed in molecular gas transitions including CO(6-5) and CO(7-6) [arXiv:2203.03658].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSO J338.2298+29.5089 as a z=6.66 quasar, measuring its Mg II redshift, UV magnitude, blue continuum slope, black hole mass, and low Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: PSO J338.2298+29.5089 PSO J338.2298+29.5089 (hereafter P338+29) was one of the z dropout candidates with a match in the WISE catalog. The discovery spectrum shows a source with a strong, narrow emission line at 9314 \ and continuum redward of the line, which we identify as Ly at a redshift of z=6.66 . From the FIRE spectrum we measure z_ MgII =6.658 0.007 , M_ 1450 =-26.04 0.09 , and a blue continuum slope =-1.85^ +0.08 _ 0.05 . Although the Mg 2 line suffers from sky residuals on its blue side, we estimate a black hole mass of M_ BH,MgII =(3.7^ +1.3 _ 1.0 ) 10^9 \ and an accretion rate of L_ Bol /L_ Edd =0.13^ +0.05 _ 0.04 .

sources.summary: The paper observes quasar host PJ338+29 with PolyFix in 2018, using its bandwidth to cover CO(6--5) and CO(7--6) and producing a calibrated final cube.

quotes.quote: The quasar PJ338+29 was observed in July 2018 (program ID: S18DM). The expanded bandwidth offered by the PolyFix correlator enables to encompass the CO(6--5) and CO(7--6) lines in our targets using a single frequency setting. The blazar 3C454.3 served as bandpass calibrator, MWC349 as absolute flux calibrator, and the quasars 2234+282 and J2217+243 as amplitude and phase calibrators. The final cubes comprises a total of 8670 visibilities (3.87 h on source, 8-antenna equivalent).

gmw_00002296.name: Jil
gmw_00002296.overview: Jil is an obscured active galactic nucleus (AGN) located at a redshift of z=3.28 [arXiv:1801.05442]. It resides in a massive, asymmetric host galaxy [arXiv:1805.09845] and forms a tight dual AGN system with the nearby quasar LBQS 0302-0019 [arXiv:1801.05442, arXiv:1805.09845].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Jil as the obscured companion AGN to LBQS 0302 0019, detects its massive asymmetric host, measures [O III] emission, and argues it supports the dual-AGN scenario.

quotes.quote: Indeed, we detect the individual host galaxies of the QSO and Jil with estimated stellar masses of (M_ /M_ )=11.4 0.5 and (M_ /M_ )=10.9 0.5 , respectively. Near-IR spectra obtained with VLT-KMOS reveal a clear [ O iii ] 5007 line detection at the location of Jil which does not contribute significantly to the K_s band flux. Both observations therefore corroborate the dual AGN scenario.

The resulting model and QSO subtracted image are shown in Fig. fig:HAWKI , from which we infer m_ K_s =19.2 0.1 mag for the QSO host galaxy and m_ K_s =20.6 0.2 mag for Jil . Host morphology The HAWK-I K_s image (Fig. fig:HAWKI ) reveals that the host galaxy of Jil appears asymmetric in the rest-frame V band with a faint extension towards the west side. It is unclear at the given spatial resolution and depth whether Jil is one distorted galaxy or itself a merger of two galaxies.

Based on deep K band spectroscopy with KMOS and high-resolution imaging with HAWK-I+GRAAL, we have identified the massive ( (M_ /M_ )=10.9 0.5 ) host galaxy of Jil , the obscured companion AGN to LBQS0302-0019 at a projected separation of about 20 kpc. This clearly supports the obscured AGN nature of Jil since the presence of a massive host galaxy implies the existence of a super-massive black hole, potentially powering an AGN.

sources.summary: Jil is a bright Ly knot 20 kpc from LBQS 0302-0019 with stated coordinates; line diagnostics identify it as likely ionized by an embedded obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: In this case the Ly surface brightness distribution is asymmetric, with a bright knot about 2.9 \ ( 20 kpc) northeast of the QSO. We refer to this source as Jil, Klingon for neighbor, with coordinates =03:04:50.03, =-00:08:12.5 (J2000), and a peak surface brightness of _ Ly =1.05 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 .

We report the detection of a He ii emission-line source, named Jil, at z=3.28 that is close to the luminous radio-quiet QSO LBQS 0302-0019. Based on emission-line ratio diagnostics we verified that Jil is ionized most likely by an embedded obscured AGN. With a projected separation of only 20 kpc to the QSO, this system represents the tightest unobscured/obscured dual AGN system reported at z>3 .

gmw_00002297.name: NGD-z9a
gmw_00002297.overview: NGD-z9a is a galaxy that exhibits a much higher star formation rate than other galaxies in its sample [arXiv:2302.04270]. This elevated rate may be due to an active galactic nucleus contribution or photometry over-correction caused by a neighboring bright source [arXiv:2302.04270].

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGD-z9a has a much higher SFR than the rest of the NGDEEP sample, possibly from AGN contamination or over-correction caused by a neighboring bright source.

quotes.quote: In terms of star formation rates amongst our sample, NGD-z9a shows a much higher SFR ( 209.18 M _ yr ^ 1 ) than other galaxies in our sample. One explanation for this high SFR is that it could include an AGN contribution. Further sub-mm observations (e.g. ALMA) could provide far-infrared information to make this clear. In addition, we believe that the neighbouring bright source may be increasing its Kron radius, meaning that we are likely over-correcting using the currently implemented FLUX AUTO to aperture corrected FLUX APER ratio, which will boost both the mass and SFR of this source. The scale of this correction is currently half a dex.

gmw_00002298.overview: The galaxy 176606 is classified as a robust quiescent galaxy candidate [arXiv:2312.12207]. It hosts an X-ray-selected active galactic nucleus that is not luminous in the optical or mid-infrared, and its spectral energy distribution indicates a dusty nature [arXiv:2312.12207].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 176606 as a robust quiescent-galaxy candidate, notes an X-ray-selected AGN that is not optically or mid-infrared luminous, and identifies dusty-SED evidence.

quotes.quote: Four of our QGs (176606, 175039, 16170 at 3<z<4 and 35453 at 4<z<6 ) pass this threshold.
We find that QG 176606 is AGN, but selected in X-ray and not the mid-infrared. This indicates the AGN is likely not luminous in the optical to mid-infrared.

Two of our QG candidates, 176606 and 172799, occupy this space, and their dusty nature is supported visually in their SEDs (Figures fig:robust ) and by their measured V-band attenuations of A_ V =1 and A_ V =2 , respectively. While photometric disentanglement of the age dust degeneracy is notoriously difficult, our photometric sampling including 4 and 2 NIRCam medium bands for 176606 and 172799, respectively, gives us confidence that we have selected bona fide candidates for dusty QGs suitable for spectroscopic follow-up.

gmw_00002299.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 172799 is a tentative dusty quiescent galaxy candidate [arXiv:2312.12207] with no indication of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2312.12207]. It serves as the base of the Cosmic Rose overdensity, where it is surrounded by lower-mass quenched companion galaxies [arXiv:2312.12207].

sources.summary: AzTEC/GS1 has a robust radio, IRAC/MIPS, X-ray, and LABOCA counterpart and is described as an optically faint likely high-redshift source with photometric redshifts near z=3.

quotes.quote: AzTEC/GS1. There is one clear, robust counterpart (GS1a in Table tab:fluxes ), which is a radio source found 4.8 \ north of AzTEC/GS1 ( P_ 1.4 =0.045 ). This faint radio source has an IRAC/MIPS counterpart ( P_ 24 =0.161 ), which is also a /X-ray source. This source has a red IRAC color with [ 3.6 m ] [ 4.5 m ] = +0.37 ( P_ color =0.133 ), similar to the AzTEC sources identified using submillimetre interferometry younger07,yun08 . The 870 \ LABOCA source LESS J033211.3 275210 ( S_ 870 =9.2 1.2 mJy) position is only 2.1 \ away from GS1a, and biggs11 also identify GS1a as the robust counterpart. No spectroscopic redshift is available for this extremely faint optical source ( i>23.5 , K>22.9 ), and it is likely a high redshift ( z 3 ) source with z_ opt =2.96 0.45 and z_ MR =3.56^ +0.66 _ 1.20 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 172799 as a tentative dusty quiescent-galaxy candidate with no AGN indication and as the base of the Cosmic Rose overdensity with nearby quenched companions.

quotes.quote: One additional QG (172799) also passes the threshold; however, its SED and placement in the UVJ diagram suggest significant dust content (see Section sec:dusty ). We label this candidate as tentative.
QG 172799 has no indication of AGN.

Environmental quenching of low-mass galaxies at high redshift: the Cosmic Rose Beyond its dusty, yet old nature, QG 172799 is the base of the so-called ``Cosmic Rose" eisenstein2023 , a visually striking structure composed of two massive red galaxies, QG candidate 172799, and a log =11 dusty SFG (JADES 172813 Also known as ALESS009.1, spectroscopically confirmed via CO(4-3) at z_ spec =3.694 birkin2021 . ) with SFR 730 yr ^ 1 and A_ V 3.5 Due to its size, the properties reported for JADES 172813 were measured here using a Kron aperture. . This pair, reminiscent of the Jekyll and Hyde galaxies observed at similar redshifts glazebrook2017, schreiber2018b, kokorev2023a , are surrounded by lower mass quenched galaxies with similar photometric redshifts (Table tbl:properties tbl:properties2 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies PID 116 as a luminous submillimetre galaxy whose Fe K line may be high-ionization, shifting its X-ray redshift to z=3.96 or z=4.16.

quotes.quote: Two exceptions are PID 116 and PID 352, for which the Fe K emission-lines, detected at 2.5 with EW 0.15 keV and 4.5 with EW 0.30 keV, respectively, were used to obtain the redshifts assuming the rest-frame line energy of 6.4 keV emitted from cold matter. This assumption may not be true for PID 116, which is a luminous submillimetre galaxy detected at 870 m with LABOCA (LESS 9, Wardlow et al 2011; Biggs et al 2011).

As the X-ray detected luminous infrared galaxies with L_ IR 10^ 13 L_ at z>2 in the COSMOS field appears to show high-ionization Fe K emission, e.g., Fe xxv at 6.70 keV and/or Fe xxvi at 6.97 keV as inferred from a spectral stacking analysis (Iwasawa et al 2012), the emission line of PID 116 could also be either of these high-ionization lines. In this case, the redshift would be z=3.96 or z=4.16 , when it was identified with Fe xxv and Fe xxvi , respectively.

gmw_00002300.name: QG 172809
gmw_00002300.overview: QG 172809 is a tentative quiescent galaxy candidate at a photometric redshift of approximately 3.7 [arXiv:2312.12207]. It is characterized by a relatively old stellar population and is part of a compact structure that includes a massive, dusty galaxy [arXiv:2312.12207].

sources.summary: The paper labels QG 172809 as a tentative quiescent-galaxy candidate near the Cosmic Rose, with old stellar properties, similar colors to 172799, and zphot about 3.7.

quotes.quote: Two other QGs (172809 and 170932) are labeled as tentative as they only clear 50-80 of their SSFR posteriors below the threshold. These sources and their measured properties are listed in Table tbl:properties .

As stated above, QG 172799 has an unusual SED suggestive of high dust content (see Section sec:dusty ) and is additionally part of a large, compact structure that includes a massive, dusty galaxy as well as QG 172809 and PSB 173604.

In Figure fig:rose , we show the F444W, F200W, F090W RGB image of the Rose, which reveals that 172799 has similar colors as 172809, which we have identified as a log =9.4 , relatively old ( age_ MW 0.6 Gyr) QG. Both QG candidates are fit with z_ phot 3.7 , which would put them at a physical separation of 11 kpc, significantly less than the distance from the Milky Way to the Magellanic Clouds 50 kpc; fitzpatrick2002 .

gmw_00002301.name: LBQS 1429-0053
aliases_external: LBQS 1429-0053 component A / quasar pair 1429 A and B
gmw_00002301.overview: LBQS 1429-0053 is a genuine binary quasar system consisting of the pair 1429 A and B [arXiv:astro-ph/0303589]. Deep imaging and weak shear analysis failed to detect a lensing galaxy or a dark matter concentration, supporting its binary nature rather than a gravitationally lensed system [arXiv:astro-ph/0303589].

sources.summary: The paper argues that LBQS 1429-0053 is a genuine binary quasar, finding no convincing lensing galaxy or dark mass concentration despite deep VLT, HST, and weak-shear tests.

quotes.quote: Very deep ESO/VLT FORS1 and ISAAC images, as well as HST NICMOS2 data are used to infer the nature of the quasar pair 1429 A and B, either a binary quasar or a doubly-imaged lensed quasar. Direct search of a putative lensing galaxy is unsuccessful down to R=27, J=24, Ks=22.5 and H=22.5. Moreover, no galaxy overdensity close to the quasar pair is found. A weak shear analysis of the FORS1 R band 6.8 6.8 field also fails at detecting any concentration of dark matter more massive than =500 km s ^ 1 and weakens the hypothesis of a dark lens.

Considering this group as the lensing potential does not allow to reproduce the image position and flux ratio of 1429 A and B. Our deep R band image shows a blue, previously unknown, extended object at the position of 1429 A, which is consistent with either being the lensed quasar A host, or being an intervening galaxy at lower redshift. Unless future very deep optical images demonstrate that this object is actually the lensed host of 1429, we conclude that there is very little evidence for 1429 being lensed. Therefore, we are led to declare 1429 A and B a genuine binary quasar.

gmw_00002302.name: IC 311
gmw_00002302.overview: IC 311 is an elliptical radio galaxy classified as a low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:2006.07631]. Its optical structure features two roughly parallel dust lanes, including an extended, strong dust lane that spans approximately 20 kiloparsecs and obscures the galactic center [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 311 as a peculiar LERG elliptical with strong dust lanes, excess millimeter emission, compact flat-spectrum radio core, and similarities to a down-scaled NGC 5128.

quotes.quote: (1) \ J031646.7+400013 ( 398, IC 311) This galaxy is classified as a LERG. In the optical, the eye-catching morphological feature is an unusual absorption structure in an otherwise nearly featureless system of approximately elliptical shape (Fig. fig:App_398_1 left). An extended ( 20 kpc), strong dust lane hides the centre of the galaxy. A second, slightly weaker dust lane runs roughly parallel.

IC 311 was observed in January 2003 with the bolometer of the 30 m IRAM telescope on Pico Veleta, Spain. We derived a continuum flux of 8.7 1.1 mJy at 1.3 mm, about two orders of magnitude higher than predicted by the stellar population model. NGC 5128 scaled to the distance of the Perseus cluster would have an only 2.5 times higher flux. Thus, IC 311 seems in fact to be a down-scaled version of NGC 5128.

gmw_00002303.name: J031755.2+405536
gmw_00002303.overview: J031755.2+405536 is a barred galaxy with a lopsided morphology and faint, arc-like structures [arXiv:2006.07631]. It features a possible second nucleus, passive to intermediate star formation properties, and an optical core classified as a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper describes J031755.2+405536 as a barred, lopsided galaxy with arc-like structures, a possible second nucleus, passive/intermediate colours, and an optical core classified as sAGN.

quotes.quote: (2) \ J031755.2+405536 ( 485, UGC 2642) In addition to its pronounced bar, this galaxy shows an extended lopsided morphology and a system of faint arc-like structures (Fig. fig:App_485 ). Both properties most indicate a previous accretion of a dynamically cold stellar system. There is perhaps a second, fainter nucleus about 2.5 ( 0.9 kpc) SW of the brighter core. The system is classified as passive based on the SFR data from Chang_2015 (Fig fig:SFR-M ) and as intermediate based on the WISE colours (Fig. fig:WISE_ccd ). However, the optical spectrum positions the core of this galaxy into the sAGN area of the WHAN diagram (Fig. fig:WHAN ).

gmw_00002304.name: J031934.2+413450
gmw_00002304.overview: J031934.2+413450 is a disk galaxy classified as an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.07631]. It features a bright, elongated ellipsoidal bulge and an extended, low surface brightness outer disk that is tilted at an angle of about 60 degrees relative to the bulge [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper treats J031934.2+413450 as an X-ray AGN candidate with twisted elliptical appearance but an exponential outer disc and misaligned bulge-disc structure that could feed nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: (4) \ J031934.2+413450 ( 667, PGC 12405) This galaxy was identified with a radio source Miller_2001 but was excluded from our AGN list after the inspection of NVSS and TGSS images. On the other hand, it was classified as a X-ray AGN by Santra_2007 and thus it is included here in the AGN subsection.

At first sight, this galaxy looks like an elliptical with a strong isophote twist (Fig. fig:App_667 , left), similar to the Perseus cluster galaxy presented by their Fig. 12 Conselice_2002 . However, the right panel of Fig. fig:App_667 shows that the outer part of the radial surface brightness profile follows an exponential law. As a whole, the radial surface brightness profile is well fitted by the combination of a bright, elongated ellipsoidal bulge with a slightly inclined, extended disc of low surface brightness. The major axis of the projected disc is tilted at an angle of about 60 to the major axis of the bulge.

gmw_00002305.name: J031954.4+420053
gmw_00002305.overview: J031954.4+420053 is an edge-on galaxy classified as a spiral or lenticular candidate [arXiv:2006.07631]. It features a central dust lane, a faint northern disc structure, and is classified as a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper describes J031954.4+420053 as an edge-on S/S0 candidate with a central dust lane, a faint northern disc feature, and a wAGN spectral classification.

quotes.quote: (5) \ J031954.4+420053 ( 711) Because viewed nearly edge-on, the morphological classification of this galaxy remains uncertain (S/S0). A dust lane is seen crossing the centre, but there is no indication of spiral structure. In the high-contrast image, a small low-surface brightness feature is indicated at the northern edge of the disc. The spectral classification as wAGN is based on the WHAN diagram.

gmw_00002306.name: J032634.7+414143
gmw_00002306.overview: J032634.7+414143 is a high-excitation radio galaxy morphologically classified as a peculiar lenticular or elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2006.07631]. It features diffuse extended components and a low-surface-brightness, one-armed spiral structure that may represent a stellar stream from a tidal relic [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J032634.7+414143 as a HERG with peculiar S0/E morphology, diffuse outer components, and a one-armed low-surface-brightness structure interpreted as a possible tidal relic.

quotes.quote: (6) \ J032634.7+414143 ( 1231) This HERG is morphologically classified as a peculiar S0 or E. The peculiarities are diffuse extended components at either side of the galaxy (Fig. fig:App_1231 ). At high contrast (right panel), a one-armed spiral structure of low surface brightness becomes visible that surrounds the main body and might indicate a stellar stream from the tidal relic from an infalling smaller system. The star-like object close to the centre seen in the left panel is very likely a foreground star.

gmw_00002307.name: J031930.3+404430
gmw_00002307.overview: J031930.3+404430 is one of the faintest spectroscopic cluster members in its catalog and is classified as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2006.07631]. This blue galaxy features a bar-like component with several knots and irregular structures at either end that may form two ring-like components [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper describes J031930.3+404430 as a faint blue cluster member whose whole-galaxy star formation makes an SF classification preferable despite its WHAN position near sAGN.

quotes.quote: (3) \ J031930.3+404430 ( 660, UGC 2664) This is one of the faintest spectroscopic cluster members ( z = 0.0178 ) in our catalogue. In the WHAN diagram, this blue galaxy lies in the sAGN part but close to the SF border. Because there is no substantial core and SF extends over the whole galaxy, the classification as a SF galaxy appears to be more applicable.
The morphology is very unusual: a bar-like component with several knots and irregular structures at either end, perhaps forming two ring-like components.

gmw_00002308.name: J032235.4+412357
gmw_00002308.overview: J032235.4+412357 is a two-armed spiral galaxy with a clearly distorted spiral structure [arXiv:2006.07631]. This distortion may be caused by a minor merger with a small galaxy, or potentially by ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper describes J032235.4+412357 as a distorted two-armed spiral, possibly affected by a minor merger and perhaps ram-pressure stripping at its projected cluster-centric distance.

quotes.quote: (4) \ J032235.4+412357 ( 986) The spiral structure of this two-armed spiral galaxy is clearly distorted, perhaps by a minor merger with a small galaxy seen between the core and the northern spiral arm. In addition, there seems to be a fainter double of the spiral structure, shifted in western direction by a few arcseconds. Because of the relatively small projected cluster-centric distance of 730 kpc, one might speculate that this structure is related to ram pressure stripping.

gmw_00002309.name: J032700.4+402838
gmw_00002309.overview: J032700.4+402838 is a peculiar system featuring a compact core surrounded by a concentric ring-like structure with several knots [arXiv:2006.07631]. The southwestern portion of this ring is brighter and appears related to a second component, suggesting the system is likely a collisional ring galaxy [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper describes J032700.4+402838 as a very peculiar system with a knotty concentric ring and brighter southwest component, interpreting it as a probable collisional ring galaxy.

quotes.quote: (5) \ J032700.4+402838 ( 1244) The compact core of this galaxy is surrounded by a concentric ring-like structure with several knots. The SW part of the ring is brighter and seems to be related to a second component to the SW of the core and ring. This very peculiar system is probably a collisional ring galaxy.

gmw_00002310.name: J031821.6+410422
gmw_00002310.overview: J031821.6+410422 is a spiral galaxy with a clearly distorted structure [arXiv:2006.07631]. A detailed evaluation of its morphology is difficult due to a bright foreground star superimposed on its central region, while its WISE colors classify it as an intermediate system [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper describes J031821.6+410422 as a distorted spiral whose morphology is hard to judge because of a foreground star, with WHAN star-forming and intermediate WISE classifications.

quotes.quote: (1) \ J031821.6+410422 ( 531, BM 183) The spiral structure of this galaxy is clearly distorted, though a detailed evaluation of the morphology is difficult due to the bright foreground star superimposed with the central region of the galaxy. In the WHAN diagram, the system lies in the SF region. The WISE colours classify it as an intermediate system.

gmw_00002311.name: J032853.7+400211
gmw_00002311.overview: J032853.7+400211 is a Perseus cluster galaxy and a candidate Hoag-type ring galaxy [arXiv:2006.07631]. It features a nearly complete 20-kiloparsec ring with a slightly blue stellar population surrounding a slightly elongated, red core [arXiv:2006.07631].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J032853.7+400211 as a Perseus member and Hoag-type ring-galaxy candidate with a nearly complete 20 kpc ring, red core, bluer ring, and elongated UGC 2751 core.

quotes.quote: (6) \ J032853.7+400211 (UGC 2751) This galaxy is just outside the area covered by the present catalogue but was listed as a Perseus cluster galaxy by Brunzendorf_1999 . We decided to add it to this selection of peculiar galaxies because it may be another candidate of the very rare class of Hoag-type ring galaxies. The morphology resembles Hoag's object Hoag_1950 in that it appears to show a nearly complete ring surrounding a roundish core. The outer diameter of the ring is approximately 20 kpc if the galaxy ( z = 0.0241 ) is at the distance of A 426.

The spectrum of the core is dominated by an old, red stellar population, the ring appears slightly bluer. Possible scenarios for the ring formation in Hoag-type galaxies include resonances in the disc, collision with a small gas-rich galaxy, or the accretion of cold gas Brosch_1985, Schweizer_1987, Buta_1996, Finkelman_2011, Finkelman_et_al_2011 . In contrast to Hoag's object, but similar to the Hoag-type galaxy NGC 6028 Wakamatsu_1990 , the core of UGC 2751 appears to be slightly elongated, reminiscent of a faint bar embedded in a lens and there is luminous material between the core and the ring with a nearly exponential surface brightness profile (scale length 1.6 kpc).

gmw_00002312.name: CEERS-43833
gmw_00002312.overview: CEERS-43833 is a high-redshift galaxy at z=8.7622 [arXiv:2308.05800, arXiv:2306.00647] that features a young stellar population [arXiv:2306.00647] and strong nebular emission lines [arXiv:2306.00647]. The galaxy exhibits a high star-formation surface density [arXiv:2306.00647] and is associated with a proximate damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:2308.05800, arXiv:2306.00647].

sources.summary: The paper describes EGS910-3 as a photometric z~9 galaxy near EGSY-z8p7, targeted with HST follow-up and included in future JWST overdensity mapping.

quotes.quote: This source has a potential companion at <10 pMpc: the galaxy EGS910-3 ( Bouwens2016d , see also Bouwens2019 ). On the sky, these two sources are separated by only 3.7 arcmin. However, the source only has a photometric redshift measurement ( z_ phot =9.0^ +0.5 _ 0.7 as derived in Bouwens2016d ) and has not been confirmed through spectroscopy so far. Hence, the actual physical separation from EGSY-z8p7 is uncertain.
The source EGS910-3 has also been targeted by F105W follow-up observations, which we retrieve from the archive and include in our analysis (see, e.g., Fig fig:sed ).
The approved JWST cycle 1 program GO-2279 NaiduJWSTprop will achieve exactly this based on NIRCam/grism observations of EGSY-z8p7 and EGS910-3, which is only separated by 3.7 arcmin.

sources.summary: The paper compares simulated IGM damping wings to CEERS-43833 at z=8.7622 and finds a proximate DLA improves the fit to its observed spectrum.

quotes.quote: We focus our comparison on CEERS-43833, a galaxy at redshift z=8.7622 arrabalharo2023,finkelstein2023 , as heintz2023 show a version of this spectrum where the transmission has been normalised by the intrinsic transmission, and so can be easily compared with our simulated damping wings. We compute our absorption spectra starting from haloes extracted from a simulation snapshot at z=9 , but rescale the gas density and velocity assuming the host halo is at the redshift of CEERS-43833. The simulated IGM transmission curves we calculate are shown in blue in the top panel of Figure fig:ceers_spec and can be compared with the normalised spectrum of CEERS-43833 shown in black. As in Section sec:compare_umeda23 , we further convolve the spectra with a Gaussian with a width corresponding to the resolution at the Ly wavelength at the redshift of CEERS-43833. We find that even after convolution, the absorption from the IGM alone is not enough to reproduce the damping wing observed in CEERS-43833.

Indeed, when we include the contribution of a DLA with column density N_ HI = 10^ 22.1 cm ^ 2 at the redshift of the host galaxy, we find a much better fit to the observed spectrum, as shown in the bottom panel of Figure fig:ceers_spec .

sources.summary: The paper measures CEERS-43833 at z=8.7622 with strong nebular lines, an extreme DLA column, young stellar population, large inferred gas reservoir, and high star-formation surface density.

quotes.quote: The reduced and photometrically-calibrated 1D spectra of the three galaxies at z=9-11 are shown in Fig. 1. We determine the redshift of CEERS-43833 to be z=8.7622 0.0002 from the multitude of nebular emission lines detected in the spectrum (see Supplementary Materials, Fig. S2), consistent with the first analysis of this spectrum presented in . Similarly, we determine z=11.409 0.001 for CEERS-16943 based on the detection of [ ] 3727 , [ Ne iii ] 3870 , and He i 3889 . This is the most distant detection of nebular emission lines to date. For MACS0647-JD we derive z=10.170 0.003 , consistent with the previous spectroscopic analysis of this galaxy .

We derive best-fit models with (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.10 0.20 for CEERS-43833, (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.20 0.40 for CEERS-16943, and (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.40 0.15 for MACS0647-JD. The uncertainties represent the 16th and 84th percentiles of the posterior distributions and includes the systematic uncertainty of 0.1 dex due to variations in the intrinsic galaxy continuum emission templates.

The inferred column densities also correspond to \ gas surface densities of ( _ gas / M_ pc ^ 2 ) = 2.0 (CEERS-43833), ( _ gas / M_ pc ^ 2 ) = 2.1 (CEERS-16943), and ( _ gas / M_ pc ^ 2 ) = 2.3 (MACS0647-JD), respectively, larger than the gas surface densities observed in typical galaxies at z 0 . Based on the SFRs and physical sizes of the galaxies, we derive SFR surface densities of ( _ SFR / M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) = 1.3 0.3 (CEERS-43833) and ( _ SFR /M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) = 0.9 0.3 (CEERS-16943), and approximately ( _ SFR / M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) 2 for MACS0647-JD.

gmw_00002313.name: host galaxy of GRB 021004
gmw_00002313.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 021004 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitter [arXiv:2105.09314, arXiv:astro-ph/0505542] and active starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574] at a redshift of approximately 2.33 [arXiv:2105.09314, arXiv:astro-ph/0503574]. It features a compact core with a faint second component [arXiv:2105.09314] and may be interacting with a neighboring galaxy located 14 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2105.09314].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 021004 host as a compact, two-component Lyman-alpha emitter with possible neighboring interaction, z=2.3298, strong asymmetric emission, and SFR about 6.7.

quotes.quote: The afterglow and host galaxy of GRB 021004 have been intensively observed (see Fynbo2005 and references therein). Moller2002 , showed the presence of emission in the optical spectrum of the GRB afterglow, which was later confirmed by many other works e.g., Mirabal2003,Starling2005a . The host galaxy of GRB 021004 has a compact core with a faint second component and the GRB site is located at the center of its host Fynbo2005 .

report the detection of [ OIII ] 5007 emission line, at the same redshift as the GRB, from the galaxy eastward of the GRB host in the spectrum obtained with the slit position angle PA=92 ^ . Its proximity to the GRB host (projected distance of 14 kpc) may indicate a possible interaction between this galaxy and the GRB host.

We derive a redshift of z=2.3298 0.0001 for the host galaxy. We find a SFR = 6.7 0.8 (without host extinction correction). The emission from the host galaxy is an asymmetric line redshifted by 180 20 \ km \ s^ 1 with an EW_0 = 105 10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 021004 host as an R 24 active starburst at z=2.328 with 15 M_ /year unobscured star formation, little obscured star formation, and no SCUBA submillimeter detection.

quotes.quote: The large number of lines in the optical spectrum complicated the determination of the redshift of the counterpart, but most likely it was an active starburst R 24 galaxy at z = 2.328 (e.g. Mirabal et al. ; Schaefer et al. ).

As shown in Table table:host , the combination of our ToO observations puts good limits on the quiescent sub-millimeter flux of the host galaxy. These results improve slightly on those shown in Tanvir et al. ( ). The unobscured star formation rate estimated from the Ly luminosity is 15 M_ /year (Djorgovski et al. ). Although this is an active starburst galaxy whose redshift is approximately at the peak for the sub-millimeter bright galaxies, our limit implies that there is not a substantial amount of obscured star formation in the host galaxy.
