The lack of detection of a sub-millimeter host galaxy agrees with the X-ray observations of the afterglow that show no evidence for absorption in excess of the Galactic value (Fox et al. ; Butler et al. ; Holland et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 021004 host as an extended high-redshift Ly emitter with large equivalent width, active star formation, and neighboring candidate Ly-emitting galaxies.

quotes.quote: We find that the host galaxy of GRB 021004 is an extended (around 8 kpc) strong Ly emitter with a restframe equivalent width (EW) of 68^ +12 _ 11 , and a star-formation rate of 10.6 2.0 M _ yr ^ 1 . We do not detect the hosts of GRB 030226 and GRB 020124, but the upper limits on their Ly fluxes do not rule out large restframe EWs. In the fields of GRB 021004 and GRB 030226 we find seven and five other galaxies, respectively, with excess emission in the narrow-band filter. These galaxies are candidate Ly emitting galaxies in the environment of the host galaxies.

The host galaxy of GRB 021004 is detected in all bands and is a Ly emitter with a restframe EW of 68^ +12 _ 11 . Deep HST/ACS images of the host have been reported by fynbo05 . These images show that the host is dominated by a single component with the fwhm extending over 0 12 or roughly 1 kpc at z = 2.335 .

sources.summary: The GRB 021004 host sightline shows saturated high-ion absorption near systemic velocity, additional detected ions, an excited-line non-detection, and two high-velocity absorber complexes.

quotes.quote: GRB 021004 =2.3290 Extremely strong \ absorption is seen at , with a fully saturated trough spreading over 300  . \ is also present and less saturated than , but we cannot discern the true component structure in \ because of low S/N. A component centered at 0  \ is seen in , , and , with absorption also detected in , , , and . ^* 1072.973 is not detected at ; we measure log N ( ^* ) < 14.19 (3 ) over the velocity range 60 to 60  , and log N ( )=15.30 0.29, so that N ( ^* )/ N ( ) < 0.08. Two HV absorbers are observed: one centered at 2 900  \ relative to , the other centered at 600  .

sources.summary: UVES spectroscopy considered six host-galaxy absorption systems toward GRB 021004 and detected multiple low- and high-ionization species; wide spectral coverage constrained gas ionization to a narrow, velocity-independent range.

quotes.quote: For this GRB we consider the absorption systems at the following redshifts and velocities (in km/s) with respect to the Ly emission of the host galaxy (Mirabal et al. 2003): z=2.328, v=0 (system A in figure sys ); z=2.328, v=--139 km/s (system B); z=2.328, v=--224 km/s (system C); z=2.321; z=2.298; z=2.296. For these systems we detected C 4 , C 2 , Si 4 , Al 2 , Al 3 , Mg 2 and Fe 2 lines (see Table 3).

The UVES wide band coverage allowed us to investigate simultaneously both high ionization lines such as C 4 and Si 4 , and low ionization lines such as Mg 2 and Fe 2 in GRB 021004. This allowed us to constrain the ionization parameter of the gas of the differerent absorption systems. Combined with photoionization results obtained with CLOUDY the ionization parameters appear to lie in a relatively small range, with no clear trend with the system velocity.

gmw_00002314.name: host galaxy of GRB 030226
gmw_00002314.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 030226 is a high-redshift galaxy that remains undetected in optical bands down to a limit of R = 26 [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574, arXiv:astro-ph/0505542]. Submillimeter observations and broadband optical spectra indicate that it is not a bright dusty galaxy and lacks substantial dust extinction [arXiv:astro-ph/0503574].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 030226 host as undetected optically to R=26 and not a bright dusty galaxy, with submillimeter limits and no optical evidence for substantial dust reddening.

quotes.quote: The redshift of the optical counterpart was determined to be 1.986 (Ando et al. ; Price et al. ; Chornock Filippenko ; Klose et al. ). No optical host galaxy was detected down to a limit of R = 26 (Klose et al. ).

As shown in Table table:host , the combination of our ToO observations puts good limits on the quiescent 850 m flux of the host galaxy. Similarly, at 450 m the combined flux density is 1 10 mJy. Although the redshift of the galaxy is at approximately the peak for sub-millimeter bright galaxies, the host for GRB 030226 is not a bright dusty galaxy. This agrees with the broadband optical spectrum, which shows no evidence for additional reddening by dust in the host galaxy, and the low polarization of the afterglow, which argues against substantial dust extinction (Klose et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper reports no detection of the GRB 030226 host in its bands, gives Ly flux and broadband limits, and says large rest-frame Ly equivalent width is still possible.

quotes.quote: GRB 030226 The host galaxy of GRB 030226 is not detected in any of the bands. We can set an upper limit ( 5 ) of n_ AB 25.8 , corresponding to an observed emission line flux of 2.4 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

The lack of Ly emission from the hosts of GRB 030226 and GRB 020124 in our deep narrow-band images does not rule out large restframe EWs. Assuming a GRB 020124 host magnitude of R_ AB 29.5 consistent with the deep limit from berger , a restframe Ly EW of 500 \ would have remained undetectable. The corresponding value for GRB 030226, assuming a host magnitude of U_ AB 26.5 , is 40 .

gmw_00002315.name: GRB 020124 host
gmw_00002315.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 020124 is an extremely faint high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0502494, arXiv:astro-ph/0505542]. It was not detected in narrow-band imaging, which does not rule out a large Lyman-alpha equivalent width [arXiv:astro-ph/0505542].

sources.summary: The paper gives stringent host-galaxy magnitude limits for GRB 020124 and argues that its redshift had to be measured from the afterglow rather than host-galaxy spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy identification \ and \ have very faint hosts: R 29.5 for \ , and V=28.0 for \ . The early localization of these two GRBs, and the quick identification of their afterglows, made possible the identification of the host galaxy of GRB 030323, and placed stringent limits on the magnitude of the host galaxy of GRB 020124.

The faintness of the host galaxies of these two bursts shows the impossibility to measure their redshifts from the spectroscopy of their host galaxies. \ and \ are examples of GRBs whose redshifts can only be measured at early times from the spectrum of the afterglow (unlike what was done for GRB 971214). The fact that two of the four GRBs known with z 3 occurred in faint galaxies, may indicate that a non-negligible fraction of star formation takes place in such faint galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper says the GRB 020124 host was not detected in narrow-band imaging; because it is very faint, the non-detection does not exclude a large Ly equivalent width.

quotes.quote: GRB 020124 Lacking broadband observations for the GRB 020124 field, we are unable to identify LEGO candidates. The host galaxy is not detected above a limit of 1.5 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ( 5 ) in the narrow-band image. Since the host is very faint berger , this non-detection does not exclude a large Ly EW (see below).

The lack of Ly emission from the hosts of GRB 030226 and GRB 020124 in our deep narrow-band images does not rule out large restframe EWs. Assuming a GRB 020124 host magnitude of R_ AB 29.5 consistent with the deep limit from berger , a restframe Ly EW of 500 \ would have remained undetectable.

gmw_00002316.name: NGC 522
gmw_00002316.overview: NGC 522 is a disk galaxy featuring a flat radial disk with an outer truncation [arXiv:1409.6727] and a broken exponential major-axis profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0703768]. The galaxy contains a boxy/peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:2203.01154] and exhibits a slight asymmetry that may indicate rare bar buckling [arXiv:2203.01154, arXiv:astro-ph/0703768].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 522 lacks a required super-thin disk, has a flat radial disk with an outer truncation, and may contain an unusually extended bar-like inner component.

quotes.quote: NGC 522's surface-brightness profile is notable for several reasons. First, the outer disk truncation at 13 kpc in radius appears to be a downward break of the global radial surface brightness profile rather than a truncation of a single disk component, as seen in NGCs 891 and 4013. Modeling such breaks in the radial slope is beyond the scope of this paper.

NGC 522's disk is also quite flat in radius, with scale-lengths roughly 1.5-2 times as large as those found for the other fast rotators in our sample. This results in a scale-length to scale-height ratio of 20 for the thin disk, significantly larger than found in normal spirals Bershady10b . We see no evidence for a super-thin disk component in NGC 522, although due to the uncertainty in our dust correction at this distance we reiterate that we may be insensitive to such a component.

sources.summary: The paper marks NGC 522 as a studied B/PS-bulge galaxy with an interesting, asymmetric residue that may indicate rare local-universe bar buckling.

quotes.quote: NGC 522 Although this galaxy appears to have a fairly usual B/PS bulge and has already been studied in , its residue is interesting. It shows clear asymmetry with respect to the major axis of the galaxy. Asymmetry can be an indicator of the bar buckling, which is very rare in the local Universe Erwin_Debattista2017,2021ApJ...909..125X . Although the dust lane here is also slightly bent towards the upper side of the galaxy, this factor can lead to an unusual residue structure. We provide the corresponding original image and the residue as the suspicious example of a buckled galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 522 as a disk-truncation comparison case: optical data show a two-slope break, while its near-infrared profile is ambiguous between smooth and two-slope behavior.

quotes.quote: One of them is NGC 522, which was measured in the optical by Pohlen et al. (2002b). This galaxy is a very good example of a two-slope with a narrow break region in between, when measured in the optical. However, as can be seen in Fig. 5, the J-profile on the right hand side seems to be smooth. The left side as well as the right Ks side could be described as a two-slope. The data does not allow us to clearly distinguish between these two types.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 522 as slightly asymmetric, with contaminating background objects in the image and a clear broken exponential major-axis profile with a very steep outer decline.

quotes.quote: NGC 522: Our image has some residual structure in the flatfield which reaches the disk on the SW(right)-side at (R,z) (+10 ,-15 ) \ and at (R,z) (+60 ,-30 ) causing the unusually shaped contours. There is a galaxy cluster in the background with an edge-on spiral galaxy superimposed at (R,z) (-13 ,-17 ) and another elliptical at (R,z) (-60 ,-20 ). The major axis radial profile shows a clear broken exponential behaviour with a very steep outer decline (dropping by 5 mag within 25 ) on both sides, starting at R 60 \ ( _ R 21.5 ). Even the edge-on profiles show clearly a decrease in the outer slope with increasing z (see below). The galaxy is slightly asymmetric with the NE(left)-side shorter than the SW(right)-side.

gmw_00002317.name: NGC 1055
gmw_00002317.overview: NGC 1055 is a spiral galaxy characterized by an extended, boxy bulge [arXiv:1409.6727, arXiv:astro-ph/0401057] and a northern dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0401057]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology that may be due to a recent interaction with Messier 77 [arXiv:1409.6727] or a soft merger with a satellite galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0401057].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1055 as a disturbed fast rotator with extended bulge and dust, difficult modeling, yet substantial very thin disk light surviving or reforming after interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 1055 NGC 1055 is morphologically very different from the other fast-rotators in our sample, notably in its bright, extended bulge (most visible in the SDSS image in Figure fig:gri_jhk_sample ), but also in the large number of coherent dust features visible away from the midplane. It is in a loose group with the extremely fast-rotating (HyperLEDA gives V_ rot 280 km sec ^ 1 ) spiral Messier 77, and a recent interaction between these two systems could explain the apparent disarray in NGC 1055's disk. While its unique features make it an especially interesting object to examine, they also conspire to make NGC 1055 tremendously difficult to model.

This model is also notable for the sheer thinness of its disks. The doubly-truncated component, with scale-height only 290 pc, is at least twice as thick as the other two disks. Despite the limits of our ability to model NGC 1055 given its disturbed morphology, the fits we can make all point strongly to the presence of significant amounts of starlight with h_ z 200 pc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1055 as a thick-boxy-bulge galaxy with an extended quadratic bulge, dust lane, possible bar too small to explain the boxiness, and H I evidence for soft merging.

quotes.quote: NGC 1055 (Fig. 1055 ) is already well studied in the literature (e.g. Shaw ). The boxy isophotal distortions within the bulge component are spatially extended and form almost a quadratic bulge. While on one side the disk is bending away from the central plane and is clearly separated from the bulge, on the other side bulge and disk overlap. Additionally, the prominent dust lane on the northern side of the galaxy is remarkable.

Regarding the merger/accretion scenario for the origin of b/p bulges (cf. Sect. 1) Vergani et al. ( ) find evidences for a soft merger between NGC 1055 and a satellite analysing 21-cm HI observations. Asymmetries in the distribution and in the kinematics of the neutral hydrogen and particularly the asymmetric clumpy HI emission in the outermost northwestern region indicate an unrelaxed structure typical for tidal interactions and accreted material enlarging the bulge.

gmw_00002318.name: NGC 4144
gmw_00002318.overview: NGC 4144 is a galaxy with an asymmetric light distribution that becomes increasingly pronounced at larger radii [arXiv:1409.6727]. Structural modeling of the galaxy favors a disk-plus-bulge configuration, featuring a flattened bulge with a Seersic index near unity [arXiv:1409.6727].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4144 difficult to model because of asymmetric light and inclination degeneracy, favoring a disk-plus-bulge model with a large disk scale height.

quotes.quote: NGC 4144 Modeling NGC 4144 is challenging because at large radii there is a clear asymmetry in the light distribution, visible both in our WHIRC and SDSS images (Figure fig:gri_jhk_sample ). This asymmetry appears to increase at larger radii. Therefore we choose to perform our fits restricted to be within a projected radius of 2 kpc from the galaxy center.

We found a single disk and a S e rsic bulge were able to fit the data at all radii and heights (shown as the red solid line in Figure fig:ngc4144fitplot ) better that the previous models, and with no failings. The AIC value for this model is 16 lower than for our initial model. The fitted parameters of this model are listed in the rightmost column of Table tab:ngc4144bestfits . The bulge is quite flattened ( a/b =3.54) and has a S e rsic index near unity, making it similar, but not identical to, an exponential disk with the same oblateness. While the bulge has a brighter central surface brightness, the disk component has twice the luminosity of the bulge due to its more extended light distribution.

gmw_00002319.name: IRAS 18030+0705
gmw_00002319.overview: IRAS 18030+0705 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy that resembles an active galactic nucleus-dominated galaxy [arXiv:2211.07758]. It exhibits a highly peaked spectrum and displays the strongest polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission of all ultraluminous infrared galaxies in its sample [arXiv:2211.07758].

sources.summary: The paper highlights IRAS 18030+0705 as an outlier ULIRG with the sample strongest PAH emission, an extreme peaked spectrum, and placement resembling an AGN-dominated galaxy.

quotes.quote: Previous studies suggest that IRAS 08572+3915, which displays deep silicate absorption and minimal PAH emission Spoon2007 , is the most luminous ULIRG in the local ( z < 0.1 ) universe and is thought to be an AGN-dominated galaxy with its torus viewed almost edge-on Efstathiou2014 ; IRAS 18030+0705 displays the strongest PAH emission of all ULIRGs in the sample Spoon2007 ; IRAS 13218+0552 is a known quasar with extreme outflows Lipari2003 ; and finally IRAS 00397-1312 is the most luminous galaxy in the sample Farrah2009 , showing deep silicate absorption and weak PAH features as in the case of IRAS 08572+3915.

On the other hand IRAS 18030+0705 - an extraordinary object with highly peaked spectrum - occupies the highest point on the vertical (PC5) axis of our diagram, indicating that its dust content does not affect its main features. Our diagram suggests that IRAS 18030+0705 resembles more an AGN-dominated galaxy, since it occupies the upper-right part of the spheroid, but it simultaneously displays other extreme features, such as extreme PAH values.

gmw_00002320.name: UGC 6894
gmw_00002320.overview: UGC 6894 is an edge-on dwarf galaxy with a visible disk [arXiv:1311.4559] and a low star formation rate [arXiv:1903.04119]. It is associated with a nearby absorption system, which may trace cool gas accreting onto the galaxy [arXiv:1311.4559, arXiv:1903.04119].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6894 as a likely absorber counterpart, giving its distance, impact parameter, velocity offset, halo scale, and arguing that the absorber may trace ancient outflow material or cool gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Here we report a blindly-detected C 4 absorption system ( z_ abs = 0.00348 ) in the spectrum of PG1148+549 that appears to be associated either with an edge-on dwarf galaxy with an obvious disk (UGC 6894, z_ gal = 0.00283) at an impact parameter of = 190 kpc or with a very faint dwarf irregular galaxy at = 23 kpc, which is closer to the sightline but has a larger redshift difference ( z_ gal = 0.00107 , i.e., v =724 km/s). We consider various gas/galaxy associations, including infall and outflows. Based on current theoretical models, we conclude that the absorber is most likely tracing (1) the remnants of an outflow from a previous epoch, a so-called ‘ancient outflow', or (2) intergalactic gas accreting onto UGC 6894, ‘cold mode' accretion.

We assume an 18.6 Mpc distance Verheijen:2001lr for UGC 6894, and we note that this distance is much greater than that indicated by a pure Hubble flow from the heliocentric redshift (12.0 Mpc) but is similar to that obtained when correcting for the LG velocity, etc. (17.1 Mpc). At a distance of 18.6 Mpc, the corresponding angular scale is 5.41 kpc/arcmin, and the galaxy's 35.210 arcmin angular distance from the sightline then corresponds to an impact parameter of 190.5 kpc. The recession velocities of the absorber and galaxy differ by 196.9 15.1 km/s, where the uncertainty is dominated by the COS wavelength calibration uncertainty of 15 km/s COShandbook ; Verheijen:2001lr report a redshift uncertainty of 2 km/s for UGC 6894. As shown in Figure 2, the QSO sightline passes almost directly to the east, lying along a direction oriented just 2 ^ south of east from the edge-on major axis of UGC 6894. Based on the four virial radii calculated in Section 3, the absorber appears to be located 1.2-2.7 from the center of UGC 6894.

sources.summary: The paper places UGC 6894 152 kpc from the absorber, notes a small velocity offset and low star formation rate, and cites prior work interpreting the absorber as cool gas accreting onto it.

quotes.quote: Figure 10 shows the two bright galaxies UGC 6894 and LEDA 2492981 close by in impact parameter to the absorber, at 152 kpc and 172 kpc respectively.
On the other hand, UGC 6894 is at | v| = 185 km/s and = 152 kpc ( 1.4R_ vir ) from the absorber. Burchett2013 infer the absorber to be a cool gas cloud accreted by UGC 6894 .
A similar low SFR of 0.01 M _ yr ^ 1 is also estimated for UGC 6894 .

gmw_00002321.name: the Grapes
gmw_00002321.overview: The Grapes is a clumpy dwarf irregular galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.00107 [arXiv:1311.4559]. It has a minimum star formation rate of 5.9 × 10⁻⁶ solar masses per year [arXiv:1311.4559] and a stellar mass estimated around 5 × 10⁵ solar masses [arXiv:1903.04119].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Grapes as a clumpy dwarf irregular 23 kpc from the sightline, gives its H-emission redshift and minimum SFR, and notes its 724 km/s velocity separation from the absorber.

quotes.quote: The Grapes is a dwarf irregular galaxy located at z=0.00107 0.0007 with an impact parameter of 23 kpc. The redshift was measured from the H emission line found in the Lick/Kast spectrum, and we measure a minimum star formation rate (SFR) of 5.9 10^ 6 /yr from this line Kennicutt:1998fj .

This is a minimum SFR for two reasons: first, we do not measure the H emission and therefore cannot correct for dust reddening; second, the Grapes is very clumpy, and our long-slit spectrum did not cover all star clusters in the galaxy. SDSS (DR10) detected the Grapes in its imaging, but resolved it into three individual clumps, two of which are labeled as faint galaxies. From the SDSS photometry, we estimate stellar masses of = 4.8 10^5 , 9.1 10^3 , and 2.3 10^4 for these three clumps.

While the Grapes has by far the smallest projected distance and therefore is a prime candidate for the absorbing gas source, the galaxy is separated in velocity by v=724 210 km/s , where uncertainties of 15 km/s and 210 km/s arise from the COS wavelength calibration and the Kast H measurement, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J 115205.58+544732.2 as a dwarf irregular galaxy near the absorber, estimates its luminosity from color and stellar mass, and rejects absorber association because of velocity separation.

quotes.quote: They found, through deep imaging and spectroscopy, a dwarf irregular galaxy (SDSS J 115205.58+544732.2 ) unregistered in the SDSS spectroscopic database, at a much closer impact parameter of 23 kpc (See Figure 10 ). Using the galaxy's g - = 0.2 color, stellar mass of 5 10^5 M _ Burchett2013 , and the (M/L) scaling relationship for dwarf irregular galaxies Herrmann2016 , we estimate the galaxy's luminosity to be L_g 10^6 L_ , which is consistent with its non-detection in the SDSS spectroscopic database. Burchett2013 conclude that the absorber is unlikely to be associated with this dwarf galaxy because of its large velocity separation ( v = +724 ) with the absorber.

gmw_00002322.name: UGC 1382
gmw_00002322.overview: UGC 1382 is a giant low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:1910.14045] that was previously misclassified as an early-type system [arXiv:2209.09906, arXiv:1910.14045]. Its extended gaseous disk counter-rotates relative to its stars [arXiv:2112.10731, arXiv:2004.14458], and it forms a close pair with an associated compact elliptical satellite [arXiv:2112.10731, arXiv:2209.09906].

sources.summary: The paper reports UGC 1382 in the seven-galaxy gLSBG sample, finding counter-rotating gas relative to stars and an associated compact elliptical satellite confirmed with Subaru imaging and SDSS spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: In this paper we briefly summarize the efforts that we made to understand the formation scenario of gLSBGs by observing a sample of 7 gLSBGs: Malin 1, Malin 2, NGC 7589, UGC 1378, UGC 1382, UGC 1922, and UGC 6614. The results of long-slit spectral observations. We performed spectral long-slit observations at the 6-m BTA telescope of SAO RAS using SCORPIO and SCORPIO-2 spectrographs AfanasievMoiseev2005, AfanasievMoiseev2011 . The details of the data reduction and analysis could be found in Saburova2018,Saburova2019,Saburova2021 . The most prominent result of these observations is the discovered kinematically decoupled components in UGC 1922 and UGC 1382. In UGC 1382, the global gaseous disk counter-rotates with respect to stars, and this suggests the external origin of gas in the extended disk.

Further visual inspection of high-quality optical image of UGC 1382 from Subaru Hyper-SuprimeCam Strategic survey revealed additional cE-satellite of gLSBG, the SDSS spectral data confirmed the physical association.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1382 as a known giant low-surface-brightness system in the HSC footprint, with elliptical misclassification, an H I measurement, and a compact satellite close pair.

quotes.quote: Two well-known examples of such systems are UGC 1382 and UGC 1922 Hagen2016, Saburovaetal2018 .

HyperLeda reports the morphology for most galaxies as early-type spirals (Sbc or earlier), and some are mis-classified as ellipticals (e.g. UGC 1382), which indicates the presence of prominent bulges. Bars are noted in 3 out of 42 galaxies. However, further visual inspection of HSC images revealed 8 barred galaxies among 42 (a fraction of 1/5.25) including 6 gLSBGs (1/6.2), where bars are found half as frequently as in HSB galaxies 1/3.2 as found by Saburova2018 . We also searched for data for the considered galaxies in ALFALFA Haynes2018 and HIPASS wong2006 and found measurements only for UGC 1382 and 2MASXJ02015377+0131087 corresponding to masses (M_ HI / ) of 10.2 and 10.3 respectively.

Another aspect of the environment is the membership in a close pair. We applied the close pair selection criterion from Ventou2019 and concluded that only UGC 1382 possesses a compact satellite projected on its disc with a close velocity that could be classified as a close pair.

sources.summary: Dragonfly detects UGC 1382 as a giant low-surface-brightness spiral, recovering smooth interarm light and a larger disk extent than previously known.

quotes.quote: The first is the giant low surface brightness spiral galaxy, UGC 1382, which falls in our survey area. This galaxy was long thought to be an early-type system, but in 2016 a giant disk was discovered in GALEX data ( 2016ApJ...826..210H ). The left panel of Figure fig:example-spiral shows the DECaLS image used in the MRF process, and the middle and right panels show the Dragonfly data. We detect smooth light in between the spiral arms in the galaxy and a larger extent of the disk than was previously known.

sources.summary: The paper links UGC 1382's gaseous counter-rotation to possible gas accretion onto a pre-formed early-type galaxy, notes an undisturbed velocity field, and rules out an unusually large dark-halo scale.

quotes.quote: UGC 1382 . The counter-rotation of gaseous disc could indicate the accretion of the gas on the pre-formed early-type galaxy. velocity field is not disturbed Hagenetal2016 which gives evidences against recent major merger. We also can rule out the scenario in which the extended disc is formed due to unusually high radial scale of dark halo, since the parameters of the halo deviate from the expected for high radius of the LSB-disc.

gmw_00002323.name: UGC 08071
gmw_00002323.overview: UGC 08071 is a galaxy with a radial velocity of 6933 km/s [arXiv:2305.03073]. Although it overlaps with LEDA 083688 along the line of sight, the galaxy shows no signs of asymmetry, making a physical interaction between the pair highly unlikely [arXiv:2305.03073].

sources.summary: The paper considers UGC 08071 as a line-of-sight candidate origin for the stream, but finds no asymmetry and a 6933 km/s radial velocity inconsistent with interaction with LEDA 083688.

quotes.quote: In particular, the pair of overlapping galaxies UGC 08071 and LEDA 083688 could be candidates to produce the stream. However, our very deep images do not show any sign of asymmetry in them. Furthermore, the radial velocities of UGC 08071 and LEDA 083688 are 6933 ± 2 km s ^ 1 and 8169 ± 2 km s ^ 1 respectively, thus located more than 1200 km s ^ 1 apart in velocity space (the radial velocity of galaxies in Coma ranges from 4000 to 10000 km s ^ 1 ). This makes it highly unlikely that they could have interacted at such a high velocity while leaving no asymmetry in the time during which the stream has expanded. The other galaxies in the line of sight also show no appreciable asymmetry that would be compatible with the occurrence of the stream.

gmw_00002324.name: LEDA 083688
gmw_00002324.overview: LEDA 083688 is a galaxy with a radial velocity of 8169 km/s [arXiv:2305.03073]. Although it overlaps with UGC 08071 along the line of sight, its high velocity separation and lack of asymmetry make a physical interaction between the two galaxies highly unlikely [arXiv:2305.03073].

sources.summary: The paper considers LEDA 083688 as a line-of-sight candidate origin for the stream, but finds no asymmetry and an 8169 km/s radial velocity inconsistent with interaction with UGC 08071.

quotes.quote: In particular, the pair of overlapping galaxies UGC 08071 and LEDA 083688 could be candidates to produce the stream. However, our very deep images do not show any sign of asymmetry in them. Furthermore, the radial velocities of UGC 08071 and LEDA 083688 are 6933 ± 2 km s ^ 1 and 8169 ± 2 km s ^ 1 respectively, thus located more than 1200 km s ^ 1 apart in velocity space (the radial velocity of galaxies in Coma ranges from 4000 to 10000 km s ^ 1 ). This makes it highly unlikely that they could have interacted at such a high velocity while leaving no asymmetry in the time during which the stream has expanded. The other galaxies in the line of sight also show no appreciable asymmetry that would be compatible with the occurrence of the stream.

gmw_00002325.name: Arp 140
gmw_00002325.overview: Arp 140 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of NGC 274 and NGC 275 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301471]. The system is surrounded by a large-scale distribution of neutral hydrogen, which includes extended tidal tails reaching north and south of the optical galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0301471].

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 140 as an interacting NGC 274/275 pair with close projected separation, extended H I structure, a large neutral-gas mass, and tidal tails inconsistent with direct collision.

quotes.quote: Arp 140 consists of two interacting galaxies; NGC 274 (SAB()0 pec) and NGC 275 (SB(rs)cd pec). At an assumed distance of 26.9 Mpc, the projected major axis diameter corresponding to D_ 25 is 11.7 kpc (1.5 ' ) for both NGC 274 and NGC 275 and the projected separation of their centres is approximately 6.6 kpc (50 '' ).

We detect a large-scale distribution of Hi around Arp 140 (Figure 1a). The total measured Hi flux is 30.5 \ Jy km s ^ 1 , corresponding to a mass of 5.1 10^ 9 M _ . An extended Hi tail reaches approximately 4' south of the optical galaxies and 2.5' north, inconsistent with a direct collision between the two galaxies.

gmw_00002326.name: Arp 104
gmw_00002326.overview: Arp 104 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of the elliptical galaxy NGC 5216 and the peculiar spiral galaxy NGC 5218 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701452, arXiv:astro-ph/0301471]. The system features a smooth stellar tidal bridge connecting the two galaxies, alongside a northern tidal tail rich in neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0701452, arXiv:1009.0219].

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 104 as a spiral--elliptical interacting pair whose H I morphology includes a northeast tidal tail, a bridge toward NGC 5216, and blue tidal features.

quotes.quote: Arp 104, shown in Fig. a104hi_mom0.fig , consists of two interacting galaxies, see NED see http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ : the southern galaxy, NGC 5216, is an E0 pec ( 2 5 1 5 ) and the northern galaxy, NGC 5218, is an SB(s)b? pec ( 1 8 1 3 ). At an assumed distance of 41.1 Mpc (from a recessional velocity of 2880 , and using a Hubble constant of 70 Mpc ^ 1 ), the projected major axis diameter ( D_ 25 ) is 29.9 kpc ( 2 5 ) for NGC 5216 and 21.5 kpc ( 1 8 ) for NGC 5218. The projected separation of the two galaxies is 49.1 kpc. 1990AJ.....99..497S have conducted a multicolour photometric study of the optical tidal features of a sample of interacting galaxies, including Arp 104. They found the tidal features of Arp 104 are bluer than the central regions of both NGC 5218 and NGC 5216, but similar to the colours of the outer disk in NGC 5218.

The combined VLA C- and D-array map of Arp 104 (Fig. a104hi_mom0.fig ) reveals a tidal tail to the north-east of the late-type system (NGC 5218), and a tidal bridge extending southward toward the early-type companion (NGC 5216).

The tidal tail to the north-east forms a quarter-circle arc with a radius of approximately 4' , corresponding to a physical radius of 48 kpc. The tidal bridge to the south is approximately 5' long, corresponding to a physical length of 60 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 104 as an interacting galaxy pair with a smooth stellar bridge, asymmetric tidal features, and evidence for a hierarchical merger pathway toward an early-type remnant.

quotes.quote: We chose the Arp 104 system for an optical imaging study based on curiosity concerning its apparently extremely smooth optically visible tidal bridge (see Schombert et al. 1990; hereafter SWS90, Smith et al. 2007), which led to its inclusion in Arp's (1966) Atlas of Peculiar Galaxies . This primarily stellar bridge connects NGC 5216, the E galaxy southern member of the pair, to the northern disturbed disk galaxy, NGC 5218, over a projected span of 38 kpc based on an assumed distance of 42 Mpc (0.20 kpc arcsec ^ 1 ) to the system. Beyond its optically visible features, Arp 104 also has several intriguing physical characteristics, many of which are discussed by Roche (2006), Cullen et al.

Arp 104 therefore shows a highly asymmetric set of tidal features. The majority of the HI gas lies in NGC 5218 and the northern tidal tail, while most of the tidally stripped stars are in the inter-galaxy bridge.

The combination of our optical measurements of the stellar structure and colors of Arp 104 and multi-wavelength studies of Roche (2006) and C07 indicate that Arp 104 is the product of an interesting sequence of astrophysical processes: As described by C07, a prograde close passage between the disky NGC 5218 galaxy and the elliptical NGC 5216 occurred about 300 Myr in the past. The smooth stellar tidal bridge is a product of that interaction and comparisons with models show the two galaxies are likely to be separating.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 104 as an interacting NGC 5216/5218 system with wide separation, H I tidal structures, a disrupted spiral, an elliptical tail, and optical features reproduced in H I.

quotes.quote: Arp 104 consists of two interacting galaxies; NGC 5216 E0 pec (angular size of NGC 5216 is 2.5' 1.5' ), and NGC 5218 SB(s)b? pec (angular size 1.8' 1.3' ). At an assumed distance of 44.3 Mpc, the projected major axis diameter corresponding to D_ 25 for NGC 5216 is 32.2 kpc (2.5 ' ) and 23.2 kpc for NGC 5218 (1.8 ' ) and the projected separation of their centres is 52.9 kpc.

The northern spiral galaxy appears disrupted with possible bar formation. It has a tidal plume extending to the north and a bridge to the south. The southern galaxy is an elliptical with a small tail in the south west. These optical features are all reproduced in the low resolution Hi map of the Arp 104 system (Figure 1b).

gmw_00002327.name: NGC 5218
gmw_00002327.overview: NGC 5218 is a barred spiral disk galaxy that forms an interacting pair with NGC 5216 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301471, arXiv:astro-ph/0701452]. It exhibits a disturbed, non-coplanar structure with a northern tidal plume, a southern bridge, and centrally concentrated molecular gas driving active star formation [arXiv:1009.0219, arXiv:astro-ph/0701452].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5218 as the disturbed disk member of Arp 104, with central dusty star formation, non-coplanar disk structure, shells, and signs of a recent merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 5218 NGC 5218, the northern member of the Arp 104 pair, is well known to have a disturbed structure. Its fundamental characteristics as a disk galaxy, however, are clear from the optical structure and from results of the radial brightness profile determined from isophotal surface photometry shown in Figure 6 as well as from surface photometry in Roche (2006). A simple interpretation of Figure 6 is to consider it as indicating the presence of two approximately exponential disks. The inner disk then has a scale length of 9 arcsec (2 kpc) while the outer disk scale length increases at radii > 75 arcsec where the luminosity is increasingly dominated by the tidal debris, which have a shallower mean radial intensity profile than that of the outer disk. A high surface brightness central linear feature with a length of 15 arcsec (3 kpc), possibly a bar as suggested by C07 and the kinematics found by Olsson et al.

We therefore conclude that the disk system in NGC 5218 is unlikely to be coplanar, but instead shows the large range in angular momentum vector direction that is typical of galaxies which have experienced recent mergers (Barnes 2002). The combination of structures and kinematics in NGC 5218 is reminiscent of the out-of-plane features seen in polar ring galaxies, such as NGC 3718 (Sparke et al. 2009), and suggests that NGC 5218 is the product of a relatively recent merger. As a result of this event, the optical structure of NGC 5218 is still evolving.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5218 as Arp 104's spiral component, describing its size, disruption, possible bar, northern tidal plume, bridge, and H I features within the interacting system.

quotes.quote: Arp 104 consists of two interacting galaxies; NGC 5216 E0 pec (angular size of NGC 5216 is 2.5' 1.5' ), and NGC 5218 SB(s)b? pec (angular size 1.8' 1.3' ). At an assumed distance of 44.3 Mpc, the projected major axis diameter corresponding to D_ 25 for NGC 5216 is 32.2 kpc (2.5 ' ) and 23.2 kpc for NGC 5218 (1.8 ' ) and the projected separation of their centres is 52.9 kpc. The measured Hi flux is 17.1 Jy km s ^ 1 , corresponding to a mass of 7.8 10^ 9 M _ The northern spiral galaxy appears disrupted with possible bar formation. It has a tidal plume extending to the north and a bridge to the south.

These optical features are all reproduced in the low resolution Hi map of the Arp 104 system (Figure 1b). A large tidal plume extends to the north east of NGC 5218 approximately 2 arcmin in length (26 kpc). Similarly, a bridge extends from the spiral to elliptical component, with an increase in column density of the Hi centred on the small tidal tail extending to the south west of the elliptical.

sources.summary: NGC 5218 is presented as the late-type barred spiral in Arp 104, with centrally concentrated molecular gas, interaction-linked bar enhancement, active star formation, and normal SBb-like efficiency.

quotes.quote: We present data probing the spatial and kinematical distribution of both the atomic ( ) and molecular (CO) gas in NGC 5218, the late-type barred spiral galaxy in the spiral--elliptical interacting pair, Arp 104. We consider these data in conjunction with far-infrared and radio continuum data, and N body simulations, to study the galaxies interactions, and the star formation properties of NGC 5218. We use these data to assess the importance of the bar and tidal interaction on the evolution of NGC 5218, and the extent to which the tidal interaction may have been important in triggering the bar. The molecular gas distribution of NGC 5218 appears to have been strongly affected by the bar; the distribution is centrally condensed with a very large surface density in the central region. The N body simulations indicate a timescale since perigalacticon of 3 10^ 8 yr, which is consistent with the interaction having triggered or enhanced the bar potential in NGC 5218, leading to inflow and the large central molecular gas density observed.

gmw_00002328.name: COSMOS-7447
gmw_00002328.overview: COSMOS-7447 is a high-redshift early-type galaxy [arXiv:1912.00084] located at a redshift of z=1.800 [arXiv:1204.3099]. It is a compact, high-mass galaxy with a post-starburst spectrum and very little ongoing star formation [arXiv:1204.3099].

sources.summary: The paper includes COSMOS-7447 as a high-redshift ETG, reanalyses its stellar population, finds discordant fitting results, and reports uncertain super-solar to sub-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the other 3 targets, COSMOS- 7447 , UDS- 19627 and UDS- 29410 have been previously analyzed by vandesande (and references therein), who performed the measurement of the velocity dispersion exploiting the high resolution of X-Shooter data, in order to study its evolution to z 0 . They derive stellar population parameters as well as performing spectral fitting on the binned spectra ( 10 ) using the FAST code kriek09 . Differently from this work they used the models with an exponentially declining star formation history and a IMF. In this work, we perform a detailed spectral and spectro-photometric analysis of the galaxy stellar populations, using a novel approach that combine optical and UV indices and consider several options for the origin of the UV flux besides that of ongoing star formation.
An exception is COSMOS-7447 for which the result from the full spectral fitting value is in disagreement with those from the other two methods.

COSMOS-7447 shows a super-solar metallicity, but its large error bar makes it consistent also with sub-solar values. A possibility similar to the previous one is that, given their high mass and the arguments previously exposed, also these galaxies have formed in a short burst at z 2-2.5 from high-metallicity gas, but their metal content have been already diluted by merging events happened soon after the formation and that involved the whole galaxy, not only its external regions.

sources.summary: NMBS-C7447 is described as a z=1.800 COSMOS-field galaxy with X-shooter velocity dispersion, compact effective radius, post-starburst spectrum, little star formation, and high stellar mass.

quotes.quote: NMBS-C7447 From an X-shooter spectrum of a redshift z=1.800 galaxy in the COSMOS field, derive a velocity dispersion of = 294 51 km s ^ 1 , a dynamical mass M _ dyn =(1.7 0.5) 10^ 11 M_ , and from HST/WFC3 observations in the F160W band, an effective radius of r_e=1.64 0.15 kpc and Sersic parameter n=5.3 0.4 . The X-shooter spectrum has a typical post starburst shape, with prominent Balmer lines, and the stellar population synthesis fits to the spectrum is consistent with little ongoing star formation ( 0.002M_ yr^ 1 ), a relatively young age 0.4 Gyr, corresponding to a formation redshift of z_f 2 and a stellar mass 1.5 10^ 11 M_ . In the MUSYC catalog gawiser2006 it has H=19.85 and K=19.75 , corresponding to an L_K 23L_K^* galaxy marchesini2007 .

gmw_00002329.name: UDS-19627
gmw_00002329.overview: UDS-19627 is a massive, compact quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z ~ 2 [arXiv:1204.3099, arXiv:1912.00084]. It exhibits a post-starburst spectrum with Balmer series absorption lines and a low star formation rate [arXiv:1204.3099].

sources.summary: UDS19627 is the z~2 compact quiescent target with measured coordinates, high stellar mass, post-starburst spectrum, low star formation, and a dynamical mass from velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The target UDS19627 (RA=02:18:17.1, DEC=-05:21:38.8 ) was chosen from the UKIRT Ultra Deep Survey (UDS), as a (K-band) bright example of a compact, quiescent, massive galaxy with z 2 williams2010 . Based on the broad band photometry (See Table tab.photometry ), the target is a very massive, quiescent galaxy at z_ phot =2.02_ 0.08 ^ +0.07 with K=20.19 0.01 . Images of the target are shown in Figure fig.images .

Adopting this value, we find M_ dyn = 3.26 0.99 10^ 11 M_ . From simulations it is predicted that will depend on the structure of the galaxy, increasing with smaller Sersic n values cappellari2006 . For the best fitting n=1.47 , a value 7.5 is predicted.

The spectrum of UDS19627 has the characteristic ``shark-tooth'' shape of a post starburst galaxy, consistent with the low derived SFR, and constraints on the stellar age and the prominent Balmer series absorption lines. The derived magnitudes (see Table tab.galfit ) corresponds to an L_K 15L_K^* galaxy assuming K^*_ z=2 =21.3 , marchesini2007 .

sources.summary: The paper includes UDS-19627 as a massive high-redshift ETG with X-Shooter spectra, reanalyses its stellar population, and places it on the local mean scaling relation.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the other 3 targets, COSMOS- 7447 , UDS- 19627 and UDS- 29410 have been previously analyzed by vandesande (and references therein), who performed the measurement of the velocity dispersion exploiting the high resolution of X-Shooter data, in order to study its evolution to z 0 . They derive stellar population parameters as well as performing spectral fitting on the binned spectra ( 10 ) using the FAST code kriek09 . Differently from this work they used the models with an exponentially declining star formation history and a IMF. In this work, we perform a detailed spectral and spectro-photometric analysis of the galaxy stellar populations, using a novel approach that combine optical and UV indices and consider several options for the origin of the UV flux besides that of ongoing star formation.

The four galaxies point to two distinct behaviours: COSMOS-307881 and COSMOS-7447 seem to lie on the local scaling relation related to the central values, while UDS-19627 and UDS-29410 on the one built with the local mean values. Different scenarios may be envisaged for the formation and evolution of these galaxies.

gmw_00002330.name: UDS-29410
gmw_00002330.overview: UDS-29410 is a massive, high-redshift early-type galaxy [arXiv:1912.00084]. Spectroscopic analysis of its stellar population places the galaxy on the local mean scaling relation [arXiv:1912.00084].

sources.summary: The paper includes UDS-29410 as a massive high-redshift ETG with X-Shooter spectra, reanalyses its stellar population, and places it on the local mean scaling relation.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the other 3 targets, COSMOS- 7447 , UDS- 19627 and UDS- 29410 have been previously analyzed by vandesande (and references therein), who performed the measurement of the velocity dispersion exploiting the high resolution of X-Shooter data, in order to study its evolution to z 0 . They derive stellar population parameters as well as performing spectral fitting on the binned spectra ( 10 ) using the FAST code kriek09 . Differently from this work they used the models with an exponentially declining star formation history and a IMF. In this work, we perform a detailed spectral and spectro-photometric analysis of the galaxy stellar populations, using a novel approach that combine optical and UV indices and consider several options for the origin of the UV flux besides that of ongoing star formation.

The four galaxies point to two distinct behaviours: COSMOS-307881 and COSMOS-7447 seem to lie on the local scaling relation related to the central values, while UDS-19627 and UDS-29410 on the one built with the local mean values. Different scenarios may be envisaged for the formation and evolution of these galaxies.

gmw_00002331.name: PG1004+130
gmw_00002331.overview: PG 1004+130 is a low-redshift, radio-loud broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:1306.3500, arXiv:1403.0958] that has also been identified as a type 1 Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2206.02129]. The source features a hybrid Fanaroff-Riley radio morphology [arXiv:1412.6931, arXiv:2212.07061] and exhibits strong variability in its broad ultraviolet absorption lines [arXiv:1505.01161, arXiv:2206.02129].

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1004+130 as a lobe-dominated BAL quasar with modest or Compton-thick X-ray absorption, atypical hybrid FR morphology, weak radio core, and weak observed X-rays partly arising from the jet.

quotes.quote: XMM-Newton observation of PG 1004+130 shows only minimal intrinsic absorption, while later Chandra X-ray observations of this source revealed modest X-ray absorption ( N_ H 10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) with partial-covering absorbed model miller . Recently, PG 1004+130 has been also detected in the softer NuSTAR band (4-20 keV) but not in its harder bands ( >20 keV ), which may imply significant Compton-thick obscuration ( N_ H 7 10^ 24 cm^ 2 ).

There is no information about the parsec scale structure of PG 1004+130, but the VLA large scale morphology is not typical. PG 1004+130 has been described as a hybrid object with two different FR structures on both sides of the source gopwii .

In the case of lobe-dominated PG 1004+130, the radio core is probably too weak to produce any significant part of observed X-ray emission. A large contribution from the X-ray emission of the accretion disk and corona is possible in our model. However, in reality the observed X-ray emission of PG 1004+130 is much weaker, and such large suppression can be caused by the absorbing gas present near the black hole. As a consequence, a large part of the X-ray emission observed in PG 1004+130 must arise in the radio jet.

sources.summary: The paper monitors PG 1004+130 as a radio-loud BAL quasar, finding stable low-column X-ray absorption, strong UV BAL variability, and a synchrotron-like X-ray knot in its radio jet.

quotes.quote: We present the results of recent Chandra , XMM-Newton , and Hubble Space Telescope observations of the radio-loud (RL), broad absorption line (BAL) quasar PG 1004+130. We compare our new observations to archival X-ray and UV data, creating the most comprehensive, high signal-to-noise, multi-epoch, spectral monitoring campaign of a RL BAL quasar to date.

The X-ray absorber has a low column density of N H =8 10 20 4 10 21 when it is assumed to be fully covering the X-ray emitting region, and its properties do not vary significantly between the 4 observations. This suggests the observed absorption is not related to the typical ``shielding gas'' commonly invoked in BAL quasar models, but is likely due to material further from the central black hole. In contrast, the BAL shows strong variability. The equivalent width (EW) in 2014 is EW=11.24 0.56 , showing a fractional increase of EW / EW =1.16 0.11 from the 2003 observation, 3183 days earlier in the rest-frame.

By combining observations we create an exposure 2.5 deeper than studied previously, with which to investigate the nature of the X-ray jet and extended diffuse X-ray emission. An X-ray knot, likely with a synchrotron origin, is detected in the radio jet 8 (30 kpc) from the central X-ray source with a spatial extent of 4 (15 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper describes Q1004+130 as a BAL quasar with two polarization components, gradual PA rotation, and possible ISP contribution.

quotes.quote: Q1004+130 ISP or second polarization component ^1 The polarization gradually increases toward shorter wavelengths in this object, and the PA rotates also gradually (Fig. res-Q1004+130 ). The data points on the q u plane align linearly (Fig. res-Q1004+130-qu ), and its alignment probably suggests that it has one polarization component at PA 45 \ and another component with P being roughly wavelength-independent and larger than 0.8 based on the distance between the alignment line and the origin (for this lower limit case, the PA of this component is at PA 90 ). The latter component might be an ISP: we do not have stars with large estimated distance within 4 , but at radii 4-5 , several stars show a polarization roughly aligned at PA 75-85 , though the polarization are all low 0.1 (one has an estimated distance of 100pc). We need a good observation of a Galactic star to check the ISP.

In any case, the polarization property is quite different from the previous objects. The polarization seems to consist of two components: one of them is probably quite wavelength-independent, but the other has quite a blue polarized flux, which might be due to dust scattering. This quasar is one of the few Broad Absorption Line (BAL) quasars having both a well-defined radio jet axis and good spectropolarimetric data. The evidence for the BAL property of this quasar was presented in Wi99 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1004+130 as a radio-loud BAL quasar excluded from correlations because of likely X-ray obscuration, estimating its X-ray luminosity is abnormally weak.

quotes.quote: Most objects in this sample are radio quiet, except for 3 sources that were reported as radio loud, i.e. PG 1004+130, RBS 0875 and PG 1512+370. High quality XMM-Newton EPIC X-ray spectra and SDSS optical spectra are available for every source in this sample. In addition, simultaneous optical/UV photometric data from the XMM-Newton OM monitor are available for 37 sources. We exclude PG 1004+130 from all correlations as it is a BAL quasar, so its X-ray flux is likely to be heavily obscured even though it does not show clear evidence for absorption edges (Miller et al. 2006).

However, PG 1004+130 is the only BAL quasar in our sample, whose X-ray emission after correcting for intrinsic absorption, was reported as 0.73 dex weaker than normal PG RLQs normalized to similar optical/UV luminosities (Miller et al. 2006). Using the above equations we estimate that PG 1004+130's X-ray luminosity is weaker than normal Type 1 AGNs by 1.0 0.3 dex, which is slightly higher than Miller et al. (2006)'s estimation of 0.73 dex weaker than normal PG radio-loud quasars with similar optical/UV luminosities based on their X-ray spectral analysis.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1004+130 as an optically bright, radio-loud BAL quasar with clear absorption lines and unusually weak X-ray emission despite only modest fitted absorption.

quotes.quote: The other three are also X-ray weak but the nature of their X-ray weakness is not as well understood; among these three, we chose the two lower redshift objects as our targets here (PG 1004+130 at z=0.241 and PG 1700+518 at z=0.292 ; the other object is PG 0043+039 at z=0.384 ). PG 1004+130 and PG 1700+518 are among the optically brightest BAL quasars known; see Figure fig-lz . PG 1004+130 and PG 1700+518 show clearly detected BALs e.g., Pettini1985,Wills1999,Brandt2000,Young2007 .

PG 1004+130 is radio loud with a radio-loudness parameter R 210 ( R=f_ 5 GHz /f_ 4400 ); such radio-loud BAL quasars are relatively rare e.g., Shankar2008,Miller2009 . In the X-ray band, PG 1004+130 and PG 1700+518 do not show the expected level of X-ray emission (determined using the _ OX - L_ 2500 relation) for a luminous quasar at energies below 7 -10 keV. The \ and XMM-Newton spectra of PG 1004+130 show modest X-ray absorption ( N_ H 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) with a partial-covering absorbed power-law model, and the derived photon index is consistent with the typical value for radio-loud quasars ( 1.55 ; e.g., Page2005 ); however, the X-ray continuum flux after correction for this absorption is still 11 times lower than that expected from its optical/UV flux Miller2006,Miller2011 .

sources.summary: J1007+1248 is a radio-loud quasar/type 1 Seyfert at z 0.24 with a 2103.1-day optical periodicity, a massive SMBH, and reported X-ray and UV variations.

quotes.quote: Analyzing the archival optical data obtained from large transient surveys, namely the Catalina Real-Transient Survey (CRTS) and the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF), we find periodicities of 2169.7, 2103.1, and 1462.6 day in sources J0122+1032, J1007+1248 (or PG 1004+1248), and J2131 1127, respectively.

J1007+1248 The source J1007+1248 (hereafter J1007), known as PG 1004+130 (or 4C 13.41), is a radio loud quasar wbl99 and has been relatively well studied. There is an optical spectrum of it, taken on 2004 Feb. 20, in the SDSS database (SDSS J100726.10+124856.2) and it has been identified as a type 1 Seyfert galaxy (e.g., tob+14 ). It has redshift z 0.24 , and based on the full width at half maximum of the broad H line and the optical continuum luminosity, was estimated to contain a SMBH with mass M_t = 1.87 10^9 M_ vp06 . sco+15 conducted detailed multi-epoch studies of the source at X-ray and ultraviolet (UV) wavelengths, and found significant variations for the X-ray flux and broad UV absorption lines.
This source is relatively bright at the optical and MIR wavelengths, with ZTF and CRTS magnitudes around 15 and WISE W1 and W2 magnitudes between 10--12.

sources.summary: The paper presents PG1004+130 as a restarted steep-spectrum quasar with jet-core-counterjet emission, hybrid FRI/FRII structure, complex hotspot magnetic fields, and environmental asymmetry.

quotes.quote: PG1004+130 This SSRQ ( aka 4C+13.41) appears to be a restarted source as observed in the bright jet-core-counterjet emission on the 30 scales (Figure fig3 ). A clear surface brightness discontinuity is observed between the jet-counterjet and the larger lobe emission. The inferred B-field is transverse to the jet direction for the optically thick radio core.

The terminal region on the western side does not show a typical B-field configuration in its hotspot (i.e., transverse to the jet direction). This could indicate weakening of the hotspot as the previous episode of activity has died down. Gopal-Krishna2000 have classified this quasar as a hybrid FRI/FRII morphology source and Miller2006 found an X-ray counterpart a little upstream of the radio FR I jet, with a flat photon index and a concave SED more typical of FR II quasar jets than FR I jets.

sources.summary: The paper uses PG1004+130 as a representative QSO fit example, showing that neighboring dust-cloud distance models fit worse and that the best fit has a well-defined 52 pc dust distance.

quotes.quote: In fig. fig:bestfit we demonstrate the quality of the fit for PG1004+130, which has been chosen as more or less representative for the whole sample. We compare the best-fit model with two fits using models that differ by a multiplicative factor of 10 in R_ dust . Inspection of the residuals shows that these two fits are significantly worse than the best fit model.

In the lower right panel of fig. fig:bestfit we show ^2 as a function of dust cloud distance for this QSO. It can be seen that ^2 is a smooth function with a quite well defined minimum at a dust distance of R_ dust =52 pc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J100726.10 + 124856.2/PG 1004+130 as a low-redshift lobe-dominated BAL radio-loud quasar with strongly variable C IV absorption, the survey's largest BAL equivalent-width changes, and O VI variability.

quotes.quote: J100726.10 + 124856.2 : This low-redshift lobe-dominated BAL RLQ (PG 1004+130, z=0.241 , M_ B =-25.6 ; Wills et al. 1999; Brandt et al. 2000) is known to show variable X-ray absorption (Miller et al. 2006). Here we show that it also possesses strongly variable C IV absorption (Figure 7), for example with the primary BAL greatly diminishing in depth from the January 1986 IUE observation to the March 2003 HST /STIS spectrum ( 14 rest-frame years). PG 1004+130 displays the greatest absolute and fractional equivalent-width variations in our survey of BAL RLQs. The lower-velocity absorption is also seen at higher ionizations, including O VI . A comparison of the O VI absorption from recent FUV COS/G130M observations with a GHRS spectrum from 1996 shows perhaps a slight increase in the depths of the troughs (Figure 7), but this modest variability is of substantially lower amplitude than is seen in the primary C IV BAL at higher velocities.

sources.summary: The paper describes J100726.10+124856.2 as low-redshift PG 1004+130, a hybrid-morphology BAL radio-loud quasar with variable X-ray absorption, X-ray jet emission, and X-ray weakness.

quotes.quote: J100726.10+124856.2 (also PG 1004+130) is a low-redshift ( z=0.24 ) RLQ in which BALs were discovered by Wills et al. (1999); we use their values of BI=850 km s ^ 1 and V_ max =10000 km s ^ 1 since the SDSS spectrum does not cover the C IV region. It is also a hybrid-morphology radio source (Gopal-Krishna Wiita 2000), with an edge-brightened lobe opposite a broadening edge-darkened jet. It is perhaps the best-studied BAL RLQ at X-ray frequencies: deep XMM-Newton and Chandra observations show X-ray absorption variability and also reveal X-ray jet emission (Miller et al. 2006). PG 1004+130 is X-ray weak relative to comparable non-BAL RLQs.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1004+130 as an anomalous BAL quasar with unusually weak, likely absorbed X-ray emission and excludes it from regression analysis.

quotes.quote: The first is PG 1004+130, a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar, whose X-ray was reported as being extraordinarily weak. Although its X-ray spectrum does not show clear absorption edges, it is nevertheless likely to be heavily absorbed, so that the remaining X-rays may have a different origin such as a sub-parsec-scale jet. It has been suggested that the X-ray emission from PG 1004+130, after correcting for its intrinsic absorption, is 0.73 dex weaker than normal PG radio loud quasars (PG RLQs) normalized to similar optical/UV luminosities (Miller et al. 2006). Due to its distinct X-ray spectrum (and correspondingly different X-ray parameters), we did not include this source in our regression analysis.

sources.summary: PG 1004+130 is one of the radio-loud quasars with FR II kiloparsec structure and resolved parsec-scale core-jet emission with high brightness temperature and variability.

quotes.quote: We have also studied the radio-emitting properties of the four RLQs. On kiloparsec scales, all four RLQs display Fanaroff-Riley type II radio structures 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1994AJ....108.1163K . On parsec scales, three RLQs (PG 1004+130, PG1048-090, PG 1425+267) exhibit resolved structures, featuring a bright and compact component located at one end of an elongated structure (Figure fig:RLQ ). This brightest component exhibits a high brightness temperature ( >10^9 K), a flat spectrum ( 0.40< ^ 7.6 _ 1.6 <-0.25 ) (Table table:Spectral_Index ), and shows significant variability (Figure fig:SED-RLQ ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies PG 1004+130 among four highly absorbed Palomar-Green quasars whose spectra require an absorbed power-law component with column densities near 10^23 cm^-2.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the remaining four objects (PG 1004+130, 1411+442, 1535+547, and 2214+139) show broad excess emission at energies above 2--3 keV. We added an absorbed power-law component to the model used above to represent this component. This model provides a good description of the spectra. The hydrogen column densities of the absorbed power law are 10^ 23 cm^ 2 for all the cases.

gmw_00002332.name: RX J1140.1+0307
gmw_00002332.overview: RX J1140.1+0307 is a resolved spiral galaxy [arXiv:1108.4700] hosting a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1108.4700, arXiv:1504.06190]. This active nucleus contains one of the lowest known black hole masses in an active galaxy [arXiv:1504.06190] and exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:0801.3663, arXiv:1504.06190].

sources.summary: The paper derives a power density spectrum for J1140+0307 from a good 40 ks X-ray lightcurve; a broken power-law improves the fit, though parameters remain poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: J1140+0307 is another AGN in our sample that has good signal-to-noise, 40 long X-ray lightcurve. We have derived the PDS of J1140+0307 using the EPIC-pn data. First we extracted a lightcurve with 7.34 bins. The source lightcurve was corrected for background contribution though the background rate was stable throughout the observation. As before we calculated the PDS and rebinned logarithmically into bins of sizes 15.

A simple power-law plus constant model provided a minimum ^2 = 213 for 177 dof with power-law index steeper than 1.26 . Using a broken power-law model in place of the simple power-law improved the fit. However, all the parameters were not well constrained.

sources.summary: The paper reverberation-maps GH08, a resolved spiral AGN host, finding a short lag, narrow Seyfert-like broad lines, and an intermediate-mass black-hole upper limit.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a reverberation mapping (RM) campaign on the black hole (BH) associated with the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in SDSS J114008.71 + 030711.4 (hereafter GH08). This object is selected from a sample of 19 candidate intermediate mass BHs (M _ BH < 10^ 6 M _ ) found by 2004ApJ...610..722G in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS). We used the Hobby-Eberly Telescope to obtain 30 spectra over a period of 178 days in an attempt to resolve the reverberation time lag ( ) between the continuum source and the broad line region (BLR) in order to determine the radius of the BLR ( ) in GH08.

GH08 has a redshift z = 0.0811 , which is actually the median of the entire sample. The SDSS images show a resolved spiral galaxy with a moderately luminous AGN. The SDSS spectrum of GH08 has fairly narrow broad lines typical of narrow line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) type AGN, which are typically thought to have lower M _ BH but are accreting at close to the Eddington limit for a review see 2000NewAR..44..455G . While line ratios as measured from the SDSS spectrum would place GH08 in the HII region of the BPT diagram see for example 2006MNRAS.372..961K , there are several detections of an X-ray point source in both ROSAT and Chandra 2007ApJ...656...84G at the AGN location.

Using the measured H full-width-at-half-maximum of 703 110 \ and an upper limit for \ = 6 light days, we find M _ BH 5.8 10^ 5 \ as an upper limit to the BH virial mass in GH08, which implies super Eddington accretion.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton observations of RX J1140.1+0307, a low-mass NLS1, finding strong soft excess and fast X-ray variability, with covariance evidence favoring low-temperature Comptonisation over smeared reflection.

quotes.quote: RX J1140.1+0307 (hereafter RX1140) is a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) with one of the lowest black hole masses known in an AGN (M 10^6 M _ ). We show results from two new XMM-Newton observations, showing soft 2-10 keV spectra, a strong excess at lower energies, and fast X-ray variability as is typical of this class. The soft excess can be equally well fit by either low temperature Comptonisation or highly smeared, ionised reflection models, but we use a covariance analysis of the fast X-ray variability as well as lag and coherence spectra to show that the low temperature Comptonisation model gives a better description of the break in variability properties between soft and hard X-rays. Both models also require an additional component at the softest energies, as expected from the accretion disc. However, this inner disc spectrum does not join smoothly onto the variable optical and far UV emission (which should be produced in the outer disc) unless the mass is underestimated by an order of magnitude.

gmw_00002333.name: HATLAS J090045.4+004125
gmw_00002333.overview: HATLAS J090045.4+004125 is a strongly lensed, dusty star-forming submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 6.02 [arXiv:1904.11185, arXiv:2209.15226]. Observations reveal a compact central region of warm, dense molecular gas [arXiv:2209.15226] alongside detected CO and dust continuum emissions [arXiv:1904.11185].

sources.summary: The paper treats HATLAS J090045.4+004125 as a z>6 submillimetre galaxy with CO and continuum detections, finding SNe would need maximal dust production while AGB stars cannot explain its dust.

quotes.quote: HATLAS J090045.4+004125 hereafter HATLAS; HATLAS is one of a few submillimetre galaxies above z = 6 (together with that reported by Riechers2013 ). HATLAS was selected using the Herschel Space Observatory within the Herschel Astrophysical Terahertz Large Area Survey Eales2010,Bourne2016,Valiante2016 . Emission lines for ^ 12 CO(6-5) and ^ 12 CO(5-4) and the 1 mm continuum were detected by the Large Millimeter Telescope HATLAS . The mass of molecular gas was determined from the CO lines, whereas the dust mass was calculated in two ways: based on fitting photometric measurements to modified black-body function and via modelling using the MAGPHYS SED code (Table Tab1 ; HATLAS ). The dynamical mass was calculated using the isotropic virial estimator (equation 5 in HATLAS ).

The dust yield per SN required to explain dust in the HATLAS galaxy is close to 1 M_ , so if they are responsible for the bulk of the dust production, then they would need to be maximally efficient and not to destroy any dust. AGB stars cannot be responsible for the dust production in this galaxy because the required dust yield per star is more than 10 times higher than the theoretical value.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA non-detection of [O I] 63 micron emission from the strongly lensed z=6.03 dusty galaxy G09.83808, overturning a previous APEX detection.

quotes.quote: We report a non-detection of the [ O 1 ] 63- m emission line from the z=6.03 galaxy G09.83808 using ALMA Band 9 observations, refuting the previously claimed detection with APEX by Rybak2020a ; the new upper limit on the [ O 1 ] 63- m flux is almost 20-times lower.

Although [ O 1 ] _ 63 has been detected in a number of z=1-3 galaxies (e.g., Sturm2010,Coppin2012, Brisbin2015, Zhang2018b, Wagg2020 ), to date, there has been just a single claimed [ O 1 ] _ 63 detection at z 3 : in henceforth R20 Rybak2020a , we reported a detection of the [ O 1 ] _ 63 line in G09.83808, a strongly lensed, dusty star-forming galaxy at redshift z=6.02 Zavala2018 , using the SEPIA660 spectrometer Baryshev2015, Belitsky2018 on the APEX telescope. The SEPIA660 data showed a significant ( 5.2 ) signal at 675.45 GHz ( z_ [ OI ] =6.0246 , consistent with the systemic redshift z=6.0244 0.0003 , Tsujita2022 ). The detection was reproduced by two team members using several approaches. The measured line flux was 22 5 Jy km s ^ 1 which corresponds to a sky-plane (lensing-uncorrected) luminosity of L_ [ OI ] 63 =(5.4 1.2) 10^ 10 L_ ; almost four times brighter than the [ C 2 ] 158- m line Zavala2018 .
We do not see any significant [ O 1 ] _ 63 emission: neither does uv plane analysis reveal any excess emission at the expected frequency of the [ O 1 ] _ 63 line.

sources.summary: The paper reports [O I] 63-micron emission from the z=6.027 lensed DSFG G09.83808, deriving high line luminosity, neutral-gas cooling dominance, dense gas, and a strong far-UV field.

quotes.quote: We report a ground-based detection of the [ O 1 ] 63- m line in a z=6.027 gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxy (DSFG) G09.83808 using the APEX SEPIA 660 receiver, the first unambiguous detection of the [ O 1 ] _ 63 line beyond redshift 3, and the first obtained from the ground. The [ O 1 ] _ 63 line is robustly detected at 22 5 Jy km s ^ 1 , corresponding to an intrinsic (de-lensed) luminosity of (5.4 1.3) 10^ 9 L _ . With the [ O 1 ] _ 63 / [ C 2 ] luminosity ratio of 4, the [ O 1 ] _ 63 line is the main coolant of the neutral gas in this galaxy, in agreement with model predictions. The high [ O 1 ] _ 63 luminosity compensates for the pronounced [ C 2 ] deficit ( [ C 2 ] /FIR 4 10^ 4 ). Using photon-dominated region models, we derive a source-averaged gas density n=10^ 4.0 cm ^ 3 , and far-UV field strength G=10^4 G_0 , comparable to the z=2-4 DSFG population.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA CO(12-11) emission from lensed z=6.02 G09-83808, finding compact warm dense molecular gas and a likely central concentration.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of the CO(12-11) line emission toward G09-83808 (or H-ATLAS J090045.4+004125), a strongly-lensed submillimeter galaxy at z = 6.02 , with Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array observations. Combining previously detected [ O III ] 88 , [ N II ] 205 , and dust continuum at 0.6 mm and 1.5 mm, we investigate the physical properties of the multi-phase interstellar medium in G09-83808. A source-plane reconstruction reveals that the region of the CO(12-11) emission is compact ( R_ e, CO =0.49^ +0.29 _ 0.19 kpc ) and roughly coincides with that of the dust continuum.

Non-local thermodynamic equilibrium radiative transfer modeling of CO spectral-line energy distribution reveals that most of the CO(12-11) emission comes from a warm (kinetic temperature of T_ kin =320 170 K) and dense ( (n_ H2 / cm^ 3 )=5.4 0.6 ) gas, indicating that the warm and dense molecular gas is concentrated in the central 0.5-kpc region.

gmw_00002334.name: SPT0311-58E
gmw_00002334.overview: SPT0311-58E is an obscured starburst galaxy located at a redshift of 6.9 [arXiv:1904.11185, arXiv:2301.02313]. It features a clumpy stellar structure with high internal nebular extinction and a physical association between its ionized and cold gas kinematics [arXiv:2301.02313].

sources.summary: The paper describes SPT0311-58E as the East component of a z=6.9 system with gas, dust, stellar mass, size, and linewidth constraints; SNe could explain its dust only with efficient production.

quotes.quote: SPT-S J031132-5823.4 (SPT0311–58) was discovered using the South Pole Telescope Mocanu2013 , and confirmed at a redshift of 6.9 Strandet2017 . With high-resolution ALMA observations, detected continuum, [ CII ] 158 m and [ OIII ] 88 m lines of two components of SPT0311–58, named East and West, and determined their gas and dust masses. Only for SPT0311–58 East was the stellar mass determined because no stellar emission was detected for the West component.

Only for one object, SPT0311-58E, was it possible to determine the required dust yield per star with the three different assumptions on stellar mass, and they do not differ from each other by more than their uncertainties. For this galaxy the required dust yield per SN is about 0.1--1 M_ . This is close to the maximum dust yield predicted by simulations and to the highest observed values. Therefore, it is in principle possible that dust in this galaxy was formed by SNe, but that would require weak dust destruction and very efficient production.

sources.summary: The paper describes the E galaxy as an obscured starburst component with high Pa-derived extinction, a 383 solar-mass-per-year SFR lower limit, clumpy stellar mass, and ionized-gas kinematics matching [C II].
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The Pa spectra were extracted in an aperture radius equal to 0.5 ( 2.7 kpc) and centred at positions (RA [ deg ] , Dec [ deg ] ) equal to (47.8891167, 58.3926333) for the E galaxy and (47.888027, 58.392719) for the W galaxy (see white apertures in Figure fig:MRS_app ).

quotes.quote: Even though the ALMA observations imply very high star formation rates (SFRs) in the eastern (E) and western (W) galaxies of the system, the H line is, strikingly, not detected in our MRS observations. This fact, together with the detection of the ionised gas phase in Pa , implies very high internal nebular extinction with lower limits ( A_ V ) of 4.2 (E) and 3.9 mag (W) as well as even larger values (5.6 (E) and 10.0 (W)) by spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting analysis. The extinction-corrected Pa lower limits of the SFRs are 383 and 230 M _ yr ^ 1 for the E and W galaxies, respectively. This represents 50 of the SFRs derived from the [ C II ] 158 m line and infrared light for the E galaxy and as low as 6 for the W galaxy. The MIRIM observations reveal a clumpy stellar structure, with each clump having 3 to 5 10 ^ 9 M _ mass in stars, leading to a total stellar mass of 2.0 and 1.5 10 ^ 10 M _ for the E and W galaxies, respectively.
The kinematics of the ionised gas in the E galaxy agrees with the known [ C II ] gas kinematics, indicating a physical association between the ionised gas and the cold ionised or neutral gas clumps.
